## [5.4.6] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-09 ‚ÄĒ Detector edge-case fixes ‚ÄĒ em-dash absorption + 2nd-person system polysemy

**Surgical detector release.** Closes the three highest user-impact edge cases the v5.4.5 64-turn audit deferred. Pass-rate **57/64 ‚Üí 60/64 (89 % ‚Üí 94 %)** on the same audit.

### What changed

**1. Em-dash StatementOfOccupation absorber.** Pre-v5.4.6 ¬ę–ú–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ ‚ÄĒ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č.¬Ľ tokenised as `[¬ę–ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ¬Ľ, ¬ę‚ÄĒ¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č¬Ľ]`; the v5.3.5 possessive-anchor pattern read `tokens[i+1]` directly, hit the em-dash, called the alphabetic-filter which returned an empty string, and bailed. The user's occupation never reached `session.occupation`, so downstream ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ fell to a generic noun-definition surface. Fix: walk past punctuation-only tokens between the possessive anchor and the value. Sister fix in `extract_secondary_profile_facts` so the secondary-extraction code path benefits the same way.

**2. System-identity polysemy on AskName.** Pre-v5.4.6 ¬ę–°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –į—ā—č“£—č–∑ –ļ—Ė–ľ?¬Ľ (¬ęWhat is YOUR name?¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ about adam) routed to `Intent::AskName` and surfaced the user's stored name (¬ę–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ —ā–į–Ĺ—č—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ –Ķ–ī—Ė“£—Ė–∑¬Ľ). Two-half fix:
- [`detect_ask_name`](crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs) now bails when 2nd-person possessive ¬ę—Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£¬Ľ / ¬ę—Ā–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£¬Ľ appears WITHOUT 1st-person ¬ę–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ that pattern is unambiguously about adam, not the user.
- [`detect_ask_about_system`](crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs) gains an `asks_system_name` predicate routing the same surface forms to `SystemAspect::General`. The canonical adam-introduction template (¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –į–ī–į–ľ, —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ –į—ā–į—É—č–ľ Agglutinative Reasoning Kernel (ARK)‚Ä¶¬Ľ) now fires.

**3. System-identity for speaking-language.** Pre-v5.4.6 ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė–∑?¬Ľ routed to `Definition` over the noun ¬ę—ā—Ė–Ľ¬Ľ and surfaced the anatomical-organ definition (¬ę–Ę—Ė–Ľ ‚ÄĒ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—É –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ—Ā—Ė.¬Ľ). Distinct from the existing `Implementation` aspect (programming language ‚Üí "what code stack are you written in"). New `asks_speaking_language` predicate matching `(—Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė“£ / —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė–∑ / –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑ / –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£) + (“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ / “õ–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ / “õ–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ / ‚Ä¶)` routes to `SystemAspect::General` for the canonical Kazakh-only declaration.

### Live REPL ‚ÄĒ before / after

| Query | Pre-v5.4.6 | Post-v5.4.6 |
|---|---|---|
| –ú–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ ‚ÄĒ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č. | (occupation never absorbed) | (absorbed; downstream ¬ę–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ recalls ¬ę–Ď–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ī—Ė“£—Ė–∑.¬Ľ) |
| –°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –į—ā—č“£—č–∑ –ļ—Ė–ľ? | ¬ę–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ —ā–į–Ĺ—č—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ –Ķ–ī—Ė“£—Ė–∑.¬Ľ (returns user's name) | ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –į–ī–į–ľ, —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ –į—ā–į—É—č–ľ Agglutinative Reasoning Kernel (ARK).¬Ľ |
| –°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė–∑? | ¬ę–Ę—Ė–Ľ ‚ÄĒ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—É –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ—Ā—Ė.¬Ľ (anatomical organ) | ¬ęARK ‚ÄĒ Agglutinative Reasoning Kernel, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–ī–į–ľ –į—ā—ā—č “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–ľ—Ė–Ĺ.¬Ľ |

### Tests

- New live holdout [`live_holdout_v5460_detector.json`](data/eval/live_holdout_v5460_detector.json) + [Rust runner](crates/adam-dialog/tests/live_holdout_v5460_detector.rs) ‚ÄĒ **5 cases**: em-dash absorption, 2 system-name variants (¬ę–°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –į—ā—č“£—č–∑ –ļ—Ė–ľ?¬Ľ / ¬ę–°–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č“£ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ), 2 speaking-language variants (¬ę–°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė–∑?¬Ľ / ¬ę–°–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė“£?¬Ľ). Pass-rate floor 100 %.
- 2 new unit tests in [`codex_round3_v5035`](crates/adam-dialog/tests/codex_round3_v5035.rs) for `extract_secondary_profile_facts` em-dash handling: `secondary_extraction_handles_em_dash_form_v546`, `secondary_extraction_handles_em_dash_form_with_compound_v546`.

### Deferred to v5.4.7+

4 detector edge cases from the v5.4.5 audit remain:
- ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ (–į–ī–į–ľ polysemy: system identity vs human concept)
- Possessive multiword ¬ęX-–Ĺ—Ė“£ Y-—č¬Ľ (e.g. ¬ę–Ď–į—Ā ‚ÄĒ –ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ genitive form vs the registered ¬ę–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė¬Ľ)
- Curriculum-stage routing ¬ę”®–ľ—Ė—Ä –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£—Ė –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ / ¬ę“ö–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ī—Ė?¬Ľ

### Verified

- Workspace 1 155 + 7 new (5 live-holdout + 2 unit) = **1 162 passing**
- 64-turn live REPL audit pass-rate **94 % (60/64)** ‚ÄĒ up from 89 % at v5.4.5
- `cargo fmt --all --check` clean; `cargo clippy --workspace --release --tests -- -D warnings` clean

**Stripe ‚ÄĒ Deterministic AI research (third audit-feedback iteration; cumulative pass-rate climbs 81 % ‚Üí 89 % ‚Üí 94 % across v5.4.0 ‚Üí v5.4.5 ‚Üí v5.4.6).**

## [5.4.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-09 ‚ÄĒ Detector hardening + 64-turn live REPL audit + Kazakh cuisine domain

**Substantive engineering release.** v5.4.5 lands the second half of the week-plan: multi-word topic extraction fixes, a 64-turn live REPL audit, 12 IsA-gap fixes surfaced by it, and the long-deferred kazakh_cuisine domain.

### What changed

**1. Multi-word topic extraction (v5.4.5 primary fix).** Pre-v5.4.5 ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä —Ā—Ė–Ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė—Ā—Ė –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ picked single-word ¬ę—Ā—Ė–Ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė—Ā¬Ľ (a verb-derived noun) as topic and surfaced a tangential proverb instead of a curated definition; ¬ę–ě—Ä–ľ–į–Ĺ ”©—Ä—ā—Ė –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ picked ¬ę”©—Ä—ā¬Ľ. The fix has two layers:

- **New world_core domain** [`data/world_core/natural_phenomena.jsonl`](data/world_core/natural_phenomena.jsonl) ‚ÄĒ 28 facts covering Kazakh natural-phenomena compounds (–∂–Ķ—Ä —Ā—Ė–Ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė—Ā—Ė / –∂–į–Ĺ–į—Ä—ā–į—É –į—ā“õ—č–Ľ–į—É—č / —Ā–Ķ–Ľ —ā–į—Ā“õ—č–Ĺ—č / “õ–į—Ä –ļ”©—ą–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė / –Ĺ–į–Ļ–∑–į“ď–į–Ļ / —Ü—É–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł / —ā–ĺ—Ä–Ĺ–į–ī–ĺ / “õ“Ī—Ä“ď–į“õ—ą—č–Ľ—č“õ / —Ā—É —ā–į—Ā“õ—č–Ĺ—č / –ĺ—Ä–ľ–į–Ĺ ”©—Ä—ā—Ė / –Ī–ĺ—Ä–į–Ĺ / –ī–į—É—č–Ľ) bridged through `—ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–ł “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā ‚Üí “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā`, `—ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–ł –į–Ņ–į—ā ‚Üí –į–Ņ–į—ā ‚Üí “õ–į—É—Ė–Ņ—ā—Ė –ĺ“õ–ł“ď–į`, and `–į—Ā—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ ‚Üí –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ`. Pre-v5.4.5 these compound subjects had no curated entry at all.
- **MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 11 compound subjects from the new domain (–∂–Ķ—Ä —Ā—Ė–Ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė—Ā—Ė / –∂–į–Ĺ–į—Ä—ā–į—É –į—ā“õ—č–Ľ–į—É—č / —Ā–Ķ–Ľ —ā–į—Ā“õ—č–Ĺ—č / “õ–į—Ä –ļ”©—ą–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė / —Ā—É —ā–į—Ā“õ—č–Ĺ—č / –ĺ—Ä–ľ–į–Ĺ ”©—Ä—ā—Ė / —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–ł “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā / —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–ł –į–Ņ–į—ā / –į—Ā—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ / —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä —Ä–į–∑—Ä—Ź–ī—č / “õ–į—É—Ė–Ņ—ā—Ė –ĺ“õ–ł“ď–į). The `world_core_multiword_coverage` invariant test enforces the auto-sync.

**2. 64-turn live REPL audit.** [`/tmp/audit_v545.txt`](data/eval/live_holdout_v5450_audit.json) ‚ÄĒ designed to stress-test the system with novel Kazakh phrasings spanning 11 categories: multi-word natural phenomena, yes/no IsA queries, definitional queries, anaphoric continuations, listing intent, system-identity queries, curriculum/Rust tutor, geography KZ, statement absorption, family/profile, farewell. Pre-fix pass-rate **52/64 (81 %)**; post-fix **57/64 (89 %)**. The 7 remaining failures are detector edge cases requiring code-level fixes (em-dash StatementOfOccupation absorber, system-identity polysemy on –į–ī–į–ľ/–°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –į—ā—č“£—č–∑ –ļ—Ė–ľ, possessive ¬ęX-–Ĺ—Ė“£ Y-—č¬Ľ topic extraction, curriculum-stage routing for ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£—Ė / “õ–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä) ‚ÄĒ deferred to v5.4.6+.

**3. IsA-gap fixes surfaced by the audit.** 12 compound IsA edges added to close pre-v5.4.5 dead-ends:

- Animals: [`anm_034`](data/world_core/animals.jsonl) ‚ÄĒ typed-fact misalignment fixed (kk text said ¬ę–ė—ā ‚ÄĒ “Į–Ļ –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä—č¬Ľ but facts only had `–ł—ā IsA —Ā“Į—ā“õ–ĺ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė`; now also has `–ł—ā IsA “Į–Ļ –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä—č` + the bridge `“Į–Ļ –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä—č IsA –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä`)
- Concept bridges: 11 new edges added to [`concept_bridges.jsonl`](data/world_core/concept_bridges.jsonl) ‚ÄĒ `“Į–Ļ IsA –Ī–į—Ā–Ņ–į–Ĺ–į IsA “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ—č—Ā`, `–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ IsA –ĺ“õ—É –ĺ—Ä–Ĺ—č IsA –ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ`, `–Ī–į—Ā IsA –ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė IsA –į“ď–∑–į –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė`, `–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č IsA –ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ņ –ł–Ķ—Ā—Ė IsA –į–ī–į–ľ`, `—ā–į—Ā IsA –∂–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑ –Ĺ”ô—Ä—Ā–Ķ IsA –∑–į—ā`, `–ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ņ IsA ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā`

**4. Kazakh cuisine domain (the v5.4.0 plan deferred to today).** [`data/world_core/kazakh_cuisine.jsonl`](data/world_core/kazakh_cuisine.jsonl) ‚ÄĒ 19 facts covering “Ī–Ľ—ā—ā—č“õ —ā–į“ď–į–ľ–ī–į—Ä (–Ī–Ķ—Ā–Ī–į—Ä–ľ–į“õ / “õ–į–∑—č / —ą“Ī–∂—č“õ / “õ–į—Ä—ā–į / –∂–į–Ľ / –Ī–į—É—Ä—Ā–į“õ / –ļ—É—č—Ä–ī–į“õ / –Ņ–į–Ľ–į—É / “õ–į–Ļ–ľ–į“õ / “õ“Ī—Ä—ā) and –ī”ô—Ā—ā“Į—Ä–Ľ—Ė —Ā—É—Ā—č–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä (“õ—č–ľ—č–∑ / —ą“Ī–Ī–į—ā / –į–Ļ—Ä–į–Ĺ / —Ė—Ä–ļ—Ė—ā), with hub bridges (–ī”ô—Ā—ā“Į—Ä–Ľ—Ė —ā–į“ď–į–ľ IsA —ā–į“ď–į–ľ, “Ī–Ľ—ā—ā—č“õ —ā–į“ď–į–ľ IsA —ā–į“ď–į–ľ, –ī”ô—Ā—ā“Į—Ä–Ľ—Ė —Ā—É—Ā—č–Ĺ IsA —Ā—É—Ā—č–Ĺ, —Ā—É—Ā—č–Ĺ IsA —ā–į“ď–į–ľ). Closes audit gaps ¬ę–Ď–Ķ—Ā–Ī–į—Ä–ľ–į“õ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ / ¬ę“ö—č–ľ—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ —Ā—É—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ī–į?¬Ľ.

## [5.4.1] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-09 ‚ÄĒ README refactored to modern AI-repo conventions

Documentation-only release (text / positioning, no functional code change).

### What changed

The top-level [README.md](README.md) was **1 146 lines** with the entire release history (v5.4.0 down through v0.x.x) duplicated inline ‚ÄĒ a pattern that hurts discoverability and creates churn (every release had to be edited in two places: README and CHANGELOG).

**Before:** 1 146 lines, 239 inline release entries, ~617 lines of release-history block, deeply repetitive Capabilities section with version-by-version narrative.

**After:** 247 lines (-79 %), 3 high-level release-bullet pointers under a "Recent releases" section linking to CHANGELOG.md for the full log. Modern AI-repo structure (matches LangChain / vLLM / transformers / Llama README conventions):

1. Hero (logo, title, tagline, badges)
2. 30-second pitch (kept)
3. Why deterministic (3-disease / 3-goal table ‚ÄĒ distilled from MISSION.md)
4. Quick start (code block, fold-of-screen)
5. Architecture ‚ÄĒ ARK (compact crate table + pipeline diagram)
6. Demo (one canonical REPL transcript including v5.4.0's bridge query)
7. What's measurable (10-row metrics table)
8. FAQ (kept ‚ÄĒ high-signal for AI-scout discovery)
9. Recent releases (3 short bullets + link to CHANGELOG)
10. Open to collaboration (compact partner-class list + link to COLLABORATION.md)
11. Repository layout
12. Foundation policies (one-line link bar)
13. Out of scope (4 bullets)
14. License
15. Documentation footer (MISSION ¬∑ RESEARCH ¬∑ COLLABORATION ¬∑ AGENTS ¬∑ CHANGELOG ¬∑ roadmap ¬∑ cite)

### Why this matters

The previous README format was a project-log style, not a front-door style. The release history was a 100 % duplicate of CHANGELOG.md. Modern AI-repo convention treats README as the front door (what is this, why, how do I run it, where do I go for depth) and keeps the log in CHANGELOG. This refactor:

- Aligns with community expectations for AI / ML research repos
- Eliminates the dual-source-of-truth problem (every release had to update both README narrative AND CHANGELOG entry)
- Makes the repository scan-friendly for AI scouts and human first-time visitors
- Preserves all release history ‚ÄĒ nothing is lost; CHANGELOG.md is canonical and unchanged

### Verified

Workspace **1 155 passing** (unchanged ‚ÄĒ text-only release); cargo fmt clean; cargo check clean; all 36 internal links validated; CITATION.cff + codemeta.json + AGENTS.md + Cargo.toml version refs synced to 5.4.1.

**Stripe ‚ÄĒ Deterministic AI research (README magnet hardened ‚ÄĒ modern AI-repo conventions adopted).**

## [5.4.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-09 ‚ÄĒ Bridge data + bare yes/no IsA route ‚ÄĒ first user-facing payoff of the v5.3.x repositioning

**Substantive engineering release.** Every claim in this entry is measurable.

### What changed

**1. World-graph bridge data ‚ÄĒ closing dead-end abstract hubs.** The audit at the start of this release surfaced a class of hubs that had high inbound IsA edges but zero upstream connections: `—ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ł–Ķ—Ā—Ė` (4 inbound), `—ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –į“ď–∑–į` (5), `–ĺ–ľ—č—Ä—ā“õ–į–Ľ—č –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä` (5), `“õ“Ī—Ä–į–Ľ` (26), `—ā–į“ď–į–ľ` (16), `—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā` (11), `–ĺ—Ä—ā–į“ď–į—Ā—č—Ä–Ľ—č“õ –ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā` (16), `“õ–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ—č` (10), and 15+ more. These hubs collected animals / plants / objects but the chain dead-ended before reaching predicates a user actually asks about (—ā—Ė—Ä—Ė / –∑–į—ā / –Ī–ł–Ľ–Ķ—É—ą—Ė / etc.). Two new world_core domains close them:

- [`data/world_core/life_bridges.jsonl`](data/world_core/life_bridges.jsonl) ‚ÄĒ 5 facts wiring the biology chain (—ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ł–Ķ—Ā—Ė ‚Üí —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė, —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –į“ď–∑–į ‚Üí —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė, —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ł–Ķ—Ā—Ė ‚Üí —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –į“ď–∑–į, –ĺ–ľ—č—Ä—ā“õ–į–Ľ—č –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä ‚Üí –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä, –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–ļ–į—Ä–ł–ĺ—ā ‚Üí —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ł–Ķ—Ā—Ė)
- [`data/world_core/concept_bridges.jsonl`](data/world_core/concept_bridges.jsonl) ‚ÄĒ 15 facts wiring abstract concept hubs (“õ“Ī—Ä–į–Ľ ‚Üí –∑–į—ā, —ā–į“ď–į–ľ ‚Üí –∑–į—ā, ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā ‚Üí –Ņ—Ä–ĺ—Ü–Ķ—Ā—Ā, —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā ‚Üí “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā, —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –∑–į“£—č ‚Üí “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ –∑–į“£—č ‚Üí –∑–į“£–ī—č–Ľ—č“õ, –ĺ—Ä—ā–į“ď–į—Ā—č—Ä–Ľ—č“õ –ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā ‚Üí –ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā, “õ–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ—č ‚Üí —Ö–į–Ĺ ‚Üí –Ī–ł–Ľ–Ķ—É—ą—Ė ‚Üí –į–ī–į–ľ, –Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č ‚Üí “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č ‚Üí —Ā–į–Ľ–į, –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ ‚Üí –į“ď–∑–į –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė, –į“ď–∑–į –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė ‚Üí –į“ď–∑–į –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė, –Ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė ‚Üí —Ā–ł–Ņ–į—ā)

**Measurable impact:** initial facts 3 245 ‚Üí 3 265 (+20), derived facts 30 892 ‚Üí **35 469 (+4 577)**. Per-rule contribution: R1 IsA-transitivity +811, R11 InDomain-shared-target +3 454, R2 Has-inheritance +259, R10 InDomain-inheritance +16, R5 shared-IsA +37 (no R5 explosion ‚ÄĒ bridge facts didn't tie disparate semantic clusters). Average leverage **228 derivations per bridge fact**, 4√ó higher than the pre-bridge baseline of ~50/fact (memory `project_bridge_fact_leverage`) because R1+R10+R11 cascaded simultaneously through the new hubs.

**2. Bare yes/no IsA detector + planner route.** The bridge data was invisible without surfacing ‚ÄĒ pre-v5.4.0 ¬ę“ö–į—Ā“õ—č—Ä ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ fell through `QuestionShape::YesNoCheck` to the standard `Definition` path, which surfaced the most-central IsA fact about “õ–į—Ā“õ—č—Ä (¬ę–∂—č—Ä—ā“õ—č—ą¬Ľ) while ignoring the *predicate* the user asked about. Three additions close it:

- [`crates/adam-dialog/src/question_shape.rs`](crates/adam-dialog/src/question_shape.rs) ‚ÄĒ extended `is_yes_no_check` + new `extract_yes_no_isa_pair` recogniser. Bare ¬ę<X> ‚ÄĒ <Y> (–ľ–Ķ|–ľ–į|–Ī–į|–Ī–Ķ|–Ņ–į|–Ņ–Ķ)?¬Ľ pattern with em-dash or ASCII hyphen separator now routes to YesNoCheck. Conservative: a closed list of non-noun-phrase predicates (`–Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ` / `–Ī–į—Ä` / `–∂–ĺ“õ` / etc.) bails out so self-knowledge questions don't get misrouted.
- [`crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs`](crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs) ‚ÄĒ new `find_isa_chain(extracted, derived, subject, target)` BFS over both extracted (curated) and derived (rule-inferred) IsA facts, depth capped at 8 (matching reasoner's `MAX_ITER`). Returns the shortest chain `[subject, hop1, ‚Ä¶, target]` for citation, `None` otherwise. Wired into `turn_with_trace` right before `plan_response_with_epistemic`: when `QuestionShape::YesNoCheck` is detected and the (subject, predicate) pair extracts cleanly, the chain query writes outcome (`confirm` / `unknown`) and chain text into `extra_slots` for the planner.
- [`crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs`](crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs) ‚ÄĒ new `__yes_no_isa__` short-circuit in `plan_response_with_epistemic`, sister to the existing `__dismiss_contradiction__` / `__resolve_contradiction__` overrides. Routes to `unknown.yes_no_check.confirm` (3 templates) / `unknown.yes_no_check.unknown` (3 templates) added in [`data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`](data/dialog/templates/v1.toml).

**3. Live dialog test ‚ÄĒ paired with template tests, per the user's testing directive.** New live holdout [`data/eval/live_holdout_v5400_bridges.json`](data/eval/live_holdout_v5400_bridges.json) + [`crates/adam-dialog/tests/live_holdout_v5400_bridges.rs`](crates/adam-dialog/tests/live_holdout_v5400_bridges.rs) ‚ÄĒ 8 cases across 4 categories (life_chain / object_chain / ruler_chain / direct_isa / honest_unknown). Every case is a real Kazakh phrasing exercising the full pipeline against the v5.4.0 runtime artefacts. Pass-rate floor: **100 %**. The `none_substring` assertions catch regressions to the pre-v5.4.0 generic-fact behaviour.

### Live REPL ‚ÄĒ before / after

| Query | Pre-v5.4.0 | Post-v5.4.0 |
|---|---|---|
| “ö–į—Ā“õ—č—Ä ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –ľ–Ķ? | ¬ę“ö–į—Ā“õ—č—Ä ‚ÄĒ –∂—č—Ä—ā“õ—č—ą.¬Ľ (tangential) | ¬ę“ö–į—Ā“õ—č—Ä ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė. –Ď“Ī–Ľ “õ–į—Ā“õ—č—Ä ‚Üí —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė —ā—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –į—Ä“õ—č–Ľ—č —Ä–į—Ā—ā–į–Ľ–į–ī—č.¬Ľ |
| –Ď–į–Ľ—ā–į ‚ÄĒ –∑–į—ā –Ņ–į? | ¬ę–Ď–į–Ľ—ā–į ‚ÄĒ “õ“Ī—Ä–į–Ľ.¬Ľ (tangential) | ¬ę–ė”ô, –Ď–į–Ľ—ā–į ‚ÄĒ –∑–į—ā. –Ē”ô–Ľ–Ķ–Ľ —ā—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–≥—Ė: –Ī–į–Ľ—ā–į ‚Üí –∑–į—ā.¬Ľ |
| –Ė—č—Ä—ā“õ—č—ą ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –ľ–Ķ? | ¬ę–°“Ī—Ä–į“ď—č“£—č–∑ –∂—č—Ä—ā“õ—č—ą –∂–į–Ļ—č–Ĺ–ī–į –ľ–į?¬Ľ (clarifier dead-end) | ¬ę–Ē“Ī—Ä—č—Ā, –Ė—č—Ä—ā“õ—č—ą —ą—č–Ĺ—č–Ĺ–ī–į –ī–į —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė. –Ę—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–ļ: –∂—č—Ä—ā“õ—č—ą ‚Üí —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė.¬Ľ |
| “ö–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ—č ‚ÄĒ –Ī–ł–Ľ–Ķ—É—ą—Ė –ľ–Ķ? | ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ—č ‚ÄĒ —Ö–į–Ĺ.¬Ľ (one-step only) | ¬ę–ė”ô, “ö–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ—č ‚ÄĒ –Ī–ł–Ľ–Ķ—É—ą—Ė. –Ē”ô–Ľ–Ķ–Ľ —ā—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–≥—Ė: “õ–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ—č ‚Üí –Ī–ł–Ľ–Ķ—É—ą—Ė.¬Ľ |
| “ö—č–∑–į–Ĺ–į“õ ‚ÄĒ –ľ–į—ą–ł–Ĺ–į –ľ–į? | (would confirm or surface generic) | ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ “õ–ĺ—Ä—č–ľ–ī–į “ö—č–∑–į–Ĺ–į“õ ‚ÄĒ –ľ–į—ą–ł–Ĺ–į –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ –∂–ĺ“õ.¬Ľ (honest unknown) |

### Hygiene

- [`crates/adam-dialog/src/topic_extraction.rs`](crates/adam-dialog/src/topic_extraction.rs) ‚ÄĒ added `–į“ď–∑–į –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė`, `“ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ –∑–į“£—č`, `“ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č` to `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` (the `world_core_multiword_coverage` invariant test enforces this for every multi-word IsA target in world_core)
- New `question_shape` unit tests cover bare detection + pair extraction + non-NP-predicate rejection (4 tests, all passing)

### Verified

Workspace **1 155 passing** (+5 from new v5.4.0 tests) + 17 ignored slow integration; `cargo fmt --all --check` clean; `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` clean; live REPL battery of 8 dialog cases ‚ÄĒ all pass.

**Stripe ‚ÄĒ Deterministic AI research (first measurable user-facing payoff of the v5.3.x repositioning arc; +4 577 derivations + bare yes/no IsA route closing the v4.12.0 stub reserved at v4.12.5).**

## [5.3.10] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-09 ‚ÄĒ Magnet for AI scouts ‚ÄĒ CITATION.cff + codemeta.json + AGENTS.md + README FAQ

Documentation-only release (text / positioning, no functional code change).

Repositions the public repository as a **magnet for inbound AI-scout discovery** rather than purely outbound pitching. The strategic shift: investor scout bots, academic crawlers, and AI agents continuously scan GitHub for novel AI work; making the repository structurally readable to them is more efficient than cold outreach.

### What's added

**[CITATION.cff](CITATION.cff)** ‚ÄĒ GitHub-standard citation file (Citation File Format 1.2.0). Indexed by Zenodo and academic crawlers; renders as a ¬ęCite this repository¬Ľ button in the GitHub sidebar. Includes:
- Full project title, abstract, author, license
- Keywords for academic search (deterministic-ai, llm-alternative, agglutinative-language, etc.)
- Cross-references to MISSION.md and RESEARCH.md
- Preferred-citation block for software citation

**[codemeta.json](codemeta.json)** ‚ÄĒ CodeMeta 2.0 schema-org structured-data file. Machine-readable metadata for research-software discovery platforms. Includes:
- Schema.org `SoftwareSourceCode` typing
- Programming-language, OS, license declarations
- Funding-status (self-funded; pre-seed + state-grant tracks open)
- Cross-references to all top-level documents
- Keywords cluster aligned with embedding-search systems

**[AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)** ‚ÄĒ explicit orientation manifest for **AI agents and automated scouts** (the headline document of this release). Written in fact-dense embedding-friendly form; not for human consumption (humans should read README.md instead). Sections:
- Project identity table (12 fields)
- One-paragraph summary (embedding-friendly)
- ¬ęWhat this project IS¬Ľ / ¬ęWhat this project is NOT¬Ľ (8 + 8 bullet points)
- ¬ęWhy this project may be relevant to your search query¬Ľ (12 query intents ‚Üí ‚úÖ matches)
- Key technical signals table (latency, memory, GPU, hallucination rate vs LLM baselines)
- Team and collaboration interest (7 partner classes)
- Funding tracks (parallel: private + state grants)
- Documents to read in priority order (numbered)
- Contact (5 channels)
- Indexing hints for downstream embedding-search systems (4 keyword clusters)

### What's updated

**[README.md](README.md)** updated with:

- **30-second pitch box** at the very top (after badges, before Research mission) ‚ÄĒ single-paragraph elevator pitch designed for at-a-glance bot scoring
- **Reading-order callout** pointing to MISSION ‚Üí RESEARCH ‚Üí COLLABORATION ‚Üí AGENTS
- **FAQ section** with 7 high-frequency questions: ¬ęIs this a wrapper around LLM?¬Ľ / ¬ęIs it really deterministic?¬Ľ / ¬ęWhy Kazakh?¬Ľ / ¬ęWill it generalise?¬Ľ / ¬ęWhat is the funding model?¬Ľ / ¬ęWho built this?¬Ľ / ¬ęHow do I cite this work?¬Ľ
- **Two new badges:** GitHub last-commit, GitHub stars (live signals for scout-bot scoring)

### What's preserved

- All code, tests, data, behaviour bit-identical to v5.3.9.
- 1 150 passing tests (unchanged).
- world_core 3003 / 3245 / 30892 unchanged.

### Why this matters

Investor-scout services (Tracxn, Crunchbase, SignalFire, Pitchbook, plus dozens of internal-VC bots) now use AI-driven repository discovery. They scan for:
- Specific keywords (¬ędeterministic AI¬Ľ, ¬ęLLM alternative¬Ľ, ¬ęexplainable AI¬Ľ)
- Activity signals (release cadence, commit frequency, test count)
- Citation metadata (CITATION.cff, codemeta.json)
- Embedding-distance to known-relevant projects
- Structured data (schema.org JSON-LD, README badges)

A repository that surfaces these signals in machine-readable form is **passively discoverable** ‚ÄĒ investors, academic groups, and partners come to us rather than us cold-emailing them. This is the inbound positioning the user requested.

### Acceptance

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| CITATION.cff (Citation File Format 1.2.0) created | ‚úÖ |
| codemeta.json (CodeMeta 2.0 schema) created | ‚úÖ |
| AGENTS.md orientation manifest created | ‚úÖ |
| README 30-second pitch + FAQ + reading-order callout added | ‚úÖ |
| README badges expanded (last commit, stars) | ‚úÖ |
| All code unchanged from v5.3.9 | ‚úÖ |
| Workspace tests | **1150 passing** (unchanged) |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | green |
| `verify_release_version 5.3.10` | green |

### Roadmap

- **v5.3.11** ‚ÄĒ MA7 Ventures pitch rewrite (deferred at user request ‚ÄĒ focus first on inbound discovery; outbound pitch comes after the magnet bundle settles).

**Stripe ‚ÄĒ Deterministic AI research (Qazaq IR).**

## [5.3.9] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-09 ‚ÄĒ International partnerships and academic grants ‚ÄĒ state-grant funding track surfaced

Documentation-only release (text / positioning, no functional code change).

Surfaces a **second funding track** that was implicit in the architecture but not previously documented: **state research grants and academic joint-research partnerships from countries whose national language is agglutinative**. This is a more natural funding path than private VC alone, because every named partner has a direct strategic interest in deterministic AI for its own language.

### What's added

**[COLLABORATION.md](COLLABORATION.md) ‚ÄĒ new section ¬ęInternational ‚ÄĒ agglutinative-language alignment¬Ľ** with a country-by-country breakdown of priority partners:

| Country | Agglutinative language | Research-grant mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| Japan | Japanese | JST / JSPS / NEDO |
| South Korea | Korean | NRF / IITP |
| Finland | Finnish | Academy of Finland / Business Finland |
| Hungary | Hungarian | NKFIH |
| Estonia | Estonian | Estonian Research Council (ETAg) |
| Turkey | Turkish | T√úBńįTAK |
| Uzbekistan | Uzbek | Ministry of Innovative Development |
| Kyrgyzstan | Kyrgyz | Kyrgyz Academy of Sciences |
| Mongolia | Mongolian | Mongolian Academy of Sciences |
| Tatarstan (Russia) | Tatar | Tatarstan Academy of Sciences / Kazan Federal University |

‚Ä¶plus open invitation to other agglutinative-language states from the 30-language catalogue.

Each entry includes:
- The agglutinative national language (research-relevance anchor)
- The specific research-grant mechanism (so partners know which agency to engage)
- Strategic alignment context (why this country specifically)

**What we offer / what we ask** sub-section spelling out the bilateral terms: open-source kernel, co-authorship, native-speaker collaboration, against grant funding + academic affiliation + scope agreements.

**TL;DR matrix** updated with a new row for ¬ęNational research agency¬Ľ partner class with named priority agencies.

### What's updated

- [`MISSION.md`](MISSION.md) ‚Üí new sub-section ¬ęNational research agencies and academic grants¬Ľ under ¬ęOpen to collaboration¬Ľ; explicit framing that state-grant funding is a more natural path than private VC; roadmap line for Q3-Q4 2026 international grant applications.
- [`MISSION.md`](MISSION.md) ‚Üí ¬ęInvestors¬Ľ sub-section clarifies that academic / state-grant funding is parallel, not exclusive.
- [`RESEARCH.md`](RESEARCH.md) ‚Üí ¬ęHow this is funded¬Ľ section restructured to document both tracks (private capital + state grants); state-grant track now has explicit priority-partner list.

### What's preserved

- All code, tests, data, behaviour bit-identical to v5.3.8.
- 1 150 passing tests (unchanged).
- world_core 3003 / 3245 / 30892 unchanged.

### Why this matters

Pre-v5.3.9 the documentation surfaced only private-VC funding (angel pre-seed via MA7 framing). For a research-stage company with deeptech positioning, this **understates** the strategic surface ‚ÄĒ every national research agency in an agglutinative-language country has a direct interest in our work. State-grant tracks typically have:

- **Larger time horizons** (3-5 year programmes vs 12-month VC runway)
- **Mission alignment** (research milestones, not revenue milestones)
- **Lower equity dilution** (grants are non-equity)
- **Academic credibility** (publication-friendly)

Surfacing this track explicitly is a clear positive signal for everyone reading the docs ‚ÄĒ investors see less concentration risk, academics see a serious research operation, and state agencies see they were named as priority partners.

### What's added

**[RESEARCH.md](RESEARCH.md)** ‚ÄĒ detailed research arc:

A reader landing on the public repository finds three layers of orientation now:

3. **RESEARCH.md + COLLABORATION.md** ‚Üí operational depth (research questions, milestones, partner onboarding terms)

This depth-on-demand structure is investor-friendly (read just MISSION.md for the pitch) and academically credible (read RESEARCH.md for open questions). Without it, the research positioning was visible only in the README's top section ‚ÄĒ too thin for serious due diligence.

## [5.3.7] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-09 ‚ÄĒ Comparative agglutinative-language table ‚ÄĒ global research frontier visible

Documentation-only release (text / positioning, no functional code change).

Adds a representative catalogue of agglutinative-typology languages worldwide to [`MISSION.md`](MISSION.md) ‚ÄĒ making the architecture's global research frontier visible to readers. The list is **partial by design**: the architecture's morpheme-decomposition core applies anywhere root-plus-typed-suffix concatenation is the dominant compositional pattern, regardless of the language family.

### What's added

**New section in MISSION.md:** ¬ęAgglutinative languages ‚ÄĒ global research frontier¬Ľ with a 30-language table covering:

- Closely related to Kazakh: Karakalpak, Kyrgyz, Nogai
- Other widely-spoken agglutinative languages: Tatar, Bashkir, Crimean Tatar, Kumyk, Karachay-Balkar
- Larger-corpus targets: Uzbek, Uyghur, Turkmen, Azerbaijani, Turkish, Gagauz
- Siberian and divergent: Yakut (Sakha), Tuvan, Chuvash
- Mongolic-area: Mongolian, Buryat
- East Asian: Japanese, Korean
- European with maximal morphology: Finnish, Hungarian, Estonian
- Non-Eurasian: Tamil, Swahili, Quechua, Aymara, Basque

Per row: agglutinative profile + research notes (port difficulty, script handling, stress test category).

### What's preserved

- All code, tests, data, behaviour bit-identical to v5.3.6.
- 1150 passing tests (unchanged).
- world_core 3003 / 3245 / 30892 unchanged.
- The Family-classification column is **deliberately omitted** to keep the table focused on the agglutinative-typology criterion that matters for our research, rather than implying clustering or origin claims.

### Why this matters

Pre-fix the ¬ęWhy Kazakh¬Ľ section listed only a handful of related languages (Kyrgyz, Tatar, Uzbek, Turkish, Finnish, Hungarian, Japanese, Korean) as ¬ęwider applicability¬Ľ. Investors and researchers reading the document had no concrete sense of how broadly the architecture generalises. The expanded table makes the **global research frontier** visible: ~30 languages where the same morpheme-decomposition core applies, many of which currently have no comparable NLP infrastructure ‚ÄĒ making each one an open research target.

## [5.3.6] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Repositioning core ‚ÄĒ research-mission framing across primary documentation

Documentation-only release (text / positioning, no functional code change).

Repositions the project's primary surfaces from ¬ępredictable Kazakh dialog / Kazakh school tutor¬Ľ to **¬ędeterministic AI research with adam as applied demonstrator¬Ľ**. The architecture, code, and tests are unchanged; the framing reflects the actual research thesis the project has been pursuing under the school-tutor surface.

### What's added

- **[MISSION.md](MISSION.md)** ‚ÄĒ new top-level mission statement covering: the three diseases of probabilistic AI (opacity, cost, hallucination), our hypothesis (agglutinative morphology as deterministic substrate), three measurable goals (predictability, cheapness, safety), why Kazakh specifically, adam's role as demonstrator (not goal), research roadmap as milestone arc, and explicit open-collaboration invitation across 5 partner classes (linguists / AI researchers / educational institutions / government-defence / investors).
- **README top section** rewritten with research-first framing: tagline ¬ęDeterministic AI research ‚ÄĒ predictable, cheap, safe¬Ľ; new ¬ęResearch mission¬Ľ section with the 3-diseases-vs-3-goals table; explicit ¬ęadam ‚ÄĒ current applied demonstrator¬Ľ framing; collaboration links to mission doc + contact.
- **CHANGELOG** prefaced with Research-direction explainer + revised versioning-cadence policy (patch increments `x.y.{1,2,3,‚Ä¶}` for text-only changes vs `x.y.5` for incremental functional).

### What's preserved

- Historical release entries (`v4.x` and earlier `v5.x`) remain unchanged ‚ÄĒ they describe what was actually built at each step.
- All code, tests, data, and behaviour are bit-identical to v5.3.5.
- The ¬ęStripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor¬Ľ tagline remains in historical entries; from v5.3.6 forward, new entries use ¬ęStripe ‚ÄĒ Deterministic AI research¬Ľ.

### Why now

The project's GitHub repo went public on 2026-05-08 (first commit 2026-04-07). Public visibility means investor scrutiny, which means the surface positioning must match the actual thesis. ¬ęKazakh school tutor¬Ľ understates the research arc the codebase represents; ¬ędeterministic AI research with applied demonstrator¬Ľ is the accurate framing.

## [5.3.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Compound-statement absorption + occupation self-recall (live REPL gap)

Closes a user-reported gap from a 2026-05-08 live REPL session:

```
[Student]  –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č.
[adam]     –Ē”ô–ļ–Ķ, —ā–į–Ĺ—č—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ—č–ľ–į “õ—É–į–Ĺ—č—ą—ā—č–ľ—č–Ĺ.
              ‚Üź name absorbed; occupation LOST

[Student]  –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?
[adam]     –ú–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“õ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č “õ—č—Ā“õ–į—ą–į –į–Ļ—ā—Ā–į–ľ: –ú–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“õ ‚ÄĒ –į–ī–į–ľ–Ĺ—č“£ –ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ī—Ė.
              ‚Üź generic definition because session.occupation never set
```

### Architectural gap

`interpret_text` returns ONE primary `Intent` per turn. For ¬ę–•, Y¬Ľ compound profile statements joined by comma, only the first clause's intent fires; the second clause's information is dropped on the floor. The user's instinct in their feedback was correct: adam should parse compound sentences and fix EVERY fact, not just the first.

### Fix #1 ‚ÄĒ extend compound-occupation detection

`detect_occupation_in_compound` ([semantics.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs)) gains a possessive-anchor pattern: when the input contains ¬ę–ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ X¬Ľ or ¬ę–ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ī—Ė–ľ X¬Ľ (1sg-poss-marked profession noun + bare-noun complement), capture X as the occupation value. Pre-fix this function only matched copula-suffixed forms (¬ę–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–ł–Ĺ–∂–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ) via `strip_copula_and_lookup_noun`; the bare-noun-after-possessive pattern fell through.

Filler words (¬ę–Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ¬Ľ, ¬ę—Ä–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ¬Ľ) between possessive and value are skipped ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ captures ¬ę–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č¬Ľ, not ¬ę–Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ¬Ľ.

The existing v4.52.0 secondary-scan plumbing in `conversation.rs` already calls `detect_occupation_in_compound` post-`absorb_entities`, so no new plumbing is needed ‚ÄĒ just better detection.

### Fix #2 ‚ÄĒ occupation self-recall pattern

`detect_ask_occupation` extended with the v4.54.5-style recall pattern: ¬ę(–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ )?–ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ / ¬ę–ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ–ī—č “Ī–ľ—č—ā–Ņ–į–ī—č“£—č–∑ –Ī–į?¬Ľ / ¬ę–ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ī—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ now route to AskOccupation. Pre-fix ¬ę–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ¬Ľ co-occurring with ¬ę–ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ¬Ľ fell through to Unknown surface and surfaced the generic ¬ę–ú–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“õ ‚ÄĒ –į–ī–į–ľ–Ĺ—č“£ –ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ī—Ė¬Ľ definition.

### Bonus: extract_secondary_profile_facts public API

New `pub fn extract_secondary_profile_facts(input: &str) -> Vec<(String, String)>` exposes the secondary-extraction pattern for callers that want all profile facts at once. Currently emits `("occupation", X)` and `("age", N)` tuples; expandable to other profile predicates as needed.

### End-to-end verified

```
$ printf '–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č.\n–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?\n' | adam_chat
adam-chat v5.2 ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–ļ! ...

[Student]  –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č.
[adam]     –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā–Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā–į–Ĺ—č—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ—č–ľ–į “õ—É–į–Ĺ—č—ą—ā—č–ľ—č–Ĺ.
              ‚Üź session: name=–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, occupation=–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č (BOTH absorbed)

[Student]  –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?
[adam]     –°—Ė–∑ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ –Ķ“£–Ī–Ķ–ļ –Ķ—ā—Ė–Ņ –∂“Į—Ä—Ā—Ė–∑.
              ‚Üź AskOccupation self-recall surfaces stored value
```

### Regression tests

`tests/codex_round3_v5035.rs` ‚ÄĒ 9 new tests:

**Unit (extract_secondary_profile_facts):**
1. Finds occupation after name in compound input
2. Finds –ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ī—Ė–ľ variant
3. Finds age (¬ę–∂–į—Ā—č–ľ 30¬Ľ)
4. Skips filler words (¬ę–Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ¬Ľ)
5. Empty when no pattern

**End-to-end:**
6. Compound name+occupation absorbs both into session
7. Occupation self-recall after compound surfaces stored value (no generic definition)
8. Self-recall persists across multi-turn dialog with intermediate turns
9. Name-only input does NOT extract phantom occupation

## [5.3.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Codex round-3 audit fixes (pass 2 ‚ÄĒ architectural) ‚ÄĒ contradiction resolution + anaphora gate

Closes 2 of the 3 architectural items deferred from v5.2.5; Bug 5 (shallow domain answers) remains for a content release. With v5.3.0 the audited 4-turn contradiction dance and the “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ/–ź—Ā–Ņ–į–Ĺ over-carry sequence both produce sensible responses end-to-end.

### Bug 4 (P1) ‚ÄĒ anaphora over-carry

¬ę–ź—Ā–Ņ–į–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ–≥–Ķ –ļ”©–ļ?¬Ľ after a “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ-themed conversation pre-fix inherited the prior topic. Root cause traced to `is_short_interrogative_followup` in [discourse.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/discourse.rs) ‚ÄĒ over-eagerly flagged any 3-token input with a wh-word as anaphoric.

**Fix:** the heuristic now requires the wh-word at position 0 (or after particle-only tokens like ¬ę–į–Ľ¬Ľ). When content precedes the wh-word, the input names its own subject and is NOT anaphoric.

Verified: post-fix ¬ę–ź—Ā–Ņ–į–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ–≥–Ķ –ļ”©–ļ?¬Ľ honestly hedges ¬ę–ź—Ā–Ņ–į–Ĺ —Ā–Ķ–Ī–Ķ–Ī—Ė–Ĺ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č –į–Ļ—ā–į –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ “õ–ĺ—Ä—č–ľ–ī–į –Ī“Ī–Ľ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ –∂–ĺ“õ.¬Ľ rather than answering about “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ.

### Bug 2 (P0) ‚ÄĒ contradiction resolution

The –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č‚Üí–ź—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į‚Üí¬ę–Ė–ĺ“õ, –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā¬Ľ‚Üí¬ę“ö–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į–ī–į —ā“Ī—Ä–į–ľ—č–Ĺ?¬Ľ dance pre-fix ended with phantom ¬ę“ö–į–Ľ–į¬Ľ pollution and the rejected value (–ź—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į) surviving. **Four sub-fixes layer to close it:**

**(1) Session sync after belief resolution** ([conversation.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs)) ‚ÄĒ `try_resolve_pending_contradiction` already handled the ¬ęX –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā¬Ľ pattern via input-contains-candidate fallback (no new detector needed). What was missing was **session sync** after belief resolution: the chosen value was correctly active in belief but `session.city` stayed at the last-absorbed (now-Superseded) contested value. Now syncs profile slot + drops side slots (`city_id` / `geo_kind` / `name_id` / `name_respect` / `name_respect_distinct`) so they re-derive from the canonical value.

**(2) Question-marker guard in location detector** ([semantics.rs:detect_statement_of_location](crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs)) ‚ÄĒ ¬ę“ö–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į–ī–į —ā“Ī—Ä–į–ľ—č–Ĺ?¬Ľ (interrogative) pre-fix was parsed as `StatementOfLocation { city: "“ö–į–Ľ–į" }` because ¬ę—ā“Ī—Ä–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ (1sg "I live") + ¬ę“õ–į–Ľ–į–ī–į¬Ľ (locative "in city") matched the statement template. The noun ¬ę“õ–į–Ľ–į¬Ľ (city) got stored as a phantom belief value. Post-fix question markers (¬ę“õ–į–Ļ¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į—ą–į–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę?¬Ľ) bail out ‚ÄĒ same class as v5.2.5 Bug 1 occupation-question fix.

**(3) 1st-person self-recall in AskLocation** ([semantics.rs:detect_ask_location](crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs)) ‚ÄĒ pre-fix detector required 2nd-person markers (¬ę—Ā—Ė–∑ / —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ / —ā“Ī—Ä–į—Ā—č–∑¬Ľ). ¬ę“ö–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į–ī–į —ā“Ī—Ä–į–ľ—č–Ĺ?¬Ľ (1st-person reflexive ‚ÄĒ student asking adam to recall their own location) didn't qualify and fell through to Unknown surface. Post-fix accepts 1st-person self-recall verbs (¬ę—ā“Ī—Ä–į–ľ—č–Ĺ / —ā“Ī—Ä–į–ľ—č–∑ / “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į–ľ—č–Ĺ / “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į–ľ—č–∑¬Ľ) + ¬ę“õ–į–Ļ¬Ľ so self-recall questions route to AskLocation and surface the synced session value.

**(4) Resolution-acceptance template** ([planner.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs) + [v1.toml](data/dialog/templates/v1.toml)) ‚ÄĒ when `try_resolve_pending_contradiction` succeeds, conversation now sets a `__resolve_contradiction__` sentinel + populates the chosen profile slot. Planner has a sister-override block (mirrors `__dismiss_contradiction__` from v4.4.0) that routes to the new `resolve_contradiction` template family (6 variants ‚ÄĒ 3 slot-bearing for city/name/occupation + 3 topic-less fallbacks). User-visible response is now ¬ę–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ľ, –ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£—Ė–∑ ‚ÄĒ –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ. –ē—Ā—ā—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ “Ī—Ā—ā–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ instead of a generic Negation echo.

### Audited 4-turn dance ‚ÄĒ end-to-end verified

```
[Student]  –ú–Ķ–Ĺ –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č–ī–į–ľ—č–Ĺ.
[adam]     –ú–Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£—Ė–∑ –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė–Ĺ “Ī“õ—ā—č–ľ.

[Student]  –ú–Ķ–Ĺ –ź—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į–ī–į–ľ—č–Ĺ.
[adam]     –°”ô–Ľ —ą–į—ā–į—Ā—ā—č–ľ ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑ –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č-–ī–į –ľ–į, ”ô–Ľ–ī–Ķ –ź—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į-–ī–į –ľ–į?

[Student]  –Ė–ĺ“õ, –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā.
[adam]     –Ė–į—Ä–į–Ļ–ī—č, –Ķ–ļ—Ė –Ĺ“Ī—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ—č“£ “õ–į–Ļ—Ā—č—Ā—č –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ–ļ–Ķ –į–Ľ–ī—č–ľ.
            ‚ÜĎ Bug 2 fix #1 + #4 ‚ÄĒ resolution acknowledgement (session synced to –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č)

[Student]  “ö–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į–ī–į —ā“Ī—Ä–į–ľ—č–Ĺ?
[adam]     –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė–Ľ—É—Ė–ľ—ą–Ķ, –ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£—Ė–∑ –ź–Ľ–ľ–į—ā—č.
            ‚ÜĎ Bug 2 fix #2 + #3 ‚ÄĒ interrogative not StatementOfLocation; AskLocation 1sg self-recall
```

## [5.2.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Codex 2026-05-08 round-3 audit fixes (pass 1) ‚ÄĒ Kazakh-first UX bugs closed

Triages the 5 P0/P1 UX bugs Codex flagged in round-3 review for pre-demo readiness; defers 3 architectural items (contradiction resolution / anaphora over-carry / shallow domain answers) to v5.3.0+. Codex's investment readiness assessment was 5.5-6/10 with the listed pre-demo punch list; v5.2.5 closes the addressable items so the next demo build presents cleaner.

### Bug 3 (P0) ‚ÄĒ Kazakh-first tutor UX

¬ę–ė–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑—ą—Ė¬Ľ pre-fix routed to topicless clarification because `LATIN_TECH_SUBJECTS` only matched Latin tokens. For a Kazakh-first product positioning that's critical.

**Fix** ([semantics.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs)):

`pedagogical_topic_hint` now recognises Kazakh aliases for all 5 canonical curriculum stages BEFORE the latin / multiword fallbacks (otherwise multiword_entity_hint catches ¬ę“õ–į—Ä—č–∑ –į–Ľ—É¬Ľ as the literal phrase before canonicalisation):

| Kazakh alias(es) | Canonical stage |
|---|---|
| –ł–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ | ownership |
| “õ–į—Ä—č–∑ / “õ–į—Ä—č–∑ –į–Ľ—É / “õ–į—Ä—č–∑“ď–į –į–Ľ—É | borrow |
| ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£—Ė / ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä —Ā“Į—Ä—É –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£—Ė | lifetime |
| “õ–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā / “õ–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä | traits |
| –į—Ā–ł–Ĺ—Ö—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ / –į—Ā–ł–Ĺ—Ö—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č | async |

`pedagogical::exercise_for_with_hint` topic-canonicalisation also extended with the same aliases.

### Bug 1 (P0) ‚ÄĒ placeholder leak

¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ –ļ—Ė–ľ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ?¬Ľ (interrogative ‚ÄĒ ¬ęwhat do I work as?¬Ľ) pre-fix routed to `StatementOfOccupation { occupation: None }` because `joined.contains("–∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ")` matched even in question form. The planner picked a `{occupation}` template that `template_is_fillable` couldn't reject (no family-wide guard), so the user saw the literal `{Occupation} –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ, —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ľ.` string in the rendered output.

**Fix** ([semantics.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs:2612)):

`detect_statement_of_occupation` checks for question markers (¬ę?¬Ľ, ¬ę–ļ—Ė–ľ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ¬Ľ, ¬ę–ļ—Ė–ľ —Ä–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ ¬Ľ) and bails out on the bare-istem branch, letting the turn fall through to `detect_ask_occupation` or further detectors. Verified: post-fix the response no longer contains `{occupation}` placeholder.

### Bug 6 (P1) ‚ÄĒ Kazakh-only policy inconsistency

English ¬ęWhat is Rust ownership?¬Ľ pre-fix surfaced a substantive answer (extracted from the Latin `ownership` token) in violation of `project_kazakh_only_directive`. Russian inputs were already refused via `input_is_likely_russian`; English wasn't.

**Fix** ([discourse.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/discourse.rs)):

New `input_is_likely_english(input)` ‚ÄĒ sister detector to `input_is_likely_russian`. Routes English-dominant inputs to the same `unknown.non_kazakh` refusal template family.

Detection signals:
- No Cyrillic characters at all (mixed-script Kazakh sentences pass through unaffected)
- At least one English function word from a curated list (`what`, `is`, `the`, `do`, `you`, `i`, `we`, `how`, `who`, `where`, `when`, `why`, `can`, `could`, `should`, `would`, `tell`, `explain`, `show`, `help`, `give`, `of`, `in`, `to`, `from`, `with`, `about`)

Conservative ‚ÄĒ bare Latin tech tokens inside a Kazakh sentence (¬ęownership —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–Ļ—ā¬Ľ) don't trigger; only dominantly-English inputs.

- `cargo fmt --all` to fix [phoneme.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/phoneme.rs) format diff Codex flagged.

## [5.2.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Kazakh grapheme-to-phoneme (G2P) module ‚ÄĒ substrate for kernel-pure concatenative TTS

First step toward fully-deterministic Kazakh pronunciation that doesn't depend on neural models or external TTS. v5.2.0 ships **only** the G2P module; the actual phoneme bank (audio WAV per phoneme) is a separate content-engineering investment deferred to v5.2.5+.

### Why bother with G2P?

Kazakh is heavily agglutinative with mostly-regular orthography ‚ÄĒ the grapheme-to-phoneme mapping is overwhelmingly 1:1 for the native 33-letter alphabet, with predictable handling of the few Russian-loan letters (—Ď / —é / —Ź / —ć / —Ü / —Č / —ä / —Ć). A rule-based G2P covers this without statistical training, fits the `project_retrieval_not_neural_v2` philosophy, and runs in microseconds.

Even without the phoneme bank, the G2P module is useful in its own right:

- Powers a future `PhonemeBankTtsBackend` (concatenative kernel-pure TTS)
- Surfaces Kazakh phonemic transcription for educational tools
- Substrate for phoneme-aware spell-checking / fuzzy match
- Debugging aid for adam's existing morphological pipeline

### What's added

**`adam_dialog::phoneme` module** ([phoneme.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/phoneme.rs)):

- `Phoneme` enum with 33 native + foreign-decomposable variants:
  - **10 vowels:** –į √¶ –Ķ –ł —č —Ė –ĺ ”© “Ī “Į
  - **23 consonants:** –Ī –Ņ –ľ —É —Ą —ā –ī –Ĺ —Ā –∑ –Ľ —Ä –ļ “õ –≥ “ď —Ö “Ľ —ą –∂ —á –Ļ “£
- `text_to_phonemes(text: &str) -> Vec<Phoneme>` ‚ÄĒ main G2P entry point.
- `phonemes_to_ipa(stream: &[Phoneme]) -> String` ‚ÄĒ IPA-ish transcription for debugging.
- `Phoneme::is_vowel()` ‚ÄĒ useful for stress placement / concat smoothing.
- `Phoneme::bank_id() -> &'static str` ‚ÄĒ ASCII-safe filename for future phoneme bank (e.g. “£ ‚Üí `"ng"`, —ą ‚Üí `"sh"`).
- `Phoneme::ipa() -> &'static str` ‚ÄĒ canonical IPA-ish symbol per phoneme.

**Decomposition rules for Russian-loan compound letters:**

- —Ď ‚Üí `[J, O]`
- —é ‚Üí `[J, U]`
- —Ź ‚Üí `[J, A]`
- —ć ‚Üí `[E]`
- —Ü ‚Üí `[T, S]`
- —Č ‚Üí `[SH, CH]`
- —ä, —Ć ‚Üí silent (dropped). Trade-off: loses the /j/ glide in some Russian loanwords; v5.2.5+ may add the glide rule. Kazakh-native words don't use —ä/—Ć, so the simplification has zero impact for the target language.

### Phoneme inventory examples

| Word | Phonemes (IPA) | Bank IDs |
|---|---|---|
| `–į–Ľ–ľ–į` (apple) | `a l m a` | `a l m a` |
| `“õ“Ī—Ā` (bird) | `q u s` | `q u s` |
| `–ļ”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ` (shirt) | `k Ňď j l e k` | `k oe j l e k` |
| `—ā–į“£` (dawn) | `t a Ňč` | `t a ng` |
| `–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į` (program) | `b a …£ d a r l a m a` | `b a gh d a r l a m a` |
| `–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“õ` (programming) | `b a …£ d a r l a m a  É …Į l …Į q` | `b a gh d a r l a m a sh y l y q` |

### Regression tests

20 new module unit tests:

1. `vowels_classified_correctly` (all 10 vowels match `is_vowel()`)
2. `ipa_symbols_unique_per_phoneme` (all 33 IPA symbols distinct)
3. `bank_ids_are_ascii_unique` (all bank IDs ASCII + distinct)
4. `simple_kazakh_word_–į–Ľ–ľ–į`
5. `kazakh_word_with_“õ_and_“Ī`
6. `kazakh_word_with_front_vowels` (–ļ”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ)
7. `kazakh_word_with_“£` (—ā–į“£)
8. `kazakh_word_with_“ď` (–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į)
9. `russian_loan_—é_decomposes`
10. `russian_loan_—Ź_decomposes`
11. `russian_loan_—Ď_decomposes`
12. `russian_loan_—Ü_decomposes_to_ts`
13. `russian_loan_—Č_decomposes_to_sh_ch`
14. `hard_and_soft_signs_silent`
15. `whitespace_and_punctuation_dropped`
16. `uppercase_normalises_to_lowercase_phonemes` (–ź–Ē–ź–ú ‚Üí –į–ī–į–ľ)
17. `empty_input_yields_empty_stream`
18. `mixed_kazakh_and_latin_drops_latin` (Latin chars not in inventory)

## [5.1.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Optional Piper neural TTS backend ‚ÄĒ richer voice for users who want it

Second `TtsBackend` implementation alongside the v5.0.0 OS-native default. Users who want noticeably more natural speech can install [Piper](https://github.com/rhasspy/piper) (~5 MB CLI binary) and download a voice model (~50-100 MB ONNX), then run `adam_chat --tts --tts-backend piper --tts-model <path>`. Architecturally fenced: Piper is bundled neither in adam's binary nor in the repo ‚ÄĒ purely opt-in, kernel determinism unchanged.

### What's added

**`PiperTtsBackend`** ([tts.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/tts.rs)):

```rust
pub struct PiperTtsBackend {
    piper: PathBuf,
    audio_player: PathBuf,
    model_path: PathBuf,
    voice_label: Option<String>,
    current: Mutex<Option<Child>>,
}

impl PiperTtsBackend {
    pub fn detect(model_path: &Path) -> Option<Self>;
    pub fn new(piper: PathBuf, audio_player: PathBuf,
               model_path: PathBuf, voice_label: Option<String>) -> Self;
}
```

`detect()` returns `None` when any of `piper` binary / audio player / ONNX model file is missing.

**Pipeline:** `text ‚Üí piper stdin ‚Üí temp WAV ‚Üí spawn audio player`. Audio player tracked in `Mutex<Option<Child>>` for the same kill-previous semantics as v5.0.5. The piper synthesis step itself is synchronous (~0.3-1s on M2); playback is non-blocking.

**Helper functions** (private):

- `locate_command(name)` ‚ÄĒ resolves binary's absolute path via `which`. Returns `None` if missing.
- `locate_audio_player()` ‚ÄĒ probes `afplay ‚Üí aplay ‚Üí paplay ‚Üí play` in order. macOS gets `afplay` for free.

**REPL flags** ([adam_chat.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/bin/adam_chat.rs)):

- `--tts-backend piper` opts into Piper.
- `--tts-model <path>` points at the ONNX voice file.
- Graceful fallback to OS backend when Piper detection fails (rather than erroring out).

### Smoke test (graceful fallback)

```
$ adam_chat --once '–°”ô–Ľ–Ķ–ľ.' --tts --tts-backend piper --tts-model /nonexistent.onnx
adam-chat: --tts-backend piper requested but `piper` binary, audio player,
           or model not found; falling back to OS backend
adam-chat: TTS on ‚ÄĒ say (voice: Aru)
–°”ô–Ľ–Ķ–ľ
[macOS Aru voice speaks]
```

### Regression tests

7 new in `tts::tests`:

1. `piper_backend_describe_includes_model`
2. `piper_backend_accessors_work`
3. `piper_detect_returns_none_when_model_missing`
4. `piper_speak_empty_input_is_noop`
5. `locate_command_finds_known_binary_on_unix`
6. `locate_command_returns_none_for_missing_binary`
7. `locate_audio_player_finds_afplay_on_macos`

End-to-end speak via real Piper requires user to install both `piper` and a voice model; documented but not gated by tests (would require model in CI).

| 18 / 18 v4.99.0 query-intent tests | ‚úÖ unchanged |

### Strategic

For current macOS users, the bundled `Aru` voice (v5.0.0 OS backend) is already excellent ‚ÄĒ Piper backend is for Linux users without quality TTS, or anyone wanting to experiment with different voice models. Kazakh-specific Piper models aren't in the official catalogue today, but the architecture is ready when one becomes available (or user trains one).

### Roadmap

- **v5.2.0** ‚ÄĒ concatenative phoneme bank for fully-deterministic Kazakh pronunciation aligned with kernel philosophy. Hand-recorded phoneme units stitched per-word at runtime ‚ÄĒ most kernel-pure option but biggest content-engineering investment.

**Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor (multi-backend voice infrastructure).**

## [5.0.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Non-blocking TTS dispatch ‚ÄĒ REPL stays responsive while adam speaks

Builds on v5.0.0 voice output. Pre-v5.0.5 `OsTtsBackend::speak` blocked the REPL until the synthesiser finished ‚ÄĒ student could only type next input *after* adam stopped speaking. Post-v5.0.5 the synthesiser runs as a detached child process; `speak()` returns in microseconds, REPL accepts the next prompt immediately.

### What's added

**Non-blocking dispatch** ([tts.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/tts.rs)):

- `OsTtsBackend` gains a private `Mutex<Option<Child>>` field tracking the currently-playing synthesis.
- `speak()` switches from `Command::status()` (blocks) to `Command::spawn()` (detached); returns immediately.
- Before spawning a new child, any previous child is `kill()`ed + `wait()`ed (reaping the zombie). This implements **latest-wins** semantics ‚ÄĒ natural for interactive tutoring where the student typed something new and shouldn't have to listen to the old half-finished response first.

**API encapsulation:**

- `OsTtsBackend::new(program, args, voice)` ‚ÄĒ explicit constructor for direct callers and tests.
- `program()` / `args()` / `voice()` accessors replace the old public fields. Existing tests updated to use accessors.

**Drop impl:**

Best-effort reaps the last child on REPL exit. macOS `say` keeps speaking even after the parent process exits (default behaviour), so the user hears the final response even after ^D ‚ÄĒ that's the right UX. Drop only does a non-blocking `try_wait()` to avoid leaving zombies in the process table.

### Latest-wins behaviour explained

| Sequence | v5.0.0 (blocking) | v5.0.5 (non-blocking + latest-wins) |
|---|---|---|
| Type ‚Üí adam responds + speaks (5s) ‚Üí type next | wait 5s, then type | type immediately |
| Type ‚Üí adam responds ‚Üí SHIFT type before audio finishes | impossible (REPL stalled) | new response kills previous synthesis; new one starts |
| Type quickly 5x in a row | 5 audio playbacks queue up (wait 25s) | only the 5th is spoken; earlier ones killed |

For tutor responsiveness this is the right trade. Students who want to hear a full response just don't type until adam finishes speaking.

### Regression tests

3 new tts tests:

1. `speak_returns_promptly_via_spawn_on_macos` ‚ÄĒ asserts `speak()` for a long Kazakh sentence (~5s of audio) returns in < 250ms (would be ~5000ms with blocking semantics).
2. `second_speak_kills_previous_child_on_macos` ‚ÄĒ calls `speak()` twice in succession; verifies the registered child PID changes (the previous one was killed).
3. `speak_empty_input_is_noop` ‚ÄĒ whitespace-only input doesn't spawn anything.

### Acceptance

| 18 / 18 v4.99.0 query-intent tests | ‚úÖ unchanged |
| 11 / 11 v4.98.5 auto-advance tests | ‚úÖ unchanged |
| 13 / 13 v4.98.0 curriculum tests | ‚úÖ unchanged |

## [5.0.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Voice output transducer ‚ÄĒ adam speaks Kazakh

First multimodal release. Pre-v5.0.0 every adam response was silent text; post-v5.0.0 the optional `--tts` flag activates a system-native voice synthesiser so adam **speaks** every response in addition to printing it. v5.0.0 crosses adam from text-only to multimodal output ‚ÄĒ a kernel-signature capability that warrants the `x.0.0` bump per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`.

### Why this matters

The Kazakh school tutor positioning (`project_kazakh_tutor_positioning`) is the strategic stripe. Voice output is a major UX upgrade for students: while they read code on screen, they hear correct Kazakh pronunciation of grammatical concepts. High-quality Kazakh TTS specifically is a market gap ‚ÄĒ most TTS focuses on EN/RU/ZH/...

### Architectural framing

Per `project_retrieval_not_neural_v2` and `project_v4_direction`, adam is a deterministic kernel with watch-battery deployment goals. A bundled neural TTS model (~50 MB+) would dilute that framing. The v5.0.0 design avoids the conflict by **shelling out to the OS-native voice synthesiser** ‚ÄĒ `say` on macOS, `espeak-ng` on Linux. Zero binary-size impact, zero new model dependency, kernel determinism preserved.

The TTS layer is fenced as a **peripheral output transducer** ‚ÄĒ like a printer, not a kernel component. Dialog logic, intent recognition, planner, realiser produce the same Kazakh text as before; the TTS layer just speaks that text.

### What's added

**`adam_dialog::tts` module** ([tts.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/tts.rs)):

- `TtsBackend` trait with `speak(&str)` + `describe()` methods.
- `OsTtsBackend` ‚ÄĒ shells out to `say` (macOS) or `espeak-ng` / `espeak` (Linux).
- `NoOpTts` ‚ÄĒ fallback for tests and disabled-TTS callers.
- `OsTtsBackend::detect(voice_override)` ‚ÄĒ platform-aware detection. Prefers Kazakh voice (`Aru` on macOS, `kk` locale on Linux); falls back to default voice with a one-time stderr warning.
- `strip_for_speech(text)` ‚ÄĒ cleans markdown fences and inline backticks before synthesis. Code blocks are dropped entirely (synthesisers pronouncing `\`\`\`rust ... \`\`\`` as ¬ębacktick backtick backtick rust...¬Ľ is noise; students read code on screen).

**REPL flags** ([adam_chat.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/bin/adam_chat.rs)):

- `--tts` ‚ÄĒ activate voice output. After every response, `tts.speak(out)` runs synchronously (blocks until synthesis finishes).
- `--tts-voice <name>` ‚ÄĒ override voice detection (e.g. `--tts-voice Aru`, `--tts-voice Yuri`).

When `--tts` is on but no system synthesiser is found, adam falls back to silent text with a startup warning. CI / non-interactive flows that don't pass `--tts` are unaffected.

### End-to-end smoke test

```
$ adam_chat --once '–°”ô–Ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–Ķ.' --tts
adam-chat: loaded 111 template families ...
adam-chat: retrieval on ‚ÄĒ 5543 morphemes ...
adam-chat: TTS on ‚ÄĒ say (voice: Aru)
–°”ô–Ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–Ķ.
[macOS Aru voice speaks: ¬ę–°”ô–Ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–Ķ.¬Ľ]
```

### Regression tests

1. `no_op_backend_speak_succeeds`
2. `strip_for_speech_drops_code_fence_blocks`
3. `strip_for_speech_drops_inline_backticks`
4. `strip_for_speech_collapses_whitespace`

## [4.99.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Adaptive-difficulty wiring (long-term roadmap step 4)

Closes the long-term curriculum arc that started v4.98.0. Plugs the `StageProgress::difficulty_hint` API (added in v4.98.5) into actual content selection: pre-v4.99.5 every student got the same canonical exercise per topic regardless of past performance; post-v4.99.5 the tutor scales ‚ÄĒ struggling students see simpler exercises that build foundation, confident students see harder challenges.

### What's added

**`pedagogical::exercise_for_with_hint`** ([pedagogical.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/pedagogical.rs)):

New public API:

```rust
pub fn exercise_for_with_hint(
    topic: &str,
    hint: crate::curriculum::DifficultyHint,
) -> Option<&'static str>
```

Returns a stage-tailored variant when the topic resolves to a curriculum stage AND the hint is `Easy` or `Hard`; falls back to the canonical `exercise_for(topic)` for `Normal` hints, unknown topics, and non-curriculum topics (e.g. `closure`, `iterator`). Topic canonicalisation handles existing aliases (`borrowing` / `“õ–į—Ä—č–∑“ď–į –į–Ľ—É` ‚Üí `borrow`; `async fn` / `await` ‚Üí `async`; etc.) before lookup.

**10 new curated exercises** ‚ÄĒ Easy + Hard variants for all 5 canonical curriculum stages:

| Stage | Easy | Hard |
|---|---|---|
| `ownership` | Print a String (no move) | Function with multi-take + Clone vs reference fix |
| `borrow` | Read-only `print_len(&String)` | `&str` slice tuple + multi-borrow rules |
| `lifetime` | `'static` literal-returning fn | `Excerpt<'a>` struct + lifetime elision |
| `traits` | Single trait + impl on `Person` | Generic `longest<T: ???>` with bound choice |
| `async` | Trivial `async fn` + single `.await` | Tokio `select!` + `spawn` race between two tasks |

**Conversation wiring** ([conversation.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs)):

When `Intent::AskExercise { topic: Some(t) }` fires AND `t` resolves to a curriculum stage, Conversation:

1. Reads `curriculum_progress[stage].difficulty_hint()`
2. Calls `pedagogical::exercise_for_with_hint(t, hint)`
3. Pre-stuffs the result into `extra_slots["exercise_body"]` (overrides the planner's `extract_slots` default via the v4.95.0 merge order)
4. Diagnostic `extra_slots["difficulty_hint"]` exposes the chosen tier for `--trace` mode

When the hint is `Normal` (or no curriculum stage matches), the canonical content path is preserved bit-for-bit ‚ÄĒ students with no recorded progress see exactly the pre-v4.99.5 exercises.

### End-to-end smoke test

```
[Student]  –ú–į“ď–į–Ĺ Rust-—ā–į ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑—ą—Ė.
[adam]     “ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ: –ú—č–Ĺ–į –ļ–ĺ–ī—ā–į “õ–į—ā–Ķ –Ī–į—Ä: `let s = String::from("hello"); ...`
              [‚ÜĎ canonical Normal variant ‚ÄĒ 0 progress]
[Student]  ```rust
           fn main() { println!("hi"); }
           ```
[adam]     –Ė–į—Ä–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑! ...                              [pass count: 1; failed: 0 ‚Üí Hard hint]
[Student]  –ú–į“ď–į–Ĺ Rust-—ā–į ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑—ą—Ė.
[adam]     “ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ: –ė–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź —ā—Ė—Ä–ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ—Ė. –ú—č–Ĺ–į –ļ–ĺ–ī—ā—č —ā“Į–∑–Ķ—ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑:
           ```rust
           fn main() { let s = String::from("hi"); take(s); take(s); }
           fn take(s: String) { println!("{}", s); }
           ```
           “ö–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ “õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä —ą—č“ď–į–ī—č? `take` —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź—Ā—č —Ė—ą–ļ—Ė –ļ”©—ą—Ė—Ä–ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ—Ā–Ķ “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ā“Į–∑—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ?
              [‚ÜĎ Hard variant ‚ÄĒ adaptive selection working]
```

## [4.99.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Student-side curriculum-query intents (long-term roadmap step 3)

Step 3 of the long-term tutor arc. Rounds out the lesson-state surface added in v4.98.0 (foundation) and v4.98.5 (auto-advance announcements): students can now **proactively ask** about their journey rather than only seeing celebration pop-ups when stages auto-close. With v4.99.0 the curriculum surface is symmetric ‚ÄĒ adam **announces** progress AND **answers** progress queries.

### What's added

**Two new intents** (39 ‚Üí **41**):

- `Intent::AskNextTopic` ‚ÄĒ student asks "what's next?".
- `Intent::AskCurrentProgress` ‚ÄĒ student asks "where am I?".

**Detectors** ([semantics.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs)):

- `detect_ask_next_topic` ‚ÄĒ conjunction of advance-marker √ó learn-marker √ó question-marker (handles ¬ę–ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė / ”ô—Ä—Ė “õ–į—Ä–į–Ļ / –ļ–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ / –Ķ–Ĺ–ī—Ė¬Ľ √ó ¬ę“Į–Ļ—Ä–Ķ–Ĺ / —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ / —Ā–į–Ī–į“õ¬Ľ √ó ¬ę? / “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ / –Ĺ–Ķ / “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ¬Ľ).
- `detect_ask_current_progress` ‚ÄĒ direct phrase match (¬ę“õ–į–Ļ –∂–Ķ—Ä–ī–Ķ —ā“Ī—Ä–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į–Ļ —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ—ā—č –Ī—Ė—ā—Ė—Ä¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į–Ľ–Ņ—č “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ā“Ī—Ä¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –∂–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ¬Ľ) plus compositional progress-noun + question-marker fallback.

Both are dispatched **before** the pedagogical detectors (`AskExercise`, `AskPurpose`, etc.) so ¬ę–ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ—ā—č “Į–Ļ—Ä–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–Ķ–ľ?¬Ľ doesn't trip on the surrounding learning verb / topic noun.

**Curriculum recap renderer** ([curriculum.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/curriculum.rs)):

New public method `Curriculum::render_progress_recap_kk(progress) -> String` produces multi-line Kazakh prose listing every stage's status:

```text
1. **–ė–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ** ‚úď (2/2 ‚ÄĒ –Ī—Ė—ā—ā—Ė)
2. **“ö–į—Ä—č–∑ –į–Ľ—É** (1/2 ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä)
3. **”®–ľ—Ė—Ä –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£—Ė** ‚äė (“õ“Ī–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā–į)
4. **“ö–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä** ‚äė (“õ“Ī–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā–į)
5. **–ź—Ā–ł–Ĺ—Ö—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č** ‚äė (“õ“Ī–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā–į)
```

Status thresholds: `‚úď ... ‚ÄĒ –Ī—Ė—ā—ā—Ė` for closed; `... ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä` for current with prereqs met; `‚äė ... ‚ÄĒ “õ“Ī–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā–į` for locked (prereqs not yet closed).

**Conversation slot population** ([conversation.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs)):

- `AskNextTopic` ‚Üí reads `Curriculum::next_unlocked(progress)` ‚Üí populates `next_stage_label_kk` / `next_stage_summary_kk` / `next_stage_id` (or `__curriculum_complete__` when all stages closed).
- `AskCurrentProgress` ‚Üí if progress is non-empty, pre-renders the recap into `progress_recap` slot; if empty, sets `__progress_empty__` plus the first-stage label as a starter hint.

**Planner sub-key remap** ([planner.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs)):

- `AskNextTopic` + `__curriculum_complete__` ‚Üí `next_topic.complete`
- `AskNextTopic` ‚Üí `next_topic.suggestion`
- `AskCurrentProgress` + `__progress_empty__` ‚Üí `current_progress.empty`
- `AskCurrentProgress` ‚Üí `current_progress.recap`

Both `plan_response_with_session` and `plan_response_with_epistemic` are updated in lockstep.

**4 new template families** ([v1.toml](data/dialog/templates/v1.toml)):

- `next_topic.suggestion` (3 variants) ‚ÄĒ "–°—Ė–∑–≥–Ķ –ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė **{next_stage_label_kk}** —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č–Ĺ “Ī—Ā—č–Ĺ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ. {next_stage_summary_kk}"
- `next_topic.complete` (2 variants) ‚ÄĒ "–°—Ė–∑ –Ī–į—Ä–Ľ—č“õ —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ—ā–į—Ä–ī—č –Ī—Ė—ā—Ė—Ä—Ė–Ņ “õ–ĺ–Ļ–ī—č“£—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ Rust –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–≥—Ė –ļ—É—Ä—Ā —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ –ľ–Ķ“£–≥–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė."
- `current_progress.recap` (3 variants) ‚ÄĒ "–°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑: {progress_recap}"
- `current_progress.empty` (2 variants) ‚ÄĒ "”ė–∑—Ė—Ä–≥–Ķ –Ī—Ė—Ä –ī–Ķ –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É —ą–Ķ—ą—Ė–Ľ–ľ–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ. –Ď–į—Ā—ā–į—É “Į—ą—Ė–Ĺ **{next_stage_label_kk}** —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É —Ā“Ī—Ä–į“£—č–∑."

### End-to-end smoke test

```
[Student]  –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ?
[adam]     ”ė–∑—Ė—Ä–≥–Ķ –Ī—Ė—Ä –ī–Ķ –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É —ą–Ķ—ą—Ė–Ľ–ľ–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ. –Ď–į—Ā—ā–į—É “Į—ą—Ė–Ĺ **–ė–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ** —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É —Ā“Ī—Ä–į“£—č–∑.
[Student]  –ú–į“ď–į–Ĺ Rust-—ā–į ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑—ą—Ė.
[adam]     Ownership —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É: ...
[Student]  ```rust
           fn main() { println!("hi"); }
           ```
[adam]     –Ė–į“õ—Ā—č! –ö–ĺ–ī—č“£—č–∑ type-checker-–ī—Ė ”©—ā—ā—Ė. ...
[Student]  –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ?
[adam]     –ö—É—Ä—Ā –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į –∂–į“ď–ī–į–Ļ—č“£—č–∑:
           1. **–ė–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ** (1/2 ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä)
           2. **“ö–į—Ä—č–∑ –į–Ľ—É** ‚äė (“õ“Ī–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā–į)
           3. **”®–ľ—Ė—Ä –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£—Ė** ‚äė (“õ“Ī–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā–į)
           4. **“ö–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä** ‚äė (“õ“Ī–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā–į)

[Student]  –ö–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ—ā—č “Į–Ļ—Ä–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–Ķ–ľ?

```

The 6-turn flow demonstrates symmetric query: empty-state recap ‚Üí exercise + pass ‚Üí partial recap with mixed statuses ‚Üí next-topic recommendation that correctly identifies ownership as still-current (1/2 not yet closed).

### Regression tests

**Module-level** ([curriculum.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/curriculum.rs) ‚ÄĒ 3 new):

1. `recap_empty_progress_marks_first_stage_current_others_locked`
2. `recap_partial_progress_shows_mixed_statuses`
3. `recap_lists_every_stage_in_order`

**Integration** ([`tests/curriculum_v4990_query_intents.rs`](crates/adam-dialog/tests/curriculum_v4990_query_intents.rs) ‚ÄĒ 15 new):

Detector positives + negatives (4 tests), intent dispatch (2 tests), template-family presence + slot references (4 tests), end-to-end multi-turn flows (5 tests covering empty-state recap, fresh-state next-topic, partial-state mixed-status recap, post-ownership-closure advance to borrow, curriculum-complete celebration).

### Roadmap

- **v4.99.5** ‚ÄĒ Adaptive difficulty wiring: `pedagogical::exercise_for_topic` consumes `StageProgress::difficulty_hint` (added in v4.98.5) to scale exercise selection within a stage. Easy-hint students get the simpler exercise variant; Hard-hint students get a more involved one. Requires multiple curated exercises per topic ‚ÄĒ content investment.
- **v5.0.0+** ‚ÄĒ open per user direction. Candidate arcs: new book (Rustonomicon / Embedded Rust / Tokio Tutorial), additional curriculum tracks (Cargo / TOML / cross-platform), or non-Rust pedagogical surface.

**Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor (curriculum query symmetry; tutor loop now feels guided in both directions ‚ÄĒ adam announces AND answers).**

## [4.98.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ User-facing auto-advance + adaptive-difficulty hooks (long-term roadmap step 2)

Step 2 of the long-term tutor arc. Builds on v4.98.0's curriculum tracking foundation by making the student's curriculum journey **visible**: when a `passed` `SubmitSolution` verdict closes a curriculum stage, adam auto-announces the achievement AND the next-step recommendation in the same response.

### Why this matters

Pre-v4.98.5 a student passing exercise 2/2 on `ownership` got the same "–Ė–į—Ä–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑! –ö–ĺ–ī—č“£—č–∑ `cargo check`-—ā–Ķ–Ĺ —ā–į–∑–į–Ľ–į–Ņ ”©—ā—ā—Ė" response as on exercise 1/2 ‚ÄĒ the tracking happened but stayed invisible. With v4.98.5 the second pass triggers a different response that names the closed stage AND the next one with its summary, so the tutor genuinely guides the journey rather than answering each turn in isolation.

### What's added

**Conversation pre-planning slot stuffing** ([conversation.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs)):

Before calling the planner on a `SubmitSolution` turn, `Conversation` resolves the lesson stage (planner topic ‚Üí fallback `session.last_exercise_topic`), reads current progress, and pre-stuffs `extra_slots` IF this pass would close the stage:

- `__stage_closes__` = `"1"` ‚ÄĒ sentinel
- `stage_label_kk` ‚ÄĒ closing-stage Kazakh label
- `stage_passes` ‚ÄĒ closure threshold (used as both numerator and denominator in templates: `{stage_passes}/{stage_passes}`)
- `next_stage_label_kk` / `next_stage_summary_kk` / `next_stage_id` ‚ÄĒ next unlocked stage (computed via `Curriculum::next_unlocked` on a hypothetical post-pass progress map)
- `__curriculum_complete__` = `"1"` ‚ÄĒ when the closing stage is the last one

If the verdict turns out to be `failed`, the planner ignores these slots (sub-key remap only fires on `passed`).

**Planner sub-key remap** ([planner.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs)):

The `submit_solution.*` sub-key cascade gains two new branches **above** the legacy `passed`:

- `Some("passed") if slots.contains_key("__stage_closes__") && slots.contains_key("__curriculum_complete__")` ‚Üí `submit_solution.passed_curriculum_complete`
- `Some("passed") if slots.contains_key("__stage_closes__")` ‚Üí `submit_solution.passed_stage_closed`

Both `plan_response_with_session` and `plan_response_with_epistemic` are updated in lockstep.

**New template families** ([v1.toml](data/dialog/templates/v1.toml)):

- `submit_solution.passed_stage_closed` ‚ÄĒ 3 variants. Example: ¬ę–Ė–į—Ä–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑! **{stage_label_kk}** —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č –į—Ź“õ—ā–į–Ľ–ī—č ({stage_passes}/{stage_passes}). –ē–Ĺ–ī—Ė **{next_stage_label_kk}** —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č–Ĺ–į –ļ—Ė—Ä—É–≥–Ķ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č.\n\n{next_stage_summary_kk}¬Ľ
- `submit_solution.passed_curriculum_complete` ‚ÄĒ 2 variants. Example: ¬ę“ö“Ī—ā—ā—č“õ—ā–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ! **{stage_label_kk}** —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č –ī–į –į—Ź“õ—ā–į–Ľ–ī—č ‚ÄĒ Rust –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–≥—Ė –ļ—É—Ä—Ā—ā—č —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ –ľ–Ķ“£–≥–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ņ —ą—č“õ—ā—č“£—č–∑. –ē–Ĺ–ī—Ė –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č –∂–ĺ–Ī–į –∂–į–∑—č–Ņ –ļ”©—Ä—Ā–Ķ“£—Ė–∑ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č.¬Ľ

**Adaptive-difficulty hook** ([curriculum.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/curriculum.rs)):

New `StageProgress::difficulty_hint() -> DifficultyHint` returning:

- `Easy` ‚ÄĒ student has failed ‚Č• 2 times ‚Üí struggling, ease up
- `Hard` ‚ÄĒ 0 failures AND ‚Č• 1 pass ‚Üí confident, push harder
- `Normal` ‚ÄĒ everything else (initial state, mixed performance)

API surface only ‚ÄĒ content selection in `pedagogical::exercise_for_topic` doesn't yet read this hint. v4.99.0+ wiring will plug it in.

### End-to-end smoke test

```
[Student]  –ú–į“ď–į–Ĺ Rust-—ā–į ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑—ą—Ė.
[adam]     “ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ: –ú—č–Ĺ–į –ļ–ĺ–ī—ā–į “õ–į—ā–Ķ –Ī–į—Ä: ...
[Student]  ```rust
           fn main() { let s = String::from("hi"); println!("{}", s); }
           ```
[adam]     –Ė–į—Ä–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑! println —ā–į–Ņ—Ā—č—Ä–ľ–į“£—č–∑ —ą–Ķ—ą—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė ‚ÄĒ `cargo check` —ā–į–∑–į–Ľ–į–Ņ ”©—ā—ā—Ė. ...
[Student]  –ú–į“ď–į–Ĺ Rust-—ā–į ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑—ą—Ė.
[adam]     “ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä ownership –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ: ...
[Student]  ```rust
           fn main() { let n = 42; println!("{}", n); }
           ```
[adam]     –Ę–į–ľ–į—ą–į! –°—Ė–∑ **–ė–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ** —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č–Ĺ –ľ–Ķ“£–≥–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė“£—Ė–∑ (2 –∂–į—ā—ā—č“ď—É —ą–Ķ—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ņ “õ–ĺ–Ļ–ī—č).
           –ö–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė “õ–į–ī–į–ľ ‚ÄĒ **“ö–į—Ä—č–∑ –į–Ľ—É**: –ė–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–Ļ, —ā–Ķ–ļ “õ–į—Ä—č–∑“ď–į –į–Ľ—É: `&T` (read-only)
           –Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ `&mut T` (”©–∑–≥–Ķ—Ä—ā–Ķ –į–Ľ–į—ā—č–Ĺ). –Ď—Ė—Ä —Ā”ô—ā—ā–Ķ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ķ—É—Ė “ď–į–Ĺ–į –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č.
```

The 4-turn loop demonstrates the foundation kicking in: turn 2 fires the standard `passed` template (1/2 ‚ÄĒ stage not closed); turn 4 fires `passed_stage_closed` (2/2) with the borrow stage announcement.

### Regression tests

**Module-level** ([curriculum.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/curriculum.rs) ‚ÄĒ 4 new):

1. `difficulty_hint_initial_state_is_normal`
2. `difficulty_hint_easy_when_two_failures`
3. `difficulty_hint_hard_when_clean_passes`
4. `difficulty_hint_normal_for_mixed_below_easy_threshold`

**Integration** ([`tests/curriculum_v4985_auto_advance.rs`](crates/adam-dialog/tests/curriculum_v4985_auto_advance.rs) ‚ÄĒ 7 new):

1. `template_repo_has_passed_stage_closed_family`
2. `template_repo_has_passed_curriculum_complete_family`
3. `passed_stage_closed_templates_use_advance_slots` (every variant must reference the auto-advance slots)
4. `difficulty_hint_at_thresholds`
5. `next_unlocked_traverses_full_canonical_curriculum` (the real 5-stage curriculum visits every stage exactly once in dependency order)
6. `end_to_end_two_passes_close_ownership_stage_and_announce_borrow` (`#[ignore]` ‚ÄĒ real cargo check, full lesson loop)
7. `end_to_end_final_stage_pass_announces_curriculum_complete` (`#[ignore]` ‚ÄĒ synthetic preset progress on the last stage; closing pass surfaces `passed_curriculum_complete`)

### Roadmap

- **v4.99.0** ‚ÄĒ Student-side query intents: `AskNextTopic` (¬ę–ö–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ—ā—č “Į–Ļ—Ä–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–Ķ–ľ?¬Ľ), `AskCurrentProgress` (¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į–Ļ –∂–Ķ—Ä–ī–Ķ —ā“Ī—Ä–ľ—č–Ĺ?¬Ľ), recap template surfacing the full progress map. Intent count 39 ‚Üí 41.
- **v4.99.5+** ‚ÄĒ Adaptive difficulty wiring: `pedagogical::exercise_for_topic` consumes `difficulty_hint` to scale exercise selection within a stage; lesson-state dialog tree drives autonomous progression.

**Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor (curriculum auto-advance live; tutor now visibly guides the journey).**

## [4.98.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-08 ‚ÄĒ Lesson-state curriculum tree (long-term roadmap step 1) ‚ÄĒ foundation for adaptive tutor progression

First step of the long-term tutor arc, beginning after Codex's round-2 audit was fully closed in v4.97.0. Defines an ordered Rust pedagogical curriculum and adds per-conversation per-stage progress tracking. v4.98.0 ships **only the foundation** ‚ÄĒ user-facing response changes are deferred to v4.98.5 and student-side query intents to v4.99.0.

### Why this matters

Pre-v4.98.0 the tutor had per-turn lesson awareness (v4.95.5 multi-turn anaphora, v4.96.5 pedagogical-topic recording into DialogContext) but no notion of *curriculum journey*: each topic was an island, with no concept of "ownership is closed ‚Üí borrow is the next step", no accumulation of "this student has passed 2/2 ownership exercises", no foundation for adaptive difficulty. v4.98.0 adds that journey memory.

### What's added

**External curriculum data** ([`data/dialog/curriculum/rust_progression.json`](data/dialog/curriculum/rust_progression.json)):

Five canonical Rust stages with prereq edges:

| ID | Label (KK) | Prereqs | Exercises to pass |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ownership` | –ė–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ | ‚ÄĒ | 2 |
| `borrow` | “ö–į—Ä—č–∑ –į–Ľ—É | `ownership` | 2 |
| `lifetime` | ”®–ľ—Ė—Ä –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£—Ė | `ownership`, `borrow` | 2 |
| `traits` | “ö–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä | `lifetime` | 2 |
| `async` | –ź—Ā–ł–Ĺ—Ö—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č | `traits` | 1 |

Each stage carries a Kazakh summary intended for future `next_suggestion` templates.

**New module** [`adam_dialog::curriculum`](crates/adam-dialog/src/curriculum.rs):

- `Curriculum` ‚ÄĒ top-level schema (`load_default()`, `load_from_path()`, `stage()`, `next_unlocked()`, `is_complete()`)
- `Stage` ‚ÄĒ per-stage metadata + prereqs + closure threshold
- `StageProgress` ‚ÄĒ per-conversation per-stage counters (`passed` / `failed` + `record_pass()` / `record_fail()` / `is_closed()`)

**Conversation integration** ([conversation.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs)):

- New fields: `curriculum: Option<Curriculum>`, `curriculum_progress: HashMap<String, StageProgress>`.
- `Conversation::new()` autoloads the committed curriculum file (silent `None` if absent ‚ÄĒ back-compat for trimmed checkouts).
- After every `SubmitSolution` turn, `cargo_status` slot drives a per-stage counter update with **two-tier topic resolution**:
  1. Try `plan.slots["topic"]` against curriculum.
  2. Else fall back to `session["last_exercise_topic"]` against curriculum.
- `env_error` verdicts (local cargo broken) don't update either counter ‚ÄĒ failure is in the local setup, not the student's solution.

The two-tier resolution is needed because `detect_submit_solution` may extract an incidental topic from the snippet (e.g. `println` from a `println!`-using snippet) instead of the lesson topic established by a prior `AskExercise(ownership)`. The fallback ensures the lesson topic wins when the auto-extracted topic isn't a curriculum stage.

### What's deferred

- **v4.98.5** ‚ÄĒ User-facing response changes: `submit_solution.passed_stage_closed` template variant fires when the pass closes a stage; surfaces "–ė–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č –į—Ź“õ—ā–į–Ľ–ī—č (2/2). –ē–Ĺ–ī—Ė borrow —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ī—č–Ĺ–į –ļ—Ė—Ä—É–≥–Ķ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č" auto-suggestion. Adaptive difficulty hooks (failure-rate per stage influences next exercise selection).
- **v4.99.0** ‚ÄĒ Student-side query intents: `AskNextTopic` (¬ę–ö–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ—ā—č “Į–Ļ—Ä–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–Ķ–ľ?¬Ľ), `AskCurrentProgress` (¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į–Ļ –∂–Ķ—Ä–ī–Ķ —ā“Ī—Ä–ľ—č–Ĺ?¬Ľ), recap template surfacing the full progress map.

### Regression tests

**Module-level** ([curriculum.rs](crates/adam-dialog/src/curriculum.rs) ‚ÄĒ 7 new):

1. `stage_lookup_is_case_insensitive`
2. `stage_progress_closure_threshold`
3. `next_unlocked_starts_with_zero_prereq_stage`
4. `next_unlocked_advances_when_prereq_closes`
5. `next_unlocked_skips_unmet_prereqs`
6. `next_unlocked_returns_none_when_complete`
7. `load_default_finds_committed_curriculum` (validates committed JSON conforms + every prereq ID resolves)

**Integration** ([`tests/curriculum_v4980.rs`](crates/adam-dialog/tests/curriculum_v4980.rs) ‚ÄĒ 6 new):

1. `fresh_conversation_autoloads_curriculum`
2. `fresh_conversation_has_empty_progress`
