## [4.79.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book chapter 1 deepening + per-chapter test invariant ‚ÄĒ Bastau

Per user pushback (¬ę–ě–Ī—É—á–į—ā—Ć –Ĺ–į–ī–ĺ —Ā –Ņ–Ķ—Ä–≤–ĺ–Ļ –≥–Ľ–į–≤—č –ł –Ņ–ĺ—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ —É—Ā–Ľ–ĺ–∂–Ĺ—Ź—Ź¬Ľ): pivot the Rust-curriculum roadmap from gap-density-driven jumps to strict pedagogical chapter-by-chapter order, starting with chapter 1. Plus new directive (¬ę–ļ–į–∂–ī—É—é –≥–Ľ–į–≤—É –Ņ–ĺ–ļ—Ä—č–≤–į–Ļ —Ä–Ķ–į–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ—č–ľ–ł —ā–Ķ—Ā—ā–į–ľ–ł –ł —Ā—Ä–į–∑—É –ł—Ā–Ņ—Ä–į–≤–Ľ—Ź–Ļ –ł–Ľ–ł –ļ–ĺ—Ä—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–ł—Ä—É–Ļ –ĺ–Ī—É—á–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ¬Ľ): this release introduces the per-chapter holdout pattern that every future chapter release follows.

### What's added

**18 new curated entries `rust_200‚Ä¶217`** in `programming_rust.jsonl` covering chapter 1 (¬ę–Ď–į—Ā—ā–į—É¬Ľ) in curriculum-grade depth:

| ID | Subject | Topic |
|---|---|---|
| rust_200 | rustup | Toolchain manager (install command, components) |
| rust_201 | rustup update | Version refresh |
| rust_202 | rustup doc | Local stdlib docs |
| rust_203 | cargo new | Project scaffolding (Cargo.toml + src/ + git init) |
| rust_204 | src/main.rs | Source-file convention |
| rust_205 | cargo build | dev profile build to target/debug/ |
| rust_206 | cargo run | Build + execute (most-used command) |
| rust_207 | cargo check | Fast compile-check, no executable |
| rust_208 | cargo build --release | Optimized build to target/release/ |
| rust_209 | dev –Ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė | dev profile (debug, fast compile, no opt) |
| rust_210 | release –Ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė | release profile (slow compile, fast execution) |
| rust_211 | target “õ–į–Ľ—ā–į—Ā—č | Cargo artifact directory layout |
| rust_212 | println | Macro vs function (! suffix), formatter syntax |
| rust_213 | fn main | Entry-point semantics |
| rust_214 | rs –ļ–Ķ“£–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–ľ—Ė | .rs extension + snake_case file naming |
| rust_215 | edition | Rust edition system (2015/2018/2021/2024) |
| rust_216 | toml | TOML configuration format |
| rust_217 | rust —Ā—ā–ł–Ľ—Ė | Style umbrella (snake_case / CamelCase / SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE / 4-space indent / cargo fmt) |

Each entry: Kazakh definition + concrete code example pulled from the translated chapter (e.g. `cargo run -- arg1 arg2` for argument passing).

### First per-chapter test invariant

- `data/eval/rust_book_chapter_01_holdout.json` ‚ÄĒ 18 natural Kazakh queries against the new entries, 6 categories (`ch01_install`, `ch01_cargo`, `ch01_project_layout`, `ch01_profile`, `ch01_hello_world`, `ch01_style`).
- `crates/adam-dialog/tests/rust_book_chapter_01.rs` ‚ÄĒ runs them with **100 % floor** (each case maps to a shipped entry). Cross-cutting `rust_holdout` keeps its 70 % floor.
- Pattern repeats for `rust_book_chapter_02.rs`, `‚Ä¶03.rs`, ‚Ä¶ going forward.

### Matcher bug fixed alongside

`multiword_entity_hint` claimed longest-first matching in its docstring but actually iterated declaration order ‚ÄĒ surfaced when ¬ęcargo build --release¬Ľ got shadowed by ¬ęcargo build¬Ľ on the smoke test. Now sorts by length descending: `sorted.sort_by_key(|b| std::cmp::Reverse(b.len()))`. Zero regressions on the workspace 977-test suite.

### Topic extraction extensions

- `LATIN_TECH_SUBJECTS` += 4 bare tokens (`rustup`, `println`, `edition`, `toml`).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 22 compounds (subjects: `rustup update`, `rustup doc`, `cargo new`, `cargo build --release`, `src/main.rs`, `dev –Ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė`, `release –Ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė`, `target “õ–į–Ľ—ā–į—Ā—č`, `fn main`, `rs –ļ–Ķ“£–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–ľ—Ė`, `rust —Ā—ā–ł–Ľ—Ė`; objects: `rust “õ“Ī—Ä–į–Ľ—č`, `rustup –ļ–ĺ–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī–į—Ā—č`, `rust –Ī–į—Ā—ā–į–Ņ“õ—č —Ą–į–Ļ–Ľ—č`, `cargo –Ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė`, `cargo –į—Ä—ā–Ķ—Ą–į–ļ—ā “õ–į–Ľ—ā–į—Ā—č`, `rust –ľ–į–ļ—Ä–ĺ—Ā—č`, `rust –ļ—Ė—Ä—É –Ĺ“Į–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ā—Ė`, `rust —Ą–į–Ļ–Ľ –ļ–Ķ“£–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–ľ—Ė`, `rust –Ī–į—Ā—č–Ľ—č–ľ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė`, `–ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ą–ł–≥—É—Ä–į—Ü–ł—Ź –Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–ľ—Ė`, `—ā—Ė–Ľ —ą–į—Ä—ā—ā—č –ļ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė`).

### Acceptance

|---|---|
| 18 / 18 chapter 1 holdout cases | ‚úÖ 100% |
| Existing `rust_concepts_holdout` | ‚úÖ unchanged (no regression from matcher fix) |
| Workspace tests | **977 passing** (was 976; +1 chapter-1 test) |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |
| world_core entries | 2526 ‚Üí **2544** (+18) |

| Derived facts | 30760 ‚Üí **30771** (+11) |

| ‚Ä¶ | ‚Ä¶ | ‚Ä¶ through ch. 20 (web server) |

Cadence: `.0` minor ‚ÄĒ first per-chapter test invariant + multiword matcher fix + 18 curated entries; architectural addition (per-chapter test pattern). Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor + Rust curriculum.

Chapter 13 (484 lines, ¬ę–§—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–į–Ľ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä: –ł—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—ā–ĺ—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –∂–į–Ī—É–Ľ–į—Ä¬Ľ) was previously thin in `programming_rust.jsonl` ‚ÄĒ ~14 one-line stubs.

## [4.78.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Codex round-3 deferred bugs closed ‚ÄĒ office-holder direct rendering + political-safety detector + biology location/causal facts

Closes the 3 architecturally-deeper Codex round-3 bugs deferred from v4.77.5. After this release, all 7 round-3 bugs are addressed (4 in v4.77.5 + 3 in v4.78.0).

### Bugs fixed

**Bug 1 ‚ÄĒ office-holder direct answer.** ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ?¬Ľ / ¬ę“ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā –ļ—Ė–ľ?¬Ľ / ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ?¬Ľ previously surfaced ¬ęX –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ Y ”©–∑–į—Ä–į –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č¬Ľ via the catch-all `RelatedToOzaraDeclarative` rule. Office‚Üíperson facts (`gov_kz_006/016/017/020/022`) are stored as RelatedTo but should render definitionally. Fix: new `RelatedToOfficeHolderDeclarative` NLG rule registered before the catch-all. Detects subjects containing office markers (–Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā / –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä / –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä / –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä / ”ô–ļ—Ė–ľ / —Ö–į–Ĺ / —Ā–Ņ–ł–ļ–Ķ—Ä / —Ö–į–Ľ–ł—Ą–į / –Ī–į—Ā—ą—č / —Ā“Ī–Ľ—ā–į–Ĺ / ”ô–ļ—Ė–ľ—ą—Ė) and renders ¬ęX ‚ÄĒ Y¬Ľ.

**Bug 3 ‚ÄĒ political-recommendation refusal.** ¬ę–ú–į“ď–į–Ĺ –Ī—Ė—Ä –Ņ–į—Ä—ā–ł—Ź–Ĺ—č “õ–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į –ī–Ķ–Ņ –ļ–Ķ“£–Ķ—Ā –Ī–Ķ—Ä¬Ľ previously routed to generic templates. Fix: new `is_political_recommendation` detector in `discourse.rs` requiring AND of two markers ‚ÄĒ political topic (–Ņ–į—Ä—ā–ł—Ź / —Ā–į—Ź—Ā–į—ā—ą—č / –ļ–į–Ĺ–ī–ł–ī–į—ā / —Ā–į–Ļ–Ľ–į—É / –ī–į—É—č—Ā / –ł–ī–Ķ–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥ / –ļ”©—ą–Ī–į—Ā—ą—č / –ĺ–Ņ–Ņ–ĺ–∑–ł—Ü–ł—Ź) and recommendation verb (“õ–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į / –ī–į—É—č—Ā –Ī–Ķ—Ä / –ļ–Ķ“£–Ķ—Ā –Ī–Ķ—Ä / “Ī—Ā—č–Ĺ / —ā–į“£–ī–į—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ / –∂–į“õ—Ā—č—Ä–į“õ / —ā–ł—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė—Ä–Ķ–ļ / —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –ĺ–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ—Ā—č“£ / –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ / —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£). Sets `__political_safety__` slot. New `political_safety` template family (3 alternates) honestly refuses partisan advice while offering structural facts. Planner routes BEFORE `__code_input__`.

**Bug 7 ‚ÄĒ location/causal facts.** 6 natural Kazakh queries previously returned unrelated proverbs/quotes:

| Query | Pre-fix | Post-fix |
|---|---|---|
| –§–ĺ—ā–ĺ—Ā–ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–∑ “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ė? | unrelated quote | ‚úÖ —Ö–Ľ–ĺ—Ä–ĺ–Ņ–Ľ–į—Ā—ā + –∂–į–Ņ—č—Ä–į“õ |
| –Ę—č–Ĺ—č—Ā –į–Ľ—É “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ė? | unrelated | ‚úÖ ”©–ļ–Ņ–Ķ + –į–Ľ—Ć–≤–Ķ–ĺ–Ľ–į–Ľ–į—Ä |
| –ź—Ā “õ–ĺ—Ä—č—ā—É “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ė? | proverb ¬ę–ź—Ā“õ–į, —ā–ĺ–Ļ“ď–į‚Ä¶¬Ľ | ‚úÖ –į—Ā“õ–į–∑–į–Ĺ + —Ė—ą–Ķ–ļ + “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ/—Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā—Ė –ĺ—Ä—ā–į |
| –ú–ł—ā–ĺ–∑ “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ė? | unrelated | ‚úÖ –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į —Ź–ī—Ä–ĺ—Ā—č |
| “ö–į–Ĺ–į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č–ľ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ĺ –Ī–į—Ā—ā–į–Ľ–į–ī—č? | ¬ę–ě–Ľ–į—Ä–ī—č“£ –ĺ“£—ā“Į—Ā—ā—Ė–≥—Ė –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ą—č“ď—č—Ā—č–Ĺ —ā–į—É–Ľ–į—Ä –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė‚Ä¶¬Ľ | ‚úÖ –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į—Ā—ā–į–Ľ–į–ī—č + –į—Ä—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź–Ľ–į—Ä/–ļ–į–Ņ–ł–Ľ–Ľ—Ź—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä |
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć –∑–į“£–ī–į—Ä—č–Ĺ –ļ—Ė–ľ –į—ą—ā—č? | ¬ę–Ď–į–Ĺ–ļ–Ĺ–ĺ—ā—ā–į—Ä–ī—č “õ–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į–Ĺ –∂–į—Ā–į—É –∑–į“£–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ “õ—É–ī–į–Ľ–į–Ĺ–į–ī—č¬Ľ | ‚úÖ –ď—Ä–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä –ú–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć + 1865 + 3 –∑–į“£ |

Fix: added 6 curated entries `bio_s_121‚Ä¶126` to `biology_school.jsonl`. `bio_s_125` and `bio_s_126` carry dual-form subjects (`“õ–į–Ĺ–į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č–ľ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė` AND `“õ–į–Ĺ–į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č–ľ`; `–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć –∑–į“£–ī–į—Ä—č` AND `–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć –∑–į“£—č`) so both natural phrasings retrieve. ## [4.77.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Codex round-3 surgical fixes ‚ÄĒ case-suffix stripping in comparison + Uranium chem disambig + –Ē–Ě“ö alias + cultural foundations

Codex round-3 audit on v4.77.0 found 7 new bugs across politics / geology / culture / biology / Rust. v4.77.5 ships 4 surgical fixes (cases that need only data + small extraction tweaks); v4.78.0 will tackle the 3 architecturally-deeper bugs (direct-answer rendering, political safety, location/causal facts).

### Bugs fixed

**Bug 2 ‚ÄĒ case-suffix in comparison Y.** ¬ę–ü–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ “ģ–ļ—Ė–ľ–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė“£ –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ?¬Ľ ‚Üí previously returned the ¬ęhedge¬Ľ template because the comparison extractor produced Y=¬ę“Į–ļ—Ė–ľ–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė“£¬Ľ (with genitive ¬ę-—ā—Ė“£¬Ľ glued on), which didn't match the bare-form ¬ę“Į–ļ—Ė–ľ–Ķ—ā¬Ľ entry in world_core. Fix: new `strip_trailing_kazakh_case` helper called inside `try_extract_comparison_topics` ‚ÄĒ strips genitive / locative / dative / ablative / accusative suffixes from both X and Y, char-aware, with ‚Č•4-char-stem guard. Result: –ü–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā vs “ģ–ļ—Ė–ľ–Ķ—ā now surfaces dual definitions.

**Bug 4 ‚ÄĒ –£—Ä–į–Ĺ chem element disambiguation.** ¬ę–£—Ä–į–Ĺ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ previously surfaced astronomy fact ¬ę–£—Ä–į–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ –ö“Į–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –∂–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—ą—Ė “ď–į–Ľ–į–ľ—ą–į—Ä¬Ľ. Fix: added curated `chem_130` entry ¬ę–£—Ä–į–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ —Ä–į–ī–ł–ĺ–į–ļ—ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė –į—É—č—Ä —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā (U, ‚ĄĖ 92, –į–ļ—ā–ł–Ĺ–ĺ–ł–ī—ā–į—Ä —ā–ĺ–Ī—č). –Į–ī—Ä–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ –ĺ—ā—č–Ĺ —Ä–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –į—ā–ĺ–ľ —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ—Ü–ł—Ź–Ľ–į—Ä—č–Ĺ–ī–į “õ–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į–Ĺ—č–Ľ–į–ī—č. “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ ”ô–Ľ–Ķ–ľ–ī–Ķ —É—Ä–į–Ĺ “õ–ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ ”©–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä—É—Ė –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į —Ė—Ä—Ė –ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė—Ä—Ė.¬Ľ that outranks the astronomy entry on the ¬ę–ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ¬Ľ query (longer, more specific, higher curation rank).

**Bug 5 partial ‚ÄĒ cultural foundations.** ¬ę–Ě–į—É—Ä—č–∑¬Ľ surfaced an unrelated 2020 article fragment; ¬ę–ö–ł—Ė–∑ “Į–Ļ¬Ľ got reduced to ¬ę“Į–Ļ¬Ľ; ¬ę“ö–ĺ–∂–į –ź—Ö–ľ–Ķ—ā –Į—Ā–į—É–ł¬Ľ surfaced an unrelated proverb. Fix: added 6 cultural entries to `society.jsonl` (–Ě–į—É—Ä—č–∑ / –ö–ł—Ė–∑ “Į–Ļ / –Ē–į—Ā—ā–į—Ä—Ö–į–Ĺ / –Ď–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ļ / “ö—č–ľ—č–∑ / –ź–Ļ—ā) and 4 historical-figure entries to `notable_kazakhstanis.jsonl` (“ö–ĺ–∂–į –ź—Ö–ľ–Ķ—ā –Į—Ā–į—É–ł, ”ė–Ľ-–§–į—Ä–į–Ī–ł, –Ė–į–ľ–Ī—č–Ľ –Ė–į–Ī–į–Ķ–≤, “ö–ĺ—Ä“õ—č—ā –į—ā–į). MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended with 15 new compounds (“õ–ĺ–∂–į –į—Ö–ľ–Ķ—ā —Ź—Ā–į—É–ł / ”ô–Ľ-—Ą–į—Ä–į–Ī–ł / “õ–ĺ—Ä“õ—č—ā –į—ā–į / –∂–į–ľ–Ī—č–Ľ –∂–į–Ī–į–Ķ–≤ / –ļ–ł—Ė–∑ “Į–Ļ / –Ĺ–į—É—Ä—č–∑ –ļ”©–∂–Ķ / –ĺ—Ä–į–∑–į –į–Ļ—ā / “õ“Ī—Ä–Ī–į–Ĺ –į–Ļ—ā / various –ī”ô—Ā—ā“Į—Ä–Ľ—Ė/“Ī–Ľ—ā—ā—č“õ/–ī—Ė–Ĺ–ł –ľ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ categories).

**Bug 6 ‚ÄĒ –Ē–Ě“ö / DNA alias.** ¬ę–Ē–Ě“ö –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ previously returned `clarify_no_topic` because only ¬ę–Ē–Ě–ö¬Ľ was registered in `chem_104`. Fix: added `chem_104a` (–Ē–Ě“ö ‚ÄĒ Kazakh-style spelling) and `chem_104b` (DNA ‚ÄĒ international name) as alias entries pointing to the same nucleic-acid concept. All three (–Ē–Ě–ö / –Ē–Ě“ö / DNA) now surface the same definition.

## [4.77.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Dual-retrieval comparison + code-switch / Latin compounds

Two independent improvements shipped together because they don't conflict and both close Codex round-2 bugs. **Codex Bug 4 fully closed** (was partial in v4.76.5) and **Codex Bug 8 closed**.

### Part B ‚ÄĒ Dual-retrieval comparison (lifts v4.76.5 hedge)

¬ę–Ę“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ?¬Ľ now surfaces **both** definitions side-by-side in a single turn. v4.76.5 hedged honestly; v4.77.0 actually answers.

**Innovation**: in `Conversation::turn`, when `try_extract_comparison_topics` fires, look up X and Y directly from `self.extracted_facts` (each `Fact` carries `raw_text` = curated kk text from world_core entries). IsA-preferred lookup (most definitional), any-predicate fallback. Two new template variants:

- `compare_topics.dual` ‚ÄĒ fires when both definitions found; surfaces ¬ę–ź–Ĺ—č“õ—ā–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—Ä–ī—č “õ–į—ā–į—Ä “õ–ĺ–Ļ–ī—č–ľ: {X_def} {Y_def}¬Ľ style.
- `compare_topics.hedge` ‚ÄĒ falls back to v4.76.5 honest-refusal when either lookup misses (e.g. unknown topics like ¬ęATP –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ ABC¬Ľ).

**Verified**: –Ę“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č+–ź–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č —ā–ĺ–ļ / “ö—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ–ī—č“õ+–Ě–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–ļ –ĺ–ļ—Ā–ł–ī / –ú–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ĺ—Ä–į+–≠–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—ā / “ģ—ą–Ī“Ī—Ä—č—ą+–®–į—Ä—ą—č all surface dual definitions; unknown topics gracefully hedge.

### Part A ‚ÄĒ Code-switch / Latin compounds (Codex Bug 8)

¬ęPresent Simple –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ previously picked ¬ęSimple¬Ľ as topic; ¬ęPython list –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ picked ¬ęPython¬Ľ and ignored ¬ęlist¬Ľ; ¬ęfor i in range(3): print(i)¬Ľ fell to math_refusal.

**Innovations**:

1. **MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 24 bilingual education compounds: 7 English tenses (Present Simple/Continuous/Perfect, Past Simple/Continuous, Future Simple/Continuous), 8 Python data types (list/dict/set/tuple/string/int/float/class), HTML/CSS/SQL terms, Rust/JS compounds. Topic now extracted as full compound; if no world_core entry exists, graceful clarify fallback (¬ęPresent Simple —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č—Ä–į“õ –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–≥—Ė“£—Ė–∑ –ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė¬Ľ) instead of misroute to ¬ęSimple¬Ľ.

2. **New `discourse::input_is_code_snippet`** ‚ÄĒ Python-style code detector. Triggers on `print(`, `def ‚Ä¶(‚Ä¶):`, `class ‚Ä¶:`, `for ‚Ä¶ in ‚Ä¶ :` or `range(`, `if ‚Ä¶ : ‚Ä¶ ==`, `import` / `from ‚Ä¶ import`. Used to gate `input_is_math_expression` so code doesn't fall to math_refusal.

3. **New `code_refusal` template family** (3 alternates) ‚ÄĒ honest ¬ęI see code, can't run it yet, but ask about syntax/keywords¬Ľ.

4. **Planner routing** ‚ÄĒ `__code_input__` slot fires `code_refusal` BEFORE math_refusal.

**Verified**: ¬ęPresent Simple –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ ‚Üí topic ¬ępresent simple¬Ľ extracted, graceful clarify; ¬ęPython list –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ ‚Üí topic ¬ępython list¬Ľ extracted, graceful clarify; ¬ęfor i in range(3): print(i)¬Ľ ‚Üí code_refusal; ¬ędef square(x): return x * x¬Ľ ‚Üí code_refusal.

### Acceptance

| Codex Bug | Status |
|---|---|
| Bug 4 ‚ÄĒ comparison shape | ‚úÖ **fully closed** (dual-retrieval surfaces both defs; hedge for unknown pairs) |
| Bug 8 ‚ÄĒ code-switch + Latin compounds | ‚úÖ **closed** (compound topics extracted; code routes to dedicated refusal) |

| Regression | Status |
|---|---|
| All v4.76.5 procedural cases (5+7*2, x+2=5, F=m*a, P=2*(a+b), check_answer, explain_steps) | ‚úÖ preserved |
| Plain solo queries (¬ę–ú–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ĺ—Ä–į –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ) | ‚úÖ unchanged |
| 100-query battery diff vs v4.76.5 | **2 intentional upgrades** (the comparison queries ‚ÄĒ hedge ‚Üí dual definitions); zero regressions |
| Workspace tests | **976 passing** |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |
| `verify_release_version.sh 4.77.0` | green |

### Codex round-2 status ‚ÄĒ all bugs addressed

- ‚úÖ Bug 1 (multi-turn anaphora) ‚ÄĒ v4.73.0
- ‚úÖ Bug 2 (procedural tutor 3/3 ‚ÄĒ solve/check/explain) ‚ÄĒ v4.74.0/v4.75.5/v4.76.0
- ‚úÖ Bug 3 partial (where/why deeper) ‚ÄĒ needs more curated data; not architectural
- ‚úÖ Bug 4 (comparison shape ‚ÄĒ full dual definitions) ‚ÄĒ **v4.77.0**
- ‚úÖ Bug 5 (function-word noise) ‚ÄĒ v4.73.5
- ‚úÖ Bug 6 (factual-location routing) ‚ÄĒ v4.73.5
- ‚úÖ Bug 7 (Earth domain disambig) ‚ÄĒ v4.73.5
- ‚úÖ Bug 8 (code-switch / Latin compounds) ‚ÄĒ **v4.77.0**

### Why combined into one release

User asked: ¬ęWhy not both in v4.77.0?¬Ľ Honest answer: they're independent (different code paths, no conflicts), both close Codex bugs, and shipping them together saves a release cycle. The risk was Part B (refactor) blocking Part A (small additions); turned out Part B was additive (new lookup function) not invasive. Both implemented + tested + zero regressions.

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ closes two Codex bug families fully + new template families + new detector. Architectural addition (dual lookup; code detector).

Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor.

## [4.76.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Comparison shape ‚ÄĒ ¬ęX –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ Y –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č¬Ľ extracts both topics + honest hedge

Closes Codex round-2 Bug 4 (partial). ¬ę–Ę“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ?¬Ľ previously surfaced an unrelated proverb about ¬ę–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė¬Ľ (single-topic search picked the wrong subject). v4.76.5 adds a comparison-shape detector that extracts X and Y, names them in the response, and tells the user how to get full definitions for each. Honest hedge ‚ÄĒ full side-by-side comparison (both definitions in one turn) requires dual retrieval and is deferred to v5+.

### Innovations

**(1) New `discourse::try_extract_comparison_topics`** ‚ÄĒ pattern detector. Returns `Some((x, y))` when input contains:
- A comparison marker: `–į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č` / `–į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“õ` / `–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ą–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė` / `–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ ”©–∑–≥–Ķ—ą–Ķ` / `–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ą–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ–ī—Ė` / `“õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ą–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ–ī—Ė` / `“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į` / `—Ā–į–Ľ—č—Ā—ā—č—Ä`
- A ¬ę –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ ¬Ľ or ¬ę –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ ¬Ľ connector splitting the input into two topics

Conservative: only the bare ¬ęX –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ Y¬Ľ / ¬ęX –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ Y¬Ľ connector pattern is supported; ablative ¬ęX-–ī–į–Ĺ Y –Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ ”©–∑–≥–Ķ—ą–Ķ?¬Ľ pattern requires FST analysis and is deferred.

**(2) Topic override in Conversation::turn** ‚ÄĒ when comparison detector fires, X is set as the primary `noun_hint` (existing retrieval pipeline finds X's facts) and Y is carried in `__compare_y__` slot.

**(3) New template family `compare_topics`** (3 alternates) ‚ÄĒ names both X and Y, suggests user query each separately for full definition. Honest about not being able to surface both definitions in one turn yet.

**(4) Planner routing** ‚ÄĒ `__compare_x__` + `__compare_y__` slots route to `compare_topics` family BEFORE explain_steps / check_answer / math_answer. Mutually exclusive with those in practice (gated upstream).

### Acceptance

| Comparison query | v4.76.0 | v4.76.5 |
|---|---|---|
| –Ę“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ? | surfaces only ¬ę–Ę“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č —ā–ĺ–ļ ‚ÄĒ DC¬Ľ (partial) | ‚úÖ ¬ę–Ę“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –Ķ–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė –ī–Ķ —ā–į–Ĺ—č–Ľ–į–ī—č, –Ī—Ė—Ä–į“õ ‚Ä¶ —ā“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –∂–į–Ļ–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ –į–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –∂–į–Ļ–Ľ—č –∂–Ķ–ļ–Ķ —Ā“Ī—Ä–į“£—č–∑¬Ľ |
| “ö—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ–ī—č“õ –ĺ–ļ—Ā–ł–ī –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–ļ –ĺ–ļ—Ā–ł–ī –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ? | surfaces only “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ–ī—č“õ –ĺ–ļ—Ā–ł–ī (partial) | ‚úÖ names both, suggests separate queries |
| –ú–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ĺ—Ä–į –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ć–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—ā –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č –Ĺ–Ķ–ī–Ķ? | refuses or partial | ‚úÖ names both correctly |

| Regression | Status |
|---|---|
| Plain solo queries (¬ę–Ę“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č —ā–ĺ–ļ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ, ¬ę–ú–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ĺ—Ä–į –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ) | ‚úÖ unchanged ‚ÄĒ return full definitions |

### Deferred

- **Full side-by-side comparison** ‚ÄĒ requires dual retrieval (lookup facts for X and Y in same turn, surface both definitions). Scoped to v5+ when retrieval refactor lands.
- **Ablative comparison form** ‚ÄĒ ¬ęX-–ī–į–Ĺ Y –Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ ”©–∑–≥–Ķ—ą–Ķ?¬Ľ requires FST case analysis, not surface-string matching.
- **Three-way comparisons** (¬ęX, Y –ł Z –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“õ—ā–į—Ä—č¬Ľ) ‚ÄĒ out of scope; vast majority of Kazakh school comparisons are pairwise.

## [4.76.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ `explain_steps` intent ‚ÄĒ narrate the solution procedure (¬ę“ö–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą—ā—Ė“£?¬Ľ)

Closes Codex round-2 Bug 2 family **fully** (3 of 3): `solve_equation` (v4.74.0), `check_answer` (v4.75.5), and now `explain_steps`. After a successful equation/formula solve, user can ask ¬ę“ö–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą—ā—Ė“£?¬Ľ / ¬ę–ü—Ä–ĺ—Ü–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ –ļ”©—Ä—Ā–Ķ—ā¬Ľ / ¬ę“ö–į–ī–į–ľ-“õ–į–ī–į–ľ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ system surfaces a step-by-step narrative built deterministically by the solver at solve time.

### Innovations

**(1) Solver signature refactor** ‚ÄĒ `try_solve_linear_equation` and `try_apply_formula` now return `(unknown, value, steps)` 3-tuples instead of `(unknown, value)` pairs. Step narratives are built at solve time, deterministically, in Kazakh:

- **Linear x op a = b**:
  - `+`: ¬ę–Ď–į—Ā—ā–į–Ņ“õ—č —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É: x + 2 = 5. 2-–ī—č –Ķ–ļ—Ė –∂–į“ď—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ –ī–į –į–Ľ—č–Ņ —ā–į—Ā—ā–į–Ļ–ľ—č–∑: x = 5 ‚ąí 2 = 3.¬Ľ
  - `-`: ¬ę‚Ä¶ 2-–ī—č –Ķ–ļ—Ė –∂–į“ď—č–Ĺ–į –ī–į “õ–ĺ—Ā–į–ľ—č–∑: x = 5 + 2.¬Ľ
  - `*`: ¬ę‚Ä¶ –ē–ļ—Ė –∂–į“ď—č–Ĺ 3-“ď–į –Ī”©–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–∑: x = 15 / 3 = 5.¬Ľ
  - `/`: ¬ę‚Ä¶ –ē–ļ—Ė –∂–į“ď—č–Ĺ 3-“ď–į –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė–∑: x = 15 ¬∑ 3.¬Ľ
- **Linear a op x = b** ‚ÄĒ symmetric narrative
- **Formula** (e.g. F=m*a, m=2, a=3): ¬ę–§–ĺ—Ä–ľ—É–Ľ–į: F = m*a. –Ď–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ: a = 3, m = 2. –ě—Ä–Ĺ—č–Ĺ–į “õ–ĺ—é: F = 2*3 = 6.¬Ľ

**(2) New `discourse::try_explain_steps`** detector ‚ÄĒ pattern: `“õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą` / `–Ņ—Ä–ĺ—Ü–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ` / `“õ–į–ī–į–ľ` / `—ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä` / `–ī”ô–Ľ–Ķ–Ľ–ī–Ķ` / `—ą–Ķ—ą—É—Ė–Ĺ –ļ”©—Ä—Ā–Ķ—ā`. Returns stored steps when present; None otherwise.

**(3) Session storage extension** ‚ÄĒ `last_math_steps` saved alongside `last_math_unknown` and `last_math_result` after every successful solve.

**(4) Conversation::turn post-pass** ‚ÄĒ explain_steps runs AFTER math eval block (which doesn't fire on bare follow-ups like ¬ę“ö–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą—ā—Ė“£?¬Ľ since they have no digits/operators). Surfaces `__explain_steps__` slot when session has stored narrative AND input contains explain phrase. Skipped when fresh solve fired this turn (priority).

**(5) New template family** `explain_steps` (3 alternates):
```toml
"–®–Ķ—ą—É “õ–į–ī–į–ľ–ī–į—Ä—č: {explain_steps}",
"“ö–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą—č“õ—ā—č: {explain_steps}",
"–ü—Ä–ĺ—Ü–Ķ—Ā—Ė: {explain_steps}",
```

**(6) Planner routing** ‚ÄĒ `__explain_steps__` slot routes to `explain_steps` family BEFORE `check_answer.*` and `math_answer` (mutually exclusive in practice; explain has no `var=N` token).

### Acceptance

| Multi-turn flow | v4.75.5 | v4.76.0 |
|---|---|---|
| ¬ę–ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į, x “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę“ö–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą—ā—Ė“£?¬Ľ | refusal / clarify | ‚úÖ ¬ę–®–Ķ—ą—É “õ–į–ī–į–ľ–ī–į—Ä—č: –Ď–į—Ā—ā–į–Ņ“õ—č —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É: x + 2 = 5. 2-–ī—č –Ķ–ļ—Ė –∂–į“ď—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ –ī–į –į–Ľ—č–Ņ —ā–į—Ā—ā–į–Ļ–ľ—č–∑: x = 5 ‚ąí 2 = 3.¬Ľ |
| ¬ęx*3=15¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę“ö–į–ī–į–ľ-“õ–į–ī–į–ľ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä¬Ľ | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–®–Ķ—ą—É “õ–į–ī–į–ľ–ī–į—Ä—č: –Ď–į—Ā—ā–į–Ņ“õ—č —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É: x ¬∑ 3 = 15. –ē–ļ—Ė –∂–į“ď—č–Ĺ 3-“ď–į –Ī”©–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–∑: x = 15 / 3 = 5.¬Ľ |
| ¬ęF=m*a, m=2, a=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į F “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę–ü—Ä–ĺ—Ü–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ –ļ”©—Ä—Ā–Ķ—ā¬Ľ | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–®–Ķ—ą—É “õ–į–ī–į–ľ–ī–į—Ä—č: –§–ĺ—Ä–ľ—É–Ľ–į: f = m*a. –Ď–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ: a = 3, m = 2. –ě—Ä–Ĺ—č–Ĺ–į “õ–ĺ—é: f = 2*3 = 6.¬Ľ |

| Regression | Status |
|---|---|
| 5+7*2 ‚Üí 19 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| –ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 ‚Üí x=3 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| F=m*a ‚Üí 6 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| P=2*(a+b) ‚Üí 16 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| check_answer correct/incorrect | ‚úÖ preserved |
| 100-query battery diff vs v4.75.5 | **0 differences** |
| Workspace tests | **976 passing** |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |
| `verify_release_version.sh 4.76.0` | green |

### Strategic note

**Codex round-2 Bug 2 family complete: 3/3.** v4.74.0 added solve_equation, v4.75.5 added check_answer, v4.76.0 adds explain_steps. The procedural-tutor loop is now functionally complete for single-unknown linear equations + multi-variable formulas: solve ‚Üí verify user's answer ‚Üí explain procedure. All deterministic; no NN.

Per `project_kazakh_tutor_positioning` ‚ÄĒ this completes the core ¬ęcan conduct a lesson¬Ľ capability needed before procedural-intent expansion (comparison, where/why/why-important, code-switch). Next is Codex Bug 4 (comparison shape) in v4.76.5.

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ closes a Codex bug family fully + adds new intent + new template family. Algorithmic upgrade to solver return type (added steps narrative).

Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor.

## [4.75.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ `check_answer` intent ‚ÄĒ verify user-submitted answer against last solved equation/formula

Closes Codex round-2 Bug 2 fully (the ¬ęcheck the user's answer¬Ľ half). Multi-turn pattern: ¬ę–ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į, x “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ęx=3¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę–Ė–į—É–į–Ī—č–ľ–ī—č —ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä: x=4¬Ľ ‚Üí system compares user's value against stored answer and surfaces correct/incorrect verdict with explanation.

### Innovations

**(1) New `discourse::try_check_answer`** ‚ÄĒ pattern detector. Returns `(user_var, user_value, correct)` triple when input contains:
- A check-phrase marker: `—ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä` / `–ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –Ņ–į` / `–ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –ľ–į` / `–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –∂–į—É–į–Ī—č–ľ`
- A `var=N` token where var matches the prior turn's `last_unknown` and N is parseable integer

Conservative: doesn't try to re-solve from scratch or guess the variable. Returns None when no marker, or when variable mismatch.

**(2) Session state extension** ‚ÄĒ `Conversation::turn` now stores `last_math_unknown` alongside `last_math_result` after every successful equation/formula solve. Persists across turns until overwritten by next solve.

**(3) Pre-check gate in math eval block** ‚ÄĒ runs `try_check_answer` BEFORE any math evaluator. When it fires, sets `__check_answer_*` slots and skips arithmetic / linear / formula evaluators (preventing ¬ęx=3¬Ľ being treated as a fresh trivial equation).

**(4) Two new template families** in `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`:

```toml
[[families]]
key = "check_answer.correct"
templates = [
    "–Ē“Ī—Ä—č—Ā! {check_unknown} = {check_correct_value} ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –∂–į—É–į–Ī—č“£—č–∑ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā.",
    "–ė”ô, {check_unknown} = {check_correct_value} ‚ÄĒ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –∂–į—É–į–Ņ. –Ė–į—Ä–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑!",
    "–Ė–į—É–į–Ī—č“£—č–∑ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā: {check_unknown} = {check_correct_value}.",
]

[[families]]
key = "check_answer.incorrect"
templates = [
    "–Ė–ĺ“õ, –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –∂–į—É–į–Ņ {check_unknown} = {check_correct_value}, –į–Ľ —Ā—Ė–∑ {check_unknown} = {check_user_value} –ī–Ķ–Ņ –∂–į–∑–ī—č“£—č–∑. “ö–į–Ļ—ā–į —ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ņ –ļ”©—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑.",
    "–Ē“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā: —Ā—Ė–∑ {check_unknown} = {check_user_value} –ī–Ķ–Ņ –∂–į–∑–ī—č“£—č–∑, –į–Ľ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā—č {check_unknown} = {check_correct_value}.",
    "{check_unknown} = {check_user_value} ‚ÄĒ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā. –Ē“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –∂–į—É–į–Ņ: {check_unknown} = {check_correct_value}.",
]
```

**(5) Planner routing** ‚ÄĒ `plan_response_with_epistemic` now checks `__check_answer_correct__` slot BEFORE `__math_answer__` and routes to either `check_answer.correct` or `check_answer.incorrect` family based on the boolean. Slot fillers (`check_unknown`, `check_user_value`, `check_correct_value`) get hooked from the dedicated `__check_answer_*` slots.

### Acceptance

| Multi-turn flow | v4.75.0 | v4.75.5 |
|---|---|---|
| ¬ę–ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į, x “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę–Ė–į—É–į–Ī—č–ľ–ī—č —ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä: x=3¬Ľ | re-solves x=3 trivially ‚Üí ¬ę–Ě”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė: 3¬Ľ | ‚úÖ ¬ę–Ē“Ī—Ä—č—Ā! x = 3 ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –∂–į—É–į–Ī—č“£—č–∑ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā.¬Ľ |
| Same ‚Üí ¬ę–Ė–į—É–į–Ī—č–ľ–ī—č —ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä: x=4¬Ľ | re-solves ‚Üí ¬ę–Ě”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė: 4¬Ľ | ‚úÖ ¬ę–Ė–ĺ“õ, –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –∂–į—É–į–Ņ x = 3, –į–Ľ —Ā—Ė–∑ x = 4 –ī–Ķ–Ņ –∂–į–∑–ī—č“£—č–∑.¬Ľ |
| Same ‚Üí ¬ęx=3 –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –Ņ–į?¬Ľ | refuses or re-solves | ‚úÖ ¬ę–Ē“Ī—Ä—č—Ā! x = 3 ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –∂–į—É–į–Ī—č“£—č–∑ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā.¬Ľ |
| ¬ęF=m*a, m=2, a=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į F “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę–Ė–į—É–į–Ī—č–ľ–ī—č —ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä: F=6¬Ľ | re-solves | ‚úÖ ¬ę–Ē“Ī—Ä—č—Ā! f = 6 ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –∂–į—É–į–Ī—č“£—č–∑ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā.¬Ľ |

| Regression | Status |
|---|---|
| 5+7*2 ‚Üí 19 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| –ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 ‚Üí x=3 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| F=m*a (m=2, a=3) ‚Üí F=6 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| P=2*(a+b) ‚Üí 16 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| 100-query battery diff vs v4.75.0 | **0 differences** |
| Workspace tests | **976 passing** |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |
| `verify_release_version.sh 4.75.5` | green |

### Strategic note

This is the **first procedural-tutor capability that requires session state**. Previously, all math handlers were stateless (each query self-contained). check_answer needs to remember the prior turn's solve. Same architectural pattern will apply to `explain_steps` (v4.75.5+1, deferred) ‚ÄĒ narrate the procedure that produced the prior answer.

Codex round-2 Bug 2 family (¬ęno procedural tutor ‚ÄĒ solve_equation / check_answer / explain_steps¬Ľ) now has 2 of 3 slots filled. `explain_steps` is the remaining slot.

### Cadence

`.5` patch ‚ÄĒ additional procedural capability + new template families. Strictly extends v4.75.0; no architectural changes.

Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor.

## [4.75.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Paren-aware arithmetic ‚ÄĒ recursive-descent parser; lifts v4.74.5 paren-rejection guard

Replaces the v4.74.x flat token-based arithmetic evaluator with a proper recursive-descent parser that respects parentheses and operator precedence. Closes the v4.74.5 deferred case where ¬ęP=2*(a+b), a=5, b=3¬Ľ honestly refused (paren guard) ‚ÄĒ now computes 16 correctly. Pure deterministic; no NN.

### Bug closed

¬ęP=2*(a+b), a=5, b=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į P “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ v4.74.5 returned `“ö–į–∑–į“õ —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ ”©—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė ”ô–Ľ—Ė —ą–Ķ—ą–Ķ –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ` (paren-rejection guard fired). Without that guard, the v4.74.x evaluator would have silently filtered parens and returned 13 (wrong). Now returns **16** correctly.

### Innovations

**Recursive-descent parser** in `discourse::try_evaluate_arithmetic` replacing the v4.74.x flat-token two-pass eval. Standard grammar:

```
expr   = term (('+' | '-') term)*
term   = factor (('*' | '/') factor)*
factor = '-' factor | '+' factor | '(' expr ')' | number
```

Handles:
- Operator precedence (√ó√∑ before +‚ąí)
- Arbitrarily nested parens
- Leading unary minus / plus
- Negative numbers via factor recursion: `-(-5)` parses as 5
- Integer overflow checked via `checked_*` ops
- Division by zero ‚Üí None (graceful)

Kazakh-wrapper stripping from v4.74.0 preserved (extracts longest arithmetic-character substring before parsing). The kept-char set now includes `(` and `)`.

**Paren guard lifted in `try_apply_formula`** ‚ÄĒ formulas with parens now evaluate correctly. The v4.74.5 honest-refusal stub is replaced by direct evaluation.

### Acceptance

| Query | v4.74.5 | v4.75.0 |
|---|---|---|
| 2*(3+4) | (mis-computed via filter) ‚ÄĒ likely refused | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 14¬Ľ |
| (5-2)*3 | similar | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 9¬Ľ |
| P=2*(a+b), a=5, b=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į P “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | honest refusal (guard) | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 16¬Ľ |
| -10+(-5) | likely refused | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: -15¬Ľ |

| Regression | Status |
|---|---|
| 5+7*2 ‚Üí 19 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| –ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 ‚Üí x=3 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| F=m*a, m=2, a=3 ‚Üí F=6 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| v=S/t, S=100, t=20 ‚Üí v=5 | ‚úÖ preserved |
| 100-query battery diff vs v4.74.5 | **0 differences** |
| Workspace tests | **976 passing** |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |

### Deferred to v4.75.5

- `check_answer` intent (¬ę–Ė–į—É–į–Ī—č–ľ–ī—č —ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä: x=4¬Ľ) ‚ÄĒ needs new template family + session state for last solved equation. Scoped to v4.75.5 as smaller focused release with curated `check_answer.correct` / `check_answer.incorrect` template families.

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ algorithmic upgrade (parser replaces flat eval). Changes the entire arithmetic engine, not just adds a feature.

Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor.

## [4.74.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Procedural formula-applier ‚ÄĒ ¬ęF=m*a, m=2, a=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į F “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ

Second procedural solver in the v4.74.x series. Closes Codex round-2 Bug 2 partial: ¬ęF=m*a, m=2, a=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į...¬Ľ previously refused. v4.74.5 ships deterministic formula application ‚ÄĒ declare a formula + concrete substitutions + ask for the unknown, get the integer answer. Still no NN per `project_kazakh_tutor_positioning`.

### Innovations

**(1) New `discourse::try_apply_formula`** ‚ÄĒ handles the curriculum pattern ¬ęX=expr, var‚āĀ=N‚āĀ, var‚āā=N‚āā, ‚Ä¶ X “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ. Parses comma-separated `var=expr` segments, categorises one as the formula (RHS contains alphabetic chars) and the rest as numeric substitutions, then character-by-character substitutes single-letter variables and re-uses `try_evaluate_arithmetic` on the substituted expression.

**(2) Paren-rejection guard.** Current `try_evaluate_arithmetic` doesn't respect parens (filters them out, evaluates left-to-right with op precedence). Without this guard, ¬ęP=2*(a+b), a=5, b=3¬Ľ would silently return 13 instead of 16 ‚ÄĒ wrong, not just degraded. Guard: if formula RHS contains `(` or `)`, return None ‚Üí graceful refusal. Lift when paren-aware expression eval lands.

**(3) `Conversation::turn` integration**: try formula-applier BEFORE linear-equation solver because formula inputs contain multiple `=` tokens (formula + substitutions); the equation solver would pick the first and fail.

### Acceptance

| Query | v4.74.0 | v4.74.5 |
|---|---|---|
| F=m*a, m=2, a=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į F “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 6 (–į–Ľ—ā—č)¬Ľ |
| v=S/t, S=100, t=20, v “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 5 (–Ī–Ķ—Ā)¬Ľ |
| S=a*b, a=4, b=6, S “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 24 (–∂–ł—č—Ä–ľ–į —ā”©—Ä—ā)¬Ľ |
| P=2*(a+b), a=5, b=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į P “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | refusal | ‚ö†ÔłŹ honest refusal (paren guard ‚ÄĒ would mis-compute as 13 without it) |

| Gate | Status |
|---|---|
| 100-query battery diff vs v4.74.0 | **0 differences** (zero regression) |
| Workspace tests | **976 passing** |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |
| `verify_release_version.sh 4.74.5` | green |
| Regressions on v4.74.0 cases | all preserved (5+7*2 ‚Üí 19, x+2=5 ‚Üí 3) |

### Deferred

- Paren-aware arithmetic evaluator (would unblock ¬ęP=2*(a+b)¬Ľ case).
- `check_answer` intent (¬ę–Ė–į—É–į–Ī—č–ľ–ī—č —ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä: x=4¬Ľ) ‚ÄĒ needs session state to track last solved equation.
- `explain_steps` intent ‚ÄĒ narrate solution procedure.
- Quadratic / multi-unknown / fractional solvers.

### Cadence

`.5` patch ‚ÄĒ additional procedural solver in same architectural family.

Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor.

## [4.74.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ First procedural solver ‚ÄĒ natural-Kazakh arithmetic wrapper + linear equation solver

First step on the procedural-tutor roadmap (Codex round-2 Bug 2 family). Per saved memory `project_kazakh_tutor_positioning.md`, project is positioned as Kazakh school tutor; Codex's #1-priority gap was ¬ęno procedural tutor ‚ÄĒ solve_equation / check_answer / explain_steps refused¬Ľ. This release ships the simplest two solvers: arithmetic wrapped in natural Kazakh + 1-unknown linear equation. Both fully deterministic ‚ÄĒ no NN involved (per roadmap, NN integration scoped to v5+).

### Bugs identified by Codex round-2

| Failing query | Pre-fix | Root cause |
|---|---|---|
| 5+7*2 “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | refusal | `try_evaluate_arithmetic` rejected because cleaned string contained Cyrillic letters from ¬ę“õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į¬Ľ |
| 5+7*2 “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č? | refusal | same |
| –ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į, x “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | refusal | no equation solver existed |

### Innovations

**(1) `discourse::try_evaluate_arithmetic` strips natural-Kazakh wrapper**: pre-extracts the longest contiguous arithmetic substring (digits + ASCII operators) before evaluation. Drops trailing ¬ę“õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į / “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č / –Ĺ–Ķ—ą–Ķ–≥–Ķ —ā–Ķ“£ / –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ / –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ī–Ķ—Ä¬Ľ natural-language tails. ¬ę5+7*2 “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ now evaluates to 19.

**(2) New `discourse::try_solve_linear_equation`** ‚ÄĒ deterministic 1-unknown linear equation solver. Handles seven forms:
- `x = b` (trivial)
- `x + a = b`, `x - a = b`, `x * a = b`, `x / a = b`
- `a + x = b`, `a - x = b`, `a * x = b`, `a / x = b`

Detection: scans whitespace-separated tokens for one containing `=`; works inside any natural-Kazakh wrapper (¬ę–ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į, x “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ). Returns `(unknown, value)` tuple. Conservative: rejects multi-unknown, fractional, or non-integer-solution forms. Caller falls through to refusal for those.

**(3) `Conversation::turn` integration**: when `try_evaluate_arithmetic` and `try_evaluate_kazakh_word_math` both fail BUT `try_solve_linear_equation` succeeds, surface the value via `__math_answer__` slot path (so existing `math_answer` template family fires) plus `__math_unknown__` slot for the variable name.

### Acceptance

| Query | v4.73.5 | v4.74.0 |
|---|---|---|
| 5+7*2 “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 19 (–ĺ–Ĺ —ā–ĺ“ď—č–∑)¬Ľ |
| 5+7*2 “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č? | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 19 (–ĺ–Ĺ —ā–ĺ“ď—č–∑)¬Ľ |
| –ē–≥–Ķ—Ä x+2=5 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į, x “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 3 (“Į—ą)¬Ľ |
| x*3=15 | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 5 (–Ī–Ķ—Ā)¬Ľ |
| 10-y=4 | refusal | ‚úÖ ¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 6 (–į–Ľ—ā—č)¬Ľ |

| Gate | Status |
|---|---|
| 100-query battery diff vs v4.73.5 | **0 differences** (zero regression) |
| Workspace tests | **976 passing** |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |
| `verify_release_version.sh 4.74.0` | green |

### Deferred

- Quadratic / multi-unknown equations (need symbolic algebra layer).
- Formula-applier (¬ęF=ma, m=2, a=3 –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į F “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ) ‚ÄĒ needs formula library + variable-substitution intent.
- `check_answer` intent ‚ÄĒ verify user-supplied solution against equation.
- `explain_steps` intent ‚ÄĒ narrate solution procedure (¬ę–į–Ľ–ī—č–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ 2-–Ĺ—Ė –Ķ–ļ—Ė –∂–į“ď—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ –ī–į –į–Ľ—č–Ņ —ā–į—Ā—ā–į–Ļ–ľ—č–∑...¬Ľ).
- All Codex round-2 Bug 3-8 categories.

### Strategic note

This is `v4.74.0` not `v4.74.5` because adding a **new procedural capability** (deterministic equation solver) is a meaningful feature increment per memory `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`. Per roadmap, v4.74.x will be pure deterministic procedural work (no NN); NN integration (disambiguator first) scoped to v5.0.0+.

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ first procedural-solver capability, dialog-architecture progression.

Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor.

## [4.73.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Codex round-2 bugs ‚ÄĒ factual-location routing fix + function-word topic blocklist + Earth-planet priority

Driven by Codex 2026-05-06 round-2 review (after v4.73.0 ship). Codex confirmed v4.73.0 multi-turn fix worked, then identified 8 additional failure patterns. This release fixes the 4 with surgical scope (security/correctness); 4 deferred to procedural-intent / data-structure work.

### Bugs fixed

**Bug 6 ‚ÄĒ Critical factual-location routing.** ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –į—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į—Ā—č “õ–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į?¬Ľ previously hit `AskLocation`, producing system-identity ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ false claim. `detect_ask_location` was too broad: any input with ¬ę“õ–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į / “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ĺ / “õ–į–Ļ –į—É–ī–į–Ĺ¬Ľ triggered it. Fix: gate on explicit user-addressing markers (2nd-person pronoun `—Ā—Ė–∑ / —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ` as separate token, possessive `—Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ / —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£`, or live-verb `—ā“Ī—Ä–į—Ā—č–∑ / —ā“Ī—Ä–į—Ā—č“£ / “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑ / “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ĺ—Ā—č“£`). Without those markers, factual location queries about third-party subjects stay Unknown so retrieval handles them.

**Bug 5 ‚ÄĒ Function-word retrieval noise.** ¬ę–ú—č—Ā–į–Ľ –ļ–Ķ–Ľ—ā—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑¬Ľ surfaced unrelated ¬ę–ú—č—Ā–į–Ľ—č, –®—č–ľ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā‚Ä¶¬Ľ quote; ¬ę–Ě”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į?¬Ľ surfaced ¬ę–Ě”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –ö–ł–Ķ–≤‚Ä¶¬Ľ quote; ¬ę–ú–į“£—č–∑–ī—č –ľ–į?¬Ľ fell to clarify; ¬ę–ē–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—Ā—č “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ?¬Ľ surfaced proverb about ¬ę–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė¬Ľ. Fix: extend NOT_A_TOPIC with discourse particles never appearing as topics ‚ÄĒ `–ľ—č—Ā–į–Ľ / –Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ / –ľ–į“£—č–∑–ī—č / “õ–į–Ļ—Ā—č / –Ķ–≥–Ķ—Ä / –Ķ–≥–Ķ—Ä–ī–Ķ / –Ķ–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė / –Ķ–ļ–Ķ—É / –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—Ā—č / –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č`. Now these queries fall to clarify_no_topic (graceful refuse) instead of producing nonsense quotes.

**Bug 7 ‚ÄĒ Earth picks economic-resource fact over planet sense.** ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä –∂–į–Ľ–Ņ–į“õ –Ņ–į?¬Ľ surfaced ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä ‚ÄĒ ”©–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä—Ė—Ā–ļ–Ķ “õ–į—ā—č—Ā–į—ā—č–Ĺ —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–ł —Ä–Ķ—Ā—É—Ä—Ā¬Ľ (economics_basic) instead of planet sense. Fix: 2 curated planet-priority entries in `astronomy.jsonl`: astro_048 ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä –∂–į–Ľ–Ņ–į“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā ‚ÄĒ —ą–į—Ä —ā”ô—Ä—Ė–∑–ī—Ė (–≥–Ķ–ĺ–ł–ī) “ď–į–Ľ–į–ľ—ą–į—Ä, R~6371 –ļ–ľ¬Ľ (exact-matches ¬ę–∂–į–Ľ–Ņ–į“õ¬Ľ in query) + astro_049 ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä ‚ÄĒ –ö“Į–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė “Į—ą—Ė–Ĺ—ą—Ė “ď–į–Ľ–į–ľ—ą–į—Ä; ~150 –ľ–Ľ–Ĺ –ļ–ľ –ö“Į–Ĺ–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ; –∂–į–Ľ“ď—č–∑ —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–į—Ä –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī—Ė–≥—Ė –ī”ô–Ľ–Ķ–Ľ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ¬Ľ.

**Bug 4 partial ‚ÄĒ Comparison topic eclipse.** ¬ę–ē–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—Ā—č “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ?¬Ľ picked ¬ę–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė¬Ľ as topic. Now blocked via NOT_A_TOPIC; falls to clarify. Full comparison support (template family + comparison logic) still scoped to v4.74+.

### Deferred per Codex round-2

- Bug 1 (follow-up just repeats prior fact) ‚ÄĒ needs procedural intents + procedural answers, not just topic resolution. Scoped to v4.73.5+ procedural work.
- Bug 2 (no procedural tutor) ‚ÄĒ `solve_equation` / `check_answer` / `explain_steps` solvers + intents. Major v4.74 scope.
- Bug 3 (where/why/compare weak) ‚ÄĒ needs deeper world_core (specific where-facts, causal explanations, comparison templates).
- Bug 8 (code-switch / Latin compounds) ‚ÄĒ separate localization release.

### Acceptance

|---|---|---|
| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –į—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į—Ā—č “õ–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į? | ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ (false self-identity) | ‚úÖ surfaces geo_kz_115 ¬ę–į—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į—Ā—č ‚ÄĒ –ź—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į¬Ľ |
| “ö–į—Ä–į —ā–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ļ “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–ī–į “õ–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į–ī–į –ĺ—Ä–Ĺ–į–Ľ–į—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ? | ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ | ‚úÖ honest ¬ę—ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä–Ķ –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ (no false claim) |
| –°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ľ–į–ī–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑? | (system identity) | ‚úÖ still works (system identity preserved when user-marker present) |
| –ú—č—Ā–į–Ľ –ļ–Ķ–Ľ—ā—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑ | unrelated –®—č–ľ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā quote | ‚úÖ clarify_no_topic |
| –Ě”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į? | unrelated Kiev quote | ‚úÖ clarify_no_topic |
| –Ė–Ķ—Ä –∂–į–Ľ–Ņ–į“õ –Ņ–į? | ¬ę—ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–ł —Ä–Ķ—Ā—É—Ä—Ā¬Ľ | ‚úÖ ¬ę—ą–į—Ä —ā”ô—Ä—Ė–∑–ī—Ė “ď–į–Ľ–į–ľ—ą–į—Ä¬Ľ |
| –ē–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į–Ļ—č—Ä–ľ–į—Ā—č “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ? | proverb about ¬ę–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—É—Ė¬Ľ | ‚úÖ clarify_no_topic |

### Strategic note (Codex hybrid proposal)

Codex's round-2 also raised a substantive architectural proposal: **agglutinative-tokenizer + neural network + FST decoder + retrieval/world-core + verifier hybrid**. Per saved memory `project_kazakh_tutor_positioning.md` the project is positioned as Kazakh school tutor (not LLM replacement), but the hybrid framing is consistent with that: NN for generation flexibility, FST for morphological correctness, world_core for facts, verifier for hallucination control. User signaled support. This is forward-looking architectural direction beyond the bug-fix scope of v4.73.5; analyzed in detail in commit message + chat response, not yet implemented.

### Cadence

`.5` patch ‚ÄĒ runtime + small data fixes from systematic review.

Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor.

## [4.73.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Multi-turn anaphora fix ‚ÄĒ block StatementOfLocation false-positive on pronouns + extend bare-interrogative follow-up resolution

Driven by Codex 2026-05-06 review (forwarded by user). Codex identified strategic positioning + 6 systemic gaps. This release closes the most critical: the multi-turn tutor loop. Strategic positioning memory saved as `project_kazakh_tutor_positioning.md` ‚ÄĒ project is a Kazakh school tutor, NOT an LLM replacement; 6 Codex recommendations sit on top of multi-turn working.

### Critical security bug closed

¬ę–Ę–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ ‚Üí answered, then ¬ę–ě–Ĺ—č “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą–Ķ–ľ—Ė–∑?¬Ľ (anaphoric "how do we solve it?") was misclassified as `StatementOfLocation { city: "–ě–Ĺ—č" }` ‚ÄĒ the system saved ¬ę–ě–Ĺ—č¬Ľ (accusative of pronoun ¬ę–ĺ–Ľ¬Ľ) as the user's city in session, a false biographical claim. Bug pattern:

1. `—ą–Ķ—ą–Ķ–ľ—Ė–∑` is 1Pl verb ‚Üí `has_first_person_location_context` returns true.
2. FST analysed ¬ę–ě–Ĺ—č¬Ľ (Acc of ¬ę–ĺ–Ľ¬Ľ) as a noun candidate.
3. The case-scan / rule-based fallback picked it as a "city".
4. `conversation.rs::Intent::StatementOfLocation` saved it to session: `"city" ‚Üí "–ě–Ĺ—č"`.

Same pattern affected ¬ę–ě–Ľ “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ė?¬Ľ / ¬ę–ö—Ė–ľ “õ“Ī—Ä–ī—č?¬Ľ / ¬ę–Ď“Ī–Ľ “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ī—Ė?¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ pronouns + 1st-person verbs producing biographical false positives.

### Innovations

**(1) `semantics.rs::interpret_text_with_lexicon` gates `detect_statement_of_location`** on `!discourse::input_contains_discourse_anaphor(input)`. Discourse anaphors (–ĺ–Ĺ—č / –ĺ–Ľ / –Ī“Ī–Ľ / —Ā–ĺ–Ľ / –ĺ–Ĺ–ī–į / etc.) are NEVER first-person location statements. When the input contains one, intent stays Unknown so the existing v4.13.0 anaphora-resolution path (lines 668-708 of conversation.rs) can substitute the prior turn's topic.

**(2) `discourse::input_contains_discourse_anaphor` extended** with bare-interrogative follow-up detection (`is_short_interrogative_followup`):
- triggers on input ‚Č§ 4 tokens
- AND containing wh-interrogative (`–ļ—Ė–ľ` / `“õ–į–Ļ–ī–į` / `“õ–į—ą–į–Ĺ` / `–Ĺ–Ķ–≥–Ķ` / `–Ĺ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ` / `“õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ`)
- AND no other content noun.

This closes Codex's ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č “õ–į—ą–į–Ĺ “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ–ī—č?¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę–ö—Ė–ľ “õ“Ī—Ä–ī—č?¬Ľ / ¬ę–§–ĺ—ā–ĺ—Ā–ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–∑ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę–ě–Ľ “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ė?¬Ľ follow-up patterns. The interrogative is treated anaphorically: implicit subject is `last_query_topic`.

### Acceptance ‚ÄĒ multi-turn tests

| Pre-fix flow | v4.72.5 behavior | v4.73.0 behavior |
|---|---|---|
| –Ę–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? ‚Üí –ě–Ĺ—č “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą–Ķ–ľ—Ė–∑? | StatementOfLocation { city: "–ě–Ĺ—č" } saved as user's city ‚ĚĆ | resolves to –Ę–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É, surfaces equation definition ‚úÖ |
| –§–ĺ—ā–ĺ—Ā–ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–∑ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? ‚Üí –ě–Ľ “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ė? | "–∂“Į—Ä–Ķ" picked as topic, low-confidence clarify | resolves to –§–ĺ—ā–ĺ—Ā–ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–∑, surfaces photosynthesis fact ‚úÖ |
| “ö–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č “õ–į—ą–į–Ĺ “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ–ī—č? ‚Üí –ö—Ė–ľ “õ“Ī—Ä–ī—č? | "“õ“Ī—Ä" verb stem picked, clarify | resolves to “ö–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č, surfaces Khanate fact ‚úÖ |
| Single-shot ¬ę–ě–Ĺ—č “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą–Ķ–ľ—Ė–∑?¬Ľ (no prior) | StatementOfLocation { city: "–ě–Ĺ—č" } ‚ĚĆ | unknown.clarify_no_topic (graceful refuse) ‚úÖ |

### Acceptance ‚ÄĒ gates

| Gate | Status |
|---|---|
| 100-query battery diff vs v4.72.5 | **0 differences** (no regression) |
| Workspace tests | **976 passing** unchanged |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green |
| `verify_release_version.sh 4.73.0` | green |

### Strategic note

Per Codex review and saved memory `project_kazakh_tutor_positioning.md`: the answer for ¬ę–ě–Ĺ—č “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ —ą–Ķ—ą–Ķ–ľ—Ė–∑?¬Ľ now correctly resolves to ¬ę–Ę–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É¬Ľ, but only surfaces the equation's DEFINITION fact, not a procedural ¬ęhere's how to solve it¬Ľ response. That's the intentional next-step boundary ‚ÄĒ procedural intents (`solve_equation` / `show_example` / `step_by_step`) are scoped to v4.73.5+. v4.73.0 closes only the security false-positive and the topic-resolution gap.

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ multi-turn architecture fix; no data changes.

Stripe ‚ÄĒ Kazakh school tutor (positioning shift from ¬ęeducational portal¬Ľ).

## [4.72.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Comprehensive 100-query battery audit + 4 fixes (passive-verb topic eclipse, low-confidence native nouns, ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä –Ī–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ phrasing variant)

Driven by user-mandated systematic battery test across all v4.66.0+ educational content. 100 natural-Kazakh queries spanning 12 sub-domains (algebra / advanced geometry / mechanics / thermodynamics / E&M / organic chem / inorganic chem / world history / literary devices / world geography / medicine). **Result: 94 PASS / 4 FAIL / 2 PARTIAL.** This release fixes the 4 hard failures; 2 partials (comparison shape) deferred to template-family work.

### Bugs identified by battery

| Failing query | Pre-fix | Root cause |
|---|---|---|
| –Ē–ł–į–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė? | clarify ¬ę–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č‚Ä¶¬Ľ | –ī–ł–į–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä not in MULTIWORD; FST picks verb stem ¬ę–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ¬Ľ as topic |
| –†–į–ī–ł—É—Ā “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė? | (same as –ī–ł–į–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä on retest) | —Ä–į–ī–ł—É—Ā not in MULTIWORD |
| –Ė–ĺ–Ľ “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė? | ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ –∂–ĺ–Ľ –∂–į–Ļ—č–Ĺ–ī–į —Ā“Ī—Ä–į–Ņ –ĺ—ā—č—Ä—Ā—č–∑ –ľ–į?¬Ľ | –∂–ĺ–Ľ is FST noun but second-pass classification ‚Üí Low confidence ‚Üí clarify_low_confidence template, even with grounded_fact populated |
| –Ė–Ķ—Ä –Ī–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł–ļ –Ī–į—Ä? | ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė—Ä —Ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–≥—Ė –Ī–į—Ä¬Ľ | retrieval picks moon-count fact over wg_013 ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä –Ņ–Ľ–į–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–į—Ā—č–Ĺ–ī–į...¬Ľ (phrasing mismatch ‚ÄĒ query says ¬ę–Ī–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ, fact says ¬ę–Ņ–Ľ–į–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–į—Ā—č–Ĺ–ī–į¬Ľ) |
| –Ė–Ķ—Ä –Ī–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā –Ī–į—Ä? | ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė—Ä —Ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–≥—Ė –Ī–į—Ä¬Ľ | same ‚ÄĒ wg_014 says ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä –Ī–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė —ā”©—Ä—ā –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā¬Ľ but retrieval ranks moon fact higher |

### Innovations

**(1) `NOT_A_TOPIC` extended** with passive ¬ęhow is X computed?¬Ľ verb forms (–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ / –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É / –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė / –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ī—Ė / –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė / –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č / –∂–į—Ā–į–Ľ–į–ī—č / –∂–į—Ā–į–Ľ–ī—č). These are predicate verbs surfaced when the actual topic X is a FST-unknown loanword; they should never be picked as topic.

**(2) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` extended** with 13 single-word native-Kazakh and loanword shamas missed in v4.71.5: –ī–ł–į–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä / —Ä–į–ī–ł—É—Ā / –Ņ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä / –į—É–ī–į–Ĺ / –ļ”©–Ľ–Ķ–ľ / –∂–ĺ–Ľ / —É–į“õ—č—ā / –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į / —ā—č“ď—č–∑–ī—č“õ / –∂—č–Ľ–ī–į–ľ–ī—č“õ / “Į–ī–Ķ—É / –ļ“Į—ą / –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā. Loanwords (–ī–ł–į–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä, —Ä–į–ī–ł—É—Ā, –Ņ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä) need this because they're not in FST lexicon. Native words (–∂–ĺ–Ľ, —É–į“õ—č—ā, –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į, etc.) need it because their FST analyses route to second-pass (Low confidence) which fires `clarify_low_confidence` template instead of surfacing the grounded_fact.

**(3) 2 alias entries added to `world_geography.jsonl`** with ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä –Ī–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ phrasing matching common query forms:
- wg_016: ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä –Ī–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –į–Ľ—ā—č –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł–ļ –Ī–į—Ä: –ē—É—Ä–į–∑–ł—Ź, –ź—Ą—Ä–ł–ļ–į, –°–ĺ–Ľ—ā“Į—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –ź–ľ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ļ–į, –ě“£—ā“Į—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –ź–ľ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ļ–į, –ź–≤—Ā—ā—Ä–į–Ľ–ł—Ź, –ź–Ĺ—ā–į—Ä–ļ—ā–ł–ī–į.¬Ľ
- wg_017: ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—Ä –Ī–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ —ā”©—Ä—ā –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā –Ī–į—Ä: –Ę—č–Ĺ—č“õ –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā, –ź—ā–Ľ–į–Ĺ—ā –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā—č, “ģ–Ĺ–ī—Ė –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā—č, –°–ĺ–Ľ—ā“Į—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –ú“Ī–∑–ī—č –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā.¬Ľ

These use `has_quantity` predicate so they don't conflict with existing wg_013/wg_014 list facts; alias coverage for retrieval keyword overlap.

**(4) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES** also registers ¬ę–į–Ľ—ā—č –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł–ļ¬Ľ / ¬ę—ā”©—Ä—ā –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ the new compound objects from wg_016/017 (required by `world_core_multiword_coverage` invariant).

| –†–į–ī–ł—É—Ā “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė? | (failed similarly) | ‚úÖ full radius definition |

## [4.72.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Listing-intent broadening + curated aggregator entries (planets / body parts / continents / oceans / equation types)

Driven by user's second live REPL session against v4.71.5 ‚ÄĒ surfaced systemic gap where natural-Kazakh listing queries (¬ęX-—ā–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė“£ –į—ā–į—É–Ľ–į—Ä—č¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ī–į—Ä–Ľ—č“õ X-—ā–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė –į—ā–į“£—č–∑¬Ľ, ¬ęX-—ā–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ“£—Ė–∑¬Ľ) fell through to Definition shape and surfaced singular-definition facts. Pattern audit: 6 of 12 transcript queries failed because aggregating list facts didn't exist for astro / body_parts / world_geography / math, while the matching Kazakhstan-specific lists in geography_kz did work (curated aggregator entries existed there).

### Bugs identified

| –ö“Į–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –Ņ–Ľ–į–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–į–Ľ–į—Ä–ī—č“£ –į—ā–į—É–Ľ–į—Ä—č | ¬ę–ö“Į–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė IsA –≥–į–Ľ–į–ļ—ā–ł–ļ–į¬Ľ | no list-bearing fact for ¬ę–ļ“Į–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė related_to “ď–į–Ľ–į–ľ—ą–į—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė¬Ľ |
| –ö“Į–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –Ī–į—Ä–Ľ—č“õ –Ņ–Ľ–į–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–į–Ľ–į—Ä–ī—č –į—ā–į“£—č–∑ | same | same |
| –ź–ī–į–ľ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ī–į—Ä–Ľ—č“õ –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ĺ –į—ā–į“£—č–∑ | unrelated programming-languages list | `–į—ā–į“£—č–∑` not in `is_listing` markers |
| –ź–ī–į–ľ–Ĺ—č“£ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė –Ī–į—Ä | ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ł–Ķ—Ā—Ė¬Ľ | no aggregate body-parts fact |
| –Ę–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É–Ľ–Ķ—Ä –∂“Ī–Ī—č–Ĺ —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ“£—Ė–∑ | —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É definition | `—ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ“£—Ė–∑` not in `is_listing` markers |
| “ö–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É–Ľ–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑ | —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É definition | ¬ę“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ ‚Ä¶ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑¬Ľ pattern not detected (deferred ‚ÄĒ broader intent work) |

### Innovations

**(1) `question_shape::is_listing` broadened** ‚ÄĒ added 5 new surface markers:
- `–į—ā–į—É–Ľ–į—Ä—č` (already there) / `–į—ā–į“£—č–∑` (NEW ‚ÄĒ imperative ¬ęname them¬Ľ) / `–į—ā–į–Ņ –Ī–Ķ—Ä` / `–į—ā–į–Ņ –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑`
- `—ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė` (already) / `—ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ` (already) / `—ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ“£—Ė–∑` (NEW ‚ÄĒ imperative ¬ęlist them¬Ľ)
- `–Ī–į—Ä–Ľ—č“õ ` (NEW ‚ÄĒ quantifier ¬ęall¬Ľ)

**(2) 9 aggregating list-bearing entries added** to existing canonical files (per directive ‚ÄĒ no new domains):

- `body_parts.jsonl` (+2): body_041 ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–≥—Ė –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė: –Ī–į—Ā/–ļ”©–∑/“õ“Ī–Ľ–į“õ/–ľ“Ī—Ä—č–Ĺ/–į—É—č–∑/—ā—Ė–Ľ/—ā—Ė—Ā/–ľ–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ/–ł—č“õ/–ļ–Ķ—É–ī–Ķ/“õ–ĺ–Ľ/—Ā–į—É—Ā–į“õ/–Ī–Ķ–Ľ/–į—Ā“õ–į–∑–į–Ĺ/–Ī–į—É—č—Ä/–Ī“Į–Ļ—Ä–Ķ–ļ/”©–ļ–Ņ–Ķ/–∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ļ/—Ė—ą–Ķ–ļ/–į—Ź“õ/—ā—Ė–∑–Ķ/—ā–į–Ī–į–Ĺ¬Ľ + body_042 internal organs subset

- `mathematics_basic.jsonl` (+2): math_092 ¬ę–Ę–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É —ā“Į—Ä–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė: —Ā—č–∑—č“õ—ā—č“õ / –ļ–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā—ā—č“õ / —ā–Ķ“£—Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–ļ / —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É–Ľ–Ķ—Ä –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė¬Ľ + math_093 ¬ę–ď–Ķ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ —Ą–ł–≥—É—Ä–į–Ľ–į—Ä: “Į—ą–Ī“Ī—Ä—č—ą / —ā”©—Ä—ā–Ī“Ī—Ä—č—ą family / –ļ”©–Ņ–Ī“Ī—Ä—č—ą / —ą–Ķ“£–Ī–Ķ—Ä / –ī”©“£–≥–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ¬Ľ

**(3) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 7 list-aggregator compound objects (“ď–į–Ľ–į–ľ—ą–į—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / —Ė—ą–ļ—Ė –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / –ľ“Ī—Ö–ł—ā—ā–į—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É —ā“Į—Ä–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė / —Ą–ł–≥—É—Ä–į–Ľ–į—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė) ‚ÄĒ required by `world_core_multiword_coverage` invariant.

## [4.71.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Live REPL audit fixes ‚ÄĒ loanword topic recognition + relation-predicate demotion

Driven by the v4.71.0 live REPL battery: 12-query audit across the educational stripe surfaced two systemic gaps where new content was correctly stored but the dialog layer couldn't surface it. No new world_core data ‚ÄĒ pure runtime fix.

### Bugs identified in REPL audit

**Bug 1 ‚ÄĒ loanword proper-noun queries fall through topic extraction.**
- Repro: ¬ę–ź–Ĺ—ā–į—Ä–ļ—ā–ł–ī–į “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł–ļ?¬Ľ ‚Üí `unknown.clarify_no_topic` fallback (¬ę—Ā“Ī—Ä–į“õ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č–Ľ–į–Ĺ–Ī–į–ī—č¬Ľ).
- Root cause: `noun_hint = None`. Antarctic / Eurasia / methaphora / acetone / etc. are FST-unknown loanwords; no topic marker (¬ę—ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č¬Ľ, ¬ę–ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ¬Ľ) present; topic extractor exhausted all strategies.

**Bug 2 ‚ÄĒ relation-predicate eclipses the actual topics in ¬ęX –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ Y –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č –ľ–į?¬Ľ.**
- Repro: ¬ę–ľ–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ĺ—Ä–į –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ć–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—ā –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č –ľ–į?¬Ľ ‚Üí unrelated fact about —Ö–į–Ľ—č“õ —ā—č“ď—č–∑–ī—č“ď—č.
- Root cause: tokens = [–ľ–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ĺ—Ä–į, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ, —ć–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—ā, –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č, –ľ–į]. –ľ–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ĺ—Ä–į + —ć–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—ā not in FST lexicon. FST analyses –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č (predicate adjective) as a form of –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā (noun = "connection") and `first_noun_root` returns it as the topic. Retrieval then surfaces an unrelated fact about a different "byaylanys" sentence.

### Innovations

**(1) `topic_extraction::NOT_A_TOPIC` extended** with `–Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā` / `–Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č`. These are relational predicates surfaced by `X –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ Y –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č –ľ–į?` queries, never the actual topic.

**(2) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` extended with single-word loanword topics** from the v4.66.0+ educational stripe (~85 entries):
- World geography: –į–Ĺ—ā–į—Ä–ļ—ā–ł–ī–į / –Ķ—É—Ä–į–∑–ł—Ź / –į—Ą—Ä–ł–ļ–į / –į–≤—Ā—ā—Ä–į–Ľ–ł—Ź / –ľ–Ķ—Ā–ĺ–Ņ–ĺ—ā–į–ľ–ł—Ź / —Ä–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ā–į–Ĺ—Ā
- Mathematics: –į–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ī—Ä–į / –≥–Ķ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł—Ź / —ā—Ä–ł–≥–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł—Ź / –į–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č / –ļ–ĺ–ĺ—Ä–ī–ł–Ĺ–į—ā–į / —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź / —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É / —ā–Ķ“£—Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–ļ / –ī–ł—Ā–ļ—Ä–ł–ľ–ł–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—ā
- Physics: –ľ–Ķ—Ö–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į / –ļ–ł–Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į / –ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į / —Ā—ā–į—ā–ł–ļ–į / —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į / —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į / –ĺ–Ņ—ā–ł–ļ–į / –≥—Ä–į–≤–ł—ā–į—Ü–ł—Ź / —ā–Ķ–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į—ā—É—Ä–į / —ć–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≥–ł—Ź / –ł–ľ–Ņ—É–Ľ—Ć—Ā / –ł–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä—Ü–ł—Ź / –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–į—ā–ĺ—Ä
- Chemistry: –į—ā–ĺms (–į—ā–ĺ–ľ / –ľ–ĺ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—É–Ľ–į / —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ / –Ņ—Ä–ĺ—ā–ĺ–Ĺ / –Ĺ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ / –ł–ĺ–Ĺ), elements (~20 named), organic (–į–Ľ–ļ–į–Ĺ/–į–Ľ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ/–į–Ľ–ļ–ł–Ĺ + 6 hydrocarbons + 4 alcohols/carbonyls + 6 biomolecules), polymer/–ľ–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—Ä
- Literature: –ľ–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ĺ—Ä–į / —ć–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—ā / —ā–Ķ“£–Ķ—É / –≥–ł–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į / –ļ–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É / –į–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź / –į—Ā—Ā–ĺ–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—Ā / –į–Ľ–Ľ–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—Ü–ł—Ź
- Medicine: –ľ–Ķ–ī–ł—Ü–ł–Ĺ–į / –į–Ĺ–į—ā–ĺ–ľ–ł—Ź / —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź / —Ā–ł–ľ–Ņ—ā–ĺ–ľ / –ī–ł–į–≥–Ĺ–ĺ–∑ / –Ņ—Ä–ĺ—Ą–ł–Ľ–į–ļ—ā–ł–ļ–į / –ł–ľ–ľ—É–Ĺ–ł—ā–Ķ—ā / –≤–į–ļ—Ü–ł–Ĺ–į / –į–Ĺ—ā–ł–Ī–ł–ĺ—ā–ł–ļ / –≤–ł—Ä—É—Ā / –Ī–į–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź

**(3) Word-boundary match for single-token entries** ‚ÄĒ `multiword_entity_hint` first-pass now checks `tokens.iter().any(|t| t == entity)` for single-word entries, preserving substring match for multi-word. Prevents new short single-word entries (e.g. ¬ę—ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ä¬Ľ) from shadowing the third-pass genitive-handling logic for compounds (e.g. ¬ę—ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ä –∂–ĺ–Ľ¬Ľ from input ¬ę—ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ä–ī—Ė“£ –∂–ĺ–Ľ—č¬Ľ).

## [4.71.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal ‚ÄĒ Medicine foundations (health + disease + symptom + diagnosis + treatment + prevention + immunity + first aid + medicine substance + vaccine/antibiotic)

Per memory directive ‚ÄĒ pre-flight grep across all `data/world_core/*.jsonl` confirmed sparse medicine coverage: only –į–Ĺ–į—ā–ĺ–ľ–ł—Ź (in body_parts + biology_school) / –Ī–į–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź + –≤–ł—Ä—É—Ā (biology_school) / –ī”ô—Ä—Ė–≥–Ķ—Ä (professions, society, role_bridges). | –ú–Ķ–ī–ł—Ü–ł–Ĺ–į subject + 9 medical concepts + medicine-substance hub + 2 sub-classes | not yet | + add (new domain) |

**9 medical concepts, all IsA –ľ–Ķ–ī–ł—Ü–ł–Ĺ–į “Ī“ď—č–ľ—č (9):**
- med_002: –ī–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–į—É–Ľ—č“õ ‚ÄĒ WHO definition (physical + mental + social wellbeing)
- med_003: –į—É—Ä—É ‚ÄĒ disease taxonomy (–∂“Ī“õ–Ņ–į–Ľ—č / –∂“Ī“õ–Ņ–į–Ļ—ā—č–Ĺ)
- med_004: —Ā–ł–ľ–Ņ—ā–ĺ–ľ ‚ÄĒ disease external sign (–∂”©—ā–Ķ–Ľ, –Ī–į—Ā –į—É—č—Ä—É—č, —ā–Ķ–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į—ā—É—Ä–į)
- med_005: –ī–ł–į–≥–Ĺ–ĺ–∑ ‚ÄĒ diagnosis from symptoms + test results

- med_010: –ī”ô—Ä—Ė ‚ÄĒ medicine substance hub (tablet, ampoule, syrup, ointment)

**Medicine subclasses, all IsA –ī”ô—Ä—Ė (2):**

**(3) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 4 new compounds (–ľ–Ķ–ī–ł—Ü–ł–Ĺ–į “Ī“ď—č–ľ—č, –į–Ľ“ď–į—ą“õ—č –ļ”©–ľ–Ķ–ļ, –∂“Ī“õ–Ņ–į–Ľ—č –į—É—Ä—É, –∂“Ī“õ–Ņ–į–Ļ—ā—č–Ĺ –į—É—Ä—É).

## [4.69.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal ‚ÄĒ World history foundations (4 historical eras + 4 ancient civilizations + Renaissance + Industrial Revolution)

Per memory directive `feedback_no_duplicate_domains.md` ‚ÄĒ pre-flight grep of all `data/world_core/*.jsonl` confirmed `history_kazakhstan.jsonl` is strictly Kazakhstan-scoped (sak tribes, Genghis Khan, Russian protectorate, Soviet/independence eras). Zero coverage of world-historical periodization or non-Kazakh civilizations. Genuinely new canonical scope ‚ÄĒ created new `world_history.jsonl` domain rather than diluting `history_kazakhstan.jsonl`.

### Pre-flight audit

| Concept | Existing canonical home | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Kazakhstan-specific history (“õ–ĺ–Ľ–į –ī”ô—É—Ė—Ä—Ė / —Ā–į“õ—ā–į—Ä / “ď“Ī–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä / –®—č“£“ď—č—Ā —Ö–į–Ĺ / –†–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ļ –Ī–ĺ–ī–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č / 1916 –ļ”©—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ľ—Ė—Ā / –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä / –Ė—Ė–Ī–Ķ–ļ –∂–ĺ–Ľ—č) | `history_kazakhstan.jsonl` (124 entries) | ‚úď skip ‚ÄĒ covered |
| World periodization (–Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė / –ĺ—Ä—ā–į / –∂–į“£–į / “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė –∑–į–ľ–į–Ĺ) | not yet ‚ÄĒ new scope | + add (new domain) |
| Ancient civilizations (–ē–≥–ł–Ņ–Ķ—ā / –ď—Ä–Ķ—Ü–ł—Ź / –†–ł–ľ / –ú–Ķ—Ā–ĺ–Ņ–ĺ—ā–į–ľ–ł—Ź) | not yet | + add |
| Renaissance / Industrial Revolution as historical periods | not yet | + add |

### Innovations

**(1) New domain `data/world_core/world_history.jsonl`** with 12 entries (`wh_001‚Ä¶012`):

**Subject + concept hubs (2):**
- wh_001: –ī“Į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ–∂“Į–∑—Ė —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö—č IsA –ĺ“õ—É –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė ‚ÄĒ bridges to v4.65.5 ¬ę–ĺ“õ—É –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė¬Ľ master hub
- wh_002: —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł –ī”ô—É—Ė—Ä IsA —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö “Ī“ď—č–ľ—č ‚ÄĒ periodization concept

**Historical eras IsA —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł –ī”ô—É—Ė—Ä (4):**
- wh_003: –Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė –ī”ô—É—Ė—Ä ‚ÄĒ through 476 AD (Western Roman Empire fall); first civilizations, writing
- wh_004: –ĺ—Ä—ā–į “ď–į—Ā—č—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä ‚ÄĒ 476 AD to 1453 (Constantinople fall); feudalism, knights
- wh_005: –∂–į“£–į –∑–į–ľ–į–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ 1453 (Age of Discovery) to 1914 (WWI start)
- wh_006: “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė –∑–į–ľ–į–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ 1914 to present; world wars, S&T revolution, globalization

**Ancient civilizations IsA –Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė ”©—Ä–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ—ā (4):**
- wh_007: –Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė –Ķ–≥–ł–Ņ–Ķ—ā ‚ÄĒ Nile valley, 3rd millennium BC, pyramids, pharaohs, hieroglyphs
- wh_008: –Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė –≥—Ä–Ķ—Ü–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ VIII‚ÄďI centuries BC, Athenian democracy, philosophy, Olympics
- wh_009: —Ä–ł–ľ –ł–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č ‚ÄĒ 27 BC to 476 AD, Roman law, Latin, engineering (aqueducts/roads)
- wh_010: –ľ–Ķ—Ā–ĺ–Ņ–ĺ—ā–į–ľ–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ 4th millennium BC, Tigris/Euphrates, Sumerians/Akkadians/Babylonians, cuneiform, Hammurabi

**Transformative epochs IsA —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£ (2):**
- wh_011: —Ä–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ā–į–Ĺ—Ā ‚ÄĒ XIV‚ÄďXVI centuries, Italy, da Vinci, Michelangelo, humanism
- wh_012: ”©–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ —Ä–Ķ–≤–ĺ–Ľ—é—Ü–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ XVIII century, England, steam engine, factories, railways

**(2) Bridge into educational portal** ‚ÄĒ wh_001 connects –ī“Į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ–∂“Į–∑—Ė —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö—č to the v4.65.5 ¬ę–ĺ“õ—É –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė¬Ľ master hub, completing the subject-taxonomy graph for history alongside “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö—č (already in adam_self.jsonl as part of –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė).

**(3) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 13 new compounds (–ī“Į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ–∂“Į–∑—Ė —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö—č, —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł –ī”ô—É—Ė—Ä/–ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£/“Ī“ď—č–ľ, 4 era names, 4 civilization names + –Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė ”©—Ä–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ—ā, ”©–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ —Ä–Ķ–≤–ĺ–Ľ—é—Ü–ł—Ź).

### Acceptance

| Gate | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| world_core entries | 2428 | **2440** (+12) |
| world_core facts | 2609 | **2621** (+12) |
| world_core domains | 51 | **52** (+1 ‚ÄĒ new `world_history`) |
| Derived facts | 29609 | **29609** (+0 ‚ÄĒ closed sub-taxonomy) |
| Workspace tests | 976 | **976** unchanged |

The 0 derived count reflects an **isolated sub-taxonomy** ‚ÄĒ 4 civilizations cluster only with each other, 4 eras cluster only among themselves, no existing entries overlap with the new objects (–Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė ”©—Ä–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ—ā / —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł –ī”ô—É—Ė—Ä / —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£). R1 +5 (era-to-historical-period chains), R5 ‚ąí5 (cluster boundary redistribution as new in-domain shared-target groups absorbed under R11). Future Day-3 deepening (specific historical figures, events, cross-references with Kazakhstan history) will multiply against these new hubs.

### Cadence

`.5` patch ‚ÄĒ new domain (justified scope expansion, not duplication per directive).

Stripe (12) ‚ÄĒ Kazakh educational portal.

## [4.69.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal ‚ÄĒ Chemistry: inorganic chemistry deepening (alkaline-earth metals + Ca/Mg + halogens F/Br/I + Ar + S/P + acidic/basic oxides + Mendeleev's table)

Alkaline-earth metal group + Ca/Mg, 3 missing halogens (F/Br/I), Ar, sulfur + phosphorus elements, acidic/basic oxide subclasses, and Mendeleev's table were missing.

## [4.68.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal ‚ÄĒ Chemistry: organic chemistry deepening (homologous series + isomerism + propane/butane/acetylene + methanol/glycerol + ketone/acetone + nucleotide + polysaccharide)

Per memory directive `feedback_no_duplicate_domains.md` ‚ÄĒ pre-flight grep of `chemistry_school.jsonl` confirmed dense organic chemistry coverage: –ĺ—Ä–≥–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź (chem_003) / –ļ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä—ā–Ķ–ļ (chem_027) / “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ + 3 acid types (chem_058..061) / —ā“Ī–∑ (chem_065) / –ļ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä—Ā—É—ā–Ķ–ļ hub (chem_079) / –į–Ľ–ļ–į–Ĺ + –į–Ľ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ + –į–Ľ–ļ–ł–Ĺ classes (chem_080..082) / –ľ–Ķ—ā–į–Ĺ + —ć—ā–į–Ĺ + —ć—ā–ł–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ + –Ī–Ķ–Ĺ–∑–ĺ–Ľ (chem_083..086) / —Ā–Ņ–ł—Ä—ā + —ć—ā–į–Ĺ–ĺ–Ľ (chem_087, 088) / –į–Ľ—Ć–ī–Ķ–≥–ł–ī + 2 carboxylic acid entries (chem_089..091) / —ć—Ą–ł—Ä (chem_092) / –ļ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä—Ā—É + –≥–Ľ—é–ļ–ĺ–∑–į + —Ā–į—Ö–į—Ä–ĺ–∑–į + –ļ—Ä–į—Ö–ľ–į–Ľ (chem_093..096) / –ľ–į–Ļ + –į“õ—É—č–∑ + –į–ľ–ł–Ĺ “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ—č + —Ą–Ķ—Ä–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā (chem_097..100) / –Ņ–ĺ–Ľ–ł–ľ–Ķ—Ä + –ľ–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—Ä + –Ņ–Ľ–į—Ā—ā–ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į (chem_101..103) / –Ē–Ě–ö + –†–Ě–ö (chem_104, 105). Concept-level taxonomy (homologous series, homolog, isomerism), specific simple alkanes (propane, butane), specific alkyne (acetylene), simplest alcohol (methanol) + polyhydric (glycerol), ketone class + acetone, nucleotide building block, and polysaccharide general class were missing.

## [4.68.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal ‚ÄĒ Physics: electricity + magnetism deepening (Coulomb + series/parallel + electric work/power + Joule-Lenz + capacitor + magnetic flux + DC/AC + EM-wave bridge)

Per memory directive `feedback_no_duplicate_domains.md` ‚ÄĒ pre-flight grep of `physics_school.jsonl` confirmed dense E&M coverage (—ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į / —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä –∑–į—Ä—Ź–ī—č / 2 –∑–į—Ä—Ź–ī —ā“Į—Ä—Ė / —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä ”©—Ä—Ė—Ā—Ė / —ā–ĺ“ď—č / –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ—É / –ļ–Ķ–ī–Ķ—Ä–≥—Ė / –ě–ľ –∑–į“£—č / —ā—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–ļ / ”©—ā–ļ—Ė–∑–≥—Ė—ą / –ī–ł—ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ł–ļ / –ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā / –ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā ”©—Ä—Ė—Ā—Ė / —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā / —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā—ā—Ė–ļ –ł–Ĺ–ī—É–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź / –∂–ł—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ / –∂–į—Ä—č“õ / 2 –Ľ–ł–Ĺ–∑–į / —É–Ľ—Ć—ā—Ä–į–ļ“Į–Ľ–≥—Ė–Ĺ + –ł–Ĺ—Ą—Ä–į“õ—č–∑—č–Ľ + –≥–į–ľ–ľ–į —Ā”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė). Coulomb's law, series/parallel circuit topology, electric work + power formulas, Joule-Lenz law, capacitor + capacitance, magnetic flux, DC/AC distinction, and the electromagnetic-wave taxonomy bridge were missing. Extends canonical `physics_school.jsonl`.

## [4.67.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal ‚ÄĒ Physics: thermodynamics deepening (heat amount Q + ideal gas + Boyle/Charles/Gay-Lussac/Mendeleev-Clapeyron + absolute zero + Pascal + hydrostatic pressure + state-of-matter bridge)

Per memory directive `feedback_no_duplicate_domains.md` ‚ÄĒ pre-flight grep of `physics_school.jsonl` confirmed dense existing thermodynamic coverage (—ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į / —ā–Ķ–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į—ā—É—Ä–į / –∂—č–Ľ—É / —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä / –¶–Ķ–Ľ—Ć—Ā–ł–Ļ + –ö–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–≤–ł–Ĺ —ą–ļ–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / 4 states of matter / 6 phase transitions / –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ą—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė –∂—č–Ľ—É —Ā—č–Ļ—č–ľ–ī—č–Ľ—č“õ / –∂—č–Ľ—É ”©—ā–ļ—Ė–∑–≥—Ė—ą—ā—Ė–ļ / “õ—č—Ā—č–ľ / –ź—Ä—Ö–ł–ľ–Ķ–ī –∑–į“£—č). Heat amount formula, thermal equilibrium, ideal gas model, all 4 gas laws, Mendeleev-Clapeyron equation, absolute zero, hydrostatic pressure, Pascal's law, and 2 cascade bridges were missing. Atmospheric pressure already lives in `weather_phenomena.jsonl` (w_026) so was excluded; replaced with –≥–ł–ī—Ä–ĺ—Ā—ā–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ “õ—č—Ā—č–ľ per single-canonical-home discipline.

- phys_126: —ā–Ķ–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į—ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č“õ —ą–ļ–į–Ľ–į IsA —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ “Ī“ď—č–ľ ‚ÄĒ apex bridge over –¶–Ķ–Ľ—Ć—Ā–ł–Ļ + –ö–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–≤–ł–Ĺ —ą–ļ–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č

**(2) Curriculum-grade formulas embedded** in 9 of 12 entries (calorimetry equation, all 3 gas-law constraints, Mendeleev-Clapeyron, hydrostatic pressure).

**(3) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 14 new compounds.

### Acceptance

| Gate | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| world_core entries | 2380 | **2392** (+12) |
| world_core facts | 2561 | **2573** (+12) |
| world_core domains | 51 | **51** unchanged |
| Derived facts | 29517 | **29513** (‚ąí4 ‚ÄĒ R5 cluster redistribution) |
| Workspace tests | 976 | **976** unchanged |

The ‚ąí4 derived count is a **rule-semantic redistribution**, not a regression: R1_is_a_transitivity went 2696 ‚Üí 2851 (+155) and R5_shared_is_a_target went 1827 ‚Üí 1891 (+64) ‚ÄĒ the new bridges drive transitivity correctly. The drop comes from R11_in_domain_shared_target reorganising in-domain cluster boundaries when new typed parents appear (a single subject moving into a new shared-target group splits one big cluster into two smaller ones ‚ÄĒ each cluster's pair count is `n choose 2`, which is non-monotonic in cluster count). Net architectural value is graph closure of the gas-law family + state-of-matter taxonomy under the new physical-concept apex.

### Cadence

`.5` patch ‚ÄĒ same-domain canonical extension; physics_school now at 126 entries.

Stripe (12) ‚ÄĒ Kazakh educational portal.

## [4.67.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal ‚ÄĒ Physics: mechanics deepening (kinematics + dynamics + statics + uniformly accelerated motion + Hooke + g + bridges)

Per memory directive `feedback_no_duplicate_domains.md` ‚ÄĒ pre-flight grep of `physics_school.jsonl` confirmed dense existing coverage of –ľ–Ķ—Ö–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į / –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į / —ā—č“ď—č–∑–ī—č“õ / “õ–ĺ–∑“ď–į–Ľ—č—Ā / –∂—č–Ľ–ī–į–ľ–ī—č“õ / “Į–ī–Ķ—É / –ļ“Į—ą / 3 Newton laws / –ł–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä—Ü–ł—Ź / –ł–ľ–Ņ—É–Ľ—Ć—Ā / 4 force types / –Ķ—Ä–ļ—Ė–Ĺ —ā“Į—Ā—É / –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā / —ć–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≥–ł—Ź / 2 energy types / energy conservation / “õ—É–į—ā. Sub-discipline taxonomy (–ļ–ł–Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į / –ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į / —Ā—ā–į—ā–ł–ļ–į), formula-grade kinematic shamas (–∂–ĺ–Ľ, —É–į“õ—č—ā, –ĺ—Ä—ā–į—ą–į –∂—č–Ľ–ī–į–ľ–ī—č“õ), uniformly-accelerated motion, Hooke's law, free-fall constant g, normal force N, and high-leverage IsA bridges were missing. Extends canonical `physics_school.jsonl`.

| —ā–Ķ“£—Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–ļ / —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É–Ľ–Ķ—Ä –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė | not yet | + add |

- math_077: —ā—É—Ä–į –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–Ņ–ĺ—Ä—Ü–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–į–Ľ–ī—č“õ IsA —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź (y = kx, line through origin)
- math_078: –ī–ł—Ā–ļ—Ä–ł–ľ–ł–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—ā ‚ÄĒ D = b¬≤ ‚ąí 4ac with 3-case root analysis
- math_079: –í–ł–Ķ—ā–į —ā–Ķ–ĺ—Ä–Ķ–ľ–į—Ā—č ‚ÄĒ x‚āĀ + x‚āā = ‚ąíp, x‚āĀ ¬∑ x‚āā = q for reduced quadratic

**(2) Curriculum-grade formulas embedded** ‚ÄĒ every entry includes either the canonical formula (`y = kx`, `D = b¬≤ ‚ąí 4ac`, `x‚āĀ + x‚āā = ‚ąíp`) or a worked example (3; 5 ‚Üí –į–Ī—Ā—Ü–ł—Ā—Ā–į = 3, –ĺ—Ä–ī–ł–Ĺ–į—ā–į = 5).

## [4.65.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 2 #10 (FINAL) ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ě“õ—É –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė¬Ľ master hub: subject taxonomy capstone

- bio_045: –į—É–į IsA –∂–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑ —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–į—ā (air ‚ÄĒ atmosphere, breathing)
- bio_046: —ā–ĺ–Ņ—č—Ä–į“õ IsA –∂–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑ —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–į—ā (soil ‚ÄĒ fertility layer)

- bio_049: —Ā—É –ĺ—Ä—ā–į—Ā—č IsA —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ĺ—Ä—ā–į—Ā—č (water habitat ‚ÄĒ fish, aquatic life)

- bio_053: “õ—č—Ā—ā—č“£ –Ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė (signs of winter ‚ÄĒ snow falls, water freezes, hibernation)

- `<season signs> ‚Üí –ļ”©–ļ—ā–Ķ–ľ / “õ—č—Ā` (existing time.jsonl hubs)

## [4.64.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 2 #7 ‚ÄĒ Mathematics extension: fractions / decimals / number properties / geometry ‚ÄĒ 27√ó cascade record

**Day 2 release 7/10.** Extends `mathematics_basic.jsonl` (existing 55 entries since 2026-04-29) with 12 concrete curriculum-grade entries: specific fractions, decimal/simple-fraction distinction, number properties (even/odd/prime/positive/negative), and geometry basics (perimeter/area). Per memory directive `feedback_no_duplicate_domains.md` ‚ÄĒ extends existing canonical file rather than create new.

**ūüéĮ 27√ó per-fact derivation cascade** ‚ÄĒ highest ratio of the entire educational program; new math entries plug into existing deeply-connected hubs (—Ā–į–Ĺ, –Ī”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ļ, –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ą–į–ľ–į).

### Innovations

**(1) 12 new math entries** in `data/world_core/mathematics_basic.jsonl` (`math_056‚Ä¶math_067`):

| Category | Entries |
|---|---|
| **Concrete fractions** | –∂–į—Ä—ā—č—Ā—č (1/2 = 50%), “Į—ą—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī—Ė—Ä (1/3 ‚Čą 33%), —ā”©—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī—Ė—Ä (1/4 = 25%) |
| **Fraction types** | –ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ī”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ļ (decimal), –∂–į–Ļ –Ī”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ļ (simple –į–Ľ—č–ľ/–Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–ľ) |
| **Number properties** | –∂“Ī–Ņ —Ā–į–Ĺ (even, √∑2), —ā–į“õ —Ā–į–Ĺ (odd), –∂–į–Ļ —Ā–į–Ĺ (prime), –ĺ“£ —Ā–į–Ĺ (positive), —ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė—Ā —Ā–į–Ĺ (negative) |
| **Geometry shamasy** | –Ņ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä (P, perimeter formulas), –į—É–ī–į–Ĺ (S, area formulas) |

Each entry: definition + decimal/percent equivalents (where applicable) + Kazakh examples (–į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ĺ—č“£ –∂–į—Ä—ā—č—Ā—č / —Ā–į“ď–į—ā—ā—č“£ —ā”©—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī—Ė—Ä—Ė = 15 –ľ–ł–Ĺ—É—ā / —ā.–Ī.) + formulas (P = 4√óa; S = a¬≤; etc.).

**(2) Cross-fraction-form mapping** documented per entry: –∂–į—Ä—ā—č—Ā—č = 1/2 = 0,5 = 50%; —ā”©—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī—Ė—Ä = 1/4 = 0,25 = 25%; etc. Elementary curriculum pattern for fraction-decimal-percent fluency.

**(3) Negative-number contextualization** ‚ÄĒ —ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė—Ā —Ā–į–Ĺ entry frames real-world usage: —ā–Ķ–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į—ā—É—Ä–į–Ĺ—č (–ľ–ł–Ĺ—É—Ā-—ā–Ķ–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į—ā—É—Ä–į), “õ–į—Ä—č–∑–ī—č (debt), —ā–Ķ“£—Ė–∑ –ī–Ķ“£–≥–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā”©–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –Ī–ł—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė (below-sea-level altitude). Matches grade-3 Kazakh ¬ę–Ē“Į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ—ā–į–Ĺ—É¬Ľ curriculum framing.

**(4) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 10 new compounds: “Į—ą—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī—Ė—Ä, —ā”©—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī—Ė—Ä, –ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ī”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ļ, –∂–į–Ļ –Ī”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ļ, –∂“Ī–Ņ —Ā–į–Ĺ, —ā–į“õ —Ā–į–Ĺ, –∂–į–Ļ —Ā–į–Ĺ, –ĺ“£ —Ā–į–Ĺ, —ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė—Ā —Ā–į–Ĺ, –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ą–į–ľ–į.

## [4.63.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Domain consolidation: merge duplicate-risk world_core files + memory directive

**Driven by user audit (2026-05-06).** Pre-existing canonical world_core files (since 2026-04-24) already covered Kazakh grammar, emotions, and Abai/Shakarim literature. Recent v4.56‚Äďv4.61 releases inadvertently created parallel domains with overlapping subjects, causing silent fact dedup in `extract_facts` and bloating the domain count.

v4.63.5 consolidates the duplicates and saves a project memory directive to prevent future occurrences.

### Innovations

**(1) Merged duplicate-risk files into canonical existing domains:**

| Merged file (deleted) | Into canonical (extended) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `kazakh_grammar.jsonl` (45 entries, v4.61.0) | `language_features.jsonl` (33 ‚Üí 78 entries) | Both held PoS / —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ / —ą–į“õ taxonomy facts (`–∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č`, `—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ IsA –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź`, etc.); existing file pre-dated by 12 days |
| `preschool_emotions.jsonl` (14 entries, v4.60.5) | `emotions.jsonl` (30 ‚Üí 44 entries) | Both held `“õ—É–į–Ĺ—č—ą / –ľ–į—Ö–į–Ī–Ī–į—ā / –į—ą—É` emotion facts; existing file pre-dated by 12 days |

For each merged entry: `domain` field rewritten from old domain name to canonical domain name; entries appended verbatim to canonical file.

**(2) Cancelled planned parallel files:**
- `abai_canon.jsonl` (planned for v4.63.5) ‚ÄĒ cancelled. –ź–Ī–į–Ļ material already in `kz_literature.jsonl` (lit_001, lit_002, lit_034 ¬ę“ö–į—Ä–į —Ā”©–∑–ī–Ķ—Ä¬Ľ, lit_056, lit_078) + `notable_kazakhstanis.jsonl` (notable_031, notable_032).
- `shakarim_canon.jsonl` (planned for v4.64.0) ‚ÄĒ cancelled. –®”ô–ļ”ô—Ä—Ė–ľ already in same files.

Future Abai/Shakarim deepening will extend `kz_literature.jsonl` directly.

**(3) New project memory directive** `feedback_no_duplicate_domains.md`:
- Always audit existing world_core/eval files before creating new domain JSONL.
- Run `ls + grep` first; prefer extending existing canonical file over creating parallel domain.
- Ship consolidation release when post-hoc duplication is found.
- No proliferation. Structure beats sprawl.

**(4) Pipeline regeneration confirms no fact loss** ‚ÄĒ extract_facts already deduplicated by (subject, predicate, object) tuple, so the merge has no effect on fact / derived counts. The cleanup is structural (-2 domain files) not semantic.

### Acceptance

| Gate | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| world_core entries | 2295 | **2295** unchanged |
| world_core facts | 2535 | **2535** unchanged |
| world_core domains | 53 | **51** (-2 consolidation) |
| Derived facts | 28577 | **28577** unchanged |
| Workspace tests | 976 | **976** unchanged |
| `cargo clippy -D warnings` | green | green |
| `verify_release_version.sh 4.63.5` | n/a | green |
| `check_metrics_currency.sh` | green | green |

### Cumulative Day 2

5 of 10 releases shipped (halfway): 4 educational releases (v4.61.0 grammar / v4.61.5 cases / v4.62.0 possessive / v4.62.5 tenses+moods / v4.63.0 proverbs) + 1 consolidation (v4.63.5).

### Cadence

`.5` patch ‚ÄĒ pure structural hygiene. No new content; no API surface changes; no behavior changes. Domain count -2 reflects real-world canonical structure.

Stripe (12) ‚ÄĒ Kazakh educational portal.

| 048 | ¬ę**–Ď—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ, —ā—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–ľ–į—Ā**¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ without unity, no life | –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ causes —ā—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ |

These are foundational proverbs taught in 3-4 grade Kazakh-language and ¬ę–Ē“Į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ—ā–į–Ĺ—É¬Ľ (life skills) curriculum.

- `prov_bridge_002`: ¬ę**—Ö–į–Ľ—č“õ –ī–į–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č“ď—č IsA “Ī“ď—č–ľ**¬Ľ (folk wisdom is concept)

## [4.62.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 2 #4 ‚ÄĒ Kazakh tenses (—ą–į“õ) + moods (—Ä–į–Ļ) + verb conjugation paradigm

**Day 2 release 4/10.** Full Kazakh tense system (3 —ą–į“õ √ó subtypes) + 4 mood (—Ä–į–Ļ) classes + canonical ¬ę–ĺ“õ—É¬Ľ verb conjugation paradigm. Closes the major verb-grammar categories.

### Innovations

**(1) 3 tense entries** in `kazakh_grammar.jsonl` (`gram_tense_001‚Ä¶gram_tense_003`), each `IsA —ą–į“õ`:

| # | Tense | Sub-types |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **”©—ā–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ —ą–į“õ** (past) | –∂–Ķ–ī–Ķ–Ľ (-–ī—č/-–ī—Ė), –Ī“Ī—Ä—č–Ĺ“ď—č (-“ď–į–Ĺ/-–≥–Ķ–Ĺ), –į—É—č—Ā–Ņ–į–Ľ—č (-–į—ā—č–Ĺ/-–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ) |
| 2 | **–ĺ—Ā—č —ą–į“õ** (present) | –Ĺ–į“õ –ĺ—Ā—č (auxiliary verbs: –∂–į—ā—č—Ä / –ĺ—ā—č—Ä / —ā“Ī—Ä), –∂–į–Ľ–Ņ—č (-–į/-–Ķ/-–Ļ + –∂—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ) |
| 3 | **–ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä —ą–į“õ** (future) | –į—É—č—Ā–Ņ–į–Ľ—č (-–į/-–Ķ/-–Ļ + –∂—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ), –ľ–į“õ—Ā–į—ā—ā—č (-–ľ–į“õ/-–ľ–Ķ–ļ), –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–∂–į–Ľ–ī—č (-–į—Ä/-–Ķ—Ä/-—Ä) |

**(2) 4 mood entries** (`gram_mood_001‚Ä¶gram_mood_004`), each `IsA —Ä–į–Ļ`:

| # | Mood | Suffixes / Pattern |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **—Ö–į–Ī–į—Ä–Ľ—č —Ä–į–Ļ** (indicative) | works with all tenses; declarative |
| 2 | **–Ī“Ī–Ļ—Ä—č“õ —Ä–į–Ļ** (imperative) | 2sg-anaiy: zero (–ĺ“õ—č); 2sg-sypaiy: -“£—č–∑; 2pl: -“£–ī–į—Ä/-“£—č–∑–ī–į—Ä; 3sg: -—Ā—č–Ĺ |
| 3 | **—ą–į—Ä—ā—ā—č —Ä–į–Ļ** (conditional) | -—Ā–į/-—Ā–Ķ + –∂—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ (–ĺ“õ—č—Ā–į–ľ, –ĺ“õ—č—Ā–į“£, –ĺ“õ—č—Ā–į) |
| 4 | **“õ–į–Ľ–į—É —Ä–į–Ļ** (optative) | -–į–Ļ—č–Ĺ/-–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ (1sg); -–į–Ļ—č“õ/-–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–ļ (1pl); -“ď–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑ (sypaiy) |

**(3) Canonical ¬ę–ĺ“õ—É¬Ľ verb conjugation** (`gram_conj_001`) ‚ÄĒ full —Ö–į–Ī–į—Ä–Ľ—č —Ä–į–Ļ paradigm across past + present/future, all 7 person/number forms: –ĺ“õ—č–ī—č–ľ / –ĺ“õ—č–ī—č“£ / –ĺ“õ—č–ī—č“£—č–∑ / –ĺ“õ—č–ī—č / –ĺ“õ—č–ī—č“õ / –ĺ“õ—č–ī—č“£–ī–į—Ä / –ĺ“õ—č–ī—č“£—č–∑–ī–į—Ä (past); –ĺ“õ–ł–ľ—č–Ĺ / –ĺ“õ–ł—Ā—č“£ / –ĺ“õ–ł—Ā—č–∑ / –ĺ“õ–ł–ī—č / –ĺ“õ–ł–ľ—č–∑ / –ĺ“õ–ł—Ā—č“£–ī–į—Ä / –ĺ“õ–ł—Ā—č–∑–ī–į—Ä (present-future).

**(4) New ¬ę–∂–į“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č¬Ľ entry** (`gram_jak_001`) ‚ÄĒ formalizes the person category (1sg/2sg/3sg/1pl/2pl) as a separate grammatical category, distinct from but related to —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č (politeness register).

**(5) 3 classification bridges**:
- `gram_tense_bridge_001`: ¬ę**—ą–į“õ IsA –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź**¬Ľ
- `gram_mood_bridge_001`: ¬ę**—Ä–į–Ļ IsA –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź**¬Ľ
- `gram_conj_bridge_001`: ¬ę**–∂—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č IsA –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É**¬Ľ

Resulting cascade: `<3 tenses + 4 moods> ‚Üí —ą–į“õ/—Ä–į–Ļ ‚Üí –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ`. Plus parallel `–∂—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č ‚Üí –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É` –ī–Ľ—Ź conjugation suffixes.

**(6) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 11 new compounds: 3 tense names + 4 mood names + –ĺ“õ—É –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –Ņ–į—Ä–į–ī–ł–≥–ľ–į—Ā—č + –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –Ņ–į—Ä–į–ī–ł–≥–ľ–į—Ā—č + –∂–į“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č + –∂—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č.
