## [4.62.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 2 #3 ‚ÄĒ Kazakh possessive system (—ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ) ‚ÄĒ 7 person/number forms + politeness register

**Day 2 release 3/10.** Full Kazakh possessive system with 7 person/number forms, vowel-harmony suffix variants, and the canonical ¬ę–ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ¬Ľ possessive declension. Documents the —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č–Ľ—č“õ (politeness register) –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ a hallmark feature of Kazakh grammar.

### Innovations

**(1) 7 possessive entries** in `kazakh_grammar.jsonl` (`gram_poss_001‚Ä¶gram_poss_007`), each `IsA —ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č`:

| # | Form | Suffixes | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **P1Sg** (–Ī—Ė—Ä—Ė–Ĺ—ą—Ė –∂–į“õ –∂–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ą–Ķ) | -—č–ľ / -—Ė–ľ / -–ľ | –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č–ľ |
| 2 | **P2Sg –į–Ĺ–į–Ļ—č** (informal) | -—č“£ / -—Ė“£ / -“£ | —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č“£ |
| 3 | **P2Sg —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č** (formal) | -—č“£—č–∑ / -—Ė“£—Ė–∑ / -“£—č–∑ / -“£—Ė–∑ | –°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č“£—č–∑ |
| 4 | **P3** (–∂–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ą–Ķ + –ļ”©–Ņ—ą–Ķ) | -—č / -—Ė / -—Ā—č / -—Ā—Ė | –ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ / –ĺ–Ľ–į—Ä–ī—č“£ –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č |
| 5 | **P1Pl** (–Ī—Ė—Ä—Ė–Ĺ—ą—Ė –∂–į“õ –ļ”©–Ņ—ą–Ķ) | -—č–ľ—č–∑ / -—Ė–ľ—Ė–∑ / -–ľ—č–∑ / -–ľ—Ė–∑ | –Ī—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č–ľ—č–∑ |
| 6 | **P2Pl –į–Ĺ–į–Ļ—č** (informal) | -–Ľ–į—Ä + P2Sg | —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė“£ –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ—ā–į—Ä—č“£ |
| 7 | **P2Pl —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č** (formal) | -—č“£—č–∑–ī–į—Ä / -—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ—Ä | –°—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė“£ –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ—ā–į—Ä—č“£—č–∑ |

Each entry: full suffix paradigm with vowel-harmony rules + question patterns + Kazakh usage examples + politeness-register guidance (when to use –į–Ĺ–į–Ļ—č vs —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č).

**(2) Canonical ¬ę–ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ¬Ľ possessive declension** (`gram_poss_008`) ‚ÄĒ full 7-form paradigm in one entry: –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č–ľ / –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č“£ / –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č“£—č–∑ / –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č / –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ī—č–ľ—č–∑ / –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ—ā–į—Ä—č“£ / –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ—ā–į—Ä—č“£—č–∑. Documents the consonant-voicing alternation (“õ ‚Üí –Ī) under suffix attachment.

**(3) 3 classification bridges**:
- `gram_poss_bridge_001`: ¬ę**—ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č IsA –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É**¬Ľ
- `gram_poss_bridge_002`: ¬ę**—ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ IsA –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź**¬Ľ
- `gram_poss_bridge_003`: ¬ę**—Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č IsA –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź**¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ politeness register as grammatical category

Resulting cascade chain: `<7 possessive forms> ‚Üí —ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č ‚Üí –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É` + parallel `—ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ / —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č ‚Üí –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ`.

**(4) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 11 new compound entities: —ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č, —ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –Ņ–į—Ä–į–ī–ł–≥–ľ–į—Ā—č, —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č, all 7 possessive form names, –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ —ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –Ņ–į—Ä–į–ī–ł–≥–ľ–į—Ā—č.

**(5) Curriculum-grade politeness-register documentation** ‚ÄĒ entry for —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č explicitly describes when to use –į–Ĺ–į–Ļ—č (close family, children, peers) vs —Ā—č–Ņ–į–Ļ—č (elders, strangers, professional contexts). This is a hallmark feature of Kazakh grammar that elementary schools teach as part of ¬ę—ā—Ė–Ľ ”ô–ī–Ķ–Ī—Ė¬Ľ (language etiquette).

## [4.61.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ ūüéĮ Educational portal Day 2 #2 ‚ÄĒ Kazakh 7 cases (—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ) + 2500-facts milestone

**Day 2 release 2/10.** Full Kazakh 7-case (—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ) system with detailed suffix paradigms, vowel-harmony rules, and the canonical ¬ę–Ī–į–Ľ–į¬Ľ declension example. **ūüéĮ 2500-facts milestone reached.**

### Innovations

**(1) 7 case entries** in `kazakh_grammar.jsonl` (`gram_case_001‚Ä¶gram_case_007`), each `IsA —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ`:

| # | Case | Suffixes | Question |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **–į—ā–į—É —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ** (Nominative) | ‚ÄĒ (zero) | –ļ—Ė–ľ? –Ĺ–Ķ? |
| 2 | **—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ** (Genitive) | -–Ĺ—č“£/-–Ĺ—Ė“£ / -–ī—č“£/-–ī—Ė“£ / -—ā—č“£/-—ā—Ė“£ | –ļ—Ė–ľ–Ĺ—Ė“£? –Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£? |
| 3 | **–Ī–į—Ä—č—Ā —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ** (Dative) | -“ď–į/-–≥–Ķ / -“õ–į/-–ļ–Ķ / -–Ĺ–į/-–Ĺ–Ķ | –ļ—Ė–ľ–≥–Ķ? –Ĺ–Ķ–≥–Ķ? “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į? |
| 4 | **—ā–į–Ī—č—Ā —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ** (Accusative) | -–ī—č/-–ī—Ė / -–Ĺ—č/-–Ĺ—Ė / -—ā—č/-—ā—Ė | –ļ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė? –Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė? |
| 5 | **–∂–į—ā—č—Ā —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ** (Locative) | -–ī–į/-–ī–Ķ / -—ā–į/-—ā–Ķ / -–Ĺ–ī–į/-–Ĺ–ī–Ķ | –ļ—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ? –Ĺ–Ķ–ī–Ķ? “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į? “õ–į—ą–į–Ĺ? |
| 6 | **—ą—č“ď—č—Ā —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ** (Ablative) | -–ī–į–Ĺ/-–ī–Ķ–Ĺ / -—ā–į–Ĺ/-—ā–Ķ–Ĺ / -–Ĺ–į–Ĺ/-–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ | –ļ—Ė–ľ–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ? –Ĺ–Ķ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ? “õ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ĺ? |
| 7 | **–ļ”©–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ā —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ** (Instrumental) | -–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ / -–Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ / -–Ī–Ķ–Ĺ | –ļ—Ė–ľ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ? –Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ? |

Each entry covers: definition + full suffix paradigm with vowel-harmony rules + question patterns + Kazakh usage example.

**(2) Canonical ¬ę–Ī–į–Ľ–į¬Ľ declension** (`gram_case_008`) ‚ÄĒ full 7-case paradigm in one entry: –Ī–į–Ľ–į / –Ī–į–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č“£ / –Ī–į–Ľ–į“ď–į / –Ī–į–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č / –Ī–į–Ľ–į–ī–į / –Ī–į–Ľ–į–ī–į–Ĺ / –Ī–į–Ľ–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ. Models the ¬ęclassical Kazakh declension paradigm¬Ľ.

**(3) 3 classification bridges**:
- `gram_case_bridge_001`: ¬ę**—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ IsA –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź**¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ case is grammatical category
- `gram_case_bridge_002`: ¬ę**–≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź IsA “Ī“ď—č–ľ**¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ grammatical category is concept
- `gram_case_bridge_003`: ¬ę**—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č IsA –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É**¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ case affix is affix

Resulting cascade chain: `<7 cases> ‚Üí —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ ‚Üí –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ` (3-hop chain) + ¬ę—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č ‚Üí –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É¬Ľ (parallel branch for affix taxonomy).

**(4) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with 11 new compound entities: 7 case names + —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É—č + —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ –Ņ–į—Ä–į–ī–ł–≥–ľ–į—Ā—č + –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź + –Ī–į–Ľ–į —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ –Ņ–į—Ä–į–ī–ł–≥–ľ–į—Ā—č.

**(5) Curriculum-grade vowel-harmony rules** documented per case ‚ÄĒ describes when each suffix variant applies based on the preceding sound (vowel / soft consonant / hard consonant / nasal). This is the elementary-grade Kazakh-language curriculum content for grades 3-4.

## [4.61.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 2 #1 ‚ÄĒ Kazakh grammar: full 10-class part-of-speech taxonomy

**Day 2 release 1/10. Day 2 STARTS** ‚ÄĒ Elementary 3-4 + Kazakh language depth. v4.61.0 establishes the full 10-class Kazakh part-of-speech taxonomy with curriculum-grade definitions for each class.

### Innovations

**(1) New 53rd domain `kazakh_grammar`** with 11 entries:

10 part-of-speech classes (each `IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č`) ‚ÄĒ first 4 already had base IsA bridges; v4.61.0 adds detailed curriculum definitions:
- `gram_pos_001` **–∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ** (noun) ‚ÄĒ defines –∂–į–Ľ–Ņ—č / –∂–į–Ľ“õ—č subtypes; questions –ļ—Ė–ľ? –Ĺ–Ķ?
- `gram_pos_002` **—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ** (adjective) ‚ÄĒ defines —Ā–į–Ņ–į–Ľ—č“õ / “õ–į—ā—č—Ā—ā—č“õ subtypes; —ą—č—Ä–į–Ļ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź—Ā—č (–∂–į–Ļ / —Ā–į–Ľ—č—Ā—ā—č—Ä–ľ–į–Ľ—č / –ļ“Į—ą–Ķ–Ļ—ā–Ņ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė / –į—Ā—č—Ä–ľ–į–Ľ—č)
- `gram_pos_003` **–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ** (verb) ‚ÄĒ defines —Ā–į–Ľ—ā / —Ā–į–Ī–į“õ—ā—č subtypes; —ą–į“õ / –∂–į“õ / —Ä–į–Ļ / –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā categories
- `gram_pos_004` **—Ā–į–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ** (numeral) ‚ÄĒ 6 subtypes: –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ / —Ä–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ / –∂–ł–Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č“õ / –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–∂–į–Ľ–ī—č“õ / –Ī”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ / —ā–ĺ–Ņ—ā–į—É
- `gram_pos_005` **–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ** (pronoun) ‚ÄĒ **NEW** ‚ÄĒ 7 subtypes: –∂—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—É / —Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā–Ķ—É / —Ā“Ī—Ä–į—É / –Ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė—Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–ļ / –∂–į–Ľ–Ņ—č–Ľ–į—É / ”©–∑–ī—Ė–ļ / –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–ľ—Ā—č–∑–ī—č“õ
- `gram_pos_006` **“Į—Ā—ā–Ķ—É** (adverb) ‚ÄĒ **NEW** ‚ÄĒ 6 subtypes: –ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ / –ľ–Ķ–∑–≥—Ė–Ľ / –ľ–į“õ—Ā–į—ā / —Ā–Ķ–Ī–Ķ–Ņ / –ľ”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ—Ä / —Ā—č–Ĺ-“õ–ł–ľ—č–Ľ
- `gram_pos_007` **—ą—č–Ľ–į—É** (particle/conjunction) ‚ÄĒ **NEW** ‚ÄĒ 3 subtypes: —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É–Ľ—Ė–ļ / –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É–Ľ—č“õ / –ī–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—É–Ľ—Ė–ļ
- `gram_pos_008` **–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—É —Ā”©–∑** (onomatopoeia) ‚ÄĒ **NEW** ‚ÄĒ 2 subtypes: –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā—ā—č“õ / –Ī–Ķ–Ļ–Ĺ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—É
- `gram_pos_009` **–ĺ–ī–į“ď–į–Ļ** (interjection) ‚ÄĒ **NEW** ‚ÄĒ 3 subtypes: –ļ”©“£—Ė–Ľ / —ą–į“õ—č—Ä—č—Ā / –Ī“Ī–Ļ—Ä—č“õ
- `gram_pos_010` **–ļ”©–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ą—Ė —Ā”©–∑** (auxiliary) ‚ÄĒ **NEW** ‚ÄĒ covers –ļ”©–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ą—Ė –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä + –ľ–ĺ–ī–į–Ľ—Ć —Ā”©–∑–ī–Ķ—Ä

**(2) 1 sub-class entry** (demonstrating sub-classification pattern):
- `gram_pos_011` **–∂–į–Ľ“õ—č –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ IsA –∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ** ‚ÄĒ proper noun is a kind of noun. Sets pattern for future subtype additions (–∂–į–Ľ–Ņ—č –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ, —Ā–į–Ņ–į–Ľ—č“õ —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ, —Ā–į–Ī–į“õ—ā—č –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ, etc.).

**(3) —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č PoS hub fully populated** ‚ÄĒ all 10 primary Kazakh word classes now classified `IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č`. The hub structure mirrors curriculum-aligned taxonomy used in Kazakh schools.

**(4) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with ¬ę–ļ”©–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ą—Ė —Ā”©–∑¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—É —Ā”©–∑¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į–Ľ“õ—č –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ topic extractor surfaces these as units for future grammar queries.

## [4.60.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ ūüéĮ Educational portal Day 1 #10 (FINAL) ‚ÄĒ Pre-school emotions + part-of-speech triangle CLOSED

**Day 1 release 10/10 ‚ÄĒ DAY 1 COMPLETE.** New 52nd domain `preschool_emotions` covering 10 fundamental emotions split into positive/negative valence, plus the closing ¬ę–∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č¬Ľ bridge that **closes the part-of-speech taxonomy triangle** (–∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ + —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ + –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ) under the —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č hub.

### Innovations

**(1) New 52nd domain `preschool_emotions`** with 14 entries:
- 5 positive emotions (`emo_001‚Ä¶emo_005`) ‚ÄĒ each `IsA –∂–į“ď—č–ľ–ī—č —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ`:
  - “õ—É–į–Ĺ—č—ą (joy) / –ľ–į—Ö–į–Ī–Ī–į—ā (love) / –Ī–į—ā—č–Ľ–ī—č“õ (courage) / –ľ–į“õ—ā–į–Ĺ—č—ą (pride) / —ā–į“£–ī–į–Ĺ—É (wonder)
- 5 negative emotions (`emo_006‚Ä¶emo_010`) ‚ÄĒ each `IsA –∂–į“ď—č–ľ—Ā—č–∑ —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ`:
  - —Ä–Ķ–Ĺ–∂—É (sadness) / “õ–ĺ—Ä“õ—č–Ĺ—č—ą (fear) / –∂–Ķ–ļ –ļ”©—Ä—É (hatred) / “õ—č–∑“ď–į–Ĺ—č—ą (envy) / “Ī—Ź—ā (shame)

Each entry: emotion definition + cultural / Kazakh-curriculum context (e.g., –Ī–į—ā—č—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä–ī—č“£ basty “õ–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā for –Ī–į—ā—č–Ľ–ī—č“õ; ¬ę“į—Ź—ā ”©–Ľ—Ė–ľ¬Ľ Kazakh proverb for “Ī—Ź—ā; psychology guidance for managing “õ—č–∑“ď–į–Ĺ—č—ą).

**(2) 4 classification bridges** establishing the emotion taxonomy AND closing the part-of-speech triangle:
- `emo_bridge_001`: ¬ę**–∂–į“ď—č–ľ–ī—č —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ IsA —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ**¬Ľ (positive emotion is emotion)
- `emo_bridge_002`: ¬ę**–∂–į“ď—č–ľ—Ā—č–∑ —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ IsA —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ**¬Ľ (negative emotion is emotion)
- `emo_bridge_003`: ¬ę**—Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ IsA “Ī“ď—č–ľ**¬Ľ (emotion is concept)
- `emo_bridge_004`: ¬ę**–∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č**¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ **closes the PoS triangle**: nouns + adjectives (v4.59.5) + verbs (v4.60.0) all hub through —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č

**(3) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with ¬ę–∂–į“ď—č–ľ–ī—č —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į“ď—č–ľ—Ā—č–∑ —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–Ķ–ļ –ļ”©—Ä—É¬Ľ, ¬ę–∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ¬Ľ.

**(4) —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č PoS hub COMPLETE** ‚ÄĒ three primary Kazakh word classes (–∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ) all classified IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č. Future grammar releases (particles, postpositions, conjunctions, etc.) extend this hub as siblings.

**(5) Cumulative cascade record** ‚ÄĒ +14 facts ‚Üí +64 derived = **4.6√ó cascade per fact**. The closing PoS bridge triggers massive R5 (shared_is_a_target) inferences across all PoS classes simultaneously.

### ūüéĮ Day 1 Final Summary

**10/10 releases shipped. 6 sub-domains complete:**

|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `preschool_alphabet` | 42/42 letters | 48 |
| 2 | `preschool_numbers` | cardinals 1-1000 + ordinals 1-10 | 36 |

| 6 | `preschool_emotions` | 10 emotions (5 pos + 5 neg) | 14 |

**Cumulative Day 1 deltas:** v4.55.5 ‚Üí v4.60.5 (10 releases)
- world_core entries: 2102 ‚Üí **2238** (+136)
- world_core facts: 2362 ‚Üí **2478** (+116)

- derived facts: 28112 ‚Üí **28400** (+288)

**Educational milestone:** adam now covers the full pre-school curriculum band (alphabet + numbers + shapes + sizes + routine + emotions) with established Kazakh academic vocabulary. The ¬ę—Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č¬Ľ hub formalizes the part-of-speech taxonomy, ready for elementary-grammar expansion in Day 2.

## [4.60.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 1 #9 ‚ÄĒ Daily routine verbs + verb-class taxonomy

**Day 1 release 9/10.** New 51st domain `preschool_routine`. 10 daily-routine action verbs (—ā“Ī—Ä—É / –∂—É—č–Ĺ—É / —ā–į–ľ–į“õ—ā–į–Ĺ—É / –ĺ–Ļ–Ĺ–į—É / –ĺ“õ—É / –∂–į–∑—É / –∂“Į—Ä—É / –∂“Į–≥—Ė—Ä—É / —ā—č“£–ī–į—É / “Ī–Ļ—č“õ—ā–į—É) + verb taxonomy bridge.

### Innovations

**(1) New 51st domain `preschool_routine`** with 13 entries:
- 10 daily-routine action entries (`routine_001‚Ä¶routine_010`):
  - **Wake/sleep:** —ā“Ī—Ä—É (wake up) / “Ī–Ļ—č“õ—ā–į—É (sleep)
  - **Hygiene:** –∂—É—č–Ĺ—É (wash)
  - **Eating:** —ā–į–ľ–į“õ—ā–į–Ĺ—É (eat)
  - **Play:** –ĺ–Ļ–Ĺ–į—É (play)
  - **Learning:** –ĺ“õ—É (read/study) / –∂–į–∑—É ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā—Ė (write)
  - **Movement:** –∂“Į—Ä—É (walk) / –∂“Į–≥—Ė—Ä—É (run)
  - **Communication:** —ā—č“£–ī–į—É (listen)

  Each entry: action description + cultural/health context (e.g., ¬ę–Ī–į–Ľ–į–Ľ–į—Ä“ď–į —ā”ô—É–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ 9‚Äď11 —Ā–į“ď–į—ā “Ī–Ļ“õ—č “õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā¬Ľ) + Kazakh usage examples.

**(2) 3 classification bridges** establishing the action / verb taxonomy:
- `routine_bridge_001`: ¬ę**–ļ“Į–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė —Ė—Ā-”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā IsA —Ė—Ā-”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā**¬Ľ
- `routine_bridge_002`: ¬ę**—Ė—Ā-”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā IsA “Ī“ď—č–ľ**¬Ľ
- `routine_bridge_003`: ¬ę**–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č**¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ adds the verb word-class to the part-of-speech taxonomy alongside v4.59.5's ¬ę—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č¬Ľ

Resulting cascade chains:
- `<verb> ‚Üí –ļ“Į–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė —Ė—Ā-”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā ‚Üí —Ė—Ā-”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ` (action chain)
- `–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ ‚Üí —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ` (verb word-class chain)

**(3) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with ¬ę–ļ“Į–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė —Ė—Ā-”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į–∑—É ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā—Ė¬Ľ.

**(4) —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č pivot strengthens** ‚ÄĒ both adjectives (v4.59.5) and verbs (v4.60.0) now hub through the ¬ę—Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č¬Ľ (part of speech) class. Future grammar releases (nouns, particles, postpositions, etc.) will plug in as additional sibling word-class entries.

## [4.59.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 1 #8 ‚ÄĒ Pre-school sizes + adjective taxonomy

**Day 1 release 8/10.** New 50th domain `preschool_sizes` covering 10 size-comparison adjectives + first formal adjective-class taxonomy (”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ ‚Üí —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ ‚Üí —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ).

### Innovations

**(1) New 50th domain `preschool_sizes`** with 13 entries:
- 10 size adjectives (`size_001‚Ä¶size_010`) in opposed pairs:
  - “Į–Ľ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ ‚ÜĒ –ļ—Ė—ą—Ė (big ‚ÜĒ small)
  - –Ī–ł—Ė–ļ ‚ÜĒ –į–Ľ–į—Ā–į (tall ‚ÜĒ short)
  - “Ī–∑—č–Ĺ ‚ÜĒ “õ—č—Ā“õ–į (long ‚ÜĒ short-length)
  - –į—É—č—Ä ‚ÜĒ –∂–Ķ“£—Ė–Ľ (heavy ‚ÜĒ light)
  - –ļ–Ķ“£ ‚ÜĒ —ā–į—Ä (wide ‚ÜĒ narrow)

  Each entry: definition + opposed pair (¬ę“õ–į—Ä–į–ľ–į-“õ–į—Ä—Ā—č—Ā—č: <opposite>¬Ľ) + Kazakh usage examples.

**(2) 3 classification bridges** establishing the first formal **adjective-class taxonomy** in world_core:
- `size_bridge_001`: ¬ę**”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ IsA —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ**¬Ľ (size adjective is a kind of adjective)
- `size_bridge_002`: ¬ę**—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ IsA —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č**¬Ľ (adjective is a part-of-speech class)
- `size_bridge_003`: ¬ę**—Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č IsA “Ī“ď—č–ľ**¬Ľ (part-of-speech class is a concept)

Resulting 4-hop cascade chain: `<adjective> ‚Üí ”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ ‚Üí —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ ‚Üí —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ` ‚ÄĒ deepest chain to date.

**(3) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** with ¬ę”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ¬Ľ, ¬ę—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ¬Ľ, ¬ę—Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č¬Ľ. The ¬ę—Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č¬Ľ (part of speech) hub will become the central pivot for future grammar releases (verbs, nouns, particles, etc.).

**(4) 50-domain milestone** ‚ÄĒ adam's world_core now spans 50 distinct knowledge domains.

## [4.59.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 1 #7 ‚ÄĒ Pre-school geometric shapes

**Day 1 release 7/10.** New 49th domain `preschool_shapes`. 10 geometric shapes for pre-school comprehension level + 2 classification bridges establishing the shape taxonomy.

### Innovations

**(1) New 49th domain `preschool_shapes`** with 12 entries:
- 10 shape entries (`shape_001‚Ä¶shape_010`):
  - **Basic shapes:** –ī”©“£–≥–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ (circle), —ą–į—Ä—ą—č (square), —ā—Ė–ļ—ā”©—Ä—ā–Ī“Ī—Ä—č—ą (rectangle), “Į—ą–Ī“Ī—Ä—č—ą (triangle), —Ā–ĺ–Ņ–į“õ—ą–į (oval), —Ä–ĺ–ľ–Ī (rhombus)
  - **N-gons:** –Ī–Ķ—Ā–Ī“Ī—Ä—č—ą (pentagon), –į–Ľ—ā—č–Ī“Ī—Ä—č—ą (hexagon)
  - **Themed shapes:** –∂“Ī–Ľ–ī—č–∑ –Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–Ĺ—Ė (star shape), –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ļ –Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–Ĺ—Ė (heart shape)

  Each entry: shape definition + side/angle properties + real-world examples (–Ĺ–į–Ĺ —ā–į–Ī–į“ď—č / —ą–į—Ö–ľ–į—ā —ā–į“õ—ā–į—Ā—č / –ü–Ķ–Ĺ—ā–į–≥–ĺ–Ĺ “ď–ł–ľ–į—Ä–į—ā—č / –į—Ä–į “Ī—Ź—ą—č“õ—ā–į—Ä—č / etc.)

**(2) 2 classification bridges**:
- `shape_bridge_001`: ¬ę**–≥–Ķ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ –Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–Ĺ IsA –Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–Ĺ**¬Ľ (geometric shape is a shape ‚ÄĒ middle layer)
- `shape_bridge_002`: ¬ę**–Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–Ĺ IsA “Ī“ď—č–ľ**¬Ľ (shape is a concept ‚ÄĒ top hub)

3-hop cascade chain: `<shape> ‚Üí –≥–Ķ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ –Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–Ĺ ‚Üí –Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–Ĺ ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ`.

- Decades: –∂–ł—č—Ä–ľ–į (20) / –ĺ—ā—č–∑ (30) / “õ—č—Ä—č“õ (40) / –Ķ–Ľ—É (50) / –į–Ľ–Ņ—č—Ā (60) / –∂–Ķ—ā–Ņ—Ė—Ā (70) / —Ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ–Ĺ (80) / —ā–ĺ“õ—Ā–į–Ĺ (90)

- `num_bridge_005`: ¬ę**–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č“õ —Ā–į–Ĺ–į—É –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė IsA —Ā–į–Ĺ–į—É –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė**¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ decimal numbering is a kind of numbering system

## [4.57.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-06 ‚ÄĒ Educational portal Day 1 #4 ‚ÄĒ Alphabet completion (letters 31-42 + closing bridges)

**Day 1 release 4/10.** Closes the pre-school Kazakh alphabet domain: letters “ļ through –Į (12 letters from positions 31-42 of 42). **42/42 = 100 % alphabet coverage.** Plus 3 closing classification bridges that unlock cross-domain cascading.

### Innovations

**(1) 12 final letter entries** in `data/world_core/preschool_alphabet.jsonl` (`alpha_031‚Ä¶alpha_042`): “Ľ / —Ü / —á / —ą / —Č / —ä / —č / —Ė / —Ć / —ć / —é / —Ź. Includes 2 Kazakh-specific letters (“Ľ ‚ÄĒ laryngeal, ~10-15 native words; —Ė ‚ÄĒ front unrounded vowel) and 8 Russian-loanword letters explicitly flagged. The hard sign (—ä) and soft sign (—Ć) carry ¬ę”©–∑—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā—č –∂–ĺ“õ¬Ľ (no own sound) descriptors. Letters —é / —Ź carry ¬ę–Ļ—É¬Ľ / ¬ę–Ļ–į¬Ľ digraph explanations.

**(2) 3 closing classification bridges** that unlock cross-domain cascading:
- `alpha_bridge_004`: ¬ę**–ī–į—É—č—Ā—ā—č –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā IsA –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā**¬Ľ (vowel is a sound; ¬ę12 –ī–į—É—č—Ā—ā—č –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā –Ī–į—Ä¬Ľ fact embedded in narration)
- `alpha_bridge_005`: ¬ę**–ī–į—É—č—Ā—Ā—č–∑ –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā IsA –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā**¬Ľ (consonant is a sound; ¬ę25 –ī–į—É—č—Ā—Ā—č–∑ –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā –Ī–į—Ä¬Ľ fact embedded)
- `alpha_bridge_006`: ¬ę**“õ–į–∑–į“õ ”ô–Ľ—Ė–Ņ–Ī–ł—Ė IsA –∂–į–∑—É –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė**¬Ľ (Kazakh alphabet is a writing system; ¬ę42 ”ô—Ä—Ė–Ņ –Ī–į—Ä¬Ľ embedded)

These chain through v4.56.0‚Äďv4.57.0's existing hubs: `–ī–į—É—č—Ā—ā—č –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā ‚Üí –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ`, `–ī–į—É—č—Ā—Ā—č–∑ –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā ‚Üí –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ`, `“õ–į–∑–į“õ ”ô–Ľ—Ė–Ņ–Ī–ł—Ė ‚Üí –∂–į–∑—É –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ ‚Üí “Ī“ď—č–ľ`. Future grammar / phonetics releases will plug into this triple-layered hub structure.

**(3) MULTIWORD_ENTITIES extended** in `crates/adam-dialog/src/topic_extraction.rs` ‚ÄĒ added 4 new compound nouns: ¬ę–ī–į—É—č—Ā—ā—č –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā¬Ľ, ¬ę–ī–į—É—č—Ā—Ā—č–∑ –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į–∑–į“õ ”ô–Ľ—Ė–Ņ–Ī–ł—Ė¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į–∑—É –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė¬Ľ. ## [4.55.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Architecture rename: NLM ‚Üí ARK (Agglutinative Reasoning Kernel)

**Driven by user decision (2026-05-05):** the architecture name ¬ęNLM¬Ľ (Nano Language Model) was buckets adam with the LLM family. **ARK** = Agglutinative Reasoning Kernel names what adam actually is ‚ÄĒ agglutinative morphology + curated knowledge graph + forward-chaining reasoner + tiny trained selection weights, packaged as a system-runtime not a probabilistic estimator.

### Naming choice

User rejected two earlier proposals:
- **AGM** (Agglutinative Grammar Model) ‚ÄĒ ¬ęGrammar¬Ľ triggered school-grammar associations
- **CALM** (Controlled Agglutinative Language Model) ‚ÄĒ ¬ęControlled¬Ľ was redundant (¬ęAgglutinative¬Ľ already implies discipline by morphological nature)

User-accepted: **ARK** ‚ÄĒ three deliberate choices:
- **A**gglutinative ‚ÄĒ the morphological algebra (already carries determinism per nature)
- **R**easoning ‚ÄĒ forward-chaining over typed facts (the actual differentiator vs LLM)
- **K**ernel ‚ÄĒ system-runtime (vs ¬ęModel¬Ľ = probabilistic estimator); aligns with the existing `adam-kernel` crate naming

Tiny ML lives, large ML doesn't: the trained perceptron is 24 bytes / 6 f32; suffix priors and root affinity are statistical but auditable. Generation is template-based, never free-text. The ¬ęKernel¬Ľ naming captures that ML layers exist but sit inside the kernel as inspectable components, not at the centre.

### Innovations

**(1) `SystemIdentity::canonical()` updated** in `crates/adam-dialog/src/system_identity.rs` ‚ÄĒ `full_name: "Agglutinative Reasoning Kernel"`, `abbreviation: "ARK"`. Doc-comment on the struct fields explicitly references the v4.55.5 rename. Unit test `canonical_identity_carries_all_required_fields` updated to assert ARK.

**(2) Latin-token whitelist extended** in `crates/adam-dialog/src/quality.rs::is_allowed_latin_token` ‚ÄĒ added `Agglutinative`, `Reasoning`, `Kernel`, `ARK`. Old tokens (`Nano`, `Language`, `Model`, `NLM`) kept on the whitelist for backwards compat with historical CHANGELOG entries + curated facts.

**(3) Templates updated** in `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`:
- `greeting.intro_proposal` line 74: ¬ę–ł”ô, ”ô—Ä–ł–Ĺ–Ķ. –ú–Ķ–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—É–≥–Ķ –į—Ä–Ĺ–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ **ARK**, –į—ā—č–ľ –į–ī–į–ľ.¬Ľ
- `ask_about_system` line 206: ¬ę**ARK** ‚ÄĒ Agglutinative Reasoning Kernel, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–ī–į–ľ –į—ā—ā—č “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ

**(4) 3 new world_core entries** in `data/world_core/adam_self.jsonl` (`adam_self_040‚Ä¶042`):
- `adam IsA ARK` ‚ÄĒ establishes the identity bridge
- `ARK IsA –ī–Ķ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ` ‚ÄĒ frames as deterministic system, not probabilistic LM
- `ARK IsA –į—Ä—Ö–ł—ā–Ķ–ļ—ā—É—Ä–į –į—ā–į—É—č` ‚ÄĒ names this as an architecture-class label

**(5) 2 new MULTIWORD_ENTITIES** in `crates/adam-dialog/src/topic_extraction.rs` ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–į—Ä—Ö–ł—ā–Ķ–ļ—ā—É—Ä–į –į—ā–į—É—č¬Ľ and ¬ę–ī–Ķ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ¬Ľ (so the topic extractor surfaces them as a unit).

**(6) Eval data updated** ‚ÄĒ `data/eval/cognitive_dialog_dataset.json`, `data/eval/repl_dialogs.json`, and `data/eval/live_holdout_2026_05_01.json`: assertions now accept ARK / Agglutinative Reasoning Kernel.

**(7) README ‚ÄĒ new ¬ęArchitecture name ‚ÄĒ ARK¬Ľ section** above the Engineering thesis. Documents the four-pillar definition + the rationale + the renaming context. CONTRIBUTING.md Latin-whitelist bullet updated.

## [4.55.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Metrics-currency CI gate (closes Codex's last hygiene gap)

**Closes the Codex review's last hygiene observation.** v4.52.5 manually fixed ~15 numeric/version drifts that had silently accumulated across 15 minor versions. v4.55.0 codifies that audit as an automated CI gate so drift can't reaccumulate. Each subsequent release either updates the docs OR fails this gate.

### Innovations

**(1) New `scripts/check_metrics_currency.sh`** ‚ÄĒ POSIX shell, depends only on `jq` + `awk` + `grep` + `sed`. 9 cross-checks against canonical artefacts:

| Check | Source-of-truth | Doc target |
|---|---|---|
| Intent count | `crates/adam-dialog/src/intent.rs` (top-level enum variants) | README badge `intents-NN` |
| World-core entries | total lines across `data/world_core/*.jsonl` | README badge `world%20core-NN`, `data/README.md` row, `data/world_core/README.md` Live totals |
| Total facts | `data/retrieval/facts.json .facts \| length` | README badge `NN%20facts`, `data/README.md` row, `data/world_core/README.md` Live totals |
| Workspace version | `Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version` | README badge `version-x.y.z`, `docs/performance.md` header |
| World-core .jsonl raw facts | sum of `.facts` arrays in jsonl | informational only (not gated; documents the world_core vs total-facts distinction) |
| Derived facts | `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json .derived \| length` | informational only |

Fails on the FIRST drift (no aggregation) ‚ÄĒ error message names the file, the claim, and the live value. Exit-non-zero contract for CI integration.

**(2) Wired into `scripts/validate_foundation.sh`** as a final post-test step. Foundation validation now also gates on docs currency.

**(3) `CONTRIBUTING.md` pre-push checklist updated** ‚ÄĒ replaces the previous ¬ęmanual README audit¬Ľ bullet with the automated gate. Adds `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (v4.54.6+) as a separate bullet for completeness. The full pre-push contract is now:

1. `cargo fmt --all --check`
2. `cargo test --workspace`
3. `bash scripts/verify_release_version.sh <x.y.z>`
4. `bash scripts/check_metrics_currency.sh` ‚Üź new
5. `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

### Acceptance

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| `bash scripts/check_metrics_currency.sh` | green (9/9 checks pass at workspace v4.55.0) |
| `bash scripts/validate_foundation.sh` | green (with new currency check wired in) |
| Workspace tests | **976 passing** unchanged |
| `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` | green |
| Foundation: 2099 / 2359 / 46 / 28109 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo build --release` | clean |

### Codex review ‚ÄĒ closing punch list

| Codex observation (2026-05-05) | Resolution |
|---|---|
| `verify_release_version.sh` failing on stale manifests | v4.52.5 ‚ÄĒ bumped 7 manifests v4.37.0 ‚Üí v4.52.5 |
| ~15 numeric/version drifts across docs | v4.52.5 ‚ÄĒ manually fixed |
| Doc currency drift can reaccumulate | **v4.55.0 ‚ÄĒ automated gate** |
| 293 unaddressed clippy warnings, no quality gate | v4.54.6 ‚ÄĒ workspace.lints policy + 73 auto-fix + 4 manual; clippy now clean with `-D warnings` |
| External blind eval / wedge selection / fundraising | Out of scope per `project_v4_direction` (no investors) |

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ new public CI script + new automated gate + CONTRIBUTING checklist update. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: ¬ęminor x.y.0 = significant capability or milestone¬Ľ. First release where doc currency is a self-checking invariant.

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.54.6] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Clippy hygiene + workspace.lints policy

**Driven by Codex review (2026-05-05) flag of 293 unaddressed clippy warnings.** Adopts an explicit `[workspace.lints.clippy]` policy: stylistic / refactor-risk classes are allowed with documented rationale; everything else passes `-D warnings`. CI / contributor experience now has a clean clippy gate.

### Innovations

**(1) `[workspace.lints.clippy]` policy** in root `Cargo.toml`. 10 lint classes explicitly allowed with one-line rationale each:
- `collapsible_if` (99) ‚ÄĒ nested `if let Some(...) { if cond { ... } }` is clearer for FST + planner layers than collapsed `&&` form
- `doc_lazy_continuation` + `doc_overindented_list_items` (40) ‚ÄĒ rustfmt + rustdoc render existing `///` lists correctly
- `type_complexity` (7) ‚ÄĒ type aliases would hide structure of FST signatures
- `too_many_arguments` (3) ‚ÄĒ Conversation::turn_with_trace public arity is stable; refactor-to-config-struct would churn every test fixture
- `items_after_test_module` (3) ‚ÄĒ minor ordering preference
- `if_same_then_else` (3) ‚ÄĒ separate semantic branches; merging would lose readability
- `empty_line_after_doc_comments` (3) ‚ÄĒ rustfmt allows them
- `field_reassign_with_default` (7) ‚ÄĒ sequential field assignment in tests + builders
- `unnecessary_get_then_check` (3) ‚ÄĒ both compile to same code
- `redundant_guards` (2) ‚ÄĒ explicit guards aid trace clarity

**(2) `lints.workspace = true`** wired to all 10 crate `Cargo.toml` files (adam-kernel, adam-kernel-fst, adam-dialog, adam-tokenizer, adam-corpus, adam-eval, adam-reasoning, adam-retrieval, adam-scaling, adam-train) ‚ÄĒ without this, the workspace policy doesn't apply per crate.

**(3) Auto-fixed via `cargo clippy --fix`** ‚ÄĒ 73 warnings cleaned automatically (mostly `redundant_closure` / `cloned_instead_of_copied` / `single_char_add_str` / `unnecessary_map_or` / `manual_range_contains` / etc.).

**(4) Manual fixes for 4 remaining genuine code-quality issues:**
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs:1463` ‚ÄĒ `nonminimal_bool` in `detect_ask_age`: factored common `has_q` conjunction.
- `crates/adam-reasoning/src/patterns.rs:2084` ‚ÄĒ `vec_init_then_push` in test fixture: `let mut frames = Vec::new(); frames.push(...);` ‚Üí `let frames = vec![...];`.
- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/process_rust_book_kk.rs:169` ‚ÄĒ `ptr_arg` for `chapter_num`: `&PathBuf` ‚Üí `&Path` (canonical Rust API). Required call-site update to closure form because of generic-fn / sort_by_key bound.
- `crates/adam-tokenizer/src/bin/train_bpe.rs:267` ‚ÄĒ `format_in_format_args` in eprintln! merged the inner format into the outer macro.

### Acceptance

| Gate | Pre-v4.54.6 | Post-v4.54.6 |
|---|---|---|
| `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets` | 293 warnings | **0 warnings** |
| `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` | failed | **green** |
| Workspace tests | 976 passing | **976 passing** unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean | clean |
| `cargo build --release` | clean | clean |
| `bash scripts/verify_release_version.sh 4.54.6` | n/a | green |
| Foundation: 2099 / 2359 / 46 / 28109 | unchanged | unchanged |

### Cadence

`.6` patch ‚ÄĒ pure hygiene policy; no behavioural changes, no new tests, no API surface changes. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: ¬ępatch = small / incremental (bug fixes, small Lexicon additions, docs, housekeeping)¬Ľ. Closes Codex's quality-gate observation; clippy is now a real CI gate.

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.54.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Session 6 transcript-driven fixes + first autonomous crash-tests

**Driven by user dialog test session 6 (2026-05-05) + autonomous crash-tests.** Per the user's directive, this is the first release where adam runs its own real-REPL crash-tests before shipping (autonomous-test cadence captured in `docs/weekly_plan_2026-W19.md`). Three concrete gaps from session 6 closed; one extra gap surfaced and fixed by autotest B; one architectural gap (G4) deferred with explicit trace.

### Real-REPL gap closure

| # | Pre-v4.54.5 | Post-v4.54.5 |
|---|---|---|
| **G1** | ¬ę–Ę–į–Ĺ—č—Ā–į–Ļ—č“õ.¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę**“ö—É–į–Ĺ–į-“õ—É–į–Ĺ–į.** –ú–Ķ–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—É–≥–Ķ –į—Ä–Ĺ–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ NLM, –į—ā—č–ľ –į–ī–į–ľ.¬Ľ (jarringly colloquial reduplication for an introductory turn) | ¬ę**–ė”ô, ”ô—Ä–ł–Ĺ–Ķ.** –ú–Ķ–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—É–≥–Ķ –į—Ä–Ĺ–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ NLM, –į—ā—č–ľ –į–ī–į–ľ.¬Ľ ‚úď |
| **G2** | ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ **–į—ā—č–ľ–ī—č** –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ ‚Üí grounded fact about ¬ę–į—ā¬Ľ (horse) ‚ÄĒ Acc-form 1sg.poss + memory-probe was treated as Unknown with noun_hint=–į—ā | ¬ę**–°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –į—ā—č“£—č–∑ –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā.**¬Ľ ‚úď (recall fired) |
| **G3** | ¬ę...“õ–ĺ—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į **–Ĺ–Ķ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į—ā—č–Ĺ—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ņ**, 2-–≥–Ķ –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ņ, 4-–ļ–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑.¬Ľ ‚Üí math_refusal (the auxiliary verb ¬ę–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ņ¬Ľ between operations triggered clause-has-math-verb failure semantics) | ¬ę**–Ě”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė: 38**¬Ľ ‚úď |
| **B2** (autotest) | ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ **–į—ā—č-–∂”©–Ĺ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė** “Ī–ľ—č—ā–Ņ–į–ī—č“£ –Ī–į?¬Ľ ‚Üí check_contradiction template (compound ¬ęname + family-name¬Ľ Acc form not in detector) | ¬ę**–°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –į—ā—č“£—č–∑ –ě–Ľ–∂–į—Ā.**¬Ľ ‚úď |

### Innovations

**(1) Template fix for `greeting.intro_proposal`** ‚ÄĒ replaced ¬ę“õ—É–į–Ĺ–į-“õ—É–į–Ĺ–į. –ú–Ķ–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ ...¬Ľ with ¬ę–ł”ô, ”ô—Ä–ł–Ĺ–Ķ. –ú–Ķ–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ ...¬Ľ (line 74, `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`). Same conversational function, neutral tone appropriate for an introduction-acceptance turn.

**(2) `detect_ask_name` extended** ‚ÄĒ handles 4 new self-recall variants combining 1sg-poss + Acc + memory-probe phrasings:
- `–į—ā—č–ľ` / `–į—ā—č–ľ–ī—č` / `–į—ā—č-–∂”©–Ĺ—Ė–ľ` / `–į—ā—č-–∂”©–Ĺ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė` √ó `–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ` / `–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£–ī–Ķ` / `“Ī–ľ—č—ā–Ņ–į*`
- `–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ—Ė–ľ` / `–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė` √ó same probe set

The compound-name ¬ę–į—ā—č-–∂”©–Ĺ¬Ľ ("name + family-name") variant was surfaced specifically by autonomous crash-test session B.

**(3) `clause_has_math_verb` ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ¬Ľ removed** (`discourse.rs`). The top-level `input_is_math_expression` gate still recognises ¬ę–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ-¬Ľ (auxiliary "calculate") for math-mode entry, but inside the v4.42.0 multi-clause loop we no longer treat it as a real math operator. The four real operators (–ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā / –Ī”©–Ľ / “õ–ĺ—Ā / –į–∑–į–Ļ—ā) plus the closed `–į–Ľ`-forms still gate failure semantics. With ¬ę–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ¬Ľ off the failure list, auxiliary appendages like ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į—ā—č–Ĺ—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–Ņ¬Ľ fall through silently to the accumulator-carry path.

**(4) First autonomous crash-test cadence** ‚ÄĒ adam's own REPL runs as a standard pre-release gate. Session A (–ź–Ļ–≥“Į–Ľ / –ī”ô—Ä—Ė–≥–Ķ—Ä / –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ activity + math chain + knowledge queries) ran clean across 12 turns. Session B (name correction + math + retrieval edges + apology) found 1 detector gap (B2, fixed) and 1 architectural concern (B1: pending name-contradiction blocks math detection ‚ÄĒ deferred to a separate release).

### Deferred

**G4** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ė”ô¬Ľ after `unknown.clarify_low_confidence` produced confused ¬ę“ö–į–Ļ—Ā—č—Ā—č –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č ‚ÄĒ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –ľ–į, —Ā“Ī—Ä–į–Ļ –ľ–į?¬Ľ. Traced to `check_contradiction` template (line 564, v1.toml) firing with malformed `{old_value}=–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č / {new_value}=—Ā“Ī—Ä–į–Ļ`. Phantom belief conflict appears to be created from prior turn's clarify response. Architectural fix needed in belief-tracker; deferred with full trace context.

**B1** ‚ÄĒ bare arithmetic ¬ę17 + 25 “õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č?¬Ľ blocked by pending name-contradiction. Behaviour is technically correct (modal-dialog semantics for unresolved conflict) but UX-debatable. Defer with discussion needed about precedence policy.

### Verification

|---|---|

| `bash scripts/verify_release_version.sh 4.54.5` | green |

`.5` patch ‚ÄĒ pure detector + template fixes (no new code surface, no new public functions). Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`. Closes 4 transcript-driven gaps and establishes the autonomous-crash-test cadence as standard pre-release practice.

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.54.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Lexicon expansion + 10 bridge-facts (highest-ROI batch)

**Per `project_bridge_fact_leverage`:** a single `{sub_hub} IsA {top_hub}` fact multiplies derivation leverage to +47‚Äď67/fact vs +15/fact without bridge. v4.54.0 lands the largest single-batch ROI in the v4.x line: **10 bridge facts ‚Üí +934 derived facts (+93/fact)** ‚ÄĒ far above both the prior empirical bound and the spec's +300 acceptance threshold.

### Innovations

**(1) 5 new morpheme roots** in `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` (the canonical lexicon location for morpheme-coverage). Spec named 3 from `project_morpheme_coverage_baseline`:
- **–į–Ľ“ď–į—ą** (adverb, back, voiceless consonant) ‚ÄĒ covers 625 corpus occurrences (+ derivatives like –į–Ľ“ď–į—ą“õ—č)
- **–ľ–Ľ–Ĺ** (numeral, back, nasal) ‚ÄĒ 1987 occurrences across cc100 + wikipedia
- **—Ė—Ā–ļ–Ķ** ‚ÄĒ initially added but reverted in this release: it broke the segmentation eval (`seg_183` expects `—Ė—Ā + –ļ–Ķ`, not `—Ė—Ā–ļ–Ķ` as a unit). The `—Ė—Ā` root (already in lexicon at 2 chars) is below the morpheme_coverage min-root-len filter; resolving that needs a separate lexicon-tooling change deferred to a later release.

Plus 3 high-frequency bonus roots needed to clear the > 86.5 % acceptance threshold:
- **–ī–į–ľ—č—ā—É** (verb, back, vowel) ‚ÄĒ develop; 1683 occurrences
- **–ľ–į“£—č–∑–ī—č** (adjective, back, vowel) ‚ÄĒ important; 1605
- **–į—ą—č“õ** (adjective, back, voiceless consonant) ‚ÄĒ open; 1487

**(2) 10 bridge facts** in `data/world_core/role_bridges.jsonl` (ids `bridge_role_054`‚Ä¶`bridge_role_063`), each linking a sub-hub noun to a top-tier IsA hub:
- –Ī–į–Ľ–į IsA –į–ī–į–ľ, –ī–ĺ—Ā IsA –į–ī–į–ľ (2 ‚Üí human-hub)
- –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā IsA –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ (1 ‚Üí system-hub)
- –∂–į–ī IsA “Ī“ď—č–ľ (1 ‚Üí concept-hub)
- –ļ–ł—Ė–ľ IsA –∑–į—ā, –∂–ł“Ľ–į–∑ IsA –∑–į—ā (2 ‚Üí thing-hub)
- –Ī“Ī–Ľ—ą—č“õ–Ķ—ā IsA –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ, –∂“Į–Ļ–ļ–Ķ IsA –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ (2 ‚Üí organ-hub)
- –Ī”©–Ľ–ľ–Ķ IsA –ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ (1 ‚Üí place-hub)
- –Ķ–Ľ IsA –ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā (1 ‚Üí state-hub)

Selection criterion: 122 world_core subjects currently lack any IsA edge (computed via `comm -23` of all-subjects vs has-isa-subjects). The 10 picked are the highest-frequency missing bridges ‚ÄĒ each subject already participates in many corpus / world_core relations, so the new IsA edge cascades through R1 (transitivity), R2 (Has inheritance), R5 (shared IsA target) rules.

**(3) Re-ran the full pipeline** ‚ÄĒ `extract_facts` + `run_reasoner` + `morpheme_coverage` + `validate_foundation`. Every artefact regenerated; `verify_release_version.sh 4.54.0` green; foundation validation green.

### Acceptance metrics

## [4.53.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Context-aware clarify (session 5 generic-fallback complaint closed)

**Closes the user's session-5 complaint:** "–ĺ—ā–≤–Ķ—ā—č –ī–ĺ–Ľ–∂–Ĺ—č –≤—č—ā–Ķ–ļ–į—ā—Ć –ł–∑ –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā—ā–į –Ī–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–ī—č, –į –Ĺ–Ķ –ĺ–Ī—Č–ł–Ļ –ĺ—ā–≤–Ķ—ā –Ĺ–į –≤—Ā–Ķ, —á—ā–ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ –∑–Ĺ–į–Ķ—ā". When `Intent::Unknown` fires AND adam can't extract a noun-hint OR the topic is low-confidence, the v4.37.5 clarify path emitted a generic ¬ę–°“Ī—Ä–į“ď—č“£—č–∑–ī—č —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ. “ö–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č —Ā“Ī—Ä–į–Ņ –ĺ—ā—č—Ä—Ā—č–∑?¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ completely ignoring stored profile slots (name, occupation, activity, city). v4.53.5 routes those clarify cases to a new `unknown.with_session_diagnostic` family that cites the stored context and asks the user to pick a topic from what adam already knows.

### Innovations

**(1) New `unknown.with_session_diagnostic` template family** in `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml` ‚ÄĒ 7 variants gating on different slot subsets:
- All 3 primary slots (name + occupation + activity) ‚ÄĒ full context-aware diagnostic.
- Name + occupation pair.
- Name + activity pair.
- Activity only (no name).
- Occupation only (no name).
- City only ‚ÄĒ fallback when only location is known.
- Name only ‚ÄĒ minimal context, but cites the name so user sees that adam tracks who they are.

`template_is_fillable` filters the variants to those whose slot requirements match the actual session contents; the seed picks among the eligible subset.

**(2) Planner override in `plan_response_with_epistemic`** ‚ÄĒ runs AFTER the existing epistemic / known-user override block. When the chosen `key` is `unknown.clarify_no_topic` OR `unknown.clarify_low_confidence` AND the session contains any of `name` / `occupation` / `activity` / `city`, override to `unknown.with_session_diagnostic`. Defends against missing template family via the standard `!repo.get(...).is_empty()` guard (mirrors v4.0.34 epistemic-override pattern).

**(3) Trace line `clarify_diagnostic override`** ‚ÄĒ emitted when the override fires; lists which slots are present (name=true/false occupation=true/false activity=true/false city=true/false). Lets `--trace` consumers see exactly which context-aware variant the planner chose and why.

**(4) 2 new unit tests** in `planner::tests`:
- `clarify_no_topic_with_session_routes_to_diagnostic` ‚ÄĒ Intent::Unknown with no noun_hint + session with name+occupation; asserts trace contains the override AND realised text cites a stored slot AND output is NOT the bare clarify line.
- `clarify_no_topic_without_session_keeps_bare_family` ‚ÄĒ regression guard: empty session ‚Üí no override ‚Üí `unknown.clarify_no_topic` still wins.

### Real-REPL gap closure

| Pre-v4.53.5 | Post-v4.53.5 |
|---|---|
| Vague follow-up after ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–ľ—č–Ĺ ...¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę–°“Ī—Ä–į“ď—č“£—č–∑–ī—č —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ. “ö–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č —Ā“Ī—Ä–į–Ņ –ĺ—ā—č—Ä—Ā—č–∑? –ź–∑–ī–į–Ņ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č–Ľ–į–Ņ –∂—Ė–Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑—ą—Ė.¬Ľ (generic, ignores 3 stored slots) | ¬ę**–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –ī–Ķ–Ņ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ. –°“Ī—Ä–į“ď—č“£—č–∑ –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č“£—č–∑“ď–į “õ–į—ā—č—Ā—ā—č –ľ–į, ”ô–Ľ–ī–Ķ –Ī–į—Ā“õ–į —ā–į“õ—č—Ä—č–Ņ—ā–į –ľ–į? –ź–∑–ī–į–Ņ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č–Ľ–į“£—č–∑—ą—č.**¬Ľ ‚úď (cites name + occupation; offers concrete topic choice) |

## [4.53.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Math gerund-clause parser (session 5 deferral closed)

**Closes the v4.52.0 deferral.** Real-REPL session 5 surfaced a gap in the multi-clause word-math parser: gerund/converb-form chains like ¬ę–ē–Ľ—É–ī—Ė –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ **–ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ** “Į—ą–ļ–Ķ **–Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–Ņ**, 7-–Ĺ—Ė **–į–∑–į–Ļ—ā“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į** –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č?¬Ľ refused to evaluate, while the same operations in imperative form (¬ę... –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑, ... –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑, ... –į–∑–į–Ļ—ā—č“£—č–∑¬Ľ) worked. v4.53.0 fixes the gap by injecting clause separators after gerund/converb math-verb forms, letting the v4.42.0 multi-clause evaluator chain operations across them.

### Innovations

**(1) `inject_gerund_clause_separators(input)`** ‚ÄĒ new pure helper in `discourse.rs`. Walks the input and inserts `__CLAUSE_SEP__` after each math-verb gerund/converb form. 8 surface forms covered: `–ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ` / `–ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ņ` / `–Ī”©–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ` / `–Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–Ņ` / `“õ–ĺ—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į` / `“õ–ĺ—Ā—č–Ņ` / `–į–∑–į–Ļ—ā“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į` / `–į–∑–į–Ļ—ā—č–Ņ`. Token-boundary-safe (pads with leading/trailing spaces; only matches whole tokens).

**(2) Multi-clause gerund support** ‚ÄĒ `try_evaluate_kazakh_word_math` calls the helper before the existing `,` / `–∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ` / `—Ā–ĺ–ī–į–Ĺ –ļ–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ` / `—Ā–ĺ“£—č–Ĺ–ī–į` clause splits. Once gerund/converb forms become explicit clause boundaries, the v4.42.0 accumulator path handles the chain unchanged.

**(3) Vowel-harmony coverage** ‚ÄĒ gerund family `-“ď–į–Ĺ–ī–į / -–≥–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ / -“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į / -–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ` and converb family `-—č–Ņ / -—Ė–Ņ / -–Ņ` selected per stem according to standard Kazakh vowel harmony (–ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā + voiceless —ā ‚Üí -–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ / -—Ė–Ņ; “õ–ĺ—Ā + voiceless —Ā back-vowel ‚Üí -“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į / -—č–Ņ; etc.).

**(4) 5 new unit tests** ‚ÄĒ `word_math_gerund_chain_session5_first_line` (the canonical session-5 case ‚Üí 26), `word_math_converb_only_chain` (pure -—Ė–Ņ chain), `word_math_gerund_only_chain` (pure -“ď–į–Ĺ–ī–į chain), `word_math_qosqanda_qosyp_forms` (add-form coverage), `word_math_gerund_does_not_break_simple_imperative` (regression guard).

### Real-REPL gap closure

| Pre-v4.53.0 | Post-v4.53.0 |
|---|---|
| ¬ę–ē–Ľ—É–ī—Ė –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ “Į—ą–ļ–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–Ņ, 7-–Ĺ—Ė –į–∑–į–Ļ—ā“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č?¬Ľ ‚Üí math_refusal | **¬ę–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 26 (–∂–ł—č—Ä–ľ–į –į–Ľ—ā—č)¬Ľ** ‚úď |

## [4.52.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Engineering hygiene + doc currency + weekly plan

**Driven by external code review (Codex, 2026-05-05).** Fixes broken release gate and stale docs that had silently drifted across ~15 minor versions. No new product behaviour; no new tests; no foundation changes. Pure hygiene.

### What was broken

- `bash scripts/verify_release_version.sh 4.52.0` failed: 7 data manifests pinned to v4.37.0 (stale since the v4.37.5 cadence). Release gate had been silently bypassed.
- README badge claimed 31 intents; the `Intent` enum has 33 variants (v4.51.0 added `StatementOfActivity` + `AskActivity`, never reflected in README).
- `data/README.md:18` listed world_core as ¬ę874 entries / 995 facts across 30 domains¬Ľ (frozen at v4.4.7); actual counts: 2089 / 2349 / 46.
- `data/README.md:19` listed retrieval as ¬ę15 521 facts / 21 415 derivations¬Ľ; actual: 2349 / 27175.
- `data/world_core/README.md:106,145` had ¬ęCurrent scale (v4.3.0)¬Ľ and ¬ę1625 entries (v4.11.7)¬Ľ as the canonical totals.
- `docs/foundation_scope.md:90` claimed ¬ę822 workspace tests at v4.22.5¬Ľ; actual: 969 at v4.52.5.
- `docs/performance.md`, `docs/corpus_audit.md` headers stamped to old versions.
- `docs/kazakh_grammar/07_dialog_architecture.md:133` listed 26-intent taxonomy.
- `crates/adam-dialog/Cargo.toml` description said ¬ę26-intent recogniser (v4.1.0)¬Ľ.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/lib.rs` docstring said ¬ęStage: v3.9.5 ‚ÄĒ 26-intent¬Ľ.
- 5 crates had no `description` field in Cargo.toml: adam-kernel, adam-tokenizer, adam-corpus, adam-eval, adam-retrieval.

### What this release does

**(1)** Bumps 7 data manifests v4.37.0 ‚Üí v4.52.5: `data/eval/benchmark_manifest.json`, `data/eval/kazakh_foundation_eval_dataset.json`, `data/eval/tokenizer_experiment_manifest.json`, `data/eval/tokenizer_segmentation_eval_dataset.json`, `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json`, `data/tokenizer/segmentation_rules.json`, `data/training/baseline_training_manifest.json`.

**(2)** Re-stamps every numeric claim to live ground truth: 969 tests, 33 intents, 2089/2349/46/27175 foundation totals, p50 1.07 ms.

**(3)** Adds `description` to 5 missing crate Cargo.toml files (adam-kernel, adam-tokenizer, adam-corpus, adam-eval, adam-retrieval).

**(4)** Updates `data/world_core/README.md` to surface live totals at the top of the ¬ęCurrent scale¬Ľ section and explicitly mark the per-domain breakdown as a v4.11.7 historical snapshot.

**(5)** New `docs/weekly_plan_2026-W19.md` ‚ÄĒ concrete day-by-day deliverables for the week (W19, 2026-05-05 ‚Üí 2026-05-11): math gerund-clause parser ‚Üí context-aware clarify ‚Üí live REPL session 6 ‚Üí Lexicon + bridge-fact expansion ‚Üí clippy hygiene ‚Üí metrics-currency CI gate. Aligned with `project_v4_direction` (no investors) and `feedback_real_human_testing_with_memory`.

## [4.52.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Transcript-driven session 5 fixes ‚ÄĒ continuous-form activity + compound occupation + demonstrative math anaphora

**Driven by 2026-05-05 user dialog test session 5.** v4.51.5 closed the participle-form activity case; session 5 surfaced four new gaps that v4.52.0 closes:

1. **Continuous-form activity not captured** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę...–∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė **–∂–į—Ā–į–Ņ –∂–į—ā—č—Ä–ľ—č–Ĺ**¬Ľ encodes the user's activity in a converb + present-progressive auxiliary (¬ę–∂–į—Ā–į–Ņ –∂–į—ā—č—Ä–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ = "I am making"). v4.51.5's `ACTIVITY_VERBS` covered finite-1sg and aorist-participle forms but missed the continuous form. Result: activity not stored.
2. **Compound occupation in non-occupation primary intent** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–ľ—č–Ĺ ...¬Ľ fired `Intent::StatementOfName` (priority winner); the secondary scan rescued activity but never tried occupation. Result: `mamandńĪńüńĪm qandai?` follow-up couldn't recall occupation.
3. **AskActivity self-recall too narrow** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ **–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė –ī–į–ľ—č—ā–į—ā—č–Ĺ—č–ľ** –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ ("what am I developing?") used the ¬ę–ī–į–ľ—č—ā—É¬Ľ root + 1sg-poss participle form. Pre-v4.52.0 self-recall list only covered ¬ę—Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ*¬Ľ stem; missed ¬ę–ī–į–ľ—č—ā–į—ā—č–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—ā—č–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į–∑–į—ā—č–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ“Ī—Ä–į—Ā—ā—č—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ¬Ľ variants.
4. **Math anaphora limited to ¬ę–į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č¬Ľ** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ē–Ĺ–ī—Ė **–Ī“Ī–Ľ –Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė** 4-–ļ–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑¬Ľ used the demonstrative ¬ę–Ī“Ī–Ľ¬Ľ ("this result") instead of ¬ę–į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č¬Ľ ("previous"). v4.51.5 anaphora resolver didn't handle it.

### Real-REPL gap closure (session 5 transcript replay)

| Pre-v4.52.0 transcript query | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–ľ—č–Ĺ –∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ ”©–∑—Ė–ľ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė –∂–į—Ā–į–Ņ –∂–į—ā—č—Ä–ľ—č–Ĺ. | only `name` captured | name + occupation + activity all captured ‚úď |
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė –ī–į–ľ—č—ā–į—ā—č–Ĺ—č–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ? | clarify | "**–Ė–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė —Ė—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑ –ī–Ķ–Ņ –∂–į–ī—č–ľ–į —ā“Į—Ā—Ė—Ä–ī—Ė–ľ.**" ‚úď |
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č–ľ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ? | "–ú–Ķ–Ĺ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—É–≥–Ķ –∂–į—Ä–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ–ľ—č–Ĺ" (no slot, fallback) | "**–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė–Ľ—É—Ė–ľ—ą–Ķ, –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č“£—č–∑ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č.**" ‚úď |
| –ē–Ĺ–ī—Ė –Ī“Ī–Ľ –Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė 4-–ļ–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑. | math_refusal (anaphora unresolved) | **6** ‚úď (26 √∑ 4 truncated) |

### Innovations

**(1) Continuous-form activity detection** ‚ÄĒ new `CONTINUOUS_CONVERBS` array (8 stems: ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ņ / –∂–į—Ā–į–Ņ / –∂–į–∑—č–Ņ / –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ņ / “õ“Ī—Ä–į—Ā—ā—č—Ä—č–Ņ / –į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č—Ā—č–Ņ / –ī–į–ľ—č—ā—č–Ņ / “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ņ). Bigram match: when a converb token is found AND the next token starts with ¬ę–∂–į—ā—č—Ä¬Ľ (covers ¬ę–∂–į—ā—č—Ä–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ 1sg / ¬ę–∂–į—ā—č—Ä–ľ—č–∑¬Ľ 1pl), treat the converb as the verb position; walk back to extract object.

**(2) Two new activity-verb roots** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ī–į–ľ—č—ā—É¬Ľ (develop) and ¬ę“õ“Ī—Ä—É¬Ľ (build/establish). 4 surface forms added to `ACTIVITY_VERBS`: –ī–į–ľ—č—ā–į–ľ—č–Ĺ / “õ“Ī—Ä–į–ľ—č–Ĺ (finite) + –ī–į–ľ—č—ā–į—ā—č–Ĺ / “õ“Ī—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ (participle).

**(3) `detect_occupation_in_compound(input, lexicon)`** ‚ÄĒ new pure function in `semantics.rs`. Mirrors `detect_activity_in_compound`. Tokenises the lowered input, calls `strip_copula_and_lookup_noun` against the lexicon for the priority path, falls back to a 15-entry fixed-table lookup when no lexicon is provided. Returns `Some(Some(root))` when a noun + 1sg-copula form is identifiable.

**(4) `Conversation::turn` secondary occupation scan** ‚ÄĒ runs after the primary intent absorption + activity scan. When the primary intent is NOT `StatementOfOccupation` AND the session doesn't already have an `occupation` slot, calls `detect_occupation_in_compound`. Captures occupation into session + belief graph. Lets a single utterance like ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ X, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ Y-–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ populate both `name` AND `occupation` slots.

**(5) AskActivity self-recall extended** ‚ÄĒ `detect_ask_activity` now treats any ¬ę–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£¬Ľ + activity-participle stem as 1sg self-recall. New stems added: –ī–į–ľ—č—ā–į—ā—č–Ĺ / ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ / –∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—ā—č–Ĺ / –∂–į–∑–į—ā—č–Ĺ / –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ / “õ“Ī—Ä–į—Ā—ā—č—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ. Pre-v4.52.0 only ¬ę—Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ was matched.

**(6) Demonstrative math anaphora ¬ę–Ī“Ī–Ľ –Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ¬Ľ** ‚ÄĒ `Conversation::turn` math anaphora resolver gains a third anaphora family alongside ¬ę–į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č¬Ľ / ¬ę—Ā–ĺ“£“ď—č¬Ľ: ¬ę–Ī“Ī–Ľ (–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É )?–Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ¬Ľ with full case-suffix preservation (Acc / Dat). Verified in transcript: ¬ę–ē–Ĺ–ī—Ė –Ī“Ī–Ľ –Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė 4-–ļ–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑¬Ľ now resolves to the prior result.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **969 passing** unchanged |
| Adam-dialog lib | **301 passing** unchanged |
| Live REPL transcript replay | ‚úď all 4 session-5 scenarios verified end-to-end |
| Foundation: 2089 / 2349 / 46 / 27175 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo build --release` | clean |

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ adds new public function (`detect_occupation_in_compound`), new architectural primitive (compound-clause occupation rescue mirroring v4.51.0 activity rescue), and 4 distinct detector innovations. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: ¬ęminor x.y.0 = significant capability or milestone¬Ľ. Closes 4 concrete session-5 transcript gaps + materially improves dialog continuity (single-utterance multi-slot capture is the v4.x context-aware-dialog directive).

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.51.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-05 ‚ÄĒ Transcript-driven session 4 fixes ‚ÄĒ participle activity + math anaphora + vowel-initial respect form

**Driven by 2026-05-04 user dialog test session 4.** v4.51.0 closed the compound-clause activity case; session 4 surfaced three new gaps that v4.51.5 closes:

1. **Activity participle form not captured** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ ”©–∑—Ė–ľ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė **”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ** –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ encodes the user's activity in an aorist-participle modifier (-–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ suffix) rather than a finite 1sg verb. v4.51.0's `detect_statement_of_activity` only matched 6 finite stems (¬ę”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ etc.) and missed all participle forms. Result: activity not stored.
2. **Math anaphora unresolved** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–Ď–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė —ā”©—Ä—ā–ļ–Ķ –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ņ, –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑¬Ľ ‚Üí 10. Follow-up ¬ę–ē–Ĺ–ī—Ė **–į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É –Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ** –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑¬Ľ needs the previous result substituted. Pre-v4.51.5: math evaluator received the literal string and refused.
3. **Vowel-initial respect form** ‚ÄĒ `kazakh_respectful_address` returned None for vowel-initial names like ¬ę–ź—Ä–ľ–į–Ĺ¬Ľ, forcing planner fallback to the instrumental template. Per cultural rule (older addressee, vowel-initial root): take the first two letters and append ¬ę”ô–ļ–Ķ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ –ź—Ä–ľ–į–Ĺ ‚Üí –ź—Ä”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ź–Ľ–ł—Ź ‚Üí –ź–Ľ”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ź–Ļ–≥“Į–Ľ ‚Üí –ź–Ļ”ô–ļ–Ķ.

### Real-REPL gap closure (session 4 transcript replay)

| Pre-v4.51.5 transcript query | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| –ė”ô, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –°–Ķ—Ä–≥–Ķ–Ļ. | "–°–Ķ—Ä–≥–Ķ–Ļ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā–į–Ĺ—č—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ—č–ľ–į “õ—É–į–Ĺ—č—ą—ā—č–ľ—č–Ĺ" (instrumental fallback) | "**–°”ô–ļ–Ķ, —ā–į–Ĺ—č—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ—č–ľ–į “õ—É–į–Ĺ—č—ą—ā—č–ľ—č–Ĺ.**" ‚úď |
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ ”©–∑—Ė–ľ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–ľ—č–Ĺ. | only `occupation` captured | both captured: `occupation=–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č` AND `activity=–∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ` ‚úď |
| ”®—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė –Ī–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė —ā”©—Ä—ā–ļ–Ķ –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ņ, –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑. | 10 | 10 ‚úď (unchanged; baseline) |
| –ē–Ĺ–ī—Ė –į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É –Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑. | math_refusal (anaphora unresolved) | **20** ‚úď |
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ? | clarify | "**–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ, –į“ď—č–ľ–ī–į“ď—č —Ė—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑ ‚ÄĒ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ.**" ‚úď |

### Innovations

**(1) Activity participle detection** ‚ÄĒ extended `ACTIVITY_VERBS` array with 6 aorist-participle forms: ¬ę”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—ā—č–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–∂–į–∑–į—ā—č–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č—Ā–į—ā—č–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ“Ī—Ä–į—Ā—ā—č—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ¬Ľ. Same backward-walk object extraction; added boundary tokens ¬ę”©–∑—Ė–ľ–Ĺ—Ė“£¬Ľ, ¬ę”©–∑—Ė“£–Ĺ—Ė“£¬Ľ, ¬ę”©–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£¬Ľ to the walk-back stop list (so reflexive-possessive prefixes don't eat the activity content).

**(2) Two-pass case-suffix stripping** ‚ÄĒ `strip_object_case_suffix` now strips Accusative, then Possessive (¬ę-—Ė–ľ / -—č–ľ / -—Ā—Ė / -—Ā—č¬Ľ), so ¬ę–ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė¬Ľ (model-1sg.poss-acc) decomposes to ¬ę–ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć¬Ľ (head noun, lemma form preserved as ¬ę–ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ¬Ľ after suffix peel).

**(3) Same-clause participle path in compound parser** ‚ÄĒ v4.51.0 `detect_activity_in_compound` only fired on ¬ę–∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ¬Ľ. v4.51.5 adds a no-¬ę–∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ¬Ľ path: when activity is encoded in same-clause participle modifier (¬ęX-–ī—č Y-–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ Z-–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ), the detector runs on the full input.

**(4) `Intent::AskActivity` extended for ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ľ¬Ľ / ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė¬Ľ** ‚ÄĒ adds 1sg participle-self-recall variants alongside the existing ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ finite form.

**(5) `kazakh_respectful_address` vowel-initial branch** ‚ÄĒ when first character is a vowel (–į/”ô/–ĺ/”©/—É/“Ī/—č/—Ė/–Ķ/—ć/—é/—Ź/—Ď), uses first 2 chars + ¬ę”ô–ļ–Ķ¬Ľ. Returns None only when name < 3 chars or 2nd char non-alphabetic. All 11 Kazakh vowel-initial names verified: –ź–Ī–į–Ļ‚Üí–ź–Ī”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ź–Ľ–ł—Ź‚Üí–ź–Ľ”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ź–Ļ–≥“Į–Ľ‚Üí–ź–Ļ”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ź–ľ–į–Ĺ‚Üí–ź–ľ”ô–ļ–Ķ, ”ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ‚Üí”ė–Ľ”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ē—Ä–ľ–Ķ–ļ‚Üí–ē—Ä”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ę—Ā“õ–į“õ‚Üí–ę—Ā”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ě–Ľ–∂–į—Ā‚Üí–ě–Ľ”ô–ļ–Ķ, “ģ—Ā–Ķ–Ĺ‚Üí“ģ—Ā”ô–ļ–Ķ.

**(6) Math anaphora resolution** ‚ÄĒ `Conversation::turn` adds a `last_math_result` session slot populated after each successful word-math evaluation. Before the next math call, the input is rewritten: ¬ę–į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č (–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É )?–Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė¬Ľ / ¬ę–į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č (–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É )?–Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ / ¬ę–į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č (–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É )?–Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ¬Ľ / ¬ę–į–Ľ–ī—č“£“ď—č (–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É )?–Ĺ”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ¬Ľ ‚Üí digit-form with case-suffix preserved (¬ę10-–Ĺ—Ė¬Ľ Acc / ¬ę10-–≥–Ķ¬Ľ Dat / ¬ę10-–ī–Ķ¬Ľ Loc), so the digit-prefix-Acc parser (v4.41.7) reads it correctly.

**(7) New `statement_of_name` variant** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė {name_respect} –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į—ā–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ, “õ–ĺ—ą –ļ–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė“£—Ė–∑.¬Ľ Reachable when `{name_respect_distinct}` is set (now true for vowel-initial names too via innovation 5).

### Verification

| Adam-dialog lib | **301 passing** unchanged |
| Live REPL transcript replay | ‚úď all 5 session-4 scenarios verified |

| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

### Cadence

`.5` patch ‚ÄĒ incremental gap-closure on top of v4.51.0 architectural addition. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: ¬ępatch = small / incremental¬Ľ. No new Intent variants, no new template families, no new session schema; this release extends existing detectors (participle forms, vowel-initial respect, math anaphora resolver). Closes 3 concrete session-4 transcript gaps.

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition.

## [4.51.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ User-activity slot extraction (Stage A architectural addition)

**Closes the v4.50.5 carry-forward.** Adam now distinguishes the user's profession label (`occupation` ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č¬Ľ, ¬ę–ł–Ĺ–∂–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä¬Ľ) from their current-work content (`activity` ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā¬Ľ, ¬ę–≤–Ķ–Ī-—Ā–į–Ļ—ā –∂–į—Ā–į—É¬Ľ). Pre-v4.51.0 a compound input like ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–ľ—č–Ĺ –∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ captured only the occupation; v4.51.0 captures both via a secondary compound-clause scan. Adam can now recall activity on follow-up queries (¬ę–Ě–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė–∑?¬Ľ / ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ?¬Ľ).

### Real-REPL gap closure (user transcript replay)

| Pre-v4.51.0 transcript query | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č–ľ—č–Ĺ –∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ. | "–Ď–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ, —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ľ" (only occupation captured) | both captured: `occupation=–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č` AND `activity=–∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā` ‚úď |
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ľ –Ķ—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ľ–Ķ? | clarify ("—Ā“Ī—Ä–į“ď—č“£—č–∑–ī—č –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č–Ľ–į–Ņ‚Ä¶") | "**–Ė–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė —Ė—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑ –ī–Ķ–Ņ –∂–į–ī—č–ľ–į —ā“Į—Ā—Ė—Ä–ī—Ė–ľ.**" ‚úď |
| –Ě–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė–∑? | (didn't engage) | "**–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ, –į“ď—č–ľ–ī–į“ď—č —Ė—Ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑ ‚ÄĒ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā.**" ‚úď |

### Innovations

**(1) `Intent::StatementOfActivity { activity: Option<String> }`** ‚ÄĒ new variant. Distinct from `StatementOfOccupation` (profession label) in that activity captures the CURRENT-WORK-CONTENT.

**(2) `Intent::AskActivity`** ‚ÄĒ companion. Detected by patterns ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė–∑ / –Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė“£¬Ľ (¬ęwhat are you doing?¬Ľ), ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ņ –∂–į—ā—č—Ä—Ā—č–∑¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č—Ā*¬Ľ, ¬ę–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ —Ė—Ā—Ė–ľ / –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ľ¬Ľ (1sg self-recall).

**(3) `IntentKind::StatementOfActivity` + `IntentKind::AskActivity`** ‚ÄĒ companion enum variants for trace + history.

**(4) `detect_statement_of_activity(tokens, joined)`** ‚ÄĒ pure-function detector in `semantics.rs`. Recognizes 6 activity-verb stems (1sg-conjugated): ¬ę”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ / ¬ę–∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ / ¬ę–∂–į–∑–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ / ¬ę–∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ / ¬ę–į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č—Ā–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ / ¬ę“õ“Ī—Ä–į—Ā—ā—č—Ä–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ. Object extraction: walks backwards from the verb position, stops at clause boundaries (¬ę–∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ¬Ľ, ¬ę–į–Ľ¬Ľ, ¬ę—Ā–ĺ–ī–į–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ī—Ė—Ä–į“õ¬Ľ, ¬ę—Ā–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č“õ—ā–į–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ¬Ľ, ¬ę–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£¬Ľ) or commas, joins remaining tokens. Strips Accusative (¬ę-–ī—č/-–ī—Ė/-–Ĺ—č/-–Ĺ—Ė/-—ā—č/-—ā—Ė¬Ľ) / Comitative (¬ę-–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ/-–Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ/-–Ī–Ķ–Ĺ¬Ľ) case suffixes from the head noun. Falls back to ¬ę–∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ bare-verb pattern (returns `Some(None)`).

**(5) `detect_ask_activity(joined)`** ‚ÄĒ pure-function ask-detector. Recognizes 2nd-person query forms (¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ*¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ņ¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ņ¬Ľ, ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č—Ā*¬Ľ) gated against the ¬ę–ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ī¬Ľ fragment (so ¬ę–ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ī—Ė“£—Ė–∑ –Ĺ–Ķ?¬Ľ stays in `AskOccupation`). Plus 1sg self-recall: ¬ę–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ —Ė—Ā—Ė–ľ¬Ľ / ¬ę–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ľ¬Ľ / ¬ę–Ĺ–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ.

**(6) Compound-input parser `detect_activity_in_compound(input)`** ‚ÄĒ public-crate scan called by `Conversation::turn` AFTER primary intent absorption. When input contains ¬ę–∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ¬Ľ token, splits on it and runs `detect_statement_of_activity` on the post-¬ę–∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ¬Ľ clause. Captures activity into session even when the primary detector fired `StatementOfOccupation`. Closes the ¬ę–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č AND –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā¬Ľ compound case.

**(7) Session slot `activity`** ‚ÄĒ populated in `absorb_entities` (direct `StatementOfActivity` path) AND in the secondary compound scan. Mirrors the `occupation` slot machinery.

**(8) Belief-graph integration** ‚ÄĒ activity facts recorded via `belief.record_user_fact(USER_SELF_KEY, "activity", &activity, turn_id)`. Enables future audit-side queries.

**(9) 2 new template families**:
- `statement_of_activity` (5 variants) ‚ÄĒ acknowledges activity statements; includes `{name_respect}` for respect-form acks.
- `ask_activity` + `ask_activity.with_known_user` (3 variants each) ‚ÄĒ ask-without-data and slot-aware-recall variants. Mirrors `ask_occupation` family pattern.

**(10) Planner integration**:
- `intent_key`: 2 new mappings (`AskActivity` ‚Üí `"ask_activity"`, `StatementOfActivity { .. }` ‚Üí `"statement_of_activity"`).
- `extract_slots`: populates `{activity}` slot from `StatementOfActivity`.
- `plan_response_with_epistemic`: known-user override `(Intent::AskActivity, _) if session.contains_key("activity") ‚Üí "ask_activity.with_known_user"`. Mirrors v4.2.5 `AskOccupation` pattern.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **969 passing** unchanged |
| Adam-dialog lib | **301 passing** unchanged |
| Live REPL transcript replay | ‚úď all 4 scenarios verified (compound capture + 2 recall paths + –Ē”ô–ļ–Ķ-form) |
| Foundation: 2089 / 2349 / 46 / unchanged | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo check --all-targets` | clean |

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ Stage A architectural addition. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: ¬ęminor x.y.0 = significant capability or milestone¬Ľ. New Intent variant + new IntentKind + 2 detectors + compound parser + session-schema addition + 2 template families + planner overrides = kernel-signature dialog-memory expansion. Closes a concrete v4.50.5-transcript carry-forward.

Stage A status (post v4.51.0):
- v4.42.0‚Äďv4.43.6 ‚ÄĒ NLG foundation + production migration + introducer migration + 11/11 predicate coverage ‚úď

## [4.50.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Transcript-driven regression fixes ‚ÄĒ math truncation + respectful address + math vocabulary

**Driven by 2026-05-04 user dialog test session 3.** Three concrete gaps surfaced in the transcript:
1. Kazakh word-form math refused on simple non-integer cases (¬ę–Ď–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė –∂–Ķ—ā—Ė–≥–Ķ –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ņ, –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑¬Ľ = 35/2 ‚ÄĒ should answer 17, was math_refusal).
2. Digit-form `:` operator handled but produced `None` on intermediate non-integer step (¬ę5+5*2:4-3¬Ľ = 5+10/4-3 ‚ÄĒ fractional 2.5 caused refusal).
3. Adam addressed the user by literal name ¬ę–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā¬Ľ instead of Kazakh respectful form ¬ę–Ē”ô–ļ–Ķ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ culturally inconsistent with the user's training expectation.

Plus one missing knowledge gap: ¬ę–ö–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā “Į—ą–ľ“Į—ą–Ķ¬Ľ (quadratic trinomial) had no curated fact.

### Real-REPL gap closures

| Pre-v4.50.5 transcript query | Pre | Post |
|---|---|---|
| –Ď–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė –∂–Ķ—ā—Ė–≥–Ķ –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ņ, –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑. | math_refusal template | "**–Ě”ô—ā–ł–∂–Ķ—Ā—Ė: 17 (–ĺ–Ĺ –∂–Ķ—ā—Ė)**" ‚úď |
| 5+5*2:4-3 | math_refusal template | "**–ē—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ: 4 (—ā”©—Ä—ā)**" ‚úď |
| 5*7/2 | math_refusal template | "**–Ė–į—É–į–Ī—č ‚ÄĒ 17 (–ĺ–Ĺ –∂–Ķ—ā—Ė)**" ‚úď |
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā. | "–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā, —ā–į–Ĺ—č—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ—č–ľ–į “õ—É–į–Ĺ—č—ą—ā—č–ľ—č–Ĺ" (literal) | "**–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –į—ā—č“£—č–∑–ī—č –Ķ—Ā—ā–Ķ —Ā–į“õ—ā–į–Ņ, —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė –Ē”ô–ļ–Ķ –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į—ā–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į.**" (respect-form by default) |
| –ö–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā “Į—ą–ľ“Į—ą–Ķ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė–ľ—Ė–∑ –Ĺ–Ķ? | clarify | "**–ö–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā “Į—ą–ľ“Į—ą–Ķ ‚ÄĒ ax¬≤ + bx + c —ā“Į—Ä—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –Ķ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—ą—Ė –ī”ô—Ä–Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ—Ė “Į—ą –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ —ā“Ī—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ ”©—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ–ļ.**" ‚úď |

### Innovations

**(1) Truncated integer division** in `try_evaluate_arithmetic` ‚ÄĒ pre-v4.50.5 the digit-form evaluator returned `None` for non-integer divisions (e.g., `7/2`), forcing the math_refusal template. v4.50.5 truncates to integer (`7/2 ‚Üí 3`, `5*7/2 ‚Üí 17`) matching Kazakh-conversational expectations. Division by zero remains `None` (still invalid).

**(2) Truncated integer division** in `try_evaluate_kazakh_word_math` ‚ÄĒ mirrors fix (1) for the word-form evaluator. ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ¬Ľ now returns `Some(3)` (was `None`). Multi-clause word-form queries that previously hit a non-integer intermediate step now compute through.

**(3) Respect-form-by-default `statement_of_name` template family** ‚ÄĒ replaced the v4.18.0 4-template family (3 literal-name + 1 respect-aware) with a 4-template family where 3 of 4 variants gate on `{name_respect_distinct}`. For consonant-initial names (–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā ‚Üí –Ē”ô–ļ–Ķ, –ú“Ī—Ä–į—ā ‚Üí –ú”ô–ļ–Ķ, –°”ô–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ ‚Üí –°”ô–ļ–Ķ) all 4 are admissible and the planner cycles among them; ~75 % of seeds produce a respect-form acknowledgment. For vowel-initial names where the diminutive collapses (–ź–Ī–į–Ļ, –ź–Ľ–ł—Ź), only the instrumental fallback (¬ę–ź–Ī–į–Ļ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā–į–Ĺ—č—Ā“õ–į–Ĺ—č–ľ–į‚Ä¶¬Ľ) remains fillable ‚ÄĒ preserving v4.18.0 behavior exactly for those cases.

**(4) Math vocabulary depth** ‚ÄĒ 3 new facts in `mathematics_basic.jsonl`:
- math_053: ¬ę–ö–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā “Į—ą–ľ“Į—ą–Ķ ‚ÄĒ ax¬≤ + bx + c —ā“Į—Ä—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –Ķ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—ą—Ė –ī”ô—Ä–Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ—Ė “Į—ą –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ —ā“Ī—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ ”©—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ–ļ.¬Ľ
- math_054: ¬ę–ö”©–Ņ–ľ“Į—ą–Ķ ‚ÄĒ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ—ą–Ķ –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ “õ“Ī—Ä–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ ”©—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ–ļ.¬Ľ
- math_055: ¬ę“ģ—ą–ľ“Į—ą–Ķ ‚ÄĒ “Į—ą –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ “õ“Ī—Ä–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ ”©—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ–ļ.¬Ľ

**(5) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 1** ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–ļ–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā “Į—ą–ľ“Į—ą–Ķ¬Ľ registered (required by `world_core_multiword_coverage`).

**(6) Test updates**:
- `discourse::math_tests::word_math_division_non_integer_returns_none` renamed to `word_math_division_truncates_non_integer` and updated to assert `Some(3)` for ¬ę–Ė–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė –Ķ–ļ—Ė–≥–Ķ –Ī”©–Ľ¬Ľ and `None` only for division-by-zero.
- `discourse::arithmetic_tests::handles_division_zero_and_remainder` updated: `7/2 ‚Üí Some(3)`, `10/4 ‚Üí Some(2)`, `5*7/2 ‚Üí Some(17)` plus the existing `5/0 ‚Üí None`.
- `tests/end_to_end.rs::response_statement_of_name_substitutes_slot` updated to reflect the new 4-template family shape (instrumental fallback + 3 respect-form variants).

**(7) NOT YET in v4.50.5** (separate carry-forward):
- User-activity slot extraction (¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ X-–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č—Ā–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ / ¬ęX ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ) ‚ÄĒ currently adam captures `occupation=–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č` but loses the activity context (¬ę–∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ). Architectural addition (new Intent variant + session schema change + recall handler); deferred to v4.51.0+.

## [4.50.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B completion ‚ÄĒ REPL-derived pairs + trained_v0 + audit baseline shift

**Stage B completion milestone** ‚ÄĒ three carry-forward bundles consolidated into one minor:
- **Bundle 9**: REPL-derived training pairs hand-curated from v4.48.5 first-run patterns.
- **Bundle 10**: First trained `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant via `OnceLock`-cached `train_perceptron`.
- **Bundle 11**: Audit baseline shift ‚ÄĒ `tool::search_graph` now calls `trained_v0()` instead of `default_v0()` so audit lines reflect the trained-vs-heuristic divergence, the meaningful Stage B signal.

Live REPL byte-identical (audit is trace-only ‚ÄĒ heuristic still wins `findings[0]`). Production ranker replacement (actually using `select_top` for `findings[0]`) deferred to v4.50.5+ once trained weights demonstrate match-or-beat heuristic on the full eval suite.

### Harvest-test signal change

Pre-v4.50.0 (`default_v0` audit baseline):
```
disagreement_traces:
  [0] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=2 score_gap=0.0600
  [1] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=3 score_gap=0.0420
```

Post-v4.50.0 (`trained_v0` audit baseline):
```
disagreement_traces:
  [0] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=2 score_gap=0.2600
  [1] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=3 score_gap=0.1820
```

**Same 2 disagreement cases, ~4√ó larger score gaps.** The trained selector is more confident the heuristic is wrong on these cases ‚ÄĒ exactly the signal v4.50.5+ production replacement will act on.

### Innovations

**(1) `repl_derived_training_pairs_v0() -> Vec<TrainingPair>`** ‚ÄĒ 8 hand-curated close-call pairs mirroring the v4.48.5 first-run patterns (small score gaps where confidence / subject-overlap close calls matter):
- HumanApproved with slightly less subject overlap beats RuleInferred with slightly more.
- HumanApproved + recency beats CuratedQuote without recency at equal overlap.
- Subject overlap + slight richness beats slightly higher object overlap.
- Rich raw_text + matching subject beats grammar with everything else.
- Small recency advantage decisive when other features tied.
- Object overlap matters when subject is tied (secondary tier).
- HumanApproved with mid signals beats high-richness grammar.
- Combined subject+object overlap with HumanApproved beats raw confidence alone (multi-signal).

**(2) `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant** via `OnceLock`-cached `train_perceptron`. Trains `default_v0()` on `canonical_training_pairs_v0() + repl_derived_training_pairs_v0()` (15 + 8 = 23 pairs total) with `TrainingConfig::default_v0()`. Result cached so subsequent calls are O(1). Trained model still occupies the same 24 bytes as the hand-set defaults ‚ÄĒ the thesis-stated "tiny inspectable weights" promise holds after training.

**(3) Audit baseline shift in `tool::search_graph`** ‚ÄĒ `crate::selection::default_v0()` ‚Üí `crate::selection::trained_v0()` at the audit-compare call site. Live REPL byte-identical (audit is trace-only). The `selection_audit:` lines now reflect trained-vs-heuristic divergence ‚ÄĒ the meaningful Stage B signal that future production-replacement bundles will act on.

**(4) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `repl_derived_training_pairs_v0`, `trained_v0` available as top-level public API.

**(5) 8 new unit tests** verify:
- `repl_derived_pairs_non_empty_and_well_formed` ‚ÄĒ at least 6 pairs, every feature ‚ąą [0, 1], positives differ from negatives.
- `repl_derived_pairs_deterministic` ‚ÄĒ same call returns same pairs.
- `trained_v0_is_deterministic` ‚ÄĒ `OnceLock` caching contract.
- `trained_v0_satisfies_canonical_pairs` ‚ÄĒ zero disagreements on canonical set.
- `trained_v0_satisfies_repl_derived_pairs` ‚ÄĒ zero disagreements on REPL set.
- `trained_v0_satisfies_combined_pairs` ‚ÄĒ zero disagreements on full 23-pair training corpus.
- `trained_v0_weights_remain_inspectable` ‚ÄĒ 24 bytes (6 f32), all finite.
- `trained_v0_differs_from_default_v0` ‚ÄĒ training actually shifted weights.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.50.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Production ranker replacement ‚ÄĒ wire `selection::select_top(matches, trained_v0(), ...)` to override `findings[0]` in `tool::search_graph`. Risk: not all heuristic tiers are captured by the v0 features (predicate-rank cascade, list-intent boost, etc.); needs careful eval-suite validation before flipping. v4.50.5+ target.
- Hand-curation of the 2 specific harvested disagreements into named TrainingPair entries (requires inspecting which queries produced them; deferred for tighter scope here).
- Harvest from `live_holdout_2026_05_02.json` and `rust_holdout.json` integration tests.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **969 passing** (was 961; +8 new trained tests) |
| Adam-dialog lib | **301 passing** (was 293; +8) |
| Selection module | **56/56** tests pass (13 v4.45.0 + 11 v4.45.5 + 10 v4.46.0 + 7 v4.47.5 + 7 v4.48.0 + 8 v4.50.0) |

| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

### Cadence

`.0` minor ‚ÄĒ Stage B completion milestone consolidating bundles 9, 10, 11. The substrate-side of Stage B is now fully closed: every primitive from "raw REPL traces" ‚Üí "trained, deployable weight table" ‚Üí "audit baseline visible in production trace" exists as typed public API. The remaining carry-forward (production ranker replacement) is risk-managed: eval-suite validation must confirm trained weights match-or-beat heuristic on the full canonical regression bank before flipping.

## [4.48.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B bundle 8 ‚ÄĒ test-harness integration: first real disagreements harvested

**First time the Stage B substrate runs against real eval data.** New `tests/selection_audit_harvest.rs` runs every query in `data/eval/live_holdout_2026_05_01.json` (32 cases) through a production-shaped `Conversation::turn_with_trace`, collects every `ToolResult.trace` line emitted across all turns, flattens into `Vec<Vec<String>>`, and feeds it to `harvest_audit_traces` (v4.48.0). The resulting `HarvestReport` is printed to stderr for offline operators.

### First-run results (live_holdout_2026_05_01)

```
total_turns:           32
multi_candidate_turns: 2 (6.2% of total)
audit_lines_found:     2
disagreement_count:    2
disagreement_rate:     1.0000
disagreement_traces:
  [0] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=2 score_gap=0.0600
  [1] selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top=3 score_gap=0.0420
```

**Two real disagreements surfaced** ‚ÄĒ both with small score gaps (0.06 / 0.04), confirming v0 weights mostly agree with the heuristic ranker but flagging two candidate gold pairs for hand-curation. These are the first concrete training data points harvested from the audit pipeline.

### Innovations

**(1) New `tests/selection_audit_harvest.rs` integration test** ‚ÄĒ production-shaped harness:
- Loads `live_holdout_2026_05_01.json` (32 cases).
- For each case, builds a `Conversation` with full runtime (lexicon, morpheme index, extracted+derived facts, suffix priors, root affinity, domain index).
- Calls `turn_with_trace` per case; flattens `tool_calls[*].trace + plan_trace` into a per-case `Vec<String>`.
- Feeds the batch to `harvest_audit_traces`.
- Prints `HarvestReport` via `eprintln!` (CI logs).
- Asserts only internal consistency (`total_turns == cases.len()`, `disagreement_count <= audit_lines_found`, `multi_candidate_turns <= total_turns`).

**(2) Diagnostic-not-gating** ‚ÄĒ no assertion on disagreement count or rate. The v4.46.5 wiring with `default_v0()` weights is calibrated to match the v4.38.0 heuristic ranker, so disagreements should be rare. This test surfaces what the audit sees and reports it; future patches that re-train the weights will start reporting different disagreement counts that operators can act on.

**(3) Skip semantics** ‚ÄĒ when runtime artifacts (`facts.json`, `morpheme_index.json`, etc.) are absent (trimmed CI checkouts, external crate consumers), the test skips silently rather than panic. Same convention as `tests/live_holdout.rs`.

**(4) First concrete disagreement data points** ‚ÄĒ the two disagreements with `score_gap=0.0600` and `score_gap=0.0420` are now visible in CI logs for every release. v4.48.6+ patches can hand-curate them into `TrainingPair` entries by inspecting which queries emitted them and which candidates the selector preferred.

**(5) NOT YET in v4.48.5** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Harvest from `live_holdout_2026_05_02.json` and `rust_holdout.json` (similar test scaffold; deferred for tighter scope).
- Hand-curation of the 2 found disagreements into `TrainingPair` entries ‚ÄĒ requires inspecting the specific queries + candidate facts behind each line, which is operator work.
- First trained `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant ‚ÄĒ once curated pairs exist.
- Production ranker replacement.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **961 passing** (was 960; +1 new harvest integration test) |
| Adam-dialog integration test | `selection_audit_harvest_live_holdout_2026_05_01`: 1/1 pass |
| Disagreement cases harvested | **2 real disagreements found** in live_holdout_2026_05_01 (6.2 % of 32 turns) |
| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.48.0 (audit is trace-only) |
| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

### Cadence

`.5` reflects: (1) integration test scaffold + (2) report printing + (3) diagnostic-not-gating policy + (4) skip-semantics for trimmed checkouts + (5) **first real disagreement data points harvested** = 4 distinct innovations + Stage B's first end-to-end pipeline run on real data.
