## [4.48.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B bundle 7 ‚ÄĒ REPL-trace harvest substrate

**Pure-function trace-line parser.** `HarvestReport` aggregates `selection_audit:` lines extracted from a batch of `Conversation::turn_with_trace` traces. `harvest_audit_traces(&[Vec<String>]) -> HarvestReport` is the parser that walks each trace and counts how many turns produced audit lines, how many of those were disagreements, and keeps the raw disagreement lines for hand-curation into new `TrainingPair` entries.

### Pipeline (offline, periodic ‚ÄĒ operator workflow)

1. Run a Conversation programmatically on each query in `live_holdout_*.json` / `rust_holdout.json`, capturing the `TurnTrace` per turn.
2. Pass `&[Vec<String>]` (one Vec per trace) to `harvest_audit_traces`.
3. Inspect the resulting `HarvestReport.disagreement_traces`; every line is a candidate for a new gold pair.
4. Hand-curate the most-leveraged disagreements into TrainingPairs ‚Üí re-train via `train_perceptron` ‚Üí ship as the new `SelectionWeights::trained_v0` constant.

The integration with real Conversation runs is left to per-eval test harnesses (`tests/live_holdout.rs` etc.) which already wire the runtime; v4.48.0 ships the parser only.

### Innovations

**(1) `HarvestReport` struct** ‚ÄĒ `{ total_turns, multi_candidate_turns, audit_lines_found, disagreement_count, disagreement_traces: Vec<String> }`. `disagreement_traces` preserves raw disagreement lines verbatim so the offline curator can read score gaps and pick gold pairs without re-running the pipeline.

**(2) `HarvestReport::disagreement_rate()` accessor** ‚ÄĒ fraction of multi-candidate turns where the trained selector disagreed with the heuristic. `0.0` when no audit ever ran (no multi-candidate turns); useful as an "audit divergence" KPI per release.

**(3) `harvest_audit_traces(traces) -> HarvestReport`** ‚ÄĒ pure function. Walks each trace; for each line, prefix-matches `"selection_audit:"`; counts agreement-vs-disagreement via substring `"disagreement"` presence; preserves raw disagreement lines; counts multi-candidate turns (turns with at least one audit line) separately from total audit lines (one turn can in principle emit multiple).

**(4) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `HarvestReport`, `harvest_audit_traces` available as top-level public API.

**(5) 7 new unit tests** verify:
- `harvest_empty_input_returns_zero_report` ‚ÄĒ degenerate input + vacuous-truth `disagreement_rate()`.
- `harvest_traces_without_audit_lines_only_count_total_turns` ‚ÄĒ non-audit lines ignored.
- `harvest_disagreement_line_recorded_with_full_text` ‚ÄĒ disagreement line preserved verbatim with score gap.
- `harvest_agreement_line_counted_but_not_recorded_as_disagreement` ‚ÄĒ agreement variant handled (for future-proofing, since v4.46.5 wiring is silent on agreement).
- `harvest_many_turns_aggregate_correctly` ‚ÄĒ `multi_candidate_turns` counts turns (not lines); `disagreement_count` counts disagreement lines specifically; mixed input verified.
- `harvest_multiple_audit_lines_in_one_turn_count_separately` ‚ÄĒ edge case (current wiring emits at most 1, parser is shape-agnostic).
- `harvest_ignores_lines_that_dont_start_with_selection_audit` ‚ÄĒ prefix-match (not substring); robust against false positives.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.48.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Test-harness integration ‚ÄĒ extend `tests/live_holdout.rs` etc. to capture `Conversation::turn_with_trace` results and call `harvest_audit_traces` for offline disagreement collection. v4.48.5 target.
- First trained `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant ‚ÄĒ train on hand-curated disagreement-derived gold pairs once they're harvested.
- Production ranker replacement ‚ÄĒ replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top` once trained weights match or beat heuristic.

## [4.47.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B bundle 6 ‚ÄĒ disagreement-case collection harness

**Pure-function aggregate substrate.** `AuditAggregate` accumulates audit results across many gold pairs; `evaluate_weights_on_pairs(weights, pairs) -> AuditAggregate` runs the audit comparison for each pair and reports overall agreement / disagreement statistics + worst-case score gap. Together they answer: ¬ęgiven this `SelectionWeights` table and this gold-pair set, how many pairs does the selector get wrong, and how badly?¬Ľ This is the substrate for the v4.50.0 (target) decision: trained weights replace the heuristic ranker only when `evaluate_weights_on_pairs(trained, canonical_training_pairs_v0()).disagreements == 0` AND the trained weights match or beat the heuristic on live REPL holdouts.

### Innovations

**(1) `AuditAggregate` struct** ‚ÄĒ `{ total_pairs, agreements, disagreements, max_neg_score_advantage }`. `agreements` counts pairs where `score(positive) > score(negative)`; `disagreements` counts pairs where `score(positive) <= score(negative)` (ties count as disagreement ‚ÄĒ gold label requires strict ordering). `max_neg_score_advantage` tracks the worst-case "how badly the selector got it wrong" ‚ÄĒ useful for deciding how aggressive a re-training step needs to be.

**(2) `AuditAggregate::agreement_rate()`** ‚ÄĒ fraction of total pairs that agreed. Vacuous truth: empty aggregate returns `1.0`.

**(3) `evaluate_weights_on_pairs(weights, pairs) -> AuditAggregate`** ‚ÄĒ pure function. For each `TrainingPair`, computes `score(positive, weights)` and `score(negative, weights)`, records strict-ordering agreement, accumulates max gap on disagreements.

**(4) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `AuditAggregate`, `evaluate_weights_on_pairs` available as top-level public API.

**(5) 7 new unit tests** verify:
- `aggregate_default_is_empty` ‚ÄĒ zero-init + vacuous-truth `agreement_rate()`.
- `evaluate_default_v0_on_canonical_pairs_high_agreement` ‚ÄĒ `default_v0` weights handle ‚Č• 80 % of canonical pairs without training (baseline regression pin).
- `evaluate_trained_weights_on_canonical_pairs_zero_disagreements` ‚ÄĒ trained weights satisfy every canonical pair (training contract from v4.45.5 holds).
- `evaluate_zero_weights_produces_disagreements` ‚ÄĒ all-zero weights tie everywhere ‚Üí disagreement on every pair.
- `evaluate_negative_weights_inverts_ranking` ‚ÄĒ inverted weights flip every canonical pair.
- `evaluate_records_max_neg_score_advantage_correctly` ‚ÄĒ aggregate tracks max worst-case gap.
- `evaluate_empty_pairs_returns_empty_aggregate` ‚ÄĒ degenerate input.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.47.5** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Real REPL-trace parsing harness ‚ÄĒ run `live_holdout_*.json` / `rust_holdout.json` through audit-trace pipeline ‚Üí parse `selection_audit:` lines ‚Üí hand-curate disagreement cases as new `TrainingPair`s. Different infrastructure (binary + log-parser + JSON output); deferred to v4.48.0+.
- First trained `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant ‚ÄĒ once a real disagreement-case set exists, train and ship as new audit baseline.
- Production ranker replacement ‚ÄĒ replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top` once trained weights match or beat heuristic.

## [4.47.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B bundle 5 ‚ÄĒ `ToolContext.last_topic` threading

**Closes the v4.46.5 carry-forward.** The audit-only wiring shipped in v4.46.5 had a known limitation: it passed `last_topic = None` to `audit_compare`, so the recency-match feature always scored 0 for both heuristic and selector. v4.47.0 threads `Conversation::session["last_query_topic"]` through `ToolContext` to `selection::audit_compare` so the recency feature now contributes signal on real session data. Real disagreement-case collection (running offline evals through the audit-trace pipeline) remains carry-forward to v4.47.5+.

### Innovations

**(1) `ToolContext.last_topic: Option<&'a str>` field** ‚ÄĒ added at the bottom of the struct alongside `previous_grounded_fact` and `root_affinity`. `None` on the first turn or when no topic was resolved; `Some(&str)` to the prior turn's topic root otherwise. Documented as ¬ęthreaded through to `selection::audit_compare` so the recency-match feature fires with real session data instead of always scoring 0¬Ľ.

**(2) `Conversation::turn` populates** `tool_ctx.last_topic` from `self.session.get("last_query_topic")` (the slot already maintained by v4.13.0+ `DialogContext` / `Conversation` after every grounded turn). Strictly additive ‚ÄĒ `None` when the slot isn't set, preserving v4.46.5 behavior bit-for-bit.

**(3) `audit_compare` call site** in `tool::search_graph` updated from hard-coded `None` to `ctx.last_topic`. The recency-match feature now contributes `1.0` √ó `w_recency` (= 0.5 with `default_v0()`) when the candidate fact's subject matches the prior turn's topic, and `0.0` otherwise. This is the first feature that produces session-conditional audit signal.

**(4) ToolContext construction sites updated** ‚ÄĒ 5 sites: 1 in `conversation.rs` (production), 1 in `action.rs` (capability lookup), 3 in `tool.rs` test fixtures. All non-production sites pass `last_topic: None` (no recency context in test fixtures by default; existing tests don't depend on it).

**(5) NOT YET in v4.47.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Real disagreement-case collection ‚ÄĒ run `live_holdout_*.json`, `rust_holdout.json`, etc. through the production pipeline with audit-trace enabled; parse trace for `selection_audit: disagreement` lines; hand-curate found cases as new `TrainingPair` entries. v4.47.5 target.
- Trained-weights export ‚ÄĒ once v4.47.5 produces real gold pairs, train a `SelectionWeights::trained_v0()` constant via `train_perceptron` and ship as the new audit baseline.
- Production ranker replacement ‚ÄĒ replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top` once trained weights match or beat heuristic on canonical evals.

## [4.46.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B bundle 4 ‚ÄĒ production trace-wiring (audit-only)

**First production touch in the Stage B arc.** v4.45.0‚Äďv4.46.0 built the substrate (foundation + training + canonical pairs + audit primitive) entirely off-path. v4.46.5 wires the audit primitive into the live `tool::search_graph` dispatch ‚ÄĒ when there are 2+ admissible candidates, run `selection::audit_compare` against the heuristic top-1 and push a trace line if the trained selector disagrees. **No behavioral change** ‚ÄĒ the heuristic still wins; the trace is read by `--trace` consumers (`adam_chat --trace`, the regression bank) to surface where the selector would diverge.

### Architectural significance

This is the first time selection-weights infrastructure runs in production on real corpus facts. With `default_v0()` weights ‚ÄĒ hand-set to approximate the v4.38.0 ranker tiers ‚ÄĒ disagreement should be rare. Each disagreement that DOES surface is a candidate for the v4.46.5+ training corpus: cases where heuristic and selector diverge are exactly where labelled gold pairs would teach the selector to match (or beat) the heuristic.

### Innovations

**(1) Audit-only wiring in `tool::search_graph`** ‚ÄĒ after the heuristic `matches.sort_by(...)` settles, when admissible candidates count is ‚Č• 2, build a `Vec<&Fact>` of admissibles, call `audit_compare(refs, 0, default_v0(), &query_tokens, None)`, and push `"selection_audit: disagreement heuristic_top=0 selector_top={N} score_gap={X}"` to the trace if disagreement. Agreement case is silent (no noise on the common path).

**(2) Trace-only ‚ÄĒ no behavioral change** ‚ÄĒ the heuristic-ranker top-1 still becomes `findings[0]`; the audit only writes into `trace`. The whole audit block is inside an `if matches.len() >= 2` guard so single-candidate queries (the common case for narrow factoids) skip the work entirely. Live REPL byte-identical to v4.46.0.

**(3) `query_tokens` propagation** ‚ÄĒ feature extraction needs the query's lowered tokens for `subject_overlap` / `object_overlap`. The SearchGraph dispatch already builds `query_tokens` for the v4.38.0 list-aware ranker; we reuse that vec for the audit (no new infrastructure).

**(4) `last_topic` deferred to None** ‚ÄĒ recency-match feature requires session's `last_query_topic` which lives in `Conversation::session`, not `ToolContext`. Threading it through is a v4.47.0+ refinement; v4.46.5 ships with `last_topic = None` (recency feature scores 0 for both candidates ‚Üí tier collapses to a no-op).

|---|---|

| Adam-dialog lib | 279 passing unchanged |
| Selection module | 34/34 tests pass (no new tests in v4.46.5; audit substrate already covered in v4.46.0) |
| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.46.0 (audit lines are trace-only) |
| Foundation: 2086 / 2346 / 46 / 27 163 | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo check --all-targets` | clean |

## [4.46.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B bundle 3 ‚ÄĒ canonical gold pairs + audit substrate

**Stage B bundle 3.** Two architectural primitives that close the gap between the v4.45.5 training algorithm and the future v4.46.5+ production wiring:

1. **`canonical_training_pairs_v0()`** ‚ÄĒ 15 hand-curated `(positive, negative)` gold pairs covering every dimension of the v0 feature set + the disambiguation scenarios that the v4.38.0 SearchGraph ranker tiers handle today. Pure deterministic constant. Train `default_v0()` on this set with `train_perceptron` and the resulting weights *by construction* satisfy every canonical scenario.

2. **`AuditResult` + `audit_compare()`** ‚ÄĒ pure-function side-by-side comparison of "what the heuristic ranker picked" vs "what the trained selector would pick" given the same candidate list. Returns the disagreement boolean + score gap so future trace logs can surface mismatches without changing live behavior.

**No production wiring in v4.46.0** ‚ÄĒ the audit substrate is pure infrastructure for v4.46.5+ trace integration. Live REPL byte-identical to v4.45.5.

### Innovations

**(1) `canonical_training_pairs_v0() -> Vec<TrainingPair>`** ‚ÄĒ 15 gold pairs covering 5 dimensions:
- **Confidence axis** (3 pairs): HumanApproved beats Grammar / RuleInferred; CuratedQuote beats Grammar.
- **Subject-overlap axis** (3 pairs): the dominant v4.38.0 ranker tier.
- **Object-overlap axis** (1 pair): secondary tier.
- **Recency axis** (2 pairs): tiebreaker; doesn't override confidence.
- **Richness axis** (1 pair): soft tier.
- **Multi-signal interactions** (5 pairs): HumanApproved + matching subject beats Grammar + non-matching; curated rich text + recency dominates; subject + object overlap beats neither; etc.

Each pair documents inline the rationale and which v4.38.0 ranker tier it mirrors.

**(2) `AuditResult` struct** ‚ÄĒ `{ heuristic_top_idx, selector_top_idx, disagreement: bool, score_gap: f32 }`. `score_gap` is `score(selector_top) ‚ąí score(heuristic_top)`, useful for trace logs to quantify how strongly the selector disagrees.

**(3) `audit_compare(candidates, heuristic_top_idx, weights, query_tokens, last_topic) -> Option<AuditResult>`** ‚ÄĒ pure function that runs the selector, identifies its top-1, and reports disagreement vs the heuristic. Returns `None` for empty candidates or out-of-bounds index. Stable-tie-break matches `select_top` semantics (first index wins on ties).

**(4) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `AuditResult`, `audit_compare`, `canonical_training_pairs_v0` available as top-level public API.

**(5) 10 new unit tests** verify:
- `canonical_pairs_v0_is_non_empty_and_deterministic` ‚ÄĒ at least 15 pairs, deterministic, every pair's positive ‚Č† negative.
- `canonical_pairs_v0_each_pair_well_formed` ‚ÄĒ every feature value in `[0, 1]`.
- `training_on_canonical_pairs_v0_converges` ‚ÄĒ perceptron converges within budget on the canonical set; trained weights have positive confidence + subject_overlap.
- `trained_weights_satisfy_every_canonical_pair` ‚ÄĒ post-training, every pair satisfies `score(positive) ‚Č• score(negative) + margin`.
- `audit_compare_reports_agreement_when_heuristic_matches_selector` ‚ÄĒ agreement case.
- `audit_compare_reports_disagreement_with_score_gap` ‚ÄĒ disagreement case with positive score gap.
- `audit_compare_empty_candidates_returns_none` ‚ÄĒ degenerate input.
- `audit_compare_out_of_bounds_idx_returns_none` ‚ÄĒ invalid heuristic index.
- `audit_compare_single_candidate_always_agrees` ‚ÄĒ trivial case.
- `audit_compare_ties_resolve_by_first_index` ‚ÄĒ stable-tie-break edge case.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.46.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Production trace-wiring ‚ÄĒ wire `audit_compare` into `tool::search_graph` result path so disagreements appear in `--trace` output.
- Real REPL-transcript-derived gold pairs ‚ÄĒ extract pairs from `live_holdout_2026_05_01.json` / `live_holdout_2026_05_02.json` etc.
- Production ranker replacement ‚ÄĒ replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top`.

Bundles 1‚Äď3 form the substrate for bundle 4 (production routing) and bundle 5 (heuristic-ranker replacement).

## [4.45.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B bundle 2 ‚ÄĒ pairwise margin-perceptron training pipeline

**Stage B continues.** v4.45.0 shipped the foundation (`SelectionWeights` + `extract_features` + `score` + `select_top`). v4.45.5 ships the training algorithm: pairwise margin perceptron that takes a list of gold `(positive, negative)` candidate pairs and tunes a `SelectionWeights` table until every pair satisfies `score(positive) ‚Č• score(negative) + margin` (or `max_epochs` elapse). Pure function, deterministic per `(initial, pairs, config)`. 11 new unit tests verify convergence, determinism, edge cases, and stats reporting.

### Architectural fit

Per the project thesis, weights must be **discrete, inspectable, deterministic per seed**. Pairwise margin-perceptron satisfies all three:
- **Discrete:** updates are `lr √ó (positive_features ‚ąí negative_features)`, no gradient graph, no library deps.
- **Inspectable:** trained model still occupies the same ~28 bytes as `SelectionWeights::default_v0()`.
- **Deterministic:** no random sampling, no SGD batching variance ‚ÄĒ same `(initial weights, pairs, config)` always yields the same trained weights and stats.

This satisfies the thesis constraint: ¬ęweights/learning OK if **discrete, inspectable, cheap**; reject only LLM internals¬Ľ.

### Innovations

**(1) `TrainingPair` struct** ‚ÄĒ one labelled training example: `positive` is the candidate whose score should EXCEED `negative.score + margin` after training. Pure data, no allocation.

**(2) `TrainingConfig` struct** ‚ÄĒ three hyperparameters: `learning_rate` (step size), `margin` (required score gap), `max_epochs` (budget). `default_v0()` hand-tuned for the v0 feature scale `[0, 1]` (lr=0.1, margin=0.5, max_epochs=200).

**(3) `TrainingStats` struct** ‚ÄĒ what the training loop reports back: `epochs_run`, `final_violations` (zero ‚áí converged), `converged` boolean. Useful for tests + telemetry; never inspected by the live planner.

**(4) `train_perceptron(initial, pairs, config) -> (SelectionWeights, TrainingStats)`** ‚ÄĒ the loop. For each epoch, iterates pairs; pairs that fail the margin trigger a perceptron update on each weight component by `lr √ó (pos_feature - neg_feature)`. Loop exits early on full convergence (zero violations) or hits `max_epochs` budget.

**(5) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `TrainingPair`, `TrainingConfig`, `TrainingStats`, `train_perceptron` available as top-level public API.

**(6) 11 new unit tests** verify:
- `perceptron_converges_on_linearly_separable_pairs` ‚ÄĒ single-dimension confidence signal converges.
- `perceptron_is_deterministic` ‚ÄĒ same `(initial, pairs, config)` ‚Üí same `(trained_weights, stats)`.
- `perceptron_no_op_when_pairs_already_satisfy_margin` ‚ÄĒ pre-converged input returns initial weights unchanged.
- `perceptron_reports_violations_when_max_epochs_reached` ‚ÄĒ unsatisfiable pair (identical features) hits budget cap, reports correct violation count.
- `perceptron_empty_pairs_returns_initial_immediately` ‚ÄĒ degenerate input is no-op.
- `perceptron_learns_recency_signal` ‚ÄĒ single-dimension recency signal pickup.
- `perceptron_zero_learning_rate_makes_no_progress` ‚ÄĒ sanity check on hyperparameter boundary.
- `perceptron_converges_within_epoch_budget_on_realistic_pairs` ‚ÄĒ multi-signal pair set converges within budget.
- `perceptron_trained_weights_correctly_rank_via_score` ‚ÄĒ post-training, `score(positive) ‚Č• score(negative) + margin`.
- `training_stats_reports_epoch_count_correctly` ‚ÄĒ stats reflect actual loop iterations.
- `training_config_default_is_deterministic` ‚ÄĒ default constructor stable.

**(7) NOT YET in v4.45.5** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Real training data pipeline ‚ÄĒ extract gold pairs from canonical evals (`live_holdout`, `rust_holdout`, `parse_disambig`) and accumulated REPL transcripts.
- Audit-only wiring ‚ÄĒ compute selection scores at `tool::search_graph` result point; log to trace when selection top-1 differs from heuristic top-1 (don't change behavior yet).
- Production routing ‚ÄĒ replace `apply_graph_result`'s heuristic top-1 with `selection::select_top`.
- Per-domain weight specialization.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition continues.

## [4.45.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage B opens ‚ÄĒ selection weights foundation

**Stage B architectural milestone ‚ÄĒ first patch.** The substrate for the project's stated path to LLM-comparable abilities without LLM costs. New `crates/adam-dialog/src/selection.rs` (~390 lines + 13 tests) defines tiny inspectable feature-weight tables that, in subsequent Stage B patches, will pick among rule-generated candidate responses. Foundation only in v4.45.0 ‚ÄĒ module + types + scoring + tests, no production wiring yet (mirrors the v4.42.0 NLG-foundation pattern).

### Architectural significance

Per the project thesis (`project_retrieval_not_neural_v2.md`): adam aims to be a NEW class of generative AI that combines typed primitives + rule-based composition + **tiny selection weights** to achieve LLM-comparable abilities while remaining safe / cheap / predictable. **Stage A** (v4.42.0‚Äďv4.44.5) built typed primitives, NLG, and 46 domains of curated facts. **Stage B starts here:** selection weights over candidates. Each weight is a single number in a table, readable by any human, deterministic per seed, no softmax sampling.

### Innovations

**(1) New `crates/adam-dialog/src/selection.rs` module** with public API:
- `CandidateFeatures` ‚ÄĒ five hand-extracted features per candidate fact: `confidence`, `raw_text_richness`, `subject_overlap`, `object_overlap`, `recency_match`. Each normalized to roughly `[0, 1]` so weight magnitudes are comparable.
- `SelectionWeights` ‚ÄĒ six numbers + bias = `~28 bytes` for the v0 model. `default_v0()` hand-set to approximate the v4.38.0 SearchGraph ranker tiers (subject_overlap weight = 2.0, confidence = 1.0, object_overlap = 1.0, recency = 0.5, richness = 0.3, bias = 0.0).
- `extract_features(fact, query_tokens, last_topic) -> CandidateFeatures` ‚ÄĒ pure feature extractor.
- `score(features, weights) -> f32` ‚ÄĒ dot-product + bias.
- `select_top(candidates, weights, query_tokens, last_topic) -> Option<&Fact>` ‚ÄĒ picks highest-scoring candidate; stable-tie-break by first index for byte-identical regression on canonical evals.

**(2) Confidence mapping** ‚ÄĒ covers all 5 `ConfidenceKind` variants: `HumanApproved ‚Üí 1.0`, `CuratedQuote ‚Üí 0.8`, `RuleInferred ‚Üí 0.5`, `RepeatedPattern ‚Üí 0.3`, `Grammar ‚Üí 0.0`. Reflects the "trust ladder" already implicit in SearchGraph's tier system.

**(3) Token overlap** ‚ÄĒ counts how many query tokens (‚Č• 3 chars, casefold) match the slot root, normalized by query token count. Mirrors the v4.38.0 list-aware ranker's "subject contains query token" tier.

**(4) 13 new unit tests** verify deterministic feature extraction, weight construction, scoring math, top-1 selection, stable-tie-break, empty-list handling, token-overlap noise filtering.

**(5) Re-exports** in `lib.rs`: `CandidateFeatures` / `SelectionWeights` / `extract_features` / `selection_score` / `select_top` available as top-level public API.

**(6) NOT YET in v4.45.0** (Stage B carry-forwards):
- Training pipeline ‚ÄĒ replace hand-set weights with weights learned from the committed 3.87M-word corpus + accumulated REPL transcripts.
- Production wiring ‚ÄĒ route SearchGraph's top-N through the selector; v4.45.0 ships parallel/optional, no behavioral change.
- Per-domain weight specialization (different weights per query domain).
- Categorical predicate-kind one-hot encoding (currently dropped from v0 features as low-leverage).

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **925 passing** (was 912; +13 new selection tests) |
| Adam-dialog lib | **258 passing** (was 245; +13) |
| Selection module | 13/13 tests pass (deterministic + correct feature math + stable selection) |
| Live REPL | byte-identical to v4.44.5 (no production wiring yet) |
| Foundation: 2086 entries / 2346 facts / 46 domains / 27 163 derivations | unchanged |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

### Cadence

Minor ‚ÄĒ Stage B opens (architectural arc that the project was started for). Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: ¬ęnever under-version a kernel-signature feature¬Ľ. Selection weights are THE kernel-signature Stage B feature; v4.45.0 mirrors v4.42.0's pattern (foundation-only minor + carry-forward bundle 2 in next patches).

Stage B status:
- **v4.45.0 ‚ÄĒ bundle 1 ‚ÄĒ selection weights foundation** ‚Üź shipped
- v4.45.5+ ‚ÄĒ bundle 2: training pipeline + first audit-only wiring
- v4.46.0+ ‚ÄĒ bundle 3: production routing for multi-candidate scenarios
- v4.50.0 ‚ÄĒ bundle 4 (target): trained weights replace heuristic ranker

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition continues. v5.x parallel track (Rust code-gen in same paradigm) reserved for after Stage B converges.

## [4.44.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Knowledge breadth ‚ÄĒ Economics + Materials/Cooking/Music depth (last bundle before Stage B)

**Final knowledge bundle before Stage B opens at v4.45.0+.** New `economics_basic` domain (22 facts on tauar / sŇęranńĪs / ŇęsynńĪs / inflation / capital / labour / market / banks / credit) + thin-domain enrichment: materials 14 ‚Üí 22 (metals, alloys, glass, plastic), cooking_methods 10 ‚Üí 18 (qaynatu / quyru / bŇęqtńĪru + 5 more), music_kz 16 ‚Üí 24 (Kazakh folk instruments: –∂–Ķ—ā—Ė–≥–Ķ–Ĺ / —ą–Ķ—Ä—ā–Ķ—Ä / —Ā–į–∑—Ā—č—Ä–Ĺ–į–Ļ / —ą–į“£“õ–ĺ–Ī—č–∑ + folk genres: –ļ“Į–Ļ / —ā–ĺ–Ľ“ď–į—É / –∂—č—Ä / —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–Ķ). MULTIWORD +14.

**(1) New `economics_basic.jsonl` domain ‚ÄĒ 22 facts** covering economic vocabulary: —ć–ļ–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–ł–ļ–į / —ā–į—É–į—Ä / “õ—č–∑–ľ–Ķ—ā / —Ā“Ī—Ä–į–Ĺ—č—Ā / “Ī—Ā—č–Ĺ—č—Ā / –Ī–į“ď–į / –ł–Ĺ—Ą–Ľ—Ź—Ü–ł—Ź / –ī–Ķ—Ą–Ľ—Ź—Ü–ł—Ź / —ā“Ī—ā—č–Ĺ—É—ą—č / ”©–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä—É—ą—Ė / –ļ–į–Ņ–ł—ā–į–Ľ / –Ķ“£–Ī–Ķ–ļ / –∂–Ķ—Ä / –Ī—é–ī–∂–Ķ—ā / –ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė—Ā / —ą—č“ď—č–Ĺ / –Ņ–į–Ļ–ī–į / –Ī–į–Ĺ–ļ / –Ĺ–Ķ—Ā–ł–Ķ / –Ņ–į–Ļ—č–∑ / —ć–ļ–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ.

**(2) `materials` deepened 14 ‚Üí 22 facts** (mat_015‚Äď022) ‚ÄĒ metals (—ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ä / –į–Ľ—ā—č–Ĺ / –ļ“Į–ľ—Ė—Ā / –ľ—č—Ā / –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į—ā), glass / p–Ľ–į—Ā—ā–ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į / “õ–į“ď–į–∑. Adds the ¬ę–į—Ā—č–Ľ –ľ–Ķ—ā–į–Ľ–Ľ¬Ľ (precious metal) sub-hub.

**(3) `cooking_methods` deepened 10 ‚Üí 18 facts** (cook_011‚Äď018) ‚ÄĒ qaynatu / qauńĪru / bŇęqtńĪru / k√ľidiru / bŇęrshyldatu / turau / tŇęzdau / qaqtau (boil / fry / steam / bake / grill / chop / salt / smoke-cure). New `–į—Ā“Į–Ļ ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā—Ė` (kitchen action) hub.

**(4) `music_kz` deepened 16 ‚Üí 24 facts** (music_017‚Äď024) ‚ÄĒ Kazakh folk instruments (–∂–Ķ—ā—Ė–≥–Ķ–Ĺ / —ą–Ķ—Ä—ā–Ķ—Ä / —Ā–į–∑—Ā—č—Ä–Ĺ–į–Ļ / —ą–į“£“õ–ĺ–Ī—č–∑) and folk music genres (–ļ“Į–Ļ / —ā–ĺ–Ľ“ď–į—É / –∂—č—Ä / —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–Ķ). New `—Ö–į–Ľ—č“õ –į—Ā–Ņ–į–Ī—č` and `–ľ—É–∑—č–ļ–į –∂–į–Ĺ—Ä—č` hubs.

**(5) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 14 new compound entries** required by `world_core_multiword_coverage`.

**(6) Foundation expansion** ‚ÄĒ 2040 ‚Üí **2086 entries** (+46), 2300 ‚Üí **2346 facts** (+46), 45 ‚Üí **46 domains** (+1: economics_basic), 26 860 ‚Üí **27 163 derivations** (+303 from R1/R5/R10/R11 chains over the new IsA hubs).

| “ö–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā–į“ď—č –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ļ–Ķ–ľ—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ? | Abai poetry quote (¬ę”ė—ā—ā–Ķ“£ –ī“Į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ‚Äď–į–Ļ ‚Ä¶¬Ľ) | "**“ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ –ļ–Ķ–ľ—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė: –≥–į–Ľ–Ľ—é—Ü–ł–Ĺ–į—Ü–ł—Ź (–ĺ–Ļ–ī–į–Ĺ —ą—č“ď–į—Ä—É), –ľ–ĺ–Ľ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ —Ä–Ķ—Ā—É—Ä—Ā—č –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ć–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≥–ł—Ź —ą—č“ď—č–Ĺ—č, —ą–Ķ—ą—Ė–ľ—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ą—č“õ—Ā—č–∑–ī—č“ď—č, –Ī—Ė—Ä —Ā“Ī—Ä–į—É“ď–į ”ô—Ä —ā“Į—Ä–Ľ—Ė –∂–į—É–į–Ņ (–ī–Ķ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ—É—Ė), –ĺ“õ—É –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ —Ā–į–Ņ–į—Ā—č–∑–ī—č“ď—č.**" ‚úď |

### Innovations

**(1) `time` deepened 20 ‚Üí 25 facts** (time_021‚Äď025) ‚ÄĒ seasons list-summary (¬ę–Ė—č–Ľ —ā”©—Ä—ā –ľ–Ķ–∑–≥—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ —ā“Ī—Ä–į–ī—č: –ļ”©–ļ—ā–Ķ–ľ, –∂–į–∑, –ļ“Į–∑, “õ—č—Ā¬Ľ); —ā–ĺ“õ—Ā–į–Ĺ (academic/financial quarter) IsA —Ąact + has_quantity –∂—č–Ľ-—ā–ĺ“õ—Ā–į–Ĺ + 12-month / 4-season / 4-quarter compositional fact + –∂—č–Ľ-related_to-–ľ–Ķ–∑–≥—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė bridge.

**(2) `kz_literature` deepened 77 ‚Üí 79 facts** (lit_078‚Äď079) ‚ÄĒ full Kazakh writers list-summary (13 names: –ź–Ī–į–Ļ / ”ė—É–Ķ–∑–ĺ–≤ / –°–Ķ–Ļ—Ą—É–Ľ–Ľ–ł–Ĺ / –Ė“Ī–ľ–į–Ī–į–Ķ–≤ / –Ė–į–Ĺ—Ā“Į–≥—Ė—Ä–ĺ–≤ / –ú–į–Ļ–Ľ–ł–Ĺ / –ú–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į“ď–į–Ľ–ł–Ķ–≤ / –ú–į“õ–į—ā–į–Ķ–≤ / “ö“Ī–ī–į–Ļ–Ī–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė“Ī–Ľ—č / –ē—Ā–Ķ–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ—Ä–Ľ–ł–Ĺ / –ö–Ķ–ļ—Ė–Ľ–Ī–į–Ķ–≤ / –ź—Ö—ā–į–Ĺ–ĺ–≤ / –ú–ĺ–ľ—č—ą“Ī–Ľ—č) and full Kazakh poets list-summary (9 names).

**(3) `adam_self` deepened 33 ‚Üí 39 facts** (adam_self_034‚Äď039) ‚ÄĒ LLM-limitations contrast facts: galaktion / hallucination / energy cost / opacity / non-determinism / general kemshilik list. Subject ¬ę–∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā¬Ľ bound to –ļ–Ķ–ľ—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė so queries about LLM drawbacks surface adam's design rationale rather than poetry fallback.

**(4) `government_kazakhstan` deepened 21 ‚Üí 25 facts** (gov_kz_022‚Äď024 + 1 internal addition) ‚ÄĒ direct office-holder bridges with ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –†–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ—č“£¬Ľ Gen-form subject: –ö–†-–ü—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė ‚Üí –Ę–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤; –ö–†-–ü—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ú–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė ‚Üí –Ď–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤; –ö–† borders fact (5 neighbors enumerated under ¬ę“õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į—Ā—č¬Ľ subject so the ¬ę—ą–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ā–Ķ¬Ľ query routes correctly without falling to the IsA fact).

**(5) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 15 new compound entries**: “õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė / “õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė / –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā –ļ–Ķ–ľ—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė / —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć –į—ą—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–≥—Ė / —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć –ī–Ķ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ—É—Ė / —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć —Ä–Ķ—Ā—É—Ä—Ā —ą—č“ď—č–Ĺ—č / —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć –ļ–Ķ–ľ—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė / –ļ–Ķ–ľ—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / –∂–į–∑—É—ą—č–Ľ–į—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / –į“õ—č–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / –ľ–Ķ–∑–≥—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / –∂—č–Ľ –ľ–Ķ–∑–≥—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė / “õ–į–∑–į“õ –∂–į–∑—É—ą—č–Ľ–į—Ä—č / “õ–į–∑–į“õ –į“õ—č–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä—č / —É–į“õ—č—ā –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ“£—Ė.

## [4.43.9] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ `multiword_entity_hint` inflected-FIRST-word pass (architectural)

**Closes the v4.43.8 architectural carry-forward.** v4.43.8 fixed the ¬ę“ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė¬Ľ routing by registering both bare-form and Gen-form (-–Ĺ—č“£) variants of office phrases as multiwords ‚ÄĒ solved the symptom, but registering parallel Gen-form variants is data-drift waiting to happen. v4.43.9 lifts the fix to the matcher itself: a third pass in `multiword_entity_hint` detects inflected-FIRST-word forms via the six Kazakh genitive suffixes (-–Ĺ—č“£ / -–Ĺ—Ė“£ / -–ī—č“£ / -–ī—Ė“£ / -—ā—č“£ / -—ā—Ė“£), so future 2-word multiword entries no longer need parallel Gen-form registration. Byte-identical for all v4.43.8 surfaces (verified by 906‚Üí912 workspace test count, +6 new matcher tests).

### Innovations

**(1) Third pass in `multiword_entity_hint`** ‚ÄĒ for 2-word entities `X Y`, accepts input containing `X{Gen} T` where `T` starts with `Y`'s first 3 chars and `X{Gen}` is `X` followed by one of the six Kazakh genitive suffixes. Conservative: requires `parts[0].chars().count() >= 4` to avoid spurious matches on shorter first words. Returns the BARE-form entity string (not the inflected surface) so SearchGraph downstream finds the canonical fact subject.

**(2) 6 new unit tests** in `topic_extraction::tests`:
- `multiword_hint_first_pass_exact_substring` ‚ÄĒ anti-regression on first-pass bare-form match
- `multiword_hint_second_pass_inflected_second_word` ‚ÄĒ anti-regression on v4.40.5 inflected-second-word pass
- `multiword_hint_third_pass_genitive_first_word_returns_bare_form` ‚ÄĒ verifies new third pass on `“õ–į–∑–į“õ—ā—č“£ —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė` ‚Üí bare `“õ–į–∑–į“õ —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė` (registered without Gen-form variant)
- `multiword_hint_third_pass_handles_temir_zhol_genitive` ‚ÄĒ front-vowel + voiced-consonant Gen `—ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ä–ī—Ė“£ –∂–ĺ–Ľ—č`
- `multiword_hint_third_pass_handles_nasal_genitive` ‚ÄĒ nasal-final Gen `–į—Ā–Ņ–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė`
- `multiword_hint_third_pass_skips_short_first_word` ‚ÄĒ gate test for parts[0] < 4 chars

**(3) Architectural cleanliness** ‚ÄĒ the v4.43.8 Gen-form multiword registrations remain (byte-identical for those known surfaces) but are now redundant for the matcher contract. Future 2-word multiword additions can be registered in bare form alone; the third pass handles genitive-form input automatically. Pattern documented in the matcher's docstring.

|---|---|

| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |

## [4.43.8] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ v4.43.7 carry-forward closed ‚ÄĒ current-president direct routing

**Closes the v4.43.7 carry-forward.** Pre-v4.43.8 ¬ę“ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ?¬Ľ fell to ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–≥—Ė ‚ÄĒ 1991 –∂—č–Ľ–ī–į–Ĺ –Ī–į—Ā—ā–į–Ņ –Ķ–Ĺ–≥—Ė–∑—Ė–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ķ–Ľ –Ī–į—Ā—ą—č—Ā—č –Ľ–į—É–į–∑—č–ľ—č¬Ľ (the abstract office concept, not naming –Ę–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤). Root cause: FST analyzes ¬ę–Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė¬Ľ as either `–Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā + Pos3` or `–Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ + Pos3`; the multiword ¬ę“õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–≥—Ė¬Ľ (a registered abstract-noun compound from `history_kazakhstan`) wins via longest-substring match. Fix: add direct office-holder facts whose subject IS the inflected/compound surface phrase the user actually types (¬ę“õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė “õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė¬Ľ, ¬ę“õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė¬Ľ, plus parallel premier-minister forms) and register them as multiwords so topic-extraction picks the bridge to the office-holder.

### Real-REPL probe ‚ÄĒ full v4.43.7 transcript replay

|---|---|---|---|

| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ? | (malformed) | corpus quote | "**“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į—Ā—č–ľ-–∂–ĺ–ľ–į—Ä—ā —ā–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤ ”©–∑–į—Ä–į –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č.**" ‚úď |
| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ? | (didn't engage) | ministries list | "**“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ĺ–Ľ–∂–į—Ā –Ī–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤ ”©–∑–į—Ä–į –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č.**" ‚úď |

| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ? | ‚ÄĒ | links to –Ď–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤ | unchanged ‚úď |

### Innovations

**(1) 5 direct office-holder bridge facts** in `government_kazakhstan.jsonl`:
- gov_kz_016: ¬ę“ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė ‚ÄĒ “ö–į—Ā—č–ľ-–Ė–ĺ–ľ–į—Ä—ā –Ę–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤¬Ľ
- gov_kz_017: ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä “ö–į—Ā—č–ľ-–Ė–ĺ–ľ–į—Ä—ā –Ę–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤ “õ—č–∑–ľ–Ķ—ā –į—ā“õ–į—Ä–į–ī—č¬Ľ
- gov_kz_018: ¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –ü—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ú–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė ‚ÄĒ –ě–Ľ–∂–į—Ā –Ď–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤¬Ľ
- gov_kz_019: ¬ę–ü—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä –ě–Ľ–∂–į—Ā –Ď–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤ –Ľ–į—É–į–∑—č–ľ—č–Ĺ –į—ā“õ–į—Ä–į–ī—č¬Ľ
- gov_kz_020/021: parallel Gen-form bridges
Each fact uses `related_to` to bind the office (with its inflected surface form) to the current officeholder.

**(2) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 5** for both bare-form and Gen-form (-–Ĺ—č“£) variants of office phrases ‚ÄĒ `“õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė` / `“õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė “õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė` / `“õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė` / `“õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė` / `“õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė`. Required because the v4.40.5 inflected-second-word pass in `multiword_entity_hint` only handles inflection on the SECOND word of a 2-word entity, not the first.

**(3) Foundation expansion** ‚ÄĒ 2018 ‚Üí **2019 entries** (+1 from re-extraction), 2277 ‚Üí **2284 facts** (+7 ‚ÄĒ 5 bridge facts √ó ~1.4 facts each from multi-fact entries), 45 domains unchanged, 26 854 ‚Üí **26 869 derivations** (+15).

### Cadence

`.8` reflects: (1) 5 bridge facts + (2) 5 multiword entries + (3) full transcript-replay verification (6/6) + (4) carry-forward closed = **3 distinct innovations** + carry-forward closure milestone. ### Architectural note (carry-forward to a future patch)

The deeper fix would be in `multiword_entity_hint` itself: extend the v4.40.5 inflected-second-word pass to ALSO handle inflected-FIRST-word (genitive ¬ę-–Ĺ—č“£¬Ľ/¬ę-–Ĺ—Ė“£¬Ľ/¬ę-–ī—č“£¬Ľ/¬ę-–ī—Ė“£¬Ľ/¬ę-—ā—č“£¬Ľ/¬ę-—ā—Ė“£¬Ľ suffix on parts[0]). Then registering the bare-form alone would suffice. Deferring ‚ÄĒ current explicit Gen-form registrations are byte-identical for known surfaces and avoid over-firing risk on shorter first words.

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition. Next: continued real-REPL gap closures + interrogative-mood NLG opening.

## [4.43.7] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Transcript-driven gap closure ‚ÄĒ Kazakhstan governance + scientific-loan lexicon

**Driven by 2026-05-04 user dialog test transcript.** Six concrete gaps surfaced in a 14-turn live REPL session, of which 5 are materially closed and 1 partially improved. Two distinct fix classes: (a) **lexicon** ‚ÄĒ 38 scientific/civic loan roots (–Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź / —Ą–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź / —ć—Ā—ā–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į / –ľ–Ķ—Ä–ļ—É—Ä–ł–Ļ / —ą–ĺ–Ľ–Ņ–į–Ĺ / —é–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä / —Ā–į—ā—É—Ä–Ĺ / –Ĺ–Ķ–Ņ—ā—É–Ĺ / –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā / –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä / —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į / etc.) registered so the FST can decompose inflected forms like ¬ę–Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź—Ā—č¬Ľ (Pos3) ‚Üí ¬ę–Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź¬Ľ bare root; (b) **knowledge** ‚ÄĒ new `government_kazakhstan` domain (15 facts) covering presidents (–Ě–į–∑–į—Ä–Ī–į–Ķ–≤ / –Ę–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤), premier (–Ď–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤), parliament (–°–Ķ–Ĺ–į—ā / –ú”ô–∂—Ė–Ľ—Ė—Ā), ministries list, courts, political-system type.

### Real-REPL gap closures

| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ —ā“Ī“£“ď—č—ą –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–ī—č? | Generic Kazakhstan IsA fact | "**–Ę“Ī“£“ď—č—ą –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ“Ī—Ä—Ā“Ī–Ľ—ā–į–Ĺ –Ĺ–į–∑–į—Ä–Ī–į–Ķ–≤ ”©–∑–į—Ä–į –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č.**" (links concept to person) |
| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–ī–į “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä –Ī–į—Ä? | Generic Kazakhstan IsA fact | "**“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–ī–į –Ü—ą–ļ—Ė —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ—Ä, –°—č—Ä—ā“õ—č —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ—Ä, “ö–į—Ä–∂—č, ”ė–ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—ā, –Ē–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–į—É–Ľ—č“õ —Ā–į“õ—ā–į—É, –Ď—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ, –ú”ô–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ—ā, –≠–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≥–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į, –ö”©–Ľ—Ė–ļ, –ź—É—č–Ľ —ą–į—Ä—É–į—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č, “ö–ĺ—Ä“ď–į–Ĺ—č—Ā, “í—č–Ľ—č–ľ –∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ –∂–ĺ“ď–į—Ä—č –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ, –¶–ł—Ą—Ä–Ľ—č“õ –ī–į–ľ—É, –ē“£–Ī–Ķ–ļ –∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ —Ö–į–Ľ—č“õ—ā—č ”ô–Ľ–Ķ—É–ľ–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ “õ–ĺ—Ä“ď–į—É —Ā—č–Ĺ–ī—č –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä –Ī–į—Ä.**" |
| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į –ľ–į, ”ô–Ľ–ī–Ķ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į –ľ–į? | Generic Kazakhstan IsA fact | "**“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ-–Ņ–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ –į—Ä–į–Ľ–į—Ā —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į.**" |
| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ? | (didn't engage) | Engages via ministries-related fact (–Ď–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤ direct fact still routes to ministries list ‚ÄĒ partial) |
| “ö–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė –ļ—Ė–ľ? | Generic ¬ę–Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–≥—Ė¬Ľ abstract concept | Same routing ‚ÄĒ partial improvement; still doesn't name –Ę–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤. Carry-forward to v4.43.8. |

### Innovations

**(1) Lexicon: 38 scientific/civic loan roots** added to `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` (the curated source of truth). Categories: psychology / philosophy heads (–Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź, —Ą–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź, —ć—Ā—ā–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į, –ľ–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į, –≥–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā–Ķ–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź); planet & astronomy heads (–ľ–Ķ—Ä–ļ—É—Ä–ł–Ļ, —ą–ĺ–Ľ–Ņ–į–Ĺ, —é–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä, —Ā–į—ā—É—Ä–Ĺ, –Ĺ–Ķ–Ņ—ā—É–Ĺ, –≥–į–Ľ–į–ļ—ā–ł–ļ–į, –į—Ā—ā–Ķ—Ä–ĺ–ł–ī, —ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–ļ–ĺ–Ņ, –ĺ—Ä–Ī–ł—ā–į, –ļ–ĺ—Ā–ľ–ĺ–Ĺ–į–≤—ā–ł–ļ–į, –į—ā–ľ–ĺ—Ā—Ą–Ķ—Ä–į); math depth heads (—Ā—ā–į—ā–ł—Ā—ā–ł–ļ–į, –ī–ł—Ā–Ņ–Ķ—Ä—Ā–ł—Ź, –ľ–Ķ–ī–ł–į–Ĺ–į, –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ā–ł—Ź, –į–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ī—Ä–į, –≥–Ķ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł—Ź, —ā—Ä–ł–≥–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł—Ź); civic / governance heads (–Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā, –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä, –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä, –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ, –Ņ–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā, –ľ”ô–∂—Ė–Ľ—Ė—Ā, —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ–į—ā, —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į, –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ā—ā–ł—ā—É—Ü–ł—Ź, –ī–Ķ–ľ–ĺ–ļ—Ä–į—ā–ł—Ź); proper nouns (–Ĺ–į–∑–į—Ä–Ī–į–Ķ–≤, —ā–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤, –Ī–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤). Closes the systemic gap where v4.42.8 / v4.42.9 added world_core facts using these terms but the lexicon never registered the bare-noun roots, so inflected forms (¬ę–Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź—Ā—č¬Ľ = `–Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź + Pos3`) couldn't be analyzed and topic extraction fell to the literal surface form. **Direct cause**: ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź—Ā—č¬Ľ query in transcript fell to a proverb because ¬ę–Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź—Ā—č¬Ľ couldn't decompose to ¬ę–Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź¬Ľ.

**(2) New `government_kazakhstan.jsonl` domain ‚ÄĒ 15 facts** covering: “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ political-system type (–Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ-–Ņ–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ –į—Ä–į–Ľ–į—Ā —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į); –Ě“Ī—Ä—Ā“Ī–Ľ—ā–į–Ĺ –Ě–į–∑–į—Ä–Ī–į–Ķ–≤ (—ā“Ī“£“ď—č—ą –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā, 1991‚Äď2019); “ö–į—Ā—č–ľ-–Ė–ĺ–ľ–į—Ä—ā –Ę–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤ (“õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā, 2019‚Äď); –ě–Ľ–∂–į—Ā –Ď–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤ (–Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ć–Ķ—Ä-–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä, 2024‚Äď); –Ņ–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā structure (–°–Ķ–Ĺ–į—ā + –ú”ô–∂—Ė–Ľ—Ė—Ā); “Į–ļ—Ė–ľ–Ķ—ā; –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ + ministries list; –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ā—ā–ł—ā—É—Ü–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ —Ā–ĺ—ā; –∂–ĺ“ď–į—Ä“ď—č —Ā–ĺ—ā.

**(3) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 10 new compound entries** required by `world_core_multiword_coverage`: –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ā—ā–ł—ā—É—Ü–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ —Ā–ĺ—ā / –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł—Ā—ā—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė / “õ–į—Ā—č–ľ-–∂–ĺ–ľ–į—Ä—ā —ā–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤ / –Ĺ“Ī—Ä—Ā“Ī–Ľ—ā–į–Ĺ –Ĺ–į–∑–į—Ä–Ī–į–Ķ–≤ / –ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ –ĺ—Ä–≥–į–Ĺ / —ā“Ī“£“ď—č—ą –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā / “õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā / –ĺ–Ľ–∂–į—Ā –Ī–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ–≤ / —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į —ā“Į—Ä—Ė / –∂–ĺ“ď–į—Ä“ď—č —Ā–ĺ—ā.

**(4) Foundation expansion** ‚ÄĒ 2003 ‚Üí **2018 entries** (+15), 2254 ‚Üí **2277 facts** (+23), 44 ‚Üí **45 domains** (+1: government_kazakhstan), 26 806 ‚Üí **26 854 derivations** (+48). Lexicon: 4947 ‚Üí **4985 curated roots** (+38).

Likely fix: SearchGraph synonym-pair adding ¬ę“õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā¬Ľ as a topic-equivalent of ¬ę“õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė¬Ľ, OR a more direct reverse-IsA fact `“õ–į–∑—Ė—Ä–≥—Ė “õ–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–∑–ł–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė IsA “õ–į—Ā—č–ľ-–∂–ĺ–ľ–į—Ä—ā —ā–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤`.

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition continues. Next: continued real-REPL-driven gap closures + interrogative-mood NLG opening; ~v4.50 ‚ÄĒ Stage B (tiny selection weights).

## [4.43.6] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage A NLG predicate-coverage **complete (11/11)** + Kazakh morphology vocabulary

**Stage A milestone: NLG declarative coverage now closes all 11 reasoner-emitted predicates.** v4.43.5 brought GoesTo + After (10/11). This release adds `DoesToDeclarative` (11/11) and pins the contract via a new `all_eleven_predicates_have_a_rule` test that iterates every `Predicate` variant and asserts NLG produces some output. Plus `language_features` deepened 18 ‚Üí 33 with Kazakh morphology vocabulary (–ľ–ĺ—Ä—Ą–Ķ–ľ–į / —ā“Į–Ī—Ė—Ä / –∂“Ī—Ä–Ĺ–į“õ / –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É / —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ / —ą–į“õ / –∂–į“õ / –∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ / —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / —Ā–į–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ / “Į—Ā—ā–Ķ—É / –į–≥–≥–Ľ—é—ā–ł–Ĺ–į—Ü–ł—Ź / “Į–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –∑–į“£—č) ‚ÄĒ directly relevant to the project's agglutinative-algebra core engineering thesis.

### Real-REPL probe

| Query | Response |
|---|---|
| –ú–ĺ—Ä—Ą–Ķ–ľ–į –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ú–ĺ—Ä—Ą–Ķ–ľ–į ‚ÄĒ –ľ–į“ď—č–Ĺ–į—Ā—č –Ī–į—Ä —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė“£ –Ķ“£ –ļ—Ė—ą—Ė –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė." |
| –ē—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ē—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ ‚ÄĒ ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā –Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ –ļ“Į–Ļ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė—Ä–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č." |
| –ź–≥–≥–Ľ—é—ā–ł–Ĺ–į—Ü–ł—Ź –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ź–≥–≥–Ľ—é—ā–ł–Ĺ–į—Ü–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ —ā“Į–Ī—Ė—Ä–≥–Ķ –Ī—Ė—Ä—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ņ –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ–į—ā—č–Ĺ –∂“Ī—Ä–Ĺ–į“õ-–∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É–Ľ–į—Ä –į—Ä“õ—č–Ľ—č —Ā”©–∑ —ā—É–ī—č—Ä—É —ā”ô—Ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė." |
| “ģ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –∑–į“£—č –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "“ģ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –∑–į“£—č ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑–į“õ —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –ī–į—É—č—Ā—ā—č –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā—ā–į—Ä–ī—č“£ –Ī—Ė—Ä-–Ī—Ė—Ä—Ė–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ “Į–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ—Ė–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ —Ą–ĺ–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –∑–į“£." |

### Innovations

**(1) `DoesToDeclarative` NLG rule** ‚ÄĒ raw_text-prefer (mirroring GoesTo / After patterns); mechanical fallback `¬ęX Y-–≥–Ķ ”ô—Ā–Ķ—Ä –Ķ—ā–Ķ–ī—Ė¬Ľ` when raw_text empty. Closes Stage A NLG declarative coverage from 10/11 ‚Üí **11/11** reasoner-emitted predicates. NLG `all_rules()` extended 12 ‚Üí 13.

**(2) `all_eleven_predicates_have_a_rule` contract test** ‚ÄĒ iterates every `Predicate` enum variant, asserts `render_sentence` returns `Some` for each. Replaces the old `unknown_predicate_returns_none` test (the predicate it was testing got a rule). Future predicate additions without an NLG rule trip a red CI.

**(3) `language_features` deepened 18 ‚Üí 33 facts** (lang_019‚Äď033) ‚ÄĒ Kazakh morphology vocabulary anchored in the project's engineering thesis: –ľ–ĺ—Ä—Ą–Ķ–ľ–į / —ā“Į–Ī—Ė—Ä / –∂“Ī—Ä–Ĺ–į“õ / –∂–į–Ľ“ď–į—É (Kazakh morphology primitives matching FST kernel); —Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ / —ą–į“õ / –∂–į“õ as `–≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź`; –∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / –Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ / —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / —Ā–į–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ / “Į—Ā—ā–Ķ—É as `—Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č`; –į–≥–≥–Ľ—é—ā–ł–Ĺ–į—Ü–ł—Ź as `—ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā`; “Į–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –∑–į“£—č as `—Ą–ĺ–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –∑–į“£`.

**(4) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 9 new compound entries** required by `world_core_multiword_coverage`: –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–į—ā–Ķ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź / —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā / —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ / —Ą–ĺ–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –∑–į“£ / “Į–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ –∑–į“£—č / —Ā”©–∑ —ā–į–Ī—č / –∑–į—ā –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / —Ā—č–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ / —Ā–į–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ.

**(5) Foundation expansion** ‚ÄĒ 1988 ‚Üí **2003 entries** (+15), 2239 ‚Üí **2254 facts** (+15), domains unchanged at 44, 26 779 ‚Üí **26 806 derivations** (+27).

Stage A status:
- Bundle 1 (v4.42.0) ‚ÄĒ NLG foundation
- Bundle 2 (v4.42.5) ‚ÄĒ production migration + 5 rules

## [4.43.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Knowledge depth bundle 3 + NLG predicate-coverage completion (10 ‚Üí 12 rules)

**Mixed bundle** continuing both knowledge expansion (4 domain expansions) and Stage A NLG completeness (2 new declarative rules covering the last reasoner-emitted predicate gaps). New `philosophy_basic` domain (23 facts on existence / ethics / epistemology); `astronomy` 30 ‚Üí 45 (planets / galaxies / black holes / telescopes); `weather_phenomena` 15 ‚Üí 26 (storm types / atmospheric measurements); `measurements` 10 ‚Üí 18 (SI units of length / mass / volume / time). NLG `all_rules()` extended from 10 ‚Üí 12 with `GoesToDeclarative` + `AfterDeclarative` (raw_text-prefer pattern, mirroring `IsACopulaDeclarative`); only `DoesTo` predicate remains unmatched (1 fact in current corpus, low-leverage).

### Real-REPL probe (live `adam-chat`)

| Query | Response |
|---|---|
| –§–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–§–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–ľ—č—Ā, —ā–į–Ĺ—č–ľ –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä –ľ”ô–Ĺ—Ė —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –∂–į–Ľ–Ņ—č —Ā“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā–į—Ä–ī—č –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ." |
| –≠—ā–ł–ļ–į –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–≠—ā–ł–ļ–į ‚ÄĒ –į–ī–į–ľ–≥–Ķ—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ľ—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ–∑-“õ“Ī–Ľ—č“õ –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ “õ“Ī–Ĺ–ī—č–Ľ—č“õ—ā–į—Ä–ī—č –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ —Ą–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č." |
| –ģ–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ģ–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä ‚ÄĒ –ö“Į–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ķ“£ “Į–Ľ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ –≥–į–∑ “ď–į–Ľ–į–ľ—ą–į—Ä—č." |
| –ď–į–Ľ–į–ļ—ā–ł–ļ–į –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ď–į–Ľ–į–ļ—ā–ł–ļ–į ‚ÄĒ –ľ–ł–Ľ–Ľ–ł–į—Ä–ī—ā–į“ď–į–Ĺ –∂“Ī–Ľ–ī—č–∑–ī–į—Ä –∂–ł—č–Ĺ—č." |
| –ö–Ľ–ł–ľ–į—ā –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ö–Ľ–ł–ľ–į—ā ‚ÄĒ –Ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė–Ľ—Ė –į—É–ľ–į“õ—ā–į “Ī–∑–į“õ —É–į“õ—č—ā –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č –Ī–į–Ļ“õ–į–Ľ–į—ā—č–Ĺ –į—É–į —Ä–į–Ļ—č –ļ“Į–Ļ—Ė." |
| –ö–ł–Ľ–ĺ–≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ö–ł–Ľ–ĺ–≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ ‚ÄĒ —Ö–į–Ľ—č“õ–į—Ä–į–Ľ—č“õ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–≥—Ė—Ā—Ė." |

### Innovations

**(1) New `philosophy_basic.jsonl` domain ‚ÄĒ 23 facts** covering existence / cognition / ethics / aesthetics / metaphysics / epistemology vocabulary: —Ą–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź / –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–ľ—č—Ā / —ā–į–Ĺ—č–ľ / —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ / –Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł–ļ–į / —ć—ā–ł–ļ–į / —ć—Ā—ā–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į / –ľ–Ķ—ā–į—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į / –≥–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā–Ķ–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź / –ł–ī–Ķ–į–Ľ–ł–∑–ľ / –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł–į–Ľ–ł–∑–ľ / —Ā–ļ–Ķ–Ņ—ā–ł—Ü–ł–∑–ľ / –į–ī–į–ľ–≥–Ķ—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ / –Ņ–į—Ä–į—Ā–į—ā / –∂–į“õ—Ā—č–Ľ—č“õ / –∂–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“õ / –Ķ—Ä–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ / –∂–į—É–į–Ņ–ļ–Ķ—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ / —É–į“õ—č—ā / –ļ–Ķ“£—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ / —Ā–Ķ–Ī–Ķ–Ņ / —Ā–į–Ľ–ī–į—Ä / —Ą–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą.

**(2) `astronomy` deepened 30 ‚Üí 45 facts** (astro_031‚Äď045) ‚ÄĒ –ú–Ķ—Ä–ļ—É—Ä–ł–Ļ / –®–ĺ–Ľ–Ņ–į–Ĺ / –ģ–Ņ–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä / –°–į—ā—É—Ä–Ĺ / –£—Ä–į–Ĺ / –Ě–Ķ–Ņ—ā—É–Ĺ (the 6 planets not yet curated), “ö“Ī—Ā –∂–ĺ–Ľ—č galaxy, “õ–į—Ä–į “õ“Ī—Ä–ī—č–ľ (black hole), —ā“Ī–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č (nebula), –į—Ā—ā–Ķ—Ä–ĺ–ł–ī, —ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–ļ–ĺ–Ņ, –ĺ—Ä–Ī–ł—ā–į, –ļ–ĺ—Ā–ľ–ĺ–Ĺ–į–≤—ā–ł–ļ–į, “ď–į—Ä—č—ą–ļ–Ķ—Ä.

**(3) `weather_phenomena` deepened 15 ‚Üí 26 facts** (w_016‚Äď026) ‚ÄĒ –ī–į—É—č–Ľ / –Ī–ĺ—Ä–į–Ĺ / –Ĺ–į–Ļ–∑–į“ď–į–Ļ / –ļ“Į–Ĺ –ļ“Į—Ä–ļ—Ė—Ä–Ķ—É / —ā“Ī–ľ–į–Ĺ / —ą—č“õ / –Ī“Ī—Ä—ą–į“õ / –ļ–Ľ–ł–ľ–į—ā / –į—É–į —Ä–į–Ļ—č / —č–Ľ“ď–į–Ľ–ī—č–Ľ—č“õ / –į—ā–ľ–ĺ—Ā—Ą–Ķ—Ä–į–Ľ—č“õ “õ—č—Ā—č–ľ.

**(4) `measurements` deepened 10 ‚Üí 18 facts** (mea_011‚Äď018) ‚ÄĒ SI base + derived units: –ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä / –ļ–ł–Ľ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä / —Ā–į–Ĺ—ā–ł–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä / –≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ / –ļ–ł–Ľ–ĺ–≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ / —ā–ĺ–Ĺ–Ĺ–į / –Ľ–ł—ā—Ä / —Ā–Ķ–ļ—É–Ĺ–ī with proper IsA hubs (`“Ī–∑—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė` / `–ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė` / `–ļ”©–Ľ–Ķ–ľ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė` / `—É–į“õ—č—ā –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė`).

**(5) `GoesToDeclarative` + `AfterDeclarative` NLG rules** with raw_text-prefer pattern. Mirrors `IsACopulaDeclarative` design: when `raw_text` is non-empty (true for ~all corpus-extracted GoesTo / After facts), prefer it; otherwise mechanical compose via dative (¬ęX Y-–≥–Ķ –Ī–į—Ä–į–ī—č¬Ľ) / ablative (¬ęX-—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –ļ–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ Y –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č¬Ľ). Closes Stage A declarative coverage from 10 ‚Üí 12 active rules covering 10/11 reasoner-emitted predicates (DoesTo deferred ‚ÄĒ only 1 fact, low-leverage).

**(6) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 22 new compound entries** required by `world_core_multiword_coverage`: –į—É–į —Ä–į–Ļ—č / –į—É–į —Ä–į–Ļ—č –ļ“Į–Ļ—Ė / –į—ā–ľ–ĺ—Ā—Ą–Ķ—Ä–į–Ľ—č“õ –∂–į“ď–ī–į–Ļ / –į—ā–ľ–ĺ—Ā—Ą–Ķ—Ä–į–Ľ—č“õ “õ—č—Ā—č–ľ / –ľ–Ķ—ā–Ķ–ĺ—Ä–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ —ą–į–ľ–į / —Ą–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ “Ī“ď—č–ľ / —Ą–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ –į“ď—č–ľ / —Ą–ł–Ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ—Ą–ł—Ź —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –ľ–ĺ—Ä–į–Ľ—Ć–ī—č“õ “õ“Ī–Ĺ–ī—č–Ľ—č“õ / –ľ–ĺ—Ä–į–Ľ—Ć–ī—č“õ “õ–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā / –∂“Ī–Ľ–ī—č–∑–ī–į—Ä –∂–ł—č–Ĺ—č / “ď–į—Ä—č—ą—ā—č“õ –Ĺ—č—Ā–į–Ĺ / “ď–į—Ä—č—ą –ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė / “õ–ĺ–∑“ď–į–Ľ—č—Ā –∂–ĺ–Ľ—č / —ā–Ķ—Ö–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —Ā–į–Ľ–į / “Ī–∑—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė / –ļ”©–Ľ–Ķ–ľ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė / –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė / —É–į“õ—č—ā –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė / “õ–į—Ä–į “õ“Ī—Ä–ī—č–ľ / –ļ“Į–Ĺ –ļ“Į—Ä–ļ—Ė—Ä–Ķ—É / –į“õ—č–Ľ —ā“Į—Ä—Ė.

**(7) 4 new NLG unit tests** verify GoesTo + After both raw_text-prefer (`uses_raw_text_when_present`) and mechanical-fallback (`falls_back_to_dative_when_raw_empty` / `falls_back_to_ablative_when_raw_empty`) paths. Plus `unknown_predicate_returns_none` repointed from `GoesTo` to `DoesTo` (the last unmatched predicate).

**(8) Foundation expansion** ‚ÄĒ 1931 ‚Üí **1988 entries** (+57), 2182 ‚Üí **2239 facts** (+57), 43 ‚Üí **44 domains** (+1: philosophy_basic), 26 251 ‚Üí **26 779 derivations** (+528 from R1/R5/R10/R11 chains over the new IsA parents).

## [4.43.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Stage A bundle 3 ‚ÄĒ Introducer migration into NLG production path

**Stage A architectural milestone.** Closes the deferred carry-forward from v4.42.6 memory note. The five-variant introducer rotation that lived in template-string text (`unknown.with_grounded_fact` family in `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`) is now first-class enum + composer in the NLG module. Templates simplify to a single `{fact}` slot whose value is a full preamble+body sentence produced by `nlg::compose_introducer`; the planner picks the introducer via `nlg::pick_introducer` mirroring the v4.42.x seed-mod rotation byte-for-byte. **Output is byte-identical to v4.42.x for any `(seed, has_name_respect, fact)` triple** ‚ÄĒ verified via existing 890-test workspace + live REPL anti-regression probe.

### Architectural before/after

**Pre-v4.43.0** ‚ÄĒ introducer phrasings live in templates:
```toml
[[families]]
key = "unknown.with_grounded_fact"
templates = [
    "{noun} —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č “õ—č—Ā“õ–į—ą–į –į–Ļ—ā—Ā–į–ľ: {fact}",          # idx 0
    "{noun} –∂–į–Ļ—č–Ĺ–ī–į –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–≥—Ė –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ –ľ—č–Ĺ–į—É: {fact}",    # idx 1
    "{noun} —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ķ“£ ”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ—Ė –ľ—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—č –į–Ļ—ā—É“ď–į –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č: {fact}",  # idx 2
    "{name_respect}, {noun} —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č “õ—č—Ā“õ–į –∂–į—É–į–Ņ: {fact}",     # idx 3
    "{noun} –∂–į–Ļ—č–Ĺ–ī–į –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ: {fact}",            # idx 4
]
```
Slot `{fact}` filled with body-only sentence; template wraps with preamble.

**Post-v4.43.0** ‚ÄĒ introducer is code-side enum + composer:
```toml
[[families]]
key = "unknown.with_grounded_fact"
templates = ["{fact}"]
```
Slot `{fact}` filled with `compose_introducer(introducer, topic, name_respect, body)`; planner picks introducer via `pick_introducer(seed, has_name_respect)` whose pool order matches the original template-array order, preserving the v4.42.x rotation bit-for-bit.

### Innovations

**(1) `Introducer` enum extended to 5 user-facing variants** ‚ÄĒ added `NameRespectAnswer` (template idx 3: ¬ę{name_respect}, {topic} —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č “õ—č—Ā“õ–į –∂–į—É–į–Ņ:¬Ľ) and `ExactFact` (template idx 4: ¬ę{topic} –∂–į–Ļ—č–Ĺ–ī–į –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ:¬Ľ). Pre-v4.43.0 only had 3 user-facing variants + Direct; the 2 new variants close the surface-coverage gap with the templates.

**(2) `SentenceFrame` extended with `name_respect: Option<&'a str>`** ‚ÄĒ required by `NameRespectAnswer` rendering. Defaults `None` for all other introducer variants and for callers without a session name; when `Some`, expected to be the respectful surface form already (e.g. ¬ę–Ē”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—ā –ľ—č—Ä–∑–į¬Ľ).

**(3) `pub fn pick_introducer(seed, has_name_respect) -> Introducer`** ‚ÄĒ deterministic introducer pick mirroring the v4.42.x template-rotation algorithm. Pool of 5 when `has_name_respect`, 4 when not (filtering out `NameRespectAnswer`); pool order matches template-array order so `seed % pool.len()` produces the same surface text as before.

**(4) `pub fn compose_introducer(introducer, topic, name_respect, body) -> String`** ‚ÄĒ public composer that wraps a body sentence in the chosen preamble. Threads topic capitalisation and (for `NameRespectAnswer`) the respectful name form. Defensive fallback: if `NameRespectAnswer` called with `name_respect = None`, falls back to `BrieflyAbout` surface to prevent crashes.

**(5) Planner integration via `apply_introducer_migration` helper** ‚ÄĒ called from both `plan_response_with_session` and `plan_response_with_epistemic` after template selection. For key == "unknown.with_grounded_fact", reads `{noun}` + `{fact}` + `{name_respect}` slots from the planner state, calls `pick_introducer` with `rng_seed` + name-respect availability, calls `compose_introducer` with the picked variant, rewrites the `{fact}` slot to the wrapped sentence. No-op for all other template families. Trace records the picked introducer variant for `--trace` mode debugging.

**(6) Template family `unknown.with_grounded_fact` simplified to `["{fact}"]`** ‚ÄĒ single template; the introducer wrap moved from data to code. Other template families using `{fact}` (`unknown.with_grounded_fact.no_causal` / `.no_function`) keep their semantic-hedge wrapping; only the pure-introducer family was migrated.

**(7) 10 new NLG unit tests** verify each `(Introducer √ó topic √ó name_respect √ó body)` combination renders the exact pre-migration surface, and that `pick_introducer` is deterministic + cycle-correct for both 5-variant (with name_respect) and 4-variant (without name_respect) pools.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **890 passing** unchanged (`cargo test --workspace --release`) |
| Adam-dialog lib | **233 passing** (was 223; +10 NLG migration tests) |
| `world_core_multiword_coverage` | ‚úď green |
| Live REPL byte-identical probe | ‚úď 6/6 outputs match v4.42.x line-for-line (–ź–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ / –†–Ķ–ļ—É—Ä—Ā–ł—Ź / –°—č–∑—č“õ—ā—č“õ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź / –ė—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—Ü–ł—Ź / –ö–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā—ā—č“õ —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É / –°—ā–į—ā–ł—Ā—ā–ł–ļ–į) |
| Foundation: 1931 entries / 2182 facts / 43 domains / 26 251 derivations | unchanged ‚úď |
| `cargo fmt --all --check` | clean |
| `cargo check --all-targets` | clean |

### Cadence

Minor ‚ÄĒ Stage A architectural milestone. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: ¬ęnever under-version a kernel-signature feature¬Ľ. The introducer migration:
- Moves a 5-way phrasing rotation from data (templates) to code (NLG enum + composer)
- Extends the `Introducer` enum with 2 new variants + `SentenceFrame` with a new field ‚ÄĒ public API surface change
- Adds 2 new public NLG functions (`pick_introducer`, `compose_introducer`)
- Closes the deferred Stage A bundle 3 carry-forward from v4.42.6

This is a kernel-signature architectural step. Bundle complete:
1. Foundation (v4.42.0)
2. Production migration (v4.42.5)
3. **Introducer migration (v4.43.0)** ‚Üź shipped

Stripe (11) ‚ÄĒ generative AI via agglutinative composition continues. Next: ~v4.50 Stage B (tiny selection weights). Interrogative-mood NLG rules deferred to a future v4.43.x.

## [4.42.9] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Knowledge depth bundle 2 ‚ÄĒ Psychology + Emotions/Society enrichment

**Continuation of v4.42.8 knowledge expansion arc.** New `psychology_basic` domain (22 facts) covers cognition / personality / mental processes ‚ÄĒ concepts adam previously lacked entirely. `emotions` deepens 18 ‚Üí 30 facts with complex emotions (envy / boredom / despair / longing / pleasure) plus a hub-defining `—Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ is_a –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ“Į–Ļ` link bridging emotions into the new psychology hub. `society` deepens 36 ‚Üí 49 facts with civic/political concepts (democracy / republic / elections / rights / freedom / justice / market / civil society / taxes). All facts compose through Stage A `IsACopulaDeclarative` NLG rule.

### Real-REPL probe (live `adam-chat`)

| Query | Response |
|---|---|
| –ü—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ü—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ –į–ī–į–ľ–Ĺ—č“£ —Ā–į–Ĺ–į-—Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ľ—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ–∑-“õ“Ī–Ľ“õ—č–Ĺ –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ." |
| –Ę“Ī–Ľ“ď–į –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–Ę“Ī–Ľ“ď–į ‚ÄĒ ”©–∑—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ —ā”ô–Ĺ –ľ—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ–∑-“õ“Ī–Ľ—č“õ, “õ“Ī–Ĺ–ī—č–Ľ—č“õ –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā –∂–ł—č–Ĺ—ā—č“ď—č –Ī–į—Ä –į–ī–į–ľ." |
| “ö—č–∑“ď–į–Ĺ—č—ą –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "“ö—č–∑“ď–į–Ĺ—č—ą ‚ÄĒ –Ī–į—Ā“õ–į“ď–į –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ą—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė –Ĺ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ —Ā“Į–Ļ—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė –į–ī–į–ľ–ī—č “õ–ł–ľ–į—É —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė." |
| –Ē–Ķ–ľ–ĺ–ļ—Ä–į—ā–ł—Ź –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–Ē–Ķ–ľ–ĺ–ļ—Ä–į—ā–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ —Ö–į–Ľ—č“õ –Ī–ł–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į—Ā“õ–į—Ä—É —ā“Į—Ä—Ė." |

| –ó–Ķ—Ä—Ė–≥—É –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ó–Ķ—Ä—Ė–≥—É ‚ÄĒ “õ—č–∑—č“õ—Ā—č–∑–ī—č“õ—ā–į–Ĺ —ā—É–į—ā—č–Ĺ –į—É—č—Ä –ļ”©“£—Ė–Ľ-–ļ“Į–Ļ." |

### Innovations

**(1) New `psychology_basic.jsonl` domain ‚ÄĒ 22 facts** covering: –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź / —Ā–į–Ĺ–į / –ĺ–Ļ / –Ķ—Ā / “õ–į–Ī—č–Ľ–ī–į—É / –∑–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ / “õ–ł—Ź–Ľ / —ā“Ī–Ľ“ď–į / –ľ—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ–∑ / —ā–Ķ–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā / “õ–į–Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—ā / –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā / –ľ–ĺ—ā–ł–≤–į—Ü–ł—Ź / “õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ / —Ā—ā—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ā / —ć–ľ–ĺ—Ü–ł—Ź / –ļ”©“£—Ė–Ľ-–ļ“Į–Ļ / –ľ—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ–∑-“õ“Ī–Ľ—č“õ / ”ô–ī–Ķ—ā / –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥ / –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ł–ļ–į / —Ä–Ķ—Ą–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—Ā. Each uses `is_a` for clean NLG composition.

**(2) `emotions` deepened 18 ‚Üí 30 facts** (em_019‚Äď030) ‚ÄĒ complex emotions: “õ—č–∑“ď–į–Ĺ—č—ą, –ļ“Į–Ĺ—ą—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ, –∑–Ķ—Ä—Ė–≥—É, –ļ“Į–Ļ–∑–Ķ–Ľ—Ė—Ā, —É–į–Ļ—č–ľ, –ľ“Ī“£, —ą–į—ā—ā—č“õ, —ā–ĺ—Ä—č“ď—É, “õ–į–Ņ–į–Ľ–į–Ĺ—É, –Ľ”ô–∑–∑–į—ā, –∂–į–Ī—č—Ä“õ–į—É + hub bridge `—Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ is_a –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ“Į–Ļ` linking emotions into the psychology hub.

**(3) `society` deepened 36 ‚Üí 49 facts** (soc_041‚Äď053) ‚ÄĒ political/civic concepts: –ī–Ķ–ľ–ĺ–ļ—Ä–į—ā–ł—Ź, —Ä–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ļ–į, —Ā–į–Ļ–Ľ–į—É, “õ“Ī“õ—č“õ, –Ī–ĺ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č“õ, ”ô–ī—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ, –Ī–Ķ–Ļ–Ī—Ė—ā—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ, —Ā–į–Ľ—č“õ, –Ĺ–į—Ä—č“õ, –ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ņ–ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ, –ļ”ô—Ā—Ė–Ņ–ļ–Ķ—Ä, –į–∑–į–ľ–į—ā—ā—č“õ “õ–ĺ“ď–į–ľ + an explicit `–ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ā—ā–ł—ā—É—Ü–ł—Ź is_a –∑–į“£ —ā“Į—Ä—Ė` IsA-hub bridge.

**(4) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 17 new compound entries** required by `world_core_multiword_coverage`: –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ “õ—č–∑–ľ–Ķ—ā / –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ “Į—Ä–ī—Ė—Ā / –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ“Į—ą / –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ“Į–Ļ / —ā“Ī–Ľ“ď–į–Ľ—č“õ “õ–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā / –ľ—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ–∑-“õ“Ī–Ľ—č“õ “Į–Ľ–≥—Ė—Ā—Ė / ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā —ā“Į—Ä—Ė / –Ī–į—Ā“õ–į—Ä—É —ā“Į—Ä—Ė / –ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā —ā“Į—Ä—Ė / –∑–į“£ —ā“Į—Ä—Ė / —Ā–į—Ź—Ā–ł –Ņ—Ä–ĺ—Ü–Ķ—Ā—Ā / “õ–ĺ“ď–į–ľ–ī—č“õ –Ņ—Ä–ł–Ĺ—Ü–ł–Ņ / “õ–ĺ“ď–į–ľ–ī—č“õ –∂–į“ď–ī–į–Ļ / “õ–ĺ“ď–į–ľ–ī—č“õ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ / —ć–ļ–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ–Ķ“£—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ / –ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė –į“õ—č / –į–∑–į–ľ–į—ā—ā—č“õ “õ–ĺ“ď–į–ľ.

**(5) NLG composition path verified end-to-end on 47 new facts** ‚ÄĒ every new psychology/emotions/society fact routes through Stage A `IsACopulaDeclarative` rule; no per-fact templates needed. Live REPL 6/6 on probes spanning all three domains.

**(6) Foundation expansion** ‚ÄĒ 1884 ‚Üí **1931 entries** (+47), 2135 ‚Üí **2182 facts** (+47), 42 ‚Üí **43 domains** (+1: psychology_basic), 25 755 ‚Üí **26 251 derivations** (+496 from R1/R5/R10/R11 chains over the new IsA parents and the `—Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ is_a –Ņ—Ā–ł—Ö–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –ļ“Į–Ļ` bridge).

## [4.42.8] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Knowledge expansion bundle ‚ÄĒ Computer Science fundamentals + Mathematics depth

**Two-domain knowledge expansion driven by adam's growing CS / math conversational reach.** A new `computer_science_basics` domain (30 facts) covers the universal CS vocabulary (algorithms / data structures / complexity / OOP / FP / debugging / API / parallelism). The existing `mathematics_basic` domain extends from 37 ‚Üí 52 facts with **function types** (linear / quadratic), **equation types** (linear / quadratic), **progressions** (arithmetic / geometric), **statistics** (mean / median / variance / probability), **set theory + calculus basics** (derivative / integral). All facts compose through the Stage A NLG `IsACopulaDeclarative` rule ‚ÄĒ typed-frame composition, no per-fact templates.

### Real-REPL probe (live `adam-chat`)

|---|---|
| –ź–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ź–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ ‚ÄĒ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė —ą–Ķ—ą—É “Į—ą—Ė–Ĺ –ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ–ī–į–Ľ–į—ā—č–Ĺ –į–Ĺ—č“õ “õ–į–ī–į–ľ–ī–į—Ä —ā—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–≥—Ė." |

| –ė—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—Ü–ł—Ź –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ė—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—Ü–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–ī–į–ľ–ī–į—Ä–ī—č –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ—ą–Ķ —Ä–Ķ—ā “õ–į–Ļ—ā–į–Ľ–į—É –į—Ä“õ—č–Ľ—č –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ —ą—č“ď–į—Ä—É —ā”ô—Ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė." |
| –°—č–∑—č“õ—ā—č“õ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–°—č–∑—č“õ—ā—č“õ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ y = kx + b —ā“Į—Ä—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–≥—Ė –Ī—Ė—Ä—Ė–Ĺ—ą—Ė –ī”ô—Ä–Ķ–∂–Ķ–Ľ—Ė —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź." |
| –ö–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā—ā—č“õ —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–ö–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā—ā—č“õ —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É ‚ÄĒ –Ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė—Ā—Ė–∑—Ė –Ķ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ—ą—Ė –ī”ô—Ä–Ķ–∂–Ķ–ī–Ķ –ļ–Ķ–∑–ī–Ķ—Ā–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É." |
| –°—ā–į—ā–ł—Ā—ā–ł–ļ–į –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ĺ–Ķ? | "–°—ā–į—ā–ł—Ā—ā–ł–ļ–į ‚ÄĒ –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė –∂–ł–Ĺ–į—É, —ā–į–Ľ–ī–į—É –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä—É–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č—Ā–į—ā—č–Ĺ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č." |

### Innovations

**(1) New `computer_science_basics.jsonl` domain ‚ÄĒ 30 facts** covering the foundational CS vocabulary: –į–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ, –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č, –∂–ł—č–ľ, —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ, —Ā—ā–Ķ–ļ, –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ–ļ, –į“ď–į—ą, –≥—Ä–į—Ą, —Ö–Ķ—ą-–ļ–Ķ—Ā—ā–Ķ, —Ā“Ī—Ä—č–Ņ—ā–į—É, —Ė–∑–ī–Ķ—É, —Ä–Ķ–ļ—É—Ä—Ā–ł—Ź, –ł—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—Ü–ł—Ź, –ļ“Į—Ä–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ (—É–į“õ—č—ā / –∂–į–ī—ā–į—Ä / O(n) / O(log n)), –į–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č / —ā“Ī—Ä–į“õ—ā—č, —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź, –ļ–Ľ–į—Ā—Ā, –ĺ–Ī—ä–Ķ–ļ—ā, –ě–Ď–ü / –§–ü, —ā–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—É, “õ–į—ā–Ķ –∂”©–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—É, –ļ—ć—ą, API, –Ņ–į—Ä–į–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ. Each fact uses `is_a` for clean NLG composition.

**(2) Mathematics depth ‚ÄĒ 15 new facts** in `mathematics_basic.jsonl` (math_038‚Äď052): linear / quadratic functions, linear / quadratic equations, arithmetic / geometric progressions, statistical measures (mean / median / variance), statistics + probability + sets as fields, calculus primitives (derivative / integral). Closes the previous gap where `mathematics_basic` only had 37 facts focused on arithmetic + geometry without algebra-of-functions, statistics, or calculus.

**(3) IsA-hub consolidation** ‚ÄĒ aligned `cs_basics` to use `–ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č` (matching the existing `informatics_basic` form) rather than fragmenting the IsA hub with the singular `–ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č`. –∂–ł—č–ľ / —ā—Ė–∑—Ė–ľ / —Ā—ā–Ķ–ļ / –ļ–Ķ–∑–Ķ–ļ / –į“ď–į—ą / –≥—Ä–į—Ą / —Ö–Ķ—ą-–ļ–Ķ—Ā—ā–Ķ all attach to the same hub used by `info_021..info_028`.

**(4) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` sync ‚ÄĒ 26 new compound entries** required by the `world_core_multiword_coverage` invariant: –ĺ–Ī—ä–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė–≥–Ķ –Ī–į“ď—č—ā—ā–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É, —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–į–Ľ–ī—č“õ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É, –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É –Ņ–į—Ä–į–ī–ł–≥–ľ–į—Ā—č, –į—Ä–ł—Ą–ľ–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ā–ł—Ź, –≥–Ķ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ā–ł—Ź, –Ľ–ĺ–≥–į—Ä–ł—Ą–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ļ“Į—Ä–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ, –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č, –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė, –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā”ô—Ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė, –∂–į–ī—ā–į—Ä –ļ“Į—Ä–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė, —É–į“õ—č—ā –ļ“Į—Ä–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė, –ļ–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā—ā—č“õ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź, –ļ–≤–į–ī—Ä–į—ā—ā—č“õ —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É, —Ā—ā–į—ā–ł—Ā—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ą–į–ľ–į, –į–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ —ā”ô—Ā—Ė–Ľ, “Ī–Ļ—č–ľ–ī–į—Ā—ā—č—Ä—É —ā”ô—Ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė, —Ā—č–∑—č“õ—ā—č“õ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź, —Ā—č–∑—č“õ—ā—č“õ —ā–Ķ“£–ī–Ķ—É, –ļ“Į—Ä–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė—Ā—Ė, –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ą–į–ľ–į, —Ā–į–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä —ā—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–≥—Ė, –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ā–ĺ–Ņ, –ļ“Į—Ä–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ —ā“Į—Ä—Ė, –∂–į–ī—ā–į—Ä —ā“Į—Ä—Ė, –ĺ—Ä—ā–į—ą–į –ľ”ô–Ĺ, “õ–į—ā–Ķ –∂”©–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—É.

**(5) NLG composition path verified end-to-end** ‚ÄĒ every new fact (45 curated additions across two domains) routes through Stage A `IsACopulaDeclarative` rule and surfaces with proper introducer + dash form. Live REPL probe confirms 6/6 queries land on the new facts (cs_001 / cs_012 / cs_013 / math_038 / math_041 / math_048).

**(6) Foundation expansion** ‚ÄĒ 1849 ‚Üí 1884 world_core entries, 2026 ‚Üí 2135 curated facts (+45 from the two-domain expansion plus secondary effects), 25 633 ‚Üí 25 755 derivations (+122 from R1 / R5 / R10 / R11 chains over the new `is_a` parents).

## [4.42.7] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Per-language purpose enrichment + Latin-tech topic-extraction fix

**Closes user note from v4.42.6 transcript** (¬ę–ĺ–Ĺ –ī–ĺ–Ľ–∂–Ķ–Ĺ –∑–Ĺ–į—ā—Ć –ī–Ľ—Ź —á–Ķ–≥–ĺ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ī–Ĺ–į–∑–Ĺ–į—á–Ķ–Ĺ –ļ–į–∂–ī—č–Ļ —Ź–∑—č–ļ¬Ľ). Adds 20 new facts about WHAT FOR each major programming language is used (Python for data science / ML / scripting; Rust for systems / safety; Go for cloud infrastructure; etc.), AND fixes the Latin-tech topic-extraction gap that prevented these facts from being reachable through natural Kazakh phrasings (¬ęPython “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ?¬Ľ / ¬ęJava “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —Ā–į–Ľ–į–ī–į “õ–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į–Ĺ—č–Ľ–į–ī—č?¬Ľ / ¬ęKotlin –Ĺ–Ķ “Į—ą—Ė–Ĺ “õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā?¬Ľ).

### Real-REPL gap closures

| Query | Pre-v4.42.7 | Post-v4.42.7 |
|---|---|---|
| Python “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ? | "–Ę—Ė–Ľ ‚ÄĒ “õ–į—Ä—č–ľ-“õ–į—ā—č–Ĺ–į—Ā “õ“Ī—Ä–į–Ľ—č" (head-noun fallback) | "**Python ‚ÄĒ –ļ–Ķ“£ —ā–į—Ä–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ, –ĺ“õ—É“ď–į “õ–į—Ä–į–Ņ–į–Ļ—č–ľ –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ—Ä–Ņ—Ä–Ķ—ā–į—Ü–ł—Ź–Ľ–į–Ĺ–į—ā—č–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė.**" |
| Java “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —Ā–į–Ľ–į–ī–į “õ–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į–Ĺ—č–Ľ–į–ī—č? | corpus citation about ¬ę—Ā–į–Ľ–į¬Ľ | "**Java –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ļ–ĺ—Ä–Ņ–ĺ—Ä–į—ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė–ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č ”©–∑–į—Ä–į –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č.**" (NEW purpose fact, NLG composition!) |
| Kotlin –Ĺ–Ķ “Į—ą—Ė–Ĺ “õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā? | clarify ("“õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā –∂–į–Ļ—č–Ĺ–ī–į...") | "**Kotlin ‚ÄĒ JVM-–≥–Ķ –į—Ä–Ĺ–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ, Java-–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ “Į–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė –≥–ł–Ī—Ä–ł–ī—ā—Ė–ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė.**" |
| Python ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ? | falls to ¬ę—ā—Ė–Ľ¬Ľ | Python definition |
| Go –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑? | general bańüdar–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä list | "**Go ‚ÄĒ Google ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –ļ–ĺ–ľ–Ņ–ł–Ľ—Ź—Ü–ł—Ź–Ľ–į–Ĺ–į—ā—č–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė.**" |
| HTML / SQL / CSS direct queries | already worked | unchanged ‚úď |

### Innovations

**(1) 20 new purpose facts** in `programming_languages.jsonl` (plang_031-050) covering 16 languages. Each language gets 1-3 facts about its WHAT FOR via `RelatedTo` predicate to a domain object (e.g. ¬ęPython –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č ”©–∑–į—Ä–į –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č¬Ľ, ¬ęRust –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č ”©–∑–į—Ä–į –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č¬Ľ). Plus key-feature facts for some (Java's GC, Rust's ownership). These compose via the v4.42.5 NLG `RelatedToOzaraDeclarative` rule ‚ÄĒ typed-frame composition, no template needed.

**(2) `LATIN_TECH_SUBJECTS` extended with 12 programming-language names** ‚ÄĒ python, java, kotlin, javascript, typescript, ruby, php, swift, go, sql, html, css. Pre-v4.42.7 only `rust` was registered. The other 12 were emitted as world_core subjects but not recognised by the topic-extractor's Latin path, so queries about them fell to head-noun fallback (¬ę—ā—Ė–Ľ¬Ľ / ¬ę—Ā–į–Ľ–į¬Ľ / ¬ę“õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā¬Ľ).

**(3) `latin_with_generic_head_marker` extended with 5 new patterns**:
- `{lang} ‚ÄĒ {head}` (¬ęPython ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ?¬Ľ dash-em)
- `{lang} - {head}` (regular hyphen)
- `{lang} “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ {head}` (¬ęPython “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ?¬Ľ)
- `{lang} “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ {head}–ī–į` (¬ęJava “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —Ā–į–Ľ–į–ī–į...¬Ľ)
- `{lang} “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ {head}–ī–Ķ` (front-vowel locative)
- `{lang} –Ĺ–Ķ “Į—ą—Ė–Ĺ` (¬ęKotlin –Ĺ–Ķ “Į—ą—Ė–Ĺ “õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā?¬Ľ purpose pattern)

These ask "what kind of X is {lang}?" / "what is {lang} for?" ‚ÄĒ topic should be {lang}, not the generic head. Pre-v4.42.7 `first_noun_root` returned the head noun, surfacing tangential definitions.

**(4) `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` sync** ‚ÄĒ 18 new compound domain objects (.net —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / android —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / ios —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / macos —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / unity –ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –Ī“Ī–Ľ—ā—ā—č“õ –ł–Ĺ—Ą—Ä–į“õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ—č–ľ —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –≤–Ķ–Ī-–Ī—ć–ļ—ć–Ĺ–ī —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –≤–Ķ–Ī-—Ą—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ī —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä –Ī–į–∑–į—Ā—č —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –∂–ĺ“ď–į—Ä—č ”©–Ĺ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –ļ–ĺ—Ä–Ņ–ĺ—Ä–į—ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė–ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –ľ–į—ą–ł–Ĺ–į –ĺ“õ—č—ā—É —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ą—Ė–ļ –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė / –ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č / –ĺ“õ—É“ď–į “õ–į—Ä–į–Ņ–į–Ļ—č–ľ —ā—Ė–Ľ / “õ–ĺ“õ—č—Ā –∂–ł–Ĺ–į—É—ą—č) required by `world_core_multiword_coverage` invariant.

## [4.42.6] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-04 ‚ÄĒ Disagreement-detection + language-classification refinement + Stage A roadmap notes

**Driven by 2026-05-04 user dialog test.** Two distinct gaps closed: (a) when the user pushes back on adam's answer (¬ę–°—Ė–∑ “õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑, X ‚ÄĒ Y –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ), pre-v4.42.6 the topic-extraction path simply re-surfaced the disputed fact verbatim ‚ÄĒ felt obtuse; (b) the programming-languages list-summary lumped SQL / HTML / CSS as ¬ę–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė¬Ľ without distinguishing query / markup / style languages. Both addressed. Plus memory roadmap captures three deferred carry-forward items (per-language purpose enrichment, advanced disagreement handling, introducer migration into NLG production path).

### Real-REPL gap closures

| Query | Pre-v4.42.6 | Post-v4.42.6 |
|---|---|---|
| –°—Ė–∑ “õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑, SQL ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā | re-surfaces disputed fact (obtuse) | "**–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ľ, “õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ņ —ā“Ī—Ä“ď–į–Ĺ —ą—č“ď–į—Ä–ľ—č–Ĺ. –Ē”ô–Ľ—Ė—Ä–Ķ–ļ –į–Ļ—ā—Ā–į“£—č–∑, –Ķ—Ā–ļ–Ķ—Ä—ā–ļ—Ė—ą —Ä–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –Ķ—Ā—ā–Ķ —Ā–į“õ—ā–į–Ņ, –ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė –∂–į—É–į–Ņ—ā–į —ā“Į–∑–Ķ—É–≥–Ķ —ā—č—Ä—č—Ā–į–ľ—č–Ĺ.**" |
| –Ď“Ī–Ľ –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā. | re-surfaces previous fact | acknowledgment + invite specifics |
| –°–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ņ —ā“Ī—Ä—Ā—č“£ | re-surfaces previous fact | acknowledgment |
| ”ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ–ī–Ķ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė –Ī–į—Ä? | "–ź–ī–į–ľ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė: Rust (—ā–Ķ—Ä–Ķ“£), Python, ... SQL, HTML, CSS." (lumped) | "–ź–ī–į–ľ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė: Rust (—ā–Ķ—Ä–Ķ“£), Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Kotlin, C, C++, C#, Go, Swift, Ruby, PHP. **–°–ĺ–Ĺ—č–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į—ā–į—Ä –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ SQL (—Ā“Ī—Ä–į–Ĺ—č—Ā —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė), HTML (–Ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė), CSS (—Ā—ā–ł–Ľ—Ć —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė) —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ –ĺ–Ľ–į—Ä –į–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ –∂–į–∑–Ī–į–Ļ–ī—č, —Ā–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č“õ—ā–į–Ĺ —ā–į–∑–į –ľ–į“ď—č–Ĺ–į–ī–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā.**" |

### Innovations

**(1) New `Intent::UserDisagrees`** + companion `IntentKind::UserDisagrees`. Triggered by 9 lexical markers: ¬ę“õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£ / “õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑ / “õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ņ —ā“Ī—Ä / “õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–Ņ / –ī“Ī—Ä—č—Ā –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā / –ĺ–Ľ–į–Ļ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā / –Ī“Ī–Ľ–į–Ļ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā / –Ī“Ī–Ľ “õ–į—ā–Ķ / —Ā—Ė–∑ “õ–į—ā–Ķ / —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į—ā–Ķ¬Ľ. Light detector ‚ÄĒ does NOT extract correction's content (deferred to advanced disagreement handling per memory note).

**(2) `disagreement_ack` template family** ‚ÄĒ 4 variants thanking the user for the correction, inviting specifics, backing off the disputed assertion. Phrasing intentionally avoids re-asserting the disputed fact OR pretending to update belief without doing so (¬ę–Ķ—Ā–ļ–Ķ—Ä—ā–ļ—Ė—ą —Ä–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –Ķ—Ā—ā–Ķ —Ā–į“õ—ā–į–Ņ, –ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė –∂–į—É–į–Ņ—ā–į —ā“Į–∑–Ķ—É–≥–Ķ —ā—č—Ä—č—Ā–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ = "I'll keep this in mind as a note and try to fix it next time").

**(3) Programming-languages list-summary refinement** ‚ÄĒ `rust_181` and `plang_027` updated to clearly separate true programming languages (Rust / C / C++ / Go / Swift / Java / Kotlin / C# / Python / JavaScript / TypeScript / Ruby / PHP) from data languages (SQL ‚ÄĒ query language, HTML ‚ÄĒ markup, CSS ‚ÄĒ style). The text explicitly notes ¬ę–ĺ–Ľ–į—Ä –į–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ –∂–į–∑–Ī–į–Ļ–ī—č, —Ā–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č“õ—ā–į–Ĺ —ā–į–∑–į –ľ–į“ď—č–Ĺ–į–ī–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ (they don't write algorithms, so they're not programming languages in the strict sense).

**(4) Memory roadmap notes for three deferred carry-forwards** in `project_retrieval_not_neural_v2.md`:
- **Per-language purpose enrichment** ‚ÄĒ describe WHAT FOR each language is used (Python for data science / web; Rust for systems / safety; SQL for database queries; etc.). User-noted gap.
- **Advanced disagreement / correction handling** ‚ÄĒ extract correction content + apply belief revision + confidence-aware response. Beyond v4.42.6 light detector.
- **Introducer migration into NLG production path** ‚ÄĒ move introducer-variant choice from templates into planner code; templates simplify to `{fact}`. Architectural cleanup; byte-identical output.
