## [4.36.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ FST coverage: ConverbPerfect + PastReportative (¬ę–∂–į–∑—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ / ¬ę–Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ now route to Hearsay)

**Patch in v4.36+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the v4.36.0 honest-gap carry-forward ‚ÄĒ the ¬ę-{Y}–Ņ(—ā—č)¬Ľ reportative form (¬ę–∂–į–∑—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ / ¬ę–Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ / ¬ę–Ī–į—Ä—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ), which is the natural Kazakh hearsay form most commonly produced in user dialog, previously didn't parse at all. The FST treated only `Tense::PastEvidential` (¬ę-“ď–į–Ĺ/-–≥–Ķ–Ĺ¬Ľ ‚Üí –∂–į–∑“ď–į–Ĺ–ľ—č–Ĺ) as reportative; ¬ę-—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ fell through to no parse, blocking v4.36.0's Hearsay routing.

### What was broken pre-v4.36.5

```
> –Ė–į–∑—č–Ņ—ā—č
sem_frames: (none)               ‚Üź didn't parse
intent: input_evidence: None     ‚Üź Hearsay not detected
response: "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ."
```

The synth side already supported the perfect-converb base `-{Y}–Ņ` (line 421 `VERB_CONV_PERFECT`), but the parser tense enumeration didn't include `ConverbPerfect`, AND there was no tense variant for the ¬ę-—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ reportative (perfect-converb base + literal `-—ā—č` particle).

### Innovations

**(1) New `Tense::PastReportative` variant.** Distinct from `PastEvidential` ‚ÄĒ both encode hearsay/reportative semantics, but the surface forms differ structurally:
- `PastEvidential` = ¬ę–∂–į–∑“ď–į–Ĺ–ľ—č–Ĺ / –Ī–Ķ—Ä–≥–Ķ–Ĺ¬Ľ (`-“ď–į–Ĺ/-–≥–Ķ–Ĺ` participle base + person-style endings)
- `PastReportative` = ¬ę–∂–į–∑—č–Ņ—ā—č / –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ņ—ā—Ė / –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ (`-{Y}–Ņ` perfect-converb base + invariant `-—ā—č` particle, no person ending)

**(2) New synth template `VERB_REPORTATIVE_PAST`** (~10 lines):
```rust
const VERB_REPORTATIVE_PAST: SuffixTemplate = &[
    SuffixAtom::Arch(Archiphoneme::Y),  // —č / —Ė buffer
    SuffixAtom::Literal('–Ņ'),            // perfect converb
    SuffixAtom::Literal('—ā'),            // -—ā—č particle (invariant)
    SuffixAtom::Literal('—č'),
];
```

`synthesise_verb` match arm dispatches `Tense::PastReportative` to this template; no personal-ending slot follows because `-—ā—č` is invariant.

**(3) `SemFrame::from_analysis` maps `PastReportative ‚Üí Some(EvidenceKind::Hearsay)`** alongside the existing `PastEvidential ‚Üí Hearsay` mapping. Both forms now feed the same v4.36.0 Hearsay routing.

**(4) Parser tenses += `ConverbPerfect, PastReportative`** (alongside v4.32.5's `ConverbImperfect`). Two new enumeration entries, ~30 % more parse-time cost per verb token (from 5 to 7 tenses).

### Live verification

| Query | sem_frame trace | Response |
|---|---|---|
| **–Ė–į–∑—č–Ņ—ā—č** | `–∂–į–∑/Verb tense=PastReportative evid=Hearsay` | "–°—Ė–∑–≥–Ķ –į–Ļ—ā—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ —Ā–ł—Ź“õ—ā—č –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–≥—Ė–ľ –∂–ĺ“õ." ‚úď |
| **–Ď–ĺ–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā—č** | `–Ī–ĺ–Ľ/Verb tense=PastReportative evid=Hearsay` | hearsay-aware ‚úď |
| **–ź–Ī–į–Ļ –∂–į–∑—č–Ņ—ā—č** | `–∂–į–∑/Verb tense=PastReportative evid=Hearsay` | hearsay-aware ‚úď |
| **–Ė–į–∑—č–Ņ** (ConverbPerfect bare) | `–∂–į–∑/Verb tense=ConverbPerfect` | now parses (was: no parse) ‚úď |
| –Ė–į–∑“ď–į–Ĺ–ľ—č–Ĺ (control, PastEvidential) | unchanged | unchanged ‚úď |
| –ź–Ī–į–Ļ –∂–į–∑–ī—č (control, PastDefinite) | unchanged | unchanged grounded fact ‚úď |

### Real-world battery (v4.36.5)

| Aggregate | v4.36.5 | őĒ vs v4.36.0 |
|---|---|---|
| Mean latency | **194.6 ms** | -127 ms (mostly OS file-cache warmth between immediate-back-to-back runs; not a true performance improvement ‚ÄĒ adding 2 tenses to enumeration costs ~30 % more parse work per verb token) |
| Mean max RSS | **177.1 MB** | +1.3 MB (within noise) |
| Coherent responses | **26/26 = 100 %** | matched |
| Neg-consumed | **5/5** | matched |
| Mod-consumed | **2/4** | matched (3/4 with cases 19/20/21 visibly modal-aware in v4.35.5; battery script case-sensitivity bug undercounts) |

The latency drop is suspicious ‚ÄĒ adding tenses should slow parsing, not speed it. Most likely OS file-cache effect after immediate `extract_facts` + `run_reasoner` runs. Honest framing: not claiming a perf improvement; the architectural cost is +2 tenses in enumeration.

### Known FST gaps still present (carry-forward to v4.36.6+)

- **¬ę–į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ short-form 1sg**: ¬ę–į–Ľ + Present + 1sg¬Ľ synth produces ¬ę–į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ (long form), not the contracted ¬ę–į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ. Adding short-form personal-ending alternates is invasive (touches the personal-ending synthesis match). Defer.
- **¬ę–ĺ“õ–ł¬Ľ back-vowel converb**: `surface_could_contain_root` requires root prefix-match, but vowel-final back-vowel verbs change shape during converb derivation (–ĺ“õ—č ‚Üí –ĺ“õ–ł), breaking prefix-match. Closing this requires either pre-stripping final —č/—Ė before lookup, or stem-alternation paradigm matcher (mirroring `pronoun_paradigm`).

Both were originally promised for v4.36.5 but turn out to need separate FST architecture work. Honest re-scope: v4.36.5 is **¬ę-—č–Ņ—ā—č¬Ľ reportative + ConverbPerfect parsing**; ¬ę–į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ / ¬ę–ĺ“õ–ł¬Ľ ‚Üí v4.36.6+.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **865 passing** unchanged |
| Real-world battery | **26/26 = 100 %** coherent |
| facts.json | 1935 ‚Üí 1934 (-1 RelatedTo from new ConverbPerfect parse path triggering a guard on a previously-unparseable sentence; IsA 1472 unchanged) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 ‚úď** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 ‚úď** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 ‚úď** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |

### Pipeline impact

- 1 new `Tense::PastReportative` enum variant
- 1 new synth template `VERB_REPORTATIVE_PAST` (~12 lines)
- 1 new match arm in `synthesise_verb`
- 1 line in SemFrame evidence mapping (PastReportative ‚Üí Hearsay)
- 2 new entries in parser tense enumeration (ConverbPerfect, PastReportative)
- facts/derived_facts regeneration

Cadence: patch ‚ÄĒ closes one specific architectural gap (Reportative path); two related gaps (¬ę–į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ / ¬ę–ĺ“õ–ł¬Ľ) defer due to separate FST architecture cost.

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.36.6** ‚ÄĒ short-form 1sg ¬ę–į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ variant + back-vowel converb ¬ę–ĺ“õ–ł¬Ľ synthesis fix.
- **v4.36.7** ‚ÄĒ EvidenceKind consumption on extraction side (refuse extraction when sentence has Hearsay evidential).

## [4.36.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ Third matcher migration + first EvidenceKind consumption (Hearsay hedging)

**First minor in v4.36+ semantic-IR arc.** Two architectural completions in one release:
1. Third extract_facts matcher migration ‚ÄĒ `locative_lives_in` joins `copula_is_a` (v4.34.5) and `nominal_conjunction` (v4.35.0) on the SemFrame stream, with the same dual-guard pattern (negation + modality refusal).
2. **First EvidenceKind consumption** ‚ÄĒ Hearsay-marked utterances now route to a respectful hedging template family instead of asserting flat facts about the topic. This is the third SemFrame field to influence user-facing answer text after Polarity (v4.33.5) and Modality (v4.34.7).

### Innovations

**(1) `locative_lives_in` signature migration**: `+ sem_frames: &[SemFrame]`. Mirrors v4.34.5's `copula_is_a` and v4.35.0's `nominal_conjunction`. Same dual guards: refuse extraction when sentence has either Polarity::Negated on a noun-class frame OR any Modality on any frame. For LivesIn this protects against modal claims like ¬ęX Y-–ī–į —ā“Ī—Ä—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ¬Ľ ("X needs to live in Y") being mistakenly extracted as factual residency.

**(2) `Intent::Unknown.input_evidence: Option<EvidenceKind>` field.** Third SemFrame-derived field on the canonical Intent type (after `noun_hint_polarity` v4.33.5 and `input_modality` v4.34.7). Default `None` preserves all pre-v4.36.0 routing exactly. Propagated through `semantics`, `verifier`, and 8 test sites.

**(3) `Conversation::turn` populates `input_evidence`** by walking the SemFrame stream for any non-None evidence ‚ÄĒ independent of noun_hint, like modality. Auto-derivation flows: FST `Tense::PastEvidential` ‚Üí `SemFrame.evidence: Some(EvidenceKind::Hearsay)` ‚Üí `Intent.input_evidence: Some(Hearsay)`.

**(4) Planner Hearsay routing.** New short-circuit AFTER polarity and modality checks (those have higher priority ‚ÄĒ when they fire on the same input, the more salient signal wins):
```rust
if matches!(input_evidence, Some(EvidenceKind::Hearsay)) {
    return "unknown.with_hearsay_hedge";
}
```

**(5) Epistemic-override bypass for Hearsay.** Mirrors v4.34.0's polarity bypass and v4.34.7's modality bypass. Hearsay-marked Unknowns skip `unknown.tentative` override ‚ÄĒ the hedging template family is already a tentative-shape response.

**(6) New template family `unknown.with_hearsay_hedge`** in `v1.toml` ‚ÄĒ 7 templates that hedge the response by echoing the user's evidential framing back:
- "–°—Ė–∑–≥–Ķ –į–Ļ—ā—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ —Ā–ł—Ź“õ—ā—č –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–≥—Ė–ľ –∂–ĺ“õ."
- "{noun} –∂–į–Ļ–Ľ—č —Ā—Ė–∑–≥–Ķ –į–Ļ—ā—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—É—č –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ, –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —Ä–į—Ā—ā–į–Ļ –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ."
- "–°—Ė–∑ –Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–≥–Ķ–Ĺ —Ā”©–∑ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ā–į –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ, –Ī—Ė—Ä–į“õ –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā–Ķ–ļ—Ā–Ķ—Ä–Ķ –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ."

Templates use ¬ę—Ā—Ė–∑–≥–Ķ –į–Ļ—ā—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ / –Ķ—Ā—ā—Ė–ī—Ė“£—Ė–∑ / –į–Ļ—ā—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ ”ô“£–≥—Ė–ľ–Ķ¬Ľ to mark the source as the user's hearsay, not adam's first-hand knowledge.

## [4.35.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ Modal-without-noun: battery case 21 ¬ę–Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ closes

**Patch in v4.35+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes battery case 21 ‚ÄĒ declarative ability claim ¬ę–Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ ("I can write") that has been falling to "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ" since v4.32.5 because no `noun_hint` was extracted (verb-only sentence) and v4.34.7's modality routing required both modality AND noun_hint. v4.35.5 relaxes the requirement at three coupling points: planner routing, epistemic-override bypass, and Conversation::turn population.

### What was broken pre-v4.35.5

```
> –Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ
–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ.
                  ^ no noun_hint ‚Üí modality routing skipped ‚Üí tentative fallback
```

Modality was correctly detected at SemFrame layer (`–∂–į–∑/Verb tense=ConverbImperfect mod=Ability` in `--trace`), but three coupling points blocked end-to-end consumption:
1. `Conversation::turn` populated `input_modality` only inside the `noun_hint: Some(_)` arm
2. Planner routing required `input_modality.is_some() && noun_hint.is_some()`
3. Epistemic-override bypass required `noun_hint: Some(_), input_modality: Some(_)`

### Innovations

**(1) `Conversation::turn` modality population moved out of `noun_hint`-Some guard.** Polarity is per-noun (no noun ‚Üí no polarity to copy), but modality is on the lexical verb frame and independent of `noun_hint`. The pre-v4.35.5 nesting silently skipped population for verb-only modal claims.

**(2) Planner modality routing relaxed.** Drop the `noun_hint.is_some()` requirement; modality alone routes to the appropriate `unknown.with_modal_*` family.

**(3) Epistemic-override bypass relaxed.** Same relaxation in the override block ‚ÄĒ when `input_modality: Some(_)`, base_key wins regardless of `noun_hint`.

**(4) +3 no-noun template variants per modal family** (9 templates total added). For verb-only modal claims, `template_is_fillable` filters to bare-modality templates that don't reference `{noun}`. Examples:
- Necessity bare: ¬ę–ė”ô, —ą—č–Ĺ—č–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā –Ĺ”ô—Ä—Ā–Ķ –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ.¬Ľ, ¬ę–°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –ĺ–Ļ—č“£—č–∑ –ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ–ī—č ‚ÄĒ –Ī“Ī–Ľ –ľ–į“£—č–∑–ī—č.¬Ľ
- Possibility bare: ¬ę–ė”ô, –ľ“Ī–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—É—č —č“õ—ā–ł–ľ–į–Ľ.¬Ľ
- Ability bare: ¬ę–Ė–į“õ—Ā—č –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ, –ĺ–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ “õ–į–Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—ā—Ė“£—Ė–∑ –Ī–į—Ä.¬Ľ, ¬ę–ė”ô, —Ā—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ –ĺ–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ –Ī–į—Ä –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ.¬Ľ

### Live verification ‚ÄĒ case 21 closes

| Query | v4.35.0 | v4.35.5 |
|---|---|---|
| **–Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ** | "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ." | "**–Ė–į“õ—Ā—č –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ, —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–≥—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ľ.**" |
| –ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ –ĺ“õ—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ (control, with noun) | "–ė”ô, –ľ–į“£—č–∑–ī—č –ľ”ô—Ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ." | unchanged ‚úď |
| –Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā (control, polarity precedence) | "–®—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ." | unchanged ‚úď |
| “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–Ļ—ā (control, no modality) | grounded fact | unchanged ‚úď |

### Known FST gaps still present (carry-forward)

Two ability variants do NOT close in v4.35.5:
- ¬ę–°”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ `—Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ` parses as ConverbImperfect ‚úď, but `–į–Ľ–į–ľ` (short 1sg form without -—č–Ĺ) doesn't parse ‚Üí only one frame in stream ‚Üí ability detector can't fire (needs both lexical-verb + –į–Ľ frames).
- ¬ę–ě“õ–ł –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ `–ĺ“õ–ł` (converb of back-vowel verb ¬ę–ĺ“õ—č¬Ľ) doesn't parse ‚Üí same.

These are FST coverage gaps in the v4.32.5 ConverbImperfect enumeration ‚ÄĒ short-form-1sg ¬ę–į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ variant isn't enumerated, and back-vowel verb converb ¬ę–ĺ“õ–ł¬Ľ isn't synthesised correctly. Documented as **v4.36+ FST coverage** alongside the remaining 8 missing parser tenses.

### Real-world battery (v4.35.5)

| Aggregate | v4.35.5 | őĒ vs v4.35.0 |
|---|---|---|

| Mean max RSS | **175.9 MB** | -0.9 MB (within noise) |
| Coherent responses | **26/26 = 100 %** | matched |
| Mod-consumed (visible) | **3/4** (cases 19, 20, **21**) | **+1** vs v4.35.0's 2/4 |

- 1 simpler match arm in epistemic-override bypass

- **v4.36.0** ‚ÄĒ third extract_facts matcher migration (likely `locative_lives_in` or `dative_goes_to`); first **EvidenceKind consumption** in templates (Hearsay-marked claims should hedge evidentiality in response).
- **v4.36.5** ‚ÄĒ FST coverage: short-form 1sg ¬ę–į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ variant + back-vowel converb ¬ę–ĺ“õ–ł¬Ľ synthesis fix (closes ¬ę–°”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į–ľ¬Ľ, ¬ę–ě“õ–ł –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ).

## [4.35.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ Second extract_facts matcher migration + modality guard on extraction side

**First minor in v4.35+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the substrate-then-consume cycle for **Modality on the extraction side** (mirroring v4.34.7's dialog-side consumption). Migrates `nominal_conjunction` to consume `&[SemFrame]` (second matcher to do so after `copula_is_a` in v4.34.5). Retroactively adds a modality guard to `copula_is_a` for symmetry. Both matchers now refuse extraction when the sentence carries either sentence-level negation (Polarity::Negated) OR periphrastic modality (Modality::Necessity / Possibility / Ability). The principle: modal claims are normative or epistemic, not factual assertions; extracting them as graph facts loses their modal nature.

### Innovations

**(1) `nominal_conjunction` signature migration**: `+ sem_frames: &[SemFrame]`. Mirrors v4.34.5's `copula_is_a` migration. Wired through `extract_facts` dispatch.

**(2) Dual guards on `nominal_conjunction`**:
- **Negation guard**: refuse RelatedTo extraction when any noun-class frame has `polarity == Negated`.
- **Modality guard**: refuse extraction when any frame has `modality.is_some()`.

Both guards skip the whole matcher when ANY frame in the stream carries the signal ‚ÄĒ conservative, mirrors `copula_is_a` behavior.

**(3) Retroactive modality guard on `copula_is_a`** for symmetry. Pre-v4.35.0, `copula_is_a` had only the negation guard (v4.34.5). v4.35.0 adds the same modality check, completing the dual-guard pattern across both matchers.

**(4) `Modality` consumption on extraction side closes the second substrate-then-consume cycle:**

| Field | Population | Consumption (dialog) | Consumption (extraction) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Polarity** | v4.33.0 | v4.33.5 | v4.34.5 |
| **Modality** | v4.32.0 (N/P) + v4.32.5 (Ability) | v4.34.7 | **v4.35.0** |
| Evidence | v4.31.0 | not yet | not yet |
| Relation | placeholder | n/a | n/a |

### Corpus impact

Re-running `extract_facts` after both guards:

| Predicate | v4.34.7 | v4.35.0 | őĒ |
|---|---|---|---|
| IsA (Grammar) | 1472 | 1472 | unchanged (copula_is_a guards rejected zero IsA ‚ÄĒ modality / negation didn't appear in IsA-shaped sentences) |
| RelatedTo (Grammar) | 65 | **63** | **-2** (nominal_conjunction guards rejected 2 sentences with negation, e.g. ¬ę–ě–Ĺ—ą–į –į—É“õ—č–ľ–ī—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –∂–Ķ—Ä–Ľ–Ķ—Ä–ī–Ķ —ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–ļ—Ė –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ą–į–Ļ ”©—Ā—Ė—Ä—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė¬Ľ) |
| Other predicates | unchanged | unchanged | ‚ÄĒ |

The 2 rejected RelatedTo facts came from sentences where ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ appeared as part of an adjective modifier earlier in the sentence (not directly negating the related-to pair). Conservative refusal is the safe move ‚ÄĒ extracting in this case would imply the source sentence asserts the relation, but the negation context muddies that.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **862 ‚Üí 865 passing** (+3 new tests: copula_refuses_when_sem_frame_has_modality, nominal_conjunction_refuses_when_sem_frame_has_negated_noun, nominal_conjunction_refuses_when_sem_frame_has_modality) |
| facts.json | 1937 ‚Üí **1935** (-2 RelatedTo from new nominal_conjunction guards; IsA 1472 unchanged) |
| derived_facts.json | regenerated cleanly |
| Real-world 26-query battery | **26/26 = 100 % coherent** unchanged |
| Mean latency | **320.3 ms** (-1.2 ms vs v4.34.7, within noise) |
| Mean RSS | **176.8 MB** (+0.8 MB, within noise) |
| Neg-consumed rate | **5/5 = 100 %** unchanged |
| Mod-consumed rate | **2/4** unchanged (cases 19, 20 visibly modal-aware) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 ‚úď** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 ‚úď** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 ‚úď** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |

### Pipeline impact

- 1 modality guard added to `copula_is_a` (~12 lines)
- 1 signature change to `nominal_conjunction` (+ `sem_frames` parameter)
- 2 guards (negation + modality) added to `nominal_conjunction` (~30 lines)
- 1 dispatch update in `extract_facts`
- 3 test-site call updates for `nominal_conjunction` (mechanical: insert `&[]`)
- 3 new unit tests
- facts/derived_facts regeneration (-2 RelatedTo facts)

Cadence: minor ‚ÄĒ closes second substrate-then-consume cycle, completing Modality coverage across both pipeline directions. Two of three SemFrame fields now have fully-closed cycles (Polarity, Modality).

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.35.5** ‚ÄĒ handle modal claims without `noun_hint` (battery case 21 ¬ę–Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ verb-only modal); EvidenceKind consumption (Hearsay-marked claims should hedge evidentiality in response).
- **v4.36.0** ‚ÄĒ third extract_facts matcher migration (likely `locative_lives_in` or `dative_goes_to`); gradual fleet-wide migration.
- **v4.37+** ‚ÄĒ remaining 8 missing parser tenses (ConverbPerfect, 3 participles, FutureIntentional, FuturePossible, Conditional, Imperative); battery edge case for ¬ęX –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –Ņ–Ķ?¬Ľ question form.

## [4.34.7] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ First Modality consumption: per-modality template families (Necessity / Possibility / Ability)

**Patch in v4.34+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the substrate-then-consume cycle for the **second** SemFrame field ‚ÄĒ `Modality`. Modality has been populated since v4.32.0 (Necessity / Possibility) and v4.32.5 (Ability), but the response generator ignored it. v4.34.7 wires three per-modality template families that engage with the user's modal claim instead of asserting a generic fact about the noun_hint.

### What was broken pre-v4.34.7

```
> –ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ –ĺ“õ—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ.
–ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č “õ—č—Ā“õ–į—ą–į –į–Ļ—ā—Ā–į–ľ: –ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ ‚ÄĒ ”ô–ī–Ķ–Ī–ł–Ķ—ā –ļ”©–∑—Ė.
                          ‚ÜĎ ignores user's necessity claim about reading

> –Ď“Ī–Ľ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—É –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ?
–Ď–ĺ–Ľ—É —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –ľ—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—č –į–Ļ—ā–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ: ¬ę–Ė–ĺ“ď–į—Ä—č –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ —Ā–į–Ņ–į—Ā—č...¬Ľ
                          ‚ÜĎ ignores user's possibility question
```

Modality was correctly detected at SemFrame layer (`mod=Necessity`, `mod=Possibility` in `--trace`) but no consumer read the field, so routing fell through to standard `unknown.with_grounded_fact`/`with_evidence`.

### Innovations

**(1) `Intent::Unknown.input_modality: Option<Modality>` field.** Second SemFrame-derived field on the canonical Intent type (after v4.33.5's `noun_hint_polarity`). Default `None` preserves all pre-v4.34.7 routing exactly. Propagated through `semantics::interpret_text_with_lexicon`, `verifier::strip_evidence`, and 5 test-site Intent::Unknown constructors.

**(2) `Conversation::turn` populates from any sem_frame with modality.** Unlike polarity (which is on the same frame as `noun_hint`), modality is on the **lexical verb** frame ‚ÄĒ a different word from the noun_hint. So we walk the entire sem_frames stream and pick the first non-None modality:
```rust
*input_modality = sem_frames.iter().find_map(|f| f.modality);
```

**(3) Per-modality planner routing.** New planner short-circuit BEFORE the standard Unknown routing chain, AFTER the polarity short-circuit (negation has higher priority ‚ÄĒ when both fire on rare ¬ęX V –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ, negation wins):
```rust
if noun_hint.is_some() {
    if let Some(modality) = input_modality {
        return match modality {
            Modality::Necessity => "unknown.with_modal_necessity",
            Modality::Possibility => "unknown.with_modal_possibility",
            Modality::Ability => "unknown.with_modal_ability",
        };
    }
}
```

**(4) Epistemic-override bypass for modality.** Same pattern as v4.34.0's polarity bypass: when `input_modality.is_some()`, return None from override block so base_key wins. Otherwise EpistemicStatus::Tentative would force `unknown.tentative` over the modal-aware family.

**(5) Three new template families** in `v1.toml`, 5 templates each:
- `unknown.with_modal_necessity` ‚ÄĒ acknowledges the necessity claim ("–ė”ô, –ľ–į“£—č–∑–ī—č –ľ”ô—Ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ", "–Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ—č–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ", "–ė”ô, –Ī“Ī–Ľ “õ–į–∂–Ķ—ā –Ĺ”ô—Ä—Ā–Ķ")
- `unknown.with_modal_possibility` ‚ÄĒ acknowledges the possibility ("–ú“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ, –ł”ô", "–ú“Ī–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ –Ī–į—Ä –ī–Ķ–Ņ –ĺ–Ļ–Ľ–į—É—č“£—č–∑ –ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ–ī—č")
- `unknown.with_modal_ability` ‚ÄĒ acknowledges the ability claim ("–Ė–į“õ—Ā—č –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ, —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–≥—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ľ", "–ě–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –Ĺ”ô—Ä—Ā–Ķ —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ—É ‚ÄĒ –∂–į“õ—Ā—č “õ–į—Ā–ł–Ķ—ā")

### Live verification ‚ÄĒ visible behavior change

| Query | v4.34.5 | v4.34.7 |
|---|---|---|
| **–ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ –ĺ“õ—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ** | "–ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ ‚ÄĒ ”ô–ī–Ķ–Ī–ł–Ķ—ā –ļ”©–∑—Ė." (definition) | "**–ė”ô, –ľ–į“£—č–∑–ī—č –ľ”ô—Ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ.**" (necessity-aware) |
| **–Ď“Ī–Ľ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—É –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ?** | "–Ď–ĺ–Ľ—É —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č: ¬ę–Ė–ĺ“ď–į—Ä—č –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ...¬Ľ" (corpus quote) | "**–ú“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ, –ł”ô.**" (possibility-aware) |
| **–Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā** (control) | "–®—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ." | unchanged (polarity has higher priority) ‚úď |
| **“ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–Ļ—ā** (control) | grounded fact about Kazakhstan | unchanged (no modality) ‚úď |

- Case 21: no `noun_hint` extracted (just verb+aux, no content noun) ‚ÄĒ modality routing requires both modality AND noun_hint. Falls to "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ" as before. v4.35+ work to handle noun-less modal claims.

- **v4.35.5** ‚ÄĒ handle modal claims without `noun_hint` (case 21 ¬ę–Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ verb-only modal); EvidenceKind consumption (Hearsay-marked claims should hedge evidentiality).

## [4.34.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ First extract_facts pattern matcher migration: copula_is_a consumes &[SemFrame], refuses negated extraction

**Patch in v4.34+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes a long-standing carry-forward (originally promised v4.32.5, then v4.34.0). First time a corpus-extraction pattern matcher reads from the `&[SemFrame]` stream ‚ÄĒ substrate-then-consume cycle now closed for the **extraction side** of the pipeline (it was already closed for the **dialog side** at v4.33.5). Negation-aware extraction guards against future corpus sentences where ¬ęX ‚ÄĒ Y –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ would otherwise mis-extract as ¬ęX IsA Y¬Ľ.

### Innovations

**(1) New `build_sem_frames(text, lexicon)` helper** in `crates/adam-reasoning/src/lib.rs`. Mirrors the `Conversation::turn` build sequence used by the dialog runtime since v4.33.5: tokenize whitespace-stripped text ‚Üí FST-analyse each token ‚Üí project to SemFrame ‚Üí run detector chain (sentential negation; periphrastic + ability modality also included for cheap symmetry). Designed to be the **single source of truth** for SemFrame views inside `extract_facts`: as more matchers migrate, they share this single computed stream instead of re-analysing the same tokens individually.

**(2) `copula_is_a` signature migration: `+ sem_frames: &[SemFrame]`.** Old `_parses: &[Analysis]` parameter preserved (still unused by this matcher; kept for sibling-matcher compatibility ‚ÄĒ they all share the same vestigial parameter). New `sem_frames` parameter is the actual SemFrame stream from `build_sem_frames`. First extract_facts matcher to consume this stream.

**(3) Negation guard inside `copula_is_a`:**
```rust
let any_negated_noun = sem_frames.iter().any(|f| {
    f.polarity == Polarity::Negated
        && matches!(f.pos, PosTag::Noun | PosTag::Adjective | PosTag::Pronoun | PosTag::Numeral)
});
if any_negated_noun { return; }
```

When the sentence carries sentence-level negation on a noun-class predicate, refuse IsA extraction entirely. Conservative ‚ÄĒ skips the whole matcher even when negation is on a different clause / sub-phrase. Audit of the v4.34.0 committed graph showed **zero** IsA Grammar facts whose source text contains ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ, so this guard is a forward-looking safety net rather than a fix for an existing wrong fact. Synthetic test ¬ę–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ confirms: pre-v4.34.5 would extract ¬ę–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź IsA “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ¬Ľ (wrong); v4.34.5 refuses.

### Substrate-then-consume cycle now closed for extraction

For the polarity feature, both directions of the pipeline now consume the SemFrame field:

| Side | Population | Consumption |
|---|---|---|
| **Dialog** (response generation) | v4.33.0 (sentential negation detector) | **v4.33.5** (`unknown.with_negated_topic` template family) |
| **Extraction** (corpus ‚Üí facts) | v4.33.0 (same detector, run inside `build_sem_frames`) | **v4.34.5** (negation guard in `copula_is_a`) |

Same architectural pattern queued for `Modality` (Necessity / Possibility / Ability ‚ÄĒ already populated in v4.32.0‚Äďv4.32.5; consumption v4.34.7+).

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **860 ‚Üí 862 passing** (+2 copula negation tests: `copula_refuses_when_sem_frame_has_negated_noun`, `copula_fires_when_no_negation_in_sem_frames`) |
| facts.json | 1934 ‚Üí **1937** (+3 from other matchers benefiting from v4.32.5 ConverbImperfect; **IsA count 1472 unchanged** confirming negation guard didn't reject any current fact, as audit predicted) |
| derived_facts.json | 6151 ‚Üí 6153 |
| Real-world 26-query battery | **26/26 = 100 % coherent** unchanged |
| Neg-consumed rate | **5/5 = 100 %** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 ‚úď** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 ‚úď** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 ‚úď** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |
| Mean latency | **324.2 ms** (+0.8 ms vs v4.34.0; build_sem_frames overhead per sample, within noise) |
| Mean RSS | **176.9 MB** (+1.0 MB, within noise) |

### What's NOT in v4.34.5

- **Other matchers (`locative_lives_in`, `dative_goes_to`, `possessive_has`, `nominal_conjunction`, `domain_membership`, `structural_part_of`)** still take `_parses: &[Analysis]` (unused) and don't consume sem_frames. Migration is per-matcher; each will get its own release as concrete sem_frame consumption value emerges.
- **Modality consumption in templates** ‚ÄĒ still v4.34.7 work.

### Pipeline impact

- 1 new helper `build_sem_frames` in lib.rs (~25 lines)
- 1 line added in `extract_facts` to compute sem_frames
- 1 signature change to `copula_is_a` (+`sem_frames` parameter)
- 1 negation-guard block in `copula_is_a` (~12 lines)
- 12 test-site call updates (mechanical: insert `&[]` for new parameter)
- 2 new unit tests
- facts/derived_facts regeneration

Cadence: patch ‚ÄĒ small migration of one matcher; the architectural pattern (single `build_sem_frames` + per-matcher consumption) is the durable payload, not the line count.

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.34.7** ‚ÄĒ first **Modality consumption** in templates: when input has `Modality::Necessity` on lexical predicate (e.g. ¬ęX –ĺ“õ—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ¬Ľ), route to `unknown.with_modal_acknowledge` family responding "–ł”ô, X-—ā—Ė V-—É –Ņ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ľ—č" instead of asserting a generic fact about X.
- **v4.35.0** ‚ÄĒ second extract_facts matcher migration (likely `nominal_conjunction` for RelatedTo extraction, since it benefits from sem_frame negation check the same way `copula_is_a` does).
- **v4.36+** ‚ÄĒ remaining 8 missing parser tenses (ConverbPerfect, 3 participles, FutureIntentional, FuturePossible, Conditional, Imperative); other particle-copula extensions if real REPL surfaces a need.

## [4.34.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ Parser particle-copula extension + epistemic-override polarity bypass (battery edge case 18 closed)

**First minor in v4.34+ semantic-IR arc.** Closes the FST coverage gap that blocked inflected ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ forms from parsing, and the planner-routing gap that let `EpistemicStatus::Tentative` override v4.33.5's polarity-aware routing. Together these two fixes finally give battery edge case 18 (¬ę–°–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ) the response it deserves ‚ÄĒ respectful acknowledgment instead of a generic "–Ď”ô–Ľ–ļ—Ė–ľ, X —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į" fallback that's been stuck since the 2026-05-02 live REPL session was first captured.

### What was broken pre-v4.34.0

Two distinct issues stacked:

**(a) FST coverage:** ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–∑ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė–∑¬Ľ (the predicate-copula inflected forms of ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ) didn't parse at all. `parser::analyse` dispatched `particle` POS through the catch-all branch which only emits an analysis when `entry.root == surface` ‚ÄĒ bare ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ worked, inflected forms fell off entirely. So ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ X –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ lost the ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ token and v4.33.0's `populate_sentential_negation` had nothing to fire on.

**(b) Planner override:** even when bare ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ fired (case 23 ¬ę–Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ worked since v4.33.5), an inflected case like ¬ę–°–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ once issue (a) was fixed ‚ÄĒ would STILL fall to the `unknown.tentative` template family. The reason: `EpistemicStatus::Tentative` triggers an override (`base_key` ignored, `unknown.tentative` returned) when the Intent has a noun_hint but no grounded_fact / example / reasoning_chain. v4.33.5's `unknown.with_negated_topic` routing happened in `plan_response_with_session::base_key` ‚ÄĒ but the override block in `plan_response_with_epistemic` ran AFTER and unconditionally won.

### Innovations

**(1) Parser particle-copula extension** (`crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/parser.rs`). New surgical branch:
```rust
"particle" if entry.root == "–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā" => {
    try_noun_analyses(surface, entry, &mut out);
    // bare-form preservation guard
}
```

Dispatches ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ through `try_noun_analyses`, which already enumerates the Predicate copulas (P1Sg / P2SgInformal / P2SgPolite / P1Pl / P2PlInformal / P2PlPolite). Other particles (–Ī–į / –Ī–Ķ / –ľ–į / –ľ–Ķ / –Ņ–į / –Ņ–Ķ question particles, –ī–į / –ī–Ķ connectors, –Ķ–ī—Ė / –Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ copulas) keep bare-only behavior ‚ÄĒ surgical scope keeps blast radius minimal. Bare-form path explicitly preserved by a guard insert.

Post-fix:
```
> –ú–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sem_frames:
‚Ēā   [0] –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ/Pronoun
‚Ēā   [1] –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č/Noun polarity=Negated
‚Ēā   [2] –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā/Function pred=P1Sg
```

**(2) Epistemic-override polarity bypass** (`crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs`). New first match arm in the override block:
```rust
(Intent::Unknown {
    noun_hint: Some(_),
    noun_hint_polarity: Polarity::Negated,
    ..
}, _) => None,  // bypass override entirely
```

When polarity is `Negated`, the base_key (computed by `plan_response_with_session` as `unknown.with_negated_topic`) wins regardless of epistemic status. Reason: the user is denying X's predicate role, not asking for evidence; a "tentative" template like "–Ď”ô–Ľ–ļ—Ė–ľ, X —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į" would force the user back into asserting X ‚ÄĒ exactly what they just denied.

### Live verification ‚ÄĒ battery edge case 18 closed

| Query | v4.33.5 | v4.34.0 |
|---|---|---|
| **–°–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?** | "–Ď”ô–Ľ–ļ—Ė–ľ, –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į." (tentative) | "**–Ď–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ.**" (acknowledgment) |
| –ú–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ | (didn't parse ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ) | "**–Ď–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ.**" |
| –Ď—Ė–∑ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–∑ | (didn't parse ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–∑¬Ľ) | "**–Ď–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ.**" |

Battery edge case 18 ‚ÄĒ surfaced in the 2026-05-02 live REPL session and tracked through every release since v4.30.0 ‚ÄĒ is finally closed.

### Real-world battery (v4.34.0)

26 queries (extends v4.33.5's 24 with 2 inflected-negation cases):

| Aggregate | v4.34.0 | őĒ vs v4.33.5 |
|---|---|---|
| Mean latency | **323.4 ms** | +0.9 ms (parser cost: one new dispatch branch + emes-specific noun-analysis enumeration) |
| Mean max RSS | **175.9 MB** | +0.8 MB (noise) |
| Coherent responses | **26/26 = 100 %** | matched |
| **Neg-consumed rate** | **5/5 = 100 %** | **+3 over v4.33.5's 2/5** (cases 18, 25, 26 now route correctly) |

Five negation cases (cases 18, 23, 24, 25, 26) all route through `unknown.with_negated_topic`. Modal-detect 4/4 unchanged.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **860 passing** unchanged |
| Real-world 26-query battery | **26/26 = 100 % coherent** |
| Neg-consumed rate | **5/5 = 100 %** (up from 2/5; +3 inflected forms now consumed) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 ‚úď** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 ‚úď** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 ‚úď** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |

### Honest framing

This release closes a 5-version-old carry-forward (battery edge case 18 surfaced in the original 2026-05-02 live REPL session that drove v4.30.0). The fix is a matched architectural pair: parser coverage + planner-override-priority. Either alone wouldn't close the gap; both together do.

Other particles (–Ī–į / –Ī–Ķ / –ľ–į / –ľ–Ķ / –Ņ–į / –Ņ–Ķ question particles, –ī–į / –ī–Ķ connectors) keep their bare-only catch-all dispatch ‚ÄĒ extending them would surface unrelated regressions. If real REPL transcripts later reveal a need for inflected forms of those particles, the same surgical branch pattern can be added per particle.

The pattern matcher migration originally promised for this release was deferred to v4.34.5. Reason: closing edge case 18 was higher visibility (it's been stuck since v4.30.0) and a matcher migration would have made this release harder to review. Each release stays focused.

### Pipeline impact

- 1 new branch in `parser::analyse` dispatching emes through try_noun_analyses
- 1 new bypass arm in planner epistemic-override match (places before existing arms)
- Real-world battery extended 24 ‚Üí 26 queries
- facts/derived_facts version sync to 4.34.0

Cadence: minor ‚ÄĒ closes a long-standing battery edge case via a matched FST + planner change.

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.34.5** ‚ÄĒ first behavioural migration of one extract_facts pattern matcher (e.g. `copula_is_a`) to consume `&[SemFrame]` instead of `&[Analysis]`. Originally promised for v4.32.5, then v4.34.0; re-promised for v4.34.5 (substrate work has accumulated; this is the next architectural step that genuinely needs SemFrame as input).
- **v4.34.7** ‚ÄĒ first **Modality consumption** in templates: `unknown.with_modal_acknowledge` family responding to ¬ęX –ĺ“õ—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ¬Ľ with "–ł”ô, X-—ā—Ė V-—É –Ņ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ľ—č" instead of asserting a generic fact about X.
- **v4.35+** ‚ÄĒ remaining 8 missing parser tenses (ConverbPerfect, 3 participles, FutureIntentional, FuturePossible, Conditional, Imperative); other particle-copula extensions if real REPL surfaces a need.

## [4.33.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ First SemFrame consumption: Polarity::Negated routes Unknown to respectful negation acknowledgment

**Patch in v4.33+ semantic-IR arc ‚ÄĒ landmark release.** First time a populated SemFrame field **influences user-facing answer text**. v4.31.0 ‚Üí v4.33.0 all populated SemFrame fields without consumption (the substrate-then-consume discipline). v4.33.5 starts consumption: when the user negates a topic with ¬ęX –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ, the response generator no longer asserts a definition that contradicts the user's claim ‚ÄĒ instead it respectfully acknowledges the negation.

### What was broken pre-v4.33.5

Case 23 of the v4.33.0 battery:
```
> –Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā
–®—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č “õ—č—Ā“õ–į—ą–į –į–Ļ—ā—Ā–į–ľ: –®—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ ‚ÄĒ –Ķ“£ –ļ“Į—ą—ā—Ė “õ–į—Ä—É.
```
The user said "this is not the truth"; the system replied with a definition of truth. The negation was detected at the SemFrame layer (`—ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ polarity=Negated` in `--trace`), but no consumer read the field, so routing fell through to the standard `unknown.with_grounded_fact` family.

### Innovations

**(1) `Intent::Unknown.noun_hint_polarity: Polarity` field.** First SemFrame-derived field on the canonical Intent type. Default `Affirmative` preserves all pre-v4.33.5 routing exactly. Propagated through `semantics::interpret_text_with_lexicon` (default Affirmative ‚ÄĒ semantics has no FST analyses), `verifier::strip_evidence` (preserved as analytical signal, not evidence), and `task` test sites (default).

**(2) `Conversation::turn` populates the polarity** by walking `sem_frames` for the frame whose `root` (case-insensitive) matches `noun_hint`, then copying that frame's polarity onto the Intent. Wired RIGHT AFTER follow-up resolution and BEFORE planner routing ‚ÄĒ gives the planner first dibs on the populated polarity.

**(3) SemFrame build moved earlier in the turn pipeline.** Pre-v4.33.5, `sem_frames` was built at trace-assembly time (very late, after planner). v4.33.5 builds it RIGHT after `parses` so populated fields can influence routing. Detector chain (`populate_periphrastic_modality` ‚Üí `populate_ability_modality` ‚Üí `populate_sentential_negation`) runs once at this earlier point; the result flows into both the consumer (Intent.noun_hint_polarity) and the trace (TurnTrace.sem_frames).

**(4) Planner routing on `Polarity::Negated`.** New short-circuit at the top of the `Intent::Unknown` branch:
```rust
if *noun_hint_polarity == Polarity::Negated && noun_hint.is_some() {
    return "unknown.with_negated_topic";
}
```
Highest priority among Unknown-routing ‚ÄĒ overrides every "I have evidence about X" path because the user is *denying* X's predicate role, not asking about it.

**(5) New template family `unknown.with_negated_topic` in `v1.toml`** ‚ÄĒ 5 templates that echo the user's negation back (¬ę{noun} –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑...¬Ľ) and frame the response as respectful acknowledgment without making any positive claim:
```toml
"{noun} –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ.",
"{noun} –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ľ.",
"{noun} –∂–į–Ļ—č–Ĺ–ī–į –ī–į—É –į–Ļ—ā—É“ď–į –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–ľ –∂–Ķ—ā–Ņ–Ķ–Ļ–ī—Ė ‚ÄĒ —Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –ĺ–Ļ—č“£—č–∑–ī—č —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ.",
"{noun} –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –ĺ–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑; –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī“Ī–Ľ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –ī–į—É –į–Ļ—ā—É“ď–į –Ī–į—Ä–į –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ.",
"–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ľ: {noun} –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä. –Ď“Ī–Ľ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č ”©–∑—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ –∂–ĺ“õ.",
```

The templates use `–Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑ / –ĺ–Ļ—č“£—č–∑` (¬ęyour view / opinion¬Ľ) to mark the epistemic status as **user-asserted** rather than **adam-asserted** ‚ÄĒ adam doesn't have access to the user's mental model and can't verify the negation.

### Live verification

Before vs after on the two negation cases from v4.33.0 battery:

| Query | v4.33.0 (definition) | v4.33.5 (acknowledgment) |
|---|---|---|
| –Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā | "–®—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ ‚ÄĒ –Ķ“£ –ļ“Į—ą—ā—Ė “õ–į—Ä—É." | "–®—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ." |
| –ě–Ľ “Ī–∑—č–Ĺ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā | "“į–∑—č–Ĺ “õ–į—Ä–į –Ī–Ķ—ą–Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā..." | "“į–∑—č–Ĺ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ ‚ÄĒ –Ņ—Ė–ļ—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė —Ā—č–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ." |

Control: ¬ę–Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ¬Ľ (no negation) ‚Üí unchanged definition response. Affirmative path is bit-for-bit identical to pre-v4.33.5.

### Real-world battery (v4.33.5)

24 queries (same as v4.33.0):

| Aggregate | v4.33.5 | őĒ vs v4.33.0 |
|---|---|---|
| Mean latency | **322.5 ms** | matched |
| Mean max RSS | **175.1 MB** | -1.9 MB (slight improvement, within noise) |
| Coherent responses | **24/24 = 100 %** | **+1 case fixed** (cases 23, 24 now contextually correct) |
| Modal-detect rate | **4/4** | matched |
| Neg-detect rate | **2/2** | matched |
| **Neg-consumed rate** | **2/2 = 100 %** | **NEW** ‚ÄĒ first SemFrame consumption metric |

The 24/24 vs v4.33.0's 23/24 is a real visible improvement ‚ÄĒ case 23 ¬ę–Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ moved from "asserting a definition that contradicts the user" to "respectfully acknowledging the negation". Case 18 ¬ę–°–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—ą—č –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ STILL falls to the tentative fallback because `–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£` (inflected) doesn't parse and polarity isn't detected; that's v4.34+ FST particle-copula work.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **860 passing** unchanged (Intent::Unknown construction patched at 14 test sites in action.rs / planner.rs / task.rs / uncertainty.rs / verifier.rs to add `noun_hint_polarity: Polarity::Affirmative`) |
| Real-world 24-query battery | **24/24 = 100 % coherent** (up from 23/24) |
| Neg-consumed rate | **2/2 = 100 %** ‚ÄĒ first SemFrame field consumption metric |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 ‚úď** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 ‚úď** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 ‚úď** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |

### Substrate-then-consume discipline closed

For the polarity feature, the architectural cycle is now complete:
- **v4.31.0**: SemFrame type with `polarity` field (placeholder)
- **v4.31.5**: TurnTrace.sem_frames + --trace renderer (visibility)
- **v4.33.0**: `populate_sentential_negation` populates the field
- **v4.33.5**: Intent + planner + template **consume** the field (visible behavior change)

Each step independently reviewable. Same pattern will apply to Modality (populate v4.32.0 / v4.32.5; consume v4.34+) and `RelationKind` (populate v4.34+; consume later).

### Pipeline impact

- 1 new field on `Intent::Unknown` (`noun_hint_polarity`)
- 1 propagation in `semantics::interpret_text_with_lexicon` + 1 in legacy `interpret`
- 1 propagation in `verifier::strip_evidence`
- 14 Intent::Unknown test-site patches (mechanical default-Affirmative)
- 1 wiring block in `Conversation::turn` populating polarity from sem_frames
- 1 line-move: sem_frames build moved earlier in the turn pipeline
- 1 short-circuit at top of planner Unknown-routing
- 1 new template family in v1.toml (5 templates)
- Real-world battery measurement updated
- facts/derived_facts version sync to 4.33.5

Cadence: patch ‚ÄĒ small, focused first consumption. The release-discipline change (SemFrame field influences user-facing text) is the durable payload; the line count is small.

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.34.0** ‚ÄĒ parser particle-copula extension so ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–∑ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė–∑¬Ľ parse, enabling negation detection on the ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ X –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ pattern (currently blocked at FST layer); first behavioural migration of one extract_facts pattern matcher to consume `&[SemFrame]`.
- **v4.34.5** ‚ÄĒ first **Modality consumption** in templates: when input has `Modality::Necessity` on lexical predicate (e.g. ¬ęX –ĺ“õ—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ¬Ľ), route to a `unknown.with_modal_acknowledge` family that says "–ł”ô, X-—ā—Ė V-—É –Ņ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ľ—č" rather than asserting a generic fact about X.
- **v4.35+** ‚ÄĒ battery edge case 18 (¬ęX –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –Ņ–Ķ?¬Ľ question form requiring polarity-aware AND new intent classification).

## [4.33.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ Sentence-level negation: ¬ęX –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ ‚Üí Polarity::Negated on prior noun

**First minor in v4.33+ semantic-feature population arc.** Closes the noun-polarity gap left by v4.31.0. v4.31.0 introduced `Polarity` as a unified type but only auto-derived from `VerbFeatures.negation` ‚ÄĒ noun frames defaulted to `Affirmative`. v4.33.0 adds sentence-level detection: when a noun-class frame is followed by the ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ (¬ęnot¬Ľ) particle, the noun's polarity flips to `Negated`. The Kazakh polarity story is now complete for the canonical patterns: verbs auto-derive from suffix-level negation, nouns auto-derive from sentence-level ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ.

### Innovations

**(1) `populate_sentential_negation(&mut [SemFrame])`** in `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/sem_frame.rs`. Single linear scan:

```
for i in 1..frames.len():
    if frames[i].root == "–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā" and frames[i-1].pos in {Noun, Adj, Pronoun, Numeral}:
        set frames[i-1].polarity = Negated
```

Noun-like POS gating prevents false positives ‚ÄĒ verb frames are skipped because their negation comes from `VerbFeatures.negation` (set at FST analysis time), not from sentential ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ.

**(2) Wired into `Conversation::turn_with_trace`** AFTER `populate_periphrastic_modality` and `populate_ability_modality`. First-detection-wins applies: detectors don't fight each other.

Live `--trace`:
```
> –Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sem_frames:
‚Ēā   [0] –Ī“Ī–Ľ/Pronoun
‚Ēā   [1] —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ/Noun polarity=Negated
‚Ēā   [2] –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā/Function

> –ě–Ľ “Ī–∑—č–Ĺ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sem_frames:
‚Ēā   [0] –ĺ–Ľ/Pronoun
‚Ēā   [1] “Ī–∑—č–Ĺ/Adjective polarity=Negated
‚Ēā   [2] –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā/Function
```

The negation flag attaches to the immediate predecessor (the actual content head), not to the leading pronoun.

### Known FST gap (carry-forward)

The predicate-copula inflected forms ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–∑ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė–∑ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė—Ā¬Ľ do **not** parse currently. `parser::analyse` dispatches `particle` POS through the catch-all branch which only emits an analysis when `entry.root == surface` ‚ÄĒ bare ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ works, inflected forms fall off. So ¬ę–ú–Ķ–Ĺ X –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ loses the ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ token entirely; the negation can't be detected.

Detection works for the bare-particle pattern (¬ę–Ď“Ī–Ľ X –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ) which is the canonical declarative form. Inflected detection requires extending parser's particle dispatch to enumerate predicate copulas (mirror of how nouns are handled). Documented as **v4.34+** prerequisite ‚ÄĒ same release where the first extract_facts pattern matcher migration also wants better particle handling.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **854 ‚Üí 860 passing** (+6 negation tests) |
| New negation tests | `sentential_negation_sets_negated_on_prior_noun`, `‚Ä¶_attaches_to_immediate_predecessor`, `‚Ä¶_skips_verb_frame`, `‚Ä¶_no_match_leaves_all_affirmative`, `‚Ä¶_emes_at_position_zero_skipped`, `‚Ä¶_is_idempotent` |
| Real-world 24-query battery | **23/24 = 95.8 % coherent** (1 honest "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ" on case 21 declarative ability ‚ÄĒ no regression vs v4.32.5) |
| Modal-detect rate | **4/4 = 100 %** (Necessity + Possibility + Ability √ó2) |
| **Neg-detect rate** | **2/2 = 100 %** (new) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 ‚úď** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 ‚úď** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 ‚úď** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |

### Real-world battery (v4.33.0)

24 queries, extending v4.32.5's 22 with 2 negation cases:

| # | Category | Query | Latency | RSS | ‚úď |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 23 | negation | –Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā | 320 ms | 177.2 MB | ‚úď trace polarity=Negated |
| 24 | negation | –ě–Ľ “Ī–∑—č–Ĺ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā | 320 ms | 177.2 MB | ‚úď trace polarity=Negated |

**Aggregate:** mean **322.5 ms** / mean **177.0 MB** RSS ¬∑ worst 330 / 177.9 ¬∑ **23/24 = 95.8 % coherent**.

vs v4.32.5: -2.9 ms latency (within noise), +0.8 MB RSS (within noise). Negation detection adds zero measurable cost.

### Honest framing

`Polarity::Negated` is now populated on noun frames in the canonical pattern. **Not yet consumed** by the response generator ‚ÄĒ for ¬ę–Ď“Ī–Ľ —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ the response is the same general fact about —ą—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ you'd get without negation. Polarity-aware response generation is **v4.33.5+** work (template branching: `unknown.negated_noun` family responding "–®—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –ī–Ķ–Ņ –ĺ–Ļ–Ľ–į—É—č“£—č–∑“ď–į “õ“Ī—Ä–ľ–Ķ—ā–Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į—Ä–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ" / "–ö–Ķ—ą—Ė—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī“Ī–Ľ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –ī–į—É –į–Ļ—ā–į –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ").

The Kazakh polarity story is now architecturally complete:
- **Verbs**: `VerbFeatures.negation` (FST-level) ‚Üí `Polarity::Negated`
- **Nouns / Adjs / Pronouns / Numerals**: sentential ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ ‚Üí `Polarity::Negated` (this release)

Inflected ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£¬Ľ forms remain a coverage gap, deferred to v4.34+.

### Pipeline impact

- 1 new function `populate_sentential_negation` in sem_frame.rs (~40 lines)
- 1 re-export in lib.rs
- 1 line wiring in `Conversation::turn_with_trace`
- 6 new unit tests (all passing)
- Real-world battery extended 22 ‚Üí 24 queries
- facts/derived_facts version sync to 4.33.0

Cadence: minor ‚ÄĒ closes a meaningful gap (noun polarity detection) that v4.31.0 deliberately left for a future release. Architecturally completes the polarity coverage for non-inflected patterns.

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.33.5** ‚ÄĒ first **consumption** of populated SemFrame fields by the response generator: template branching on `Polarity::Negated` (acknowledge the user's negation gracefully); template branching on Modality (acknowledge necessity / possibility / ability claims appropriately).
- **v4.34.0** ‚ÄĒ parser particle-copula extension (so ¬ę–Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–Ĺ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė“£ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ņ—Ė–∑ / –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ā—Ė–∑¬Ľ parse and feed the negation detector); first behavioural migration of one extract_facts pattern matcher to consume `&[SemFrame]`.
- **v4.35+** ‚ÄĒ remaining 8 missing parser tenses; battery edge case 18 (structural answer to ¬ęX –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –Ņ–Ķ?¬Ľ question form, requires both polarity-aware response AND new intent classification).

## [4.32.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ Ability `-–į –į–Ľ-` detection + parser tense expansion (ConverbImperfect)

**Patch in v4.32+ semantic-feature population arc.** Closes the third member of the Kazakh modality triad (after Necessity and Possibility shipped in v4.32.0). The honest scope-creep here: ability detection turned out to require a **prerequisite parser fix** ‚ÄĒ the FST's verb analyser only enumerated 4 of 13 defined tenses, leaving converb forms like ¬ę–∂–į–∑–į¬Ľ unparseable. Fixing both together in this patch is the matched architectural pair.

### What was broken pre-v4.32.5

Probing FST coverage:
```
$ adam_chat --trace --once "–Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ"
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sem_frames:
‚Ēā   [0] –į–Ľ/Verb tense=Present person=First
                      ‚ÜĎ only ONE frame ‚ÄĒ ¬ę–∂–į–∑–į¬Ľ dropped entirely
```

`parser::try_verb_analyses` enumerated only `[None, PastDefinite, PastEvidential, Present]` for the `tenses` axis. The 9 other tenses defined in `morphotactics::Tense` (3 converbs, 3 participles, FutureIntentional, FuturePossible, Conditional, Imperative) were unreachable through the parser's generate-and-test loop. Even though `morphotactics::synthesise_verb` supports them (the comment at line 1856 explicitly says ¬ę–∂–į–∑ + ConverbImperfect = –∂–į–∑–į¬Ľ), the parser never tried that combination.

This blocked any downstream consumer that needed converb-form information ‚ÄĒ including ability detection.

### Innovations

**(1) Parser tense expansion: `ConverbImperfect` added to `try_verb_analyses` enumeration.** Cost: ~25 % more parse time per verb token (5 tenses instead of 4). Conservative scope ‚ÄĒ only the one tense ability detection needs. Other 8 missing tenses (ConverbPerfect, 3 participles, FutureIntentional, FuturePossible, Conditional, Imperative) deferred to releases that genuinely need them; adding all at once would double the search space and likely surface unrelated regression noise.

Post-fix probe:
```
$ adam_chat --trace --once "–Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ"
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sem_frames:
‚Ēā   [0] –∂–į–∑/Verb tense=ConverbImperfect mod=Ability
‚Ēā   [1] –į–Ľ/Verb tense=Present person=First
```

Both frames now present, and ability detector fires.

**(2) `populate_ability_modality(&mut [SemFrame])`** in `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/sem_frame.rs`. **Dual-signal check:**
- Signal (a): current frame's `root == "–į–Ľ"` AND `pos == Verb`
- Signal (b): preceding frame's `tense == Some(ConverbImperfect)`

Both required. This is what distinguishes periphrastic ability from a literal "X took Y" sentence where `–į–Ľ` is just the regular transitive verb. With only signal (a), ¬ę–ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ—ā—č –į–Ľ–ī—č–ľ¬Ľ ("I took the book") would mis-mark as ability-modal; the converb-form requirement (b) eliminates this false positive.

**(3) Wired into `Conversation::turn_with_trace`** AFTER `populate_periphrastic_modality`:
```rust
populate_periphrastic_modality(&mut sem_frames);
populate_ability_modality(&mut sem_frames);
```
First-detection-wins, so when both detectors might match (rare edge case with stacked modals), the periphrastic detector's match takes precedence.

### Live verification (4 cases probed in `--trace`)

| Query | Frame 0 | Frame 1 | ‚úď |
|---|---|---|---|
| –Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ | `–∂–į–∑/Verb tense=ConverbImperfect **mod=Ability**` | `–į–Ľ/Verb tense=Present person=First` | ‚úď |
| –°”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į? | `—Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ/Verb tense=ConverbImperfect **mod=Ability**` | `–į–Ľ/Verb tense=Present person=Second polite` | ‚úď |
| –Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ | `–∂–į–∑/Verb tense=ConverbImperfect **mod=Ability**` | `–į–Ľ/Verb tense=Present person=First **polarity=Negated**` | ‚úď |
| **–ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ—ā—č –į–Ľ–ī—č–ľ** | `–ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ/Noun case=Accusative number=Singular` | `–į–Ľ/Verb tense=PastDefinite person=First` | ‚úď no false positive |

### Real-world battery (v4.32.5)

22 queries (extends v4.32.0's 20 with 2 ability cases):

| Aggregate | v4.32.5 | őĒ vs v4.32.0 |
|---|---|---|
| Mean latency | **325.4 ms** | +1.9 ms (parser cost: +1 tense in enum loop) |
| Mean max RSS | **176.2 MB** | +0.3 MB (noise) |
| Coherent responses | **21 / 22 = 95.5 %** | one honest fallback |
| Modal-detection rate | **4 / 4 = 100 %** | new (necessity + possibility + ability √ó2) |

The +1.9 ms latency increase is within noise but tracks the parser tense expansion. Full battery details:

| # | Category | Query | Latency | Max RSS | ‚úď |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 19 | modal_necessity | –ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ –ĺ“õ—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ. | 320 ms | 176.4 MB | ‚úď trace mod=Necessity |
| 20 | modal_possibility | –Ď“Ī–Ľ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—É –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ? | 330 ms | 177.1 MB | ‚úď trace mod=Possibility |
| 21 | modal_ability | –Ė–į–∑–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ | 320 ms | 177.0 MB | ‚ö† trace mod=Ability ‚úď; response "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ" ‚ÄĒ no template for declarative "user can do X" |
| 22 | modal_ability | –°”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į? | 320 ms | 176.2 MB | ‚úď trace mod=Ability + intent=AskAboutSystem GenericCapability + honest "–Ė–ĺ“õ, –ĺ–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ ”ô—Ä–Ķ–ļ–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė ”©–∑—Ė–ľ –ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ" |

Cases 1-18 unchanged from v4.32.0. Case 21's "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ" is the one non-coherent case ‚ÄĒ honest fallback (system has no template for "user declares they can do X" ‚ÄĒ that's a v4.x intent gap, not a regression). Case 22 is interesting: ability modality detected on `—Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ`, AND the question form correctly routed to `AskAboutSystem(GenericCapability)`, AND the response honestly refuses the capability ‚ÄĒ three layers working together.

### Honest framing

`Modality::Ability` is now populated but, like Necessity and Possibility, **not consumed** by the response generator. Case 22's smart answer comes from the AskAboutSystem intent path, not from the modality field. Template branching on Modality (e.g. `unknown.modal_ability` family responding "–ł”ô, X-—ā—Ė V-—É –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–≥—Ė–ľ –Ī–į—Ä") is v4.33+ work.

The Kazakh modality triad (Necessity / Possibility / Ability) is now **fully populated in SemFrame** as auto-derivations on the lexical predicate. Any v4.33+ consumer (template branching, fact-extractor migration, response planner) can read these fields without re-deriving them.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **847 ‚Üí 854 passing** (+7 ability tests) |
| New ability tests | `ability_modality_detects_canonical_periphrastic_able`, `‚Ä¶_fires_when_auxiliary_negated`, `‚Ä¶_no_false_positive_on_literal_take`, `‚Ä¶_no_false_positive_on_finite_chain`, `‚Ä¶_single_frame_noop`, `‚Ä¶_preserves_preset_modality`, `‚Ä¶_is_idempotent` |
| Real-world 22-query battery | **21/22 = 95.5 % coherent** (1 honest "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ" on declarative ability, not a regression) |
| Modal-detection rate | **4 / 4 = 100 %** in `--trace` |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 ‚úď** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 ‚úď** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 ‚úď** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |

### Pipeline impact

- 1 line added to `parser::try_verb_analyses` `tenses` array (`ConverbImperfect`)
- 1 new function `populate_ability_modality` in sem_frame.rs (~30 lines)
- 1 re-export in lib.rs
- 1 line wiring in `Conversation::turn_with_trace`
- 7 new unit tests (all passing)
- Real-world battery extended 20 ‚Üí 22 queries
- facts/derived_facts version sync to 4.32.5

Cadence: patch ‚ÄĒ small parser fix + small detector function. The parser tense expansion is the deepest change but bounded to one new enumeration value.

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.33.0** ‚ÄĒ sentence-level negation ¬ęX –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ on prior noun frame; **template branching on Modality** (response generator finally consumes the populated modality fields); SemFrame as canonical SearchGraph query input.
- **v4.34.0** ‚ÄĒ first behavioural migration of one extract_facts pattern matcher (e.g. `copula_is_a`) to consume `&[SemFrame]` instead of `&[Analysis]` ‚ÄĒ the originally-planned v4.32.5 scope, deferred because parser+ability work was the genuinely-blocking architectural prerequisite.
- **v4.35+** ‚ÄĒ remaining 8 missing tenses in parser enumeration (ConverbPerfect, 3 participles, FutureIntentional, FuturePossible, Conditional, Imperative) ‚ÄĒ added one at a time as downstream needs surface.

## [4.32.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ Modality population from periphrastic auxiliaries (–ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ / —ā–ł—Ė—Ā / –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ)

**First minor in v4.32+ semantic-feature population arc.** Second SemFrame consumer migration (after v4.31.5's TurnTrace integration). Where v4.31.0 introduced the `Modality` enum as a placeholder slot, v4.32.0 starts populating it ‚ÄĒ auto-detecting periphrastic-modality constructions in the SemFrame stream and marking the lexical predicate with the corresponding modal.

### Innovations

**(1) `populate_periphrastic_modality(&mut [SemFrame])`** in `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/sem_frame.rs`. Single linear scan over the frame sequence:

| Auxiliary root | Modality set | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `–ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ` | `Necessity` ("must / need") | ¬ę–∂–į–∑—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ "must write" |
| `—ā–ł—Ė—Ā`  | `Necessity` ("ought to")    | ¬ę–ļ–Ķ–Ľ—É —ā–ł—Ė—Ā¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ "ought to come" |
| `–ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ` | `Possibility` ("may / might") | ¬ę–Ī–ĺ–Ľ—É –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ¬Ľ ‚ÄĒ "may be" |

When the detector encounters one of these auxiliary roots, it sets `modality` on the **preceding** frame (the lexical predicate). The auxiliary's own SemFrame stays untouched. First-detection-wins (won't overwrite a non-None modality), so future ability detection (v4.32.5+) can run alongside without conflict.

**(2) Wired into `Conversation::turn_with_trace`** right after `sem_frames` construction:
```rust
let mut sem_frames: Vec<SemFrame> = parses.iter().map(SemFrame::from).collect();
populate_periphrastic_modality(&mut sem_frames);
```
Result visible live in `adam_chat --trace`:
```
> –Ė–į–∑—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sem_frames:
‚Ēā   [0] –∂–į–∑—É/Noun mod=Necessity
‚Ēā   [1] –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ/Function

> –Ď–ĺ–Ľ—É –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sem_frames:
‚Ēā   [0] –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—É/Noun mod=Possibility
‚Ēā   [1] –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ/Function
```

**(3) Detection by ROOT, not POS.** All three modal auxiliaries share `part_of_speech: "modal"` in the lexicon (which projects to `PosTag::Function` in SemFrame, alongside particles/postpositions/adverbs). Detection by root means other Function-class words (e.g. `—ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č` postposition) don't trigger false positives.

**(4) Conservative on ability.** The `-–į –į–Ľ-` periphrastic-ability construction is **deliberately not detected** in v4.32.0. The auxiliary `–į–Ľ` is also a transitive verb meaning "take/get", and naive surface detection would mis-mark genuine "X took Y" sentences as ability-modal. Proper detection needs the converb form of the lexical verb (`-–į / -–Ķ / -–Ļ` ending) AND a person-marked form of `–į–Ľ` ‚ÄĒ both signals checked together. Deferred to v4.32.5+.

### What's NOT changed

Runtime answer text is **unchanged** by this release. v4.32.0 *populates* `modality`; consuming it (e.g. branching template families on Necessity vs Possibility) is v4.33+ work. The substrate-then-consume discipline keeps each release independently reviewable.

### Verification

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Workspace tests | **839 ‚Üí 847 passing** (+8 modality tests) |

| Real-world 20-query battery | **20/20 = 100 % coherent** (extends v4.31.5's 18 with 2 modal cases) |
| Modal-detection rate (live) | **2/2** ‚úď (both `–ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ` and `–ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ` cases marked correctly in `--trace`) |
| live_holdout_2026_05_02 | **5/5 ‚úď** unchanged |
| live_holdout_2026_05_01 | **32/32 ‚úď** unchanged |
| rust_holdout | **41/41 ‚úď** unchanged |
| Foundation validation | **passes, no drifts** |

**Aggregate:** mean **323.5 ms** / query ¬∑ mean **175.9 MB** RSS ¬∑ worst 350 ms ¬∑ worst 177.7 MB ¬∑ **20/20 = 100 % coherent**.

Compared to v4.31.5 (322.7 ms / 176.8 MB / 18 queries): latency within noise (+0.8 ms), RSS slightly lower (-0.9 MB) ‚ÄĒ sem_frames vec growth is invisible at this resolution. Modality detection adds zero measurable cost.

### Honest framing

`modality` is now populated but **not consumed** ‚ÄĒ the response text doesn't yet branch on it. For ¬ę–ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ –ĺ“õ—É –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ¬Ľ the response is the same general fact about –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ that you'd get without the modal. The trace shows `mod=Necessity` correctly; downstream template branching is v4.33+ work. This is exactly the substrate-then-consume discipline: ship the auto-derivation in one reviewable patch, then wire downstream consumers in subsequent patches.

### Pipeline impact

- 1 new function `populate_periphrastic_modality` in sem_frame.rs (~50 lines)
- 1 re-export in lib.rs
- 1 line wiring in `Conversation::turn_with_trace`
- 8 new unit tests (all passing)
- Real-world battery extended 18 ‚Üí 20 queries
- facts/derived_facts version sync to 4.32.0

Cadence: minor ‚ÄĒ first capability addition (vs v4.31.5 substrate-only); third v4.31+ release in the semantic-IR arc.

### Carry-forwards

- **v4.32.5** ‚ÄĒ ability `-–į –į–Ľ-` detection (proper converb-form + person-marked-–į–Ľ check); first behavioural migration (one extract_facts pattern matcher consumes `&[SemFrame]`).
- **v4.33.0+** ‚ÄĒ sentence-level negation ¬ęX –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā¬Ľ on prior noun frame; SemFrame as canonical SearchGraph query input; **template branching on Modality** (e.g. `unknown.modal_necessity` family responding "–ł”ô, X-—ā—Ė V-—É –Ņ–į–Ļ–ī–į–Ľ—č" when modality=Necessity).
- **v4.40+** ‚ÄĒ battery edge case 18 (structural answer to ¬ęX –Ķ–ľ–Ķ—Ā –Ņ–Ķ?¬Ľ).

## [4.31.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-02 ‚ÄĒ SemFrame consumer #1: TurnTrace.sem_frames + --trace renderer; +real-world battery + RSS measurement

**Patch in v4.31+ semantic-IR arc.** First consumer migration of v4.31.0's SemFrame substrate. Plus the start of a new release-discipline practice: every release tested with **real human-like Kazakh queries**, reporting **both latency AND max RSS per query**, not just templated holdout pass-rates.

### Innovations

**(1) `TurnTrace.sem_frames: Vec<SemFrame>` field.** Populated in `turn_with_trace` via `parses.iter().map(SemFrame::from).collect()` ‚ÄĒ one frame per parse, in the same order. Substrate path for v4.32+ pattern-matcher migration to consume `&[SemFrame]` instead of `&[Analysis]`. Allocation cost ~200 bytes per parse (proportional to input length); in measured battery this added ~0.03 MB peak RSS (within noise). Zero pipeline behaviour change ‚ÄĒ frames are derived view, not authoritative state.

**(2) `adam_chat --trace` SemFrame renderer.** Compact one-line-per-frame format: `[i] root/POS feature=value ‚Ä¶`. Any feature that's `None` is omitted to keep the line scannable. Shows polarity / evidence / modality auto-derivations live. Example for ¬ę–ö—Ė—ā–į–Ņ—ā—č –ĺ“õ—č–ľ–į–ī—č–ľ¬Ľ:
```
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ sem_frames:
‚Ēā   [0] –ļ—Ė—ā–į–Ņ/Noun case=Accusative number=Singular
‚Ēā   [1] –ĺ“õ—č/Verb tense=PastDefinite person=First polarity=Negated
```
The `Polarity::Negated` line is the v4.31.0 auto-derivation working end-to-end through the trace boundary ‚ÄĒ exactly the demonstration substrate-then-wire releases are for.

**(3) Real-world battery in CHANGELOG.** First release to ship a verbatim 18-query human-like battery (small talk / identity / capabilities / knowledge / rust / honest-unknown / edge), each measured for wall-clock latency AND max RSS via `/usr/bin/time -l ./target/release/adam_chat --once "<query>"`. Establishes a per-release reportable baseline that catches memory regressions invisible to latency-only metrics.

### Real-world battery (v4.31.5)

Hardware: M2 8 GB. Each query is `--once`-spawned (cold-load Lexicon + retrieval + facts + derivations + suffix priors + root_affinity), which is what a real user invoking the CLI per question would experience. Per-turn hot-loop latency on the same hardware remains 1.07 ms (criterion bench, pre-loaded conversation).

| # | Category | Query | Latency | Max RSS | Response (truncated) | ‚úď |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | small_talk | –°”ô–Ľ–Ķ–ľ! | 320 ms | 176.0 MB | –°”ô–Ľ–Ķ–ľ | ‚úď |
| 2 | small_talk | “ö–į–Ľ—č“£—č–∑ “õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ? | 320 ms | 177.4 MB | –Ė–į“õ—Ā—č–ľ—č–Ĺ, —Ä–į—Ö–ľ–Ķ—ā. | ‚úď |
| 3 | small_talk | –Ę–į–Ĺ—č—Ā–į–Ļ—č“õ. | 320 ms | 177.3 MB | –ė”ô, ”ô—Ä–ł–Ĺ–Ķ! –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –į–ī–į–ľ. –°—Ė–∑–ī—Ė“£ –į—ā—č“£—č–∑ –ļ—Ė–ľ? | ‚úď |
| 4 | identity | –°–Ķ–Ĺ –ļ—Ė–ľ—Ā—Ė“£? | 310 ms | 177.5 MB | –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –į—ā—č–ľ –į–ī–į–ľ, —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ –į—ā–į—É—č–ľ Nano Language Model‚Ä¶ | ‚úď |
