**Patch (v4.13.5)** per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`. Closes the two known gaps explicitly listed in v4.13.0 release notes. **Stripe (2) ‚ÄĒ humanness ‚ÄĒ final patch closing all three 2026-05-01 transcript failures.** Next:

- **v4.14.0** ‚ÄĒ predicate decomposition + domain-TF-IDF + semantic-cohesion graph traversal. Closes the third transcript pattern (¬ę–ě“õ—É—ą—č–Ľ–į—Ä –Ĺ–Ķ –ĺ“õ–ł–ī—č?¬Ľ).
- **v4.15.0** ‚ÄĒ first ML layer: `P(suffix_chain)` priors. Pure compositional, no embeddings.

## [4.13.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-05-01 ‚ÄĒ sentence-decomposition foundation + DialogContext multi-turn topic memory + closed-class hygiene

**Second minor in the v4.12+ humanness research arc.** A 2026-05-01 live-REPL transcript surfaced two systemic gaps that v4.12.x couldn't close:

1. **Greedy first-noun-match.** ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ –ĺ–Ĺ—č –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į, **”ô–Ľ—Ė** –∂–ĺ“õ –Ņ–į?¬Ľ pre-v4.13.0 surfaced a poetry quote about `”ô–Ľ—Ė`. The pipeline was hitting modal particles and existential negators (`”ô–Ľ—Ė / ”ô–Ľ–ī–Ķ / –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ / —ā–į“ď—č / –∂–ĺ“õ / –ł”ô / –Ņ–į / –Ņ–Ķ`) as if they were content nouns.
2. **Goldfish memory.** The conversation tracked only one back-reference (`session["last_query_topic"]`), so a turn referring to a topic established 3-4 turns earlier could not be resolved.

v4.13.0 lays the **foundation** to fix both. Generic capability detection + honest-fallback templates are deferred to v4.13.5 to keep this release coherent.

### Architectural addition: `sentence_decomp` module

The Kazakh agglutinative grammar IS typed function composition: every word is `root + suffix-chain` where each case suffix is a typed function `Noun ‚Üí CaseMarkedNoun[Role]`. The FST already decomposes this; pre-v4.13.0 we discarded the case markers downstream and fell back to first-noun-match. v4.13.0 wires the case markers into a **semantic role table** that is linguistically standard and has been stable for ~150 years of Kazakh case-grammar tradition:

```text
  Nominative   ‚Üí Subject
  Accusative   ‚Üí Object
  Locative     ‚Üí Locus
  LocativeAttr ‚Üí Locus-modifier
  Ablative     ‚Üí Source / Topic-from
  Dative       ‚Üí Recipient / Goal
  Genitive     ‚Üí Possessor
  Instrumental ‚Üí Instrument
```

`crates/adam-dialog/src/sentence_decomp.rs` exposes:

- `enum SentenceType { Question, Statement, Imperative, Exclamation }` ‚ÄĒ top-level sentence-type classifier (pure surface scan: punctuation + closed-list imperative cues).
- `enum Role` ‚ÄĒ 11 semantic roles (Subject / Object / Locus / Source / Recipient / Possessor / Instrument / Predicate / QuestionWord / Closed / Coord).
- `struct TokenRole { surface, root, role, is_anaphor }` ‚ÄĒ per-token tagging.
- `struct SentenceDecomposition { sentence_type, tokens, question_word, focus, focus_role, predicate, topic_list, cohesion }` ‚ÄĒ full structural decomposition with derived focus.
- `fn decompose(input, parses, last_topic) -> SentenceDecomposition` ‚ÄĒ pure function, O(n) over tokens, hardmap lookups only. Microseconds per query.

Question-word focus override: `–Ĺ–Ķ` asks for OBJECT, `“õ–į–Ļ–ī–į` for LOCUS, `“õ–į—ą–į–Ĺ/“õ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ/–Ĺ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ` for PREDICATE, `–ļ—Ė–ľ/“õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ` for SUBJECT. When question_word disagrees with greedy first-noun, decomposition.focus reflects the question-driven choice. The planner can switch on `(SentenceType, question_word, focus_role)` instead of just topic-noun.

**Zero ML.** All operations are deterministic table lookups ‚ÄĒ fits a CPU register. A probabilistic suffix-chain prior (`P(suffix_chain) ‚ÄĒ root + function^n` learning) is reserved for v4.15+; v4.13.0 is purely structural foundation.

### Architectural addition: `DialogContext` multi-turn memory

`crates/adam-dialog/src/dialog_context.rs` introduces:

- `struct TopicMention { turn_id, topic, domain, from_anaphora }`.
- `struct DialogContext { topic_history: Vec<TopicMention>, last_topic, subject_under_discussion, current_domain }`.
- Capped history (`MAX_HISTORY = 64` entries, FIFO eviction) ‚ÄĒ memory bounded.
- `subject_under_discussion` computed via majority vote over the last `STICKY_WINDOW = 6` turns; one-off mentions don't displace it.
- `current_domain` from majority vote over the last `DOMAIN_WINDOW = 4` turns.
- `resolve_anaphor()` consults `subject_under_discussion` first, falls back to `last_topic`.

Updated each turn from `Conversation::turn_with_trace` after the resolved Intent's noun_hint surfaces. Used by the discourse-anaphora resolver instead of the v4.6.0 single-string `session["last_query_topic"]` (which is preserved as legacy fallback for callers that never populate `dialog_context`).

### Innovations bundled

1. **`sentence_decomp` module** ‚ÄĒ 11 unit tests cover SentenceType detection, case-role table, focus-picker, anaphor surface forms, cohesion scoring, closed-class filter.

2. **`DialogContext` module** ‚ÄĒ 8 unit tests cover topic-history append, subject persistence under one-off mentions, domain majority vote, anaphora-only suppression, history cap, fallback semantics.

3. **Closed-class additions to `NOT_A_TOPIC`** ‚ÄĒ `”ô–Ľ—Ė / ”ô–Ľ–ī–Ķ / –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ / —ā–į“ď—č` (modal/discourse particles) + `–∂–ĺ“õ / –ł”ô` (existential negator + affirmation particle) + `–Ņ–į / –Ņ–Ķ` (post-voiceless allomorphs of the question particle, completing the `–ľ–į/–ľ–Ķ/–Ī–į/–Ī–Ķ/–Ņ–į/–Ņ–Ķ` paradigm). These leaked into noun_hint as greedy first-noun before v4.13.0.

4. **Accusative/Dative/Genitive anaphor extension to `DISCOURSE_ANAPHORS`** ‚ÄĒ pre-v4.13.0 only Locative + Ablative cases (`–ĺ–Ĺ–ī–į / —Ā–ĺ–ī–į–Ĺ / –Ī“Ī–ī–į–Ĺ / ...`) triggered anaphora resolution. v4.13.0 adds Acc (`–ĺ–Ĺ—č / —Ā–ĺ–Ĺ—č / –ľ“Ī–Ĺ—č / –Ī“Ī–Ĺ—č`), Dat (`–ĺ“ď–į–Ĺ / —Ā–ĺ“ď–į–Ĺ / –ľ“Ī“ď–į–Ĺ / –Ī“Ī“ď–į–Ĺ`), Gen (`–ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ / —Ā–ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ / –ľ“Ī–Ĺ—č“£ / –Ī“Ī–Ĺ—č“£`) ‚ÄĒ the full 12-form paradigm. Live-REPL ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ –ĺ–Ĺ—č –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į?¬Ľ pre-v4.13.0 ignored `–ĺ–Ĺ—č` because Accusative wasn't registered.

5. **`Conversation.dialog_context` field** + per-turn record_turn integration. Updated after each Intent::Unknown { noun_hint } resolves. Consulted on next turn's anaphora resolution.

6. **REPL replay regression** ‚ÄĒ 2 new dialogs: closed-class anti-regression (verifies adam doesn't surface poetry on modal-particle questions) + multi-turn anaphora (verifies turn 2 ¬ę–ĺ–Ĺ—č¬Ľ resolves to turn 1 topic).

### Pipeline impact

- New module: `crates/adam-dialog/src/sentence_decomp.rs` (~470 lines + tests).
- New module: `crates/adam-dialog/src/dialog_context.rs` (~250 lines + tests).
- New `Conversation.dialog_context: DialogContext` field.
- `NOT_A_TOPIC`: +8 closed-class entries.
- `DISCOURSE_ANAPHORS`: +12 Acc/Dat/Gen anaphor forms.
- `interpret_text_with_lexicon` unchanged ‚ÄĒ sentence_decomp is exposed for v4.13.5+ consumers.
- `audit_response::contains_latin` v4.12.0 backtick carve-out unchanged.

### Live REPL ‚ÄĒ multi-turn anaphora

```
Q1: –°—Ė–∑ Rust —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑?
A1: Rust —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ķ“£ ”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ—Ė –ľ—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—č –į–Ļ—ā—É“ď–į –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č: `Rust` ‚ÄĒ –∂–į–ī—č–Ĺ—č
    “õ–į—É—Ė–Ņ—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–į—Ā“õ–į—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė.
    [DialogContext: last_topic=rust, subject_under_discussion=rust]

Q2: –°—Ė–∑ –ĺ–Ĺ—č –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į?
A2: Rust —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ķ“£ ”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ—Ė –ľ—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—č –į–Ļ—ā—É“ď–į –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–į–ī—č: `Rust` ‚ÄĒ –∂–į–ī—č–Ĺ—č
    “õ–į—É—Ė–Ņ—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–į—Ā“õ–į—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė.
    [Pre-v4.13.0: –Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ.]
```

(The post-v4.13.0 answer is a definition of Rust rather than the proper capability response. Generic verb-capability detection is v4.13.5; v4.13.0 ensures the topic at least carries forward correctly via DialogContext.)

### Known remaining for v4.13.5

- **Generic capability detector for arbitrary verbs.** ¬ęverb-–į–Ľ–į-—Ā—č“£/—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į¬Ľ pattern ‚Üí routes to ¬ę–Ė–ĺ“õ, –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ X —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ¬Ľ honest fallback.
- **Honest "I don't have curriculum data" fallback** for `¬ę–ě“õ—É—ą—č–Ľ–į—Ä –Ĺ–Ķ –ĺ“õ–ł–ī—č?¬Ľ`-shaped questions where adam has the subject but no answer-bearing fact.
- **Multi-topic capability response.** ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į, —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į... –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ ‚Üí ¬ę–Ď“Ī–Ľ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ä –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–≥—Ė —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä –Ī–į—Ä, –Ī—Ė—Ä–į“õ –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ—č“£ —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ –ľ–į–∑–ľ“Ī–Ĺ—č –∂–ĺ“õ.¬Ľ
- **Domain inference from world_core** to populate `DialogContext.current_domain` (currently always None ‚ÄĒ subject inference works without it).

### Tests + counters

- New unit tests: **11 sentence_decomp + 8 dialog_context = +19**.
- New REPL replay dialogs: **+2** (closed-class anti-regression + multi-turn anaphora).
- Workspace tests: 758 ‚Üí **777 passing** (+19).
- `validate_world_core`: 1 625 / 1 625 approved / 1 791 facts (unchanged).
- `cognitive_eval`: 25/25 canonical (unchanged).

### Cadence

**Minor (v4.13.0)** per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0` ‚ÄĒ substantial architectural foundation: 2 new modules + new field on `Conversation` + 12 closed-class additions. **Stripe (2) ‚ÄĒ humanness ‚ÄĒ continues with foundation laid.** Next:

- **v4.13.5** ‚ÄĒ generic capability detector + honest fallback templates (multi-topic capability + curriculum-content "I don't know" + verb-capability honest no).
- **v4.14.0** ‚ÄĒ predicate decomposition + domain-TF-IDF + semantic-cohesion graph traversal.
- **v4.15.0** ‚ÄĒ first ML layer: `P(suffix_chain)` priors trained offline on the corpus, used as FST-disambiguation tiebreaker. Pure compositional, no embeddings, no inference-time gradient.

## [4.12.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ humanness milestone: question-shape classifier + Implementation aspect + causal-reasoning routing + backtick carve-out for user output

**First minor in the v4.12+ humanness research arc.** Three patches in v4.11.5/.6/.7 closed stripe (1) ‚ÄĒ "answer-by-the-point" ‚ÄĒ across all 38 world_core domains. v4.12.0 opens stripe (2) ‚ÄĒ "human-like reasoning" ‚ÄĒ by giving the planner an analytical signal it never had: the **form** of the user's question (definition vs causal vs listing vs comparison vs yes/no), independent of topic.

### Architectural addition: `QuestionShape`

A new module `crates/adam-dialog/src/question_shape.rs` introduces:

- **`QuestionShape` enum** ‚ÄĒ five variants: `Definition` (default), `Causal`, `YesNoCheck`, `Listing`, `Comparison`. Orthogonal to `Intent`: the same `Intent::Unknown` can carry any shape, and the planner picks different template families per `(intent, shape)`.
- **`question_shape::detect(input: &str) -> Option<QuestionShape>`** ‚ÄĒ pure surface-level detector (regex-style substring matching, no FST). Order: more specific shapes first (Causal ‚Üí Comparison ‚Üí Listing ‚Üí YesNoCheck), Definition as the catch-all. Returns `None` for non-questions.
- **`Intent::Unknown.question_shape: Option<QuestionShape>`** ‚ÄĒ new field, populated at the top of `interpret_text_with_lexicon`. Strip-and-restore preserved across `verifier::strip_evidence`.

### Innovations bundled

1. **`question_shape` module** ‚ÄĒ 6 unit tests cover the five shapes + non-question fallthrough + Listing/Definition disambiguation (`“ö–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ X –Ī–į—Ä?` is Listing; `X “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ?` is Definition).

2. **`SystemAspect::Implementation`** + detector + template family `ask_about_system.implementation`. Closes the v4.11.7 known gap on ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ (–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É) —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –∂–į–∑—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑?¬Ľ / ¬ę–Ě–Ķ —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –∂–į—Ā–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ—Ā—č“£?¬Ľ. New `SystemIdentity::implementation_summary` field renders a comprehensive answer mentioning `Rust`, the crate stack (`adam-kernel`, `adam-tokenizer`, `adam-dialog`, `adam-reasoning`, `adam-retrieval`, `adam-corpus`), the FST + rule-based architecture, and platform support (`macOS`, `Linux`).

3. **Causal-reasoning routing** ‚ÄĒ when `Intent::Unknown` carries `QuestionShape::Causal`, the planner short-circuits to `unknown.causal.with_fact` (when grounded_fact present) or `unknown.causal.bare` (noun-only). Honest hedging: adam states what it knows about X, then explicitly says it cannot pinpoint the cause from its dataset. Pre-v4.12.0 ¬ę–Ě–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į ”©–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė?¬Ľ surfaced a generic `–∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į IsA –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź` fact ‚ÄĒ logically wrong because the user asked WHY –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į dies, not WHAT it is.

4. **Backtick carve-out in `audit_response::contains_latin`** ‚ÄĒ mirrors the v4.7.0 corpus-purity carve-out for world_core, now applied to user-facing output. Backtick-quoted spans bypass the `LatinCharactersForbidden` audit so technical proper nouns (`Rust`, `Cargo`, `String`, `macOS`, `Linux`, `adam-kernel`) appear verbatim. Bare Latin prose outside backticks still trips the gate. 3 new unit tests lock the behavior.

5. **REPL replay regression dialogs** ‚ÄĒ 5 new dialogs in `data/eval/repl_dialogs.json` covering Implementation aspect (2 phrasings) + Causal questions (2 variants including vocative composition) + reuse of v4.11.5 vocative-strip + v4.12.0 question-shape detector together.

### Pipeline impact

- `Intent::Unknown` gains the `question_shape: Option<QuestionShape>` field; all construction sites updated.
- New `SystemAspect::Implementation` variant + `template_key_suffix` returns `.implementation`.
- `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`: 3 new families (`ask_about_system.implementation`, `unknown.causal.with_fact`, `unknown.causal.bare`).
- `audit_response::contains_latin` now backtick-aware; 3 new tests, 0 regressions on existing 5.

### Live REPL regression

Both v4.11.7-known gaps now answer correctly:

```
Q: –Ě–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į ”©–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė?
Pre-v4.12.0:  –Ė–į—Ā—É—ą–į —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –ľ—č–Ĺ–į–ī–į–Ļ –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā –į–Ĺ—č“õ—ā–į–ī—č–ľ: –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā
              –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į, –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į ‚ÄĒ —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —Ā—É–Ī—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ—Ü–ł—Ź.
Post-v4.12.0: –Ė–į—Ā—É—ą–į –∂”©–Ĺ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ—Ė–ľ: –Ė–į—Ā—É—ą–į ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –į“ď–∑–į–Ĺ—č“£
              “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ—č—Ā –∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–į–Ľ–ī—č –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė. –Ē”ô–Ľ —Ā–Ķ–Ī–Ķ–Ī—Ė–Ĺ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č
              –į–Ļ—ā–į –į–Ľ–ľ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ ‚ÄĒ –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ “õ–ĺ—Ä—č–ľ–ī–į –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č —Ā–Ķ–Ī–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė–ļ
              –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ –∂–ĺ“õ.

Q: –°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī–Ķ –∂–į–∑—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑?
Pre-v4.12.0:  –Ď–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č “õ—č—Ā“õ–į—ą–į –į–Ļ—ā—Ā–į–ľ: –Ď–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É
              —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė ‚ÄĒ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į –∂–į–∑—É“ď–į –į—Ä–Ĺ–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ —Ą–ĺ—Ä–ľ–į–Ľ–ī—č —ā—Ė–Ľ.
Post-v4.12.0: –ú–Ķ–Ĺ `Rust` –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –∂–į–∑—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ–ľ—č–Ĺ. –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£
              –Ī–į—Ā—ā–į–Ņ“õ—č –ļ–ĺ–ī—č–ľ –į—ą—č“õ, –ĺ–Ľ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ—ą–Ķ Rust-—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č“õ—ā–į—Ä—č–Ĺ–į
              (`adam-kernel`, `adam-tokenizer`, `adam-dialog`,
              `adam-reasoning`, `adam-retrieval`, `adam-corpus`)
              –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ. –ź—Ä—Ö–ł—ā–Ķ–ļ—ā—É—Ä–į–ľ —ā–ĺ–Ľ—č“ď—č–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –Ķ—Ä–Ķ–∂–Ķ–≥–Ķ –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ:
              –ľ–ĺ—Ä—Ą–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ FST, —ą–į–Ī–Ľ–ĺ–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ –ī–ł–į–Ľ–ĺ–≥
              “õ–ĺ–∑“ď–į–Ľ—ā“õ—č—ą—č, —Ą–į–ļ—ā—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ä –≥—Ä–į—Ą—č, –ľ–ĺ—Ä—Ą–Ķ–ľ–į –Ī–ĺ–Ļ—č–Ĺ—ą–į –ļ–ĺ—Ä–Ņ—É—Ā—ā—č
              —Ė–∑–ī–Ķ—É. –°—ā–į—ā–ł—Ā—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ –≥–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–į—Ü–ł—Ź –∂–ĺ“õ ‚ÄĒ ”ô—Ä –∂–į—É–į–Ī—č–ľ –Ĺ–į“õ—ā—č
              –ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ļ–ļ”©–∑–≥–Ķ —Ā“Į–Ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ–ī—Ė. –ú–Ķ–Ĺ `macOS` –Ņ–Ķ–Ĺ `Linux` –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ
              –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā —Ė—Ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ, –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–ļ–Ķ —ą—č“õ–Ņ–į–Ļ–ľ—č–Ĺ.
```

- New REPL replay dialogs: **+5** (Implementation √ó 2 + Causal √ó 2 + history_kazakhstan_query carryover unchanged).

- **v4.12.5** ‚ÄĒ discourse-acknowledgement (–ė”ô/”ė—Ä–ł–Ĺ–Ķ/–ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –Ī—Ė–Ľ—É—Ė–ľ—ą–Ķ) + template-rotation against repetition + follow-up offers.

## [4.11.7] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ coverage gap closure: question-particle filter + object-length priority + bare-name geo + birthdate verbs + language-capability detector

User directive 2026-04-30: confirm v4.12.0 readiness via live-REPL test. 20-question battery surfaced 5 systemic gaps that should ship as a patch before the minor bump, so v4.12.0 starts on a clean baseline.

### Innovations bundled (5 patches)

1. **Question-particle filter in `NOT_A_TOPIC`** ‚ÄĒ added `–Ī–į` and `–ľ–Ķ` (sister forms of the existing `–Ī–Ķ` / `–ľ–į`). Real-REPL gap: ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į?¬Ľ pre-v4.11.7 extracted `–Ī–į` as topic and surfaced a poetry quote about `“Ī“õ–Ņ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ī–į`. The four-form set (`–Ī–į / –Ī–Ķ / –ľ–į / –ľ–Ķ`) is the closed Kazakh interrogative-particle paradigm; the lexicon has `–Ī–į` registered as a particle, but FST occasionally emits a Noun reading too.

2. **Object-length component in `user_facing_fact_priority` inverted to longer-wins** ‚ÄĒ was `+(object.root.chars().count())` (ASC, shorter wins), now `-(...)` (DESC, longer wins). For "what is X?" / "tell me about X" questions, the more informative object wins: `–∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į IsA —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė` (compound) over `–∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į IsA –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź` (bare noun); `—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į IsA —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–į—ā “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č` (compound) over `—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į IsA “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ`. Pre-v4.11.7 the v4.11.6 length tiebreaker never fired because this priority tier already chose the scant version.

3. **Bare-name geo aliases + case-insensitive subject lookup** ‚ÄĒ three new geo_kz entries (`geo_kz_112..114`) with bare subjects `–∂–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—É`, `–į“õ–ľ–ĺ–Ľ–į`, `“Ī–Ľ—č—ā–į—É` (oblasts only known via compound subject `<X> –ĺ–Ī–Ľ—č—Ā—č` pre-v4.11.7). Plus `Tool::dispatch(SearchGraph)` lowercases the requested subject before equality comparison, so a title-cased proper-noun form like `“į–Ľ—č—ā–į—É` (returned by `normalize_proper_noun` when FST has no lemma) matches lowercase world_core subjects. Closes the live-REPL gap on `–Ė–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—É / “į–Ľ—č—ā–į—É —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č` queries that pre-v4.11.7 fell to `unknown.tentative` ("–Ď”ô–Ľ–ļ—Ė–ľ, ... –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į"). +1 compound `—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł ”©“£—Ė—Ä` to MULTIWORD_ENTITIES.

4. **Birthdate detector verb-form variants** ‚ÄĒ `“õ–į–Ĺ—ą–į/–Ĺ–Ķ—ą–Ķ + –∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—Ā—č“£/–∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑` added alongside the existing adessive `–∂–į—Ā—ā–į—Ā—č“£/–∂–į—Ā—ā–į—Ā—č–∑` patterns. `–∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—Ā—č“£/–∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑` (= 2nd-person of `–∂–į—Ā–į—É` "to live") is colloquial Kazakh for "how old are you?". Real-REPL: ¬ę“ö–į–Ĺ—ą–į –∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑?¬Ľ pre-v4.11.7 returned "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ" because the existing `–∂–į—Ā—ā–į—Ā—č“£/–∂–į—Ā—ā–į—Ā—č–∑` patterns required the adessive form.

5. **Language-capability detector** ‚ÄĒ closed list of {language adverb} + {2nd-person knowledge verb} pairs added to `capabilities_marker`. Catches ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ, ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ –į“ď—č–Ľ—ą—č–Ĺ—ą–į —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į?¬Ľ, ¬ę–°–Ķ–Ĺ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į —ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ (and same for –ĺ—Ä—č—Ā—ą–į/—ā“Į—Ä—Ė–ļ—ą–Ķ). Routes to `SystemAspect::Capabilities` so adam surfaces its `capabilities_summary` (¬ę“ö–į–∑–į“õ —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ; ‚Ä¶¬Ľ). Pre-v4.11.7 these queries returned "–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ" because no detector matched the `{lang}-—ą–į + –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£/–Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑/—Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č“£/—Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑/—ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£/—ā“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑` pattern.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 622 ‚Üí **1 625 entries** (+3 bare-name geo); 1 785 ‚Üí **1 791 facts** (+6 ‚ÄĒ geo entries carry IsA + PartOf each).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += **1 compound** (`—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł ”©“£—Ė—Ä`).
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: rerun-stable.

### Live REPL regression ‚ÄĒ 20 subject queries

All 20 queries from the empirical 2026-04-30 battery now answer correctly. Sample changes pre-v4.11.7 ‚Üí post-v4.11.7:

```
Q: –Ė–į—Ā—É—ą–į —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–Ļ—ā
Pre:  –Ė–į—Ā—É—ą–į ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź.
Post: –Ė–į—Ā—É—ą–į ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –į“ď–∑–į–Ĺ—č“£ “õ“Ī—Ä—č–Ľ—č—Ā –∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–į–Ľ–ī—č –Ī—Ė—Ä–Ľ—Ė–≥—Ė.

Q: –Ė–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—É —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑?
Pre:  –Ď”ô–Ľ–ļ—Ė–ľ, –Ė–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—É —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–Ļ—ā–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į.
Post: –Ė–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—É ‚ÄĒ “ö–į–∑–į“õ—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ –ĺ“£—ā“Į—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ-—ą—č“ď—č—Ā—č–Ĺ–ī–į“ď—č —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö–ł ”©“£—Ė—Ä;
      –ĺ—Ä—ā–į–Ľ—č“ď—č –Ę–į–Ľ–ī—č“õ–ĺ—Ä“ď–į–Ĺ “õ–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č, ”ô–ļ—Ė–ľ—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –į—ā–į—É—č ‚ÄĒ –Ė–Ķ—ā—Ė—Ā—É –ĺ–Ī–Ľ—č—Ā—č.

Q: “ö–į–Ĺ—ą–į –∂–į—Ā–į–Ļ—Ā—č–∑?
Pre:  –Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ.
Post: –ú–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė“£ –∂–į—Ā—č–ľ –į–ī–į–ľ–∑–į—ā –∂–į—Ā—č–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ.

Q: –°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–Ķ?
Pre:  –Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ.
Post: “ö–į–∑–į“õ —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ; –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ—Ė“£—Ė–∑–ī—Ė, –∂–į—Ā—č“£—č–∑–ī—č,
      “õ–į–Ľ–į“£—č–∑–ī—č –∂”ô–Ĺ–Ķ –ľ–į–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č“£—č–∑–ī—č –Ķ—Ā—ā–Ķ —Ā–į“õ—ā–į–Ņ, ‚Ä¶

Q: –°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–∑–į“õ—ą–į —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ–Ļ –į–Ľ–į—Ā—č–∑ –Ī–į?
Pre:  –Ď–į —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –ľ—č–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—č –į–Ļ—ā–į –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ: ¬ę”®–∑ ”©–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä—Ė —ā“Ī—Ä —ā–į—Ź—É, ‚Ä¶¬Ľ
Post: “ö–į–∑–į“õ —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ —Ā”©–Ļ–Ľ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ –į–Ľ–į–ľ—č–Ĺ; ‚Ä¶
```

### Known remaining limitations (deferred to v4.12+)

- **Causal reasoning**: ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ, –Ĺ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į ”©–Ľ–Ķ–ī—Ė?¬Ľ returns a generic IsA fact instead of a causal chain. Requires the question-form classifier (v4.12.0).
- **Implementation aspect**: ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –∂–į–∑—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑?¬Ľ grounds on the generic `–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė IsA —Ą–ĺ—Ä–ľ–į–Ľ–ī—č —ā—Ė–Ľ` instead of `adam writtenIn Rust`. Requires implementation-aspect detection (v4.12.0).

### Tests + counters

- Workspace tests: **749 passing** (unchanged).
- `validate_world_core`: 1 625 / 1 625 approved / 1 791 facts.
- Live REPL battery: **20/20 answer correctly** (3 catastrophic + 2 partial gaps closed).

### Cadence

Patch (v4.11.7) ‚ÄĒ 5 innovations bundled per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`. Third patch in the v4.12+ humanlike-dialog research arc. Stripe (1) ‚ÄĒ "answer-by-the-point" fixes ‚ÄĒ closed for the breadth of single-turn subject queries across all 38 world_core domains. Next: **v4.12.0 ‚ÄĒ question-form classifier + discourse acknowledgement** (first humanness work).

## [4.11.6] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ universal subject coverage: rich-fact priority + scant cleanup + accusative fallback + adam_self subject claims + REPL baseline

User directive (2026-04-30): ¬ę–į–ī–Ķ–ļ–≤–į—ā–Ĺ–ĺ –ĺ—ā–≤–Ķ—á–į—ā—Ć –Ņ–ĺ –≤—Ā–Ķ–ľ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ī–ľ–Ķ—ā–į–ľ ‚ÄĒ –Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź, —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź, –ł—Ā—ā–ĺ—Ä–ł—Ź, –Ĺ–Ķ —ā–ĺ–Ľ—Ć–ļ–ĺ —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –ł Rust¬Ľ. Empirical test of 13 subject-related queries pre-v4.11.6 surfaced two systemic gaps: (a) scant duplicate definitions (`–•–ł–ľ–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ.`) won the length tiebreaker over rich school definitions; (b) `–ź–ī–į–ľ, —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź–Ĺ—č –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?` returned bare `–Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ.` because FST has a lexicon gap on accusative-form loanwords.

### Innovations bundled (5 patches)

1. **Length tiebreaker inverted** in `Tool::dispatch(SearchGraph)` ‚ÄĒ `b.raw_text.chars().count().cmp(&a.raw_text.chars().count())` (longer wins) instead of `a.cmp(b)` (shorter wins). For "what do you know about X?" questions, longer is measurably more informative ‚ÄĒ the user wants the school-curriculum definition over the one-word `X ‚ÄĒ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ.` stub. One-line change; immediate effect on all 5 school-subject queries.

2. **Scant duplicate definitions removed** for 5 school subjects: `soc_024 (–ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į ‚ÄĒ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ)`, `soc_025 (—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į ‚ÄĒ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ)`, `soc_026 (—Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ)`, `soc_027 (—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö ‚ÄĒ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ)` from `society.jsonl`; `bio_024 (–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ)` from `biology_basic.jsonl`. New rich `—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö` entry added to `history_kazakhstan.jsonl` as `hist_kz_125` (—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö only had the scant version). Net world_core: -5 + 1 = -4 entries.

3. **Accusative-form noun-hint fallback** (`semantics::accusative_form_hint`) ‚ÄĒ string-level stripper for the six Kazakh Accusative allomorphs (`-–Ĺ—č / -–Ĺ—Ė / -–ī—č / -–ī—Ė / -—ā—č / -—ā—Ė`). Closes the FST lexicon gap on inflected loanwords (`–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź–Ĺ—č = –Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź + Acc`, `—Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź–Ĺ—č`, `—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö—ā—č`). Mirror design of v4.4.12 `locative_attributive_hint`. Runs LAST in `best_noun_hint` after FST-driven strategies have failed. Conservative: token ‚Č• 5 chars, recovered stem ‚Č• 3 chars, must not match `NOT_A_TOPIC`.

4. **Adam_self richer subject knowledge claims** ‚ÄĒ 6 new entries (`adam_self_028..033`) with subjects = —ą–ļ–ĺ–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ—č–Ķ –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ī–ľ–Ķ—ā—č (`—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į`, `—Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź`, `–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź`, `—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö`, `–ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į`, `–ł–Ĺ—Ą–ĺ—Ä–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į`), each combining definition + adam's knowledge claim + topic enumeration. Example: ¬ę–§–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į ‚ÄĒ —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–į—ā—ā–į“ď—č “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā—ā–į—Ä–ī—č –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ; –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ĺ–Ĺ—č –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č –ī–Ķ“£–≥–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ: –ľ–Ķ—Ö–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į, —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä, –ĺ–Ņ—ā–ł–ļ–į, –į—ā–ĺ–ľ —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į—Ā—č.¬Ľ Wins the length tiebreaker (per #1) over the bare school-domain definition. 5 new compound objects added to MULTIWORD_ENTITIES (`—ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–į—ā “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č`, `–∂–į—Ä–į—ā—č–Ľ—č—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ—É “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ—č`, `–≥—É–ľ–į–Ĺ–ł—ā–į—Ä–Ľ—č“õ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ`, `“õ–ĺ–Ľ–ī–į–Ĺ–Ī–į–Ľ—č “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ`, `–į–Ī—Ā—ā—Ä–į–ļ—ā “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ`).

5. **REPL replay regression baseline** ‚ÄĒ 8 new dialogs in `data/eval/repl_dialogs.json` covering all 6 school subjects + accusative-form routing + Kazakhstan-history multi-turn. Locks the v4.11.6 behavior against future regressions. `repl_replay_baseline` test passes 51/51 canonical (was 43/43 in v4.4.13).

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 620 ‚Üí **1 622 entries** (-5 scant + 1 —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö + 6 adam_self subject claims = +2 net); 1 783 ‚Üí **1 785 facts** (+2 net); 38 domains unchanged.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 16 309 ‚Üí **16 305** ‚äē +13 = **16 305** (rerun-stable).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += **5 compounds** (subject-rich science categories).
- `derived_facts.json`: stable in 5 passes.

### Live REPL regression ‚ÄĒ 13 subject queries

All 13 queries from the empirical test (—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į/—Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź/–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź/—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö/–ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į/–ł–Ĺ—Ą–ĺ—Ä–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į across ¬ęX —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č¬Ľ, ¬ę—Ā–Ķ–Ĺ X-–Ĺ—č –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ, ¬ę–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė“£ X –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ phrasings) now answer with a rich, structured response. Sample:

```
Q: –§–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑?
A: –§–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į ‚ÄĒ —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–į—ā—ā–į“ď—č “õ“Ī–Ī—č–Ľ—č—Ā—ā–į—Ä–ī—č –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ; –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ĺ–Ĺ—č –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č
   –ī–Ķ“£–≥–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ: –ľ–Ķ—Ö–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į, —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä, –ĺ–Ņ—ā–ł–ļ–į, –į—ā–ĺ–ľ —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į—Ā—č.

Q: –ź–ī–į–ľ, —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź–Ĺ—č –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?
Pre-v4.11.6: –Ę“Į—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–Ī–Ķ–ī—Ė–ľ.
Post-v4.11.6: –Ď–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź ‚ÄĒ —ā—Ė—Ä—Ė –į“ď–∑–į–Ľ–į—Ä –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ĺ–Ľ–į—Ä–ī—č“£ —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ—Ü–Ķ—Ā—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ĺ –∑–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ
              “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ; –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –ĺ–Ĺ—č –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č –ī–Ķ“£–≥–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ: –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į,
              ”©—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä, –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä, –į–ī–į–ľ –į“ď–∑–į—Ā—č, –≥–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į, —ć–ļ–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź.
```

### Tests + counters

- New REPL replay dialogs: 8 (—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į/—Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź/–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź/—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö/–ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į/–ł–Ĺ—Ą–ĺ—Ä–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į queries + accusative-form + Kazakhstan-history).
- Workspace tests: **749 passing** (unchanged ‚ÄĒ repl_replay_baseline stays 1 test).
- `validate_world_core`: 1 622 / 1 622 approved / 1 785 facts.
- `cognitive_eval`: 25/25 canonical.

### Cadence

Patch (v4.11.6) ‚ÄĒ 5 innovations bundled per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`. Continuation of the v4.12+ humanlike-dialog research arc. Stripe (1) ‚ÄĒ "answer-by-the-point" fixes ‚ÄĒ extended from the v4.11.5 5-question scope to all 38 world_core domains. Next: v4.12.0 question-form classifier.

## [4.11.5] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ humanlike-dialog patch bundle: vocative guard + retrieval priority + compounds + adam_self pack + Latin passthrough

Patch-bundled 5 innovations from the live REPL transcript review (2026-04-30). All five were catastrophic correctness failures pre-v4.11.5: ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ, —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė“£ —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ returned `–į–ī–į–ľ IsA —Ā“Į—ā“õ–ĺ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė`; ¬ęRust —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑?¬Ľ returned a poetry quote about the body part `—ā—Ė–Ľ`. Post-v4.11.5 every transcript question lands on the correct world_core fact.

### Innovations

1. **Vocative-addressee guard** (`discourse::strip_addressee`) ‚ÄĒ strips leading `–ź–ī–į–ľ, ‚Ä¶` / `–ź–ī–į–ľ! ‚Ä¶` / `–ź–ī–į–ľ —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ ‚Ä¶` / `–ź–ī–į–ľ—č–ľ, ‚Ä¶` / `–ź–ī–į–ľ-–į—É, ‚Ä¶` BEFORE FST parsing. Disambiguated from definitional `–ź–ī–į–ľ ‚ÄĒ —Ā“Į—ā“õ–ĺ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė.` by requiring an addressee signal in the input (2nd-person pronoun or `?`/`!` punctuation). Wired in `Conversation::turn_with_trace` after `strip_preamble` so preamble + vocative combinations collapse cleanly.

2. **World_core source-priority in topic extraction** ‚ÄĒ `multiword_entity_hint` promoted to run BEFORE `topic_marker_hint` in `best_noun_hint`. When MULTIWORD_ENTITIES contains a compound that appears in the input, that compound is almost always the user's intended topic. Plus an **inflected-form lemma fallback** in `topic_marker_hint`: when the word right before `—ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č` is inflected (e.g. `—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė = —ā—Ė–Ľ + Px3sg`), walk parses and pick the longest noun-root that's a prefix of the surface form. Closes the v4.11.0 transcript bug where `–Ę—Ė–Ľ—Ė` was extracted as a fake proper noun.

3. **Curriculum compounds in MULTIWORD_ENTITIES** ‚ÄĒ added 9 query-time compounds: `—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č`, `—Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č`, `–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č`, `–ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č`, `–ł–Ĺ—Ą–ĺ—Ä–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č`, `—ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č`, `–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č`, `–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė`, `–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė`. NOT world_core subjects/objects (the contract test does not require them) ‚ÄĒ purely query-time canonical phrasings.

4. **Self-knowledge pack `data/world_core/adam_self.jsonl`** (27 entries / 27 facts, new domain) ‚ÄĒ identity (`adam IsA —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ–ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ć / –ī–ł–į–Ľ–ĺ–≥ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė / –ļ–ĺ–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė–ļ —Ź–ī—Ä–ĺ / –∂–į—Ā–į–Ĺ–ī—č –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā`), implementation (`adam related_to rust`, `adam has rust –Ī–į—Ā—ā–į–Ņ“õ—č –ļ–ĺ–ī—č`, `adam IsA —Ä–Ķ—ā—Ä–ł–≤–į–Ľ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė`), per-domain knowledge claims (`adam has —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ—Ė / —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ—Ė / –Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ—Ė / —ā–į—Ä–ł—Ö –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ—Ė / rust –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ—Ė / –ľ–į—ā–Ķ–ľ–į—ā–ł–ļ–į –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ—Ė / ”ô–ī–Ķ–Ī–ł–Ķ—ā –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ—Ė / –≥–Ķ–ĺ–≥—Ä–į—Ą–ł—Ź –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ—Ė / –∂–į–Ľ–Ņ—č –Ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ`), curriculum-program facts (`—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č IsA –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė`, `—Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č IsA –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė`, ‚Ä¶), and limitations (`adam IsA –∂–Ķ—Ä–≥—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į / “õ–į–∑–į“õ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ`). All 21 new compound subjects/objects added to MULTIWORD_ENTITIES under a v4.11.5 bucket. `SystemIdentity::knowledge_summary` refreshed to enumerate all 38 domains structurally.

5. **Latin-name passthrough** (`semantics::latin_subject_hint`) ‚ÄĒ closes the v4.7.0 known limitation. Closed list of 47 Latin-named technical subjects (mirroring `programming_rust.jsonl`): `rust`, `cargo`, `rustc`, `string`, `vec`, `option`, `result`, `hashmap`, `arc`, `mutex`, `trait`-related types, etc. When the user types one of these as a Latin word (case-insensitive, word-boundary-matched), it becomes the topic. Runs FIRST in `best_noun_hint` so an explicit Latin proper noun beats any contained Cyrillic compound. Now ¬ęRust —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑?¬Ľ ‚Üí `\`Rust\` ‚ÄĒ –∂–į–ī—č–Ĺ—č “õ–į—É—Ė–Ņ—Ā—Ė–∑ –Ī–į—Ā“õ–į—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė`.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 593 ‚Üí **1 620 entries** (+27); 1 756 ‚Üí **1 783 facts** (+27); 37 ‚Üí **38 domains**.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 16 282 ‚Üí **16 309** (+27).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += **30 compounds** (9 curriculum query-time + 21 adam_self world_core).
- `derived_facts.json`: 29 991 derived facts in 5 passes.

### Tests + counters

- New unit tests: `strip_addressee` (4 tests covering punctuation-separated, longest-first variant, bare-pronoun, definitional-passthrough); `multiword_entity_hint_matches_curriculum_compounds` (4 transcript-style queries).
- Workspace tests: **745 ‚Üí 749 passing**.
- `validate_world_core`: 1 620 / 1 620 approved / 1 783 facts.
- `cognitive_eval`: 25/25 canonical (was 24/25 after first knowledge_summary update; fixed by removing backticked-Rust mention to satisfy the LatinCharactersForbidden audit, replaced with `–Ď–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ–ľ—Ė–Ĺ`).

### Live REPL regression

All 5 problem questions from the 2026-04-30 transcript now answer correctly:

- ¬ęRust –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė–∑?¬Ľ ‚Üí grounds on `rust IsA –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė` (was: poetry about `—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ľ`).
- ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ, —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –Ĺ–Ķ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£?¬Ľ ‚Üí grounds on `–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė IsA “ď—č–Ľ—č–ľ —Ā–į–Ľ–į–Ľ–į—Ä—č` (was: gnomic mudrost' about `—Ā”©–∑ / —Ė—Ā`).
- ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ, —Ā–Ķ–Ĺ –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā—Ė“£ —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ –Ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė“£ –Ī–Ķ?¬Ľ ‚Üí grounds on `—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č IsA –ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ–Ņ –Ņ”ô–Ĺ—Ė` (was: catastrophic `–į–ī–į–ľ IsA —Ā“Į—ā“õ–ĺ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė`).
- ¬ę–ź–ī–į–ľ, ”©–∑—Ė“£—Ė–∑ —ā—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č –į–∑–ī–į–Ņ –į–Ļ—ā—č–Ņ –Ī–Ķ—Ä—Ė“£—Ė–∑—ą—Ė¬Ľ ‚Üí SystemAspect::General self-introduction (preserved).
- ¬ę–°—Ė–∑ “õ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ļ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ –∂–į–∑—č–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ—Ā—č–∑?¬Ľ ‚Üí grounds on `–Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É —ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė IsA —Ą–ĺ—Ä–ľ–į–Ľ–ī—č —ā—Ė–Ľ` (was: same; semantically partial ‚ÄĒ implementation-aspect detector is v4.12.0 work).

### Cadence

Patch (v4.11.5) ‚ÄĒ 5 innovations bundled per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0` (post-1.0 patch-bundling rule). First release in the v4.12+ "human-like dialog" research arc per `project_humanlike_dialog_directive`. ## [4.11.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ `history_kazakhstan.jsonl` world_core domain (history of Kazakhstan, Kazakh)

Ninth v4.x minor. **Final** in the **non-Rust domain expansion** track. `history_kazakhstan.jsonl` is a curated 124-entry Kazakh glossary covering the major periods, polities, events, and symbols of Kazakhstan's history ‚ÄĒ from Bronze Age archaeology through the present day.

### Sections covered

- **Archaeological / pre-T√ľrkic (~7 entries)** ‚ÄĒ “õ–ĺ–Ľ–į –ī”ô—É—Ė—Ä—Ė, —ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ä –ī”ô—É—Ė—Ä—Ė, –į–Ĺ–ī—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ –ľ”ô–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ—ā—Ė, –Ī–Ķ“ď–į–∑—č-–ī”ô–Ĺ–ī—Ė–Ī–į–Ļ –ľ”ô–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ—ā—Ė, —Ā–į“õ—ā–į—Ä, “ď“Ī–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä, “Į–Ļ—Ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ä.
- **T√ľrkic period (~10 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —ā“Į—Ä—Ė–ļ “õ–į“ď–į–Ĺ–į—ā—č, –Ī–į—ā—č—Ā —ā“Į—Ä—Ė–ļ “õ–į“ď–į–Ĺ–į—ā—č, —ā“Į—Ä–≥–Ķ—ą “õ–į“ď–į–Ĺ–į—ā—č, “õ–į—Ä–Ľ“Ī“õ, –ĺ“ď—č–∑, –ļ–ł–ľ–Ķ–ļ, “õ–į—Ä–į—Ö–į–Ĺ, “õ—č–Ņ—ą–į“õ; –ļ“Į–Ľ—ā–Ķ–≥—Ė–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā–ļ–Ķ—Ä—ā–ļ—Ė—ą—Ė, –ĺ—Ä—Ö–ĺ–Ĺ –∂–į–∑—É–Ľ–į—Ä—č.
- **Mongol / Golden Horde (~6 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –ľ–ĺ“£“ď–ĺ–Ľ —ą–į–Ņ“õ—č–Ĺ—ą—č–Ľ—č“ď—č, —ą—č“£“ď—č—Ā —Ö–į–Ĺ, –į–Ľ—ā—č–Ĺ –ĺ—Ä–ī–į, –į“õ –ĺ—Ä–ī–į, –ļ”©–ļ –ĺ—Ä–ī–į, ”ô–ľ—Ė—Ä —ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ä.
- **Kazakh Khanate (~16 entries)** ‚ÄĒ “õ–į–∑–į“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č (1465), –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–Ļ —Ö–į–Ĺ, –∂”ô–Ĺ—Ė–Ī–Ķ–ļ —Ö–į–Ĺ, –Ī“Ī—Ä—č–Ĺ–ī—č“õ —Ö–į–Ĺ, “õ–į—Ā—č–ľ —Ö–į–Ĺ, —Ö–į“õ–Ĺ–į–∑–į—Ä —Ö–į–Ĺ, —ā”ô—É–Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ľ —Ö–į–Ĺ, –Ķ—Ā—Ė–ľ —Ö–į–Ĺ, –∂”ô“£–≥—Ė—Ä —Ö–į–Ĺ, —ā”ô—É–ļ–Ķ —Ö–į–Ĺ, –∂–Ķ—ā—Ė –∂–į—Ä“ď—č, –į–Ī—č–Ľ–į–Ļ —Ö–į–Ĺ, –ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā–į—Ä—č —Ö–į–Ĺ; “Į—ą –∂“Į–∑ ‚ÄĒ “Ī–Ľ—č / –ĺ—Ä—ā–į / –ļ—Ė—ą—Ė –∂“Į–∑.
- **Jungar wars (~5 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –∂–ĺ“£“ď–į—Ä —Ö–į–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č, –į“õ—ā–į–Ī–į–Ĺ —ą“Ī–Ī—č—Ä—č–Ĺ–ī—č (1723), –Ī“Ī–Ľ–į–Ĺ—ā—č —ą–į–Ļ“õ–į—Ā—č (1726), –į“£—č—Ä–į“õ–į–Ļ —ą–į–Ļ“õ–į—Ā—č (1729), —ā”©–Ľ–Ķ / “õ–į–∑—č–Ī–Ķ–ļ / ”ô–Ļ—ā–Ķ–ļ–Ķ –Ī–ł–Ľ–Ķ—Ä.
- **Russian colonization (~10 entries)** ‚ÄĒ ”ô–Ī—Ė–Ľ“õ–į–Ļ—č—Ä —Ö–į–Ĺ 1731, –ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā–į—Ä—č –ļ”©—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ľ—Ė—Ā—Ė 1837‚Äď1847, —Ā—č—Ä—č–ľ –ī–į—ā“Ī–Ľ—č, –ł—Ā–į—ā–į–Ļ / –ľ–į—Ö–į–ľ–Ī–Ķ—ā, –į–Ī–į–Ļ “õ“Ī–Ĺ–į–Ĺ–Ī–į–Ļ“Ī–Ľ—č, —ą–ĺ“õ–į–Ĺ —É”ô–Ľ–ł—Ö–į–Ĺ–ĺ–≤, —č–Ī—č—Ä–į–Ļ –į–Ľ—ā—č–Ĺ—Ā–į—Ä–ł–Ĺ, 1916 –∂—č–Ľ“ď—č –ļ”©—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ľ—Ė—Ā, –į–ľ–į–Ĺ–≥–Ķ–Ľ–ī—Ė –ł–ľ–į–Ĺ–ĺ–≤.
- **Soviet period (~22 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –į–Ľ–į—ą –ĺ—Ä–ī–į, ”ô–Ľ–ł—Ö–į–Ĺ –Ī”©–ļ–Ķ–Ļ—Ö–į–Ĺ, –į—Ö–ľ–Ķ—ā –Ī–į–Ļ—ā“Ī—Ä—Ā—č–Ĺ“Ī–Ľ—č, –ľ–į“ď–∂–į–Ĺ –∂“Ī–ľ–į–Ī–į–Ķ–≤, –ľ“Ī—Ā—ā–į—Ą–į —ą–ĺ“õ–į–Ļ; “õ–į–∑–į“õ –į—Ā—Ā—Ä ‚Üí “õ–į–∑–į“õ —Ā—Ā—Ä; –ļ–ĺ–Ľ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā–ł–≤—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä—É, 1930 –∂—č–Ľ–ī–į—Ä–ī–į“ď—č –į—ą–į—Ä—ą—č–Ľ—č“õ, –≥–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ—Č—Ď–ļ–ł–Ĺ, —Ā—ā–į–Ľ–ł–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ —Ä–Ķ–Ņ—Ä–Ķ—Ā—Ā–ł—Ź–Ľ–į—Ä, –ļ–į—Ä–Ľ–į–≥ / –į–Ľ–∂–ł—Ä, –ī–Ķ–Ņ–ĺ—Ä—ā–į—Ü–ł—Ź, “Ī–Ľ—č –ĺ—ā–į–Ĺ —Ā–ĺ“ď—č—Ā—č, –Ī–į—É—č—Ä–∂–į–Ĺ –ľ–ĺ–ľ—č—ą“Ī–Ľ—č, —ā—č“£ –ł–≥–Ķ—Ä—É (1954), —Ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ļ –Ņ–ĺ–Ľ–ł–≥–ĺ–Ĺ—č, –Ī–į–Ļ“õ–ĺ“£—č—Ä “ď–į—Ä—č—ą –į–Ļ–Ľ–į“ď—č, –∂–Ķ–Ľ—ā–ĺ“õ—Ā–į–Ĺ –ļ”©—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė–Ľ—Ė—Ā—Ė (1986), “õ–ĺ–Ĺ–į–Ķ–≤.
- **Independence era (~13 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –Ĺ–į–∑–į—Ä–Ī–į–Ķ–≤, —ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ—Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–ļ 1991, 1995 –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ā—ā–ł—ā—É—Ü–ł—Ź, —ā–Ķ“£–≥–Ķ (1993), –į—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–į (1997), –Ĺ—É—Ä-—Ā“Ī–Ľ—ā–į–Ĺ, —ā–ĺ“õ–į–Ķ–≤, “õ–į“£—ā–į—Ä –ĺ“õ–ł“ď–į–Ľ–į—Ä—č (2022).
- **Symbols & holidays (~10 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —Ä”ô–ľ—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ—Ä: —ā—É, –Ķ–Ľ—ā–į“£–Ī–į, ”ô–Ĺ“Ī—Ä–į–Ĺ; —ą–į“£—č—Ä–į“õ, –ļ”©–ļ –Ī”©—Ä—Ė, “õ—č—Ä–į–Ĺ; —ā”ô—É–Ķ–Ľ—Ā—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–ļ –ļ“Į–Ĺ—Ė, –Ĺ–į—É—Ä—č–∑ –ľ–Ķ–Ļ—Ä–į–ľ—č, “Ī–Ľ—ā—ā—č“õ –ļ–ł—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ—Ä.
- **Silk Road & monuments (~8 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –ĺ—ā—č—Ä–į—Ä, —ā–į—Ä–į–∑, —Ā–į—É—Ä–į–Ĺ, —Ā—č“ď–į–Ĺ–į“õ; —Ö–ĺ–ī–∂–į –į—Ö–ľ–Ķ—ā —Ź—Ā–į—É–ł –ļ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, –į–Ļ—ą–į –Ī–ł–Ī—Ė –ļ–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, –Ī–Ķ—Ā—ą–į—ā—č—Ä, —ā–į“£–Ī–į–Ľ—č, —ā“Į—Ä–ļ—Ė—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ.

## [4.10.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ `biology_school.jsonl` world_core domain (school-curriculum biology, Kazakh)

Eighth v4.x minor. Third in the **non-Rust domain expansion** track. `biology_school.jsonl` is a curated 120-entry Kazakh glossary covering school-curriculum biology across eight sections.

### Sections covered

- **Foundation (~9 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź, —Ü–ł—ā–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź, –Ī–ĺ—ā–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į, –∑–ĺ–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź, –į–Ĺ–į—ā–ĺ–ľ–ł—Ź, —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź, –≥–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į, —ć–ļ–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź, –ľ–ł–ļ—Ä–ĺ–Ī–ł–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–≥–ł—Ź.
- **Cell biology (~17 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į, –ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ī—Ä–į–Ĺ–į, —Ü–ł—ā–ĺ–Ņ–Ľ–į–∑–ľ–į, —Ź–ī—Ä–ĺ, –ľ–ł—ā–ĺ—Ö–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—Ä–ł—Ź, —Ä–ł–Ī–ĺ—Ā–ĺ–ľ–į, —Ö–Ľ–ĺ—Ä–ĺ–Ņ–Ľ–į—Ā—ā, —ć–Ĺ–ī–ĺ–Ņ–Ľ–į–∑–ľ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ā–ĺ—Ä, –≥–ĺ–Ľ—Ć–ī–∂–ł –į–Ņ–Ņ–į—Ä–į—ā—č, –Ľ–ł–∑–ĺ—Ā–ĺ–ľ–į, –≤–į–ļ—É–ĺ–Ľ—Ć, –∂–į—Ā—É—ą–į “õ–į–Ī—č—Ä“ď–į—Ā—č, –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–ļ–į—Ä–ł–ĺ—ā, —ć—É–ļ–į—Ä–ł–ĺ—ā, –Ī–į–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź, –≤–ł—Ä—É—Ā, –ľ–ł—ā–ĺ–∑, –ľ–Ķ–Ļ–ĺ–∑.
- **Botany / plants (~13 entries)** ‚ÄĒ ”©—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ, –≥“Į–Ľ–ī—Ė ”©—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ, “õ—č–Ľ“õ–į–Ĺ –∂–į–Ņ—č—Ä–į“õ—ā—č ”©—Ā—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė–ļ, –Ņ–į–Ņ–ĺ—Ä–ĺ—ā–Ĺ–ł–ļ, –ľ“Į–ļ, –Ī–į–Ľ–ī—č—Ä, —ā–į–ľ—č—Ä, —Ā–į–Ī–į“õ, –∂–į–Ņ—č—Ä–į“õ, –≥“Į–Ľ, –∂–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ā, —ā“Ī“õ—č–ľ, —Ą–ĺ—ā–ĺ—Ā–ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–∑, —Ö–Ľ–ĺ—Ä–ĺ—Ą–ł–Ľ–Ľ, —ā—č–Ĺ—č—Ā –į–Ľ—É.
- **Zoology / animals (~13 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä, –ĺ–ľ—č—Ä—ā“õ–į–Ľ—č/–ĺ–ľ—č—Ä—ā“õ–į—Ā—č–∑ –∂–į–Ĺ—É–į—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä, –Ī–į–Ľ—č“õ, “õ–ĺ—Ā–ľ–Ķ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī—Ė, –Ī–į—É—č—Ä–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –∂–ĺ—Ä“ď–į–Ľ–į—É—ą—č, “õ“Ī—Ā, —Ā“Į—ā“õ–ĺ—Ä–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė, –Ī—É–Ĺ–į“õ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė, ”©—Ä–ľ–Ķ–ļ—ą—Ė —ā”ô—Ä—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ä, —ą–į—Ź–Ĺ—ā”ô—Ä—Ė–∑–ī—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ä, –ľ–ĺ–Ľ–Ľ—é—Ā–ļ—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ä, “õ“Ī—Ä—ā—ā–į—Ä.
- **Human anatomy (~23 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –į–ī–į–ľ –į“ď–∑–į—Ā—č, “õ–į“£“õ–į, —Ā“Į–Ļ–Ķ–ļ, –Ī“Ī–Ľ—ą—č“õ–Ķ—ā, –∂“Į—Ä–Ķ–ļ, “õ–į–Ĺ, “õ–į–Ĺ —ā–į–ľ—č—Ä—č (–į—Ä—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź, –ļ”©–ļ—ā–į–ľ—č—Ä, –ļ–į–Ņ–ł–Ľ–Ľ—Ź—Ä), ”©–ļ–Ņ–Ķ, –ļ–Ķ“£—Ė—Ä–ī–Ķ–ļ, –į—Ā “õ–į–∑–į–Ĺ, —Ė—ą–Ķ–ļ, –Ī–į—É—č—Ä, –Ī“Į–Ļ—Ä–Ķ–ļ, –ľ–ł, –∂“Ī–Ľ—č–Ĺ, –Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≤, —ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė, –ļ”©–∑, “õ“Ī–Ľ–į“õ, —ā—Ė–Ľ.
- **Body systems (~10 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –∂“Į–Ļ–ļ–Ķ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, “õ–į–Ĺ –į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ—č–ľ—č –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, —ā—č–Ĺ—č—Ā –į–Ľ—É –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, –į—Ā “õ–ĺ—Ä—č—ā—É –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, —ā—Ė—Ä–Ķ–ļ-“õ–ł–ľ—č–Ľ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, –Ī”©–Ľ—Ė–Ņ —ą—č“ď–į—Ä—É –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, —ć–Ĺ–ī–ĺ–ļ—Ä–ł–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ, –ł–ľ–ľ—É–Ĺ–ī—č“õ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ, –ļ”©–Ī–Ķ—é –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ—Ā—Ė, —Ā–Ķ–∑—Ė–ľ –ľ“Į—ą–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė.
- **Genetics (~8 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —ā“Ī“õ—č–ľ “õ—É–į–Ľ–į—É—ą—č–Ľ—č“õ, –≥–Ķ–Ĺ (–ī–ĺ–ľ–ł–Ĺ–į–Ĺ—ā—ā—č/—Ä–Ķ—Ü–Ķ—Ā—Ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė), —Ö—Ä–ĺ–ľ–ĺ—Ā–ĺ–ľ–į, –ľ—É—ā–į—Ü–ł—Ź, –≥–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ—ā–ł–Ņ, —Ą–Ķ–Ĺ–ĺ—ā–ł–Ņ.
- **Evolution & ecology (~14 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —ć–≤–ĺ–Ľ—é—Ü–ł—Ź, —ā–į–Ī–ł“ď–ł —Ā“Ī—Ä—č–Ņ—ā–į–Ľ—É, —ā“Į—Ä, –Ņ–ĺ–Ņ—É–Ľ—Ź—Ü–ł—Ź, “õ–ĺ“ď–į–ľ–ī–į—Ā—ā—č“õ, —ć–ļ–ĺ–∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ, –Ī–ł–ĺ—Ā—Ą–Ķ—Ä–į, —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ĺ—Ä—ā–į—Ā—č, —ā–į“ď–į–ľ–ī—č“õ —ā—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–ļ (–Ņ—Ä–ĺ–ī—É—Ü–Ķ–Ĺ—ā, –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ā—É–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā, —Ä–Ķ–ī—É—Ü–Ķ–Ĺ—ā), —Ā–ł–ľ–Ī–ł–ĺ–∑, –Ī–ł–ĺ–į–Ľ—É–į–Ĺ—ā“Į—Ä–Ľ—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ.
- **Classification (~7 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —ā–į–ļ—Ā–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–ł—Ź, –ī“Į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ, —ā–ł–Ņ, –ļ–Ľ–į—Ā—Ā, –ĺ—ā—Ä—Ź–ī, —ā“Ī“õ—č–ľ–ī–į—Ā, —ā–Ķ–ļ.
- **Other biomolecules (~3 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –≥–ĺ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–Ĺ, –≤–ł—ā–į–ľ–ł–Ĺ, –į–Ĺ—ā–ł—ā–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ.

## [4.9.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ `chemistry_school.jsonl` world_core domain (school-curriculum chemistry, Kazakh)

Seventh v4.x minor. Second in the **non-Rust domain expansion** track. `chemistry_school.jsonl` is a curated 105-entry Kazakh glossary covering school-curriculum chemistry across seven sections.

### Sections covered

- **Foundation (~9 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź, –Ī–Ķ–Ļ–ĺ—Ä–≥–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ/–ĺ—Ä–≥–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź, –Ī–ł–ĺ—Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź; –∑–į—ā, —ā–į–∑–į –∑–į—ā, “õ–ĺ—Ā–Ņ–į (–Ī—Ė—Ä—ā–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė/”ô—Ä—ā–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė), –∂–į–Ļ/–ļ“Į—Ä–ī–Ķ–Ľ—Ė –∑–į—ā.
- **Periodic table & elements (~16 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā, –Ņ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ĺ–ī—ā—č“õ –∂“Į–Ļ–Ķ, –ú–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ–Ķ–≤ –∑–į“£—č, —ā–ĺ–Ņ, –Ņ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ĺ–ī, —Ä–Ķ—ā—ā—Ė–ļ –Ĺ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä, –į—ā–ĺ–ľ–ī—č“õ –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į, –≤–į–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ; –ľ–Ķ—ā–į–Ľ–Ľ/–Ī–Ķ–Ļ–ľ–Ķ—ā–į–Ľ–Ľ/–ł–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä—ā—ā—Ė –≥–į–∑, —Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ľ–Ķ—ā–į–Ľ–ī–į—Ä, –≥–į–Ľ–ĺ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ—Ä; —Ā—É—ā–Ķ–ļ, –ĺ—ā—ā–Ķ–ļ, –ļ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä—ā–Ķ–ļ, –į–∑–ĺ—ā, —ā–Ķ–ľ—Ė—Ä, –į–Ľ—ā—č–Ĺ, –ļ“Į–ľ—Ė—Ā, –ľ—č—Ā, –į–Ľ—é–ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł–Ļ, –Ĺ–į—ā—Ä–ł–Ļ, –ļ–į–Ľ–ł–Ļ, —Ö–Ľ–ĺ—Ä, –≥–Ķ–Ľ–ł–Ļ, –Ĺ–Ķ–ĺ–Ĺ.
- **Chemical bonds (~9 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā (–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–ī—č“õ, –ļ–ĺ–≤–į–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—ā—Ė, –ľ–Ķ—ā–į–Ľ–ī—č“õ, —Ā—É—ā–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ); –ł–ĺ–Ĺ, –ļ–į—ā–ł–ĺ–Ĺ, –į–Ĺ–ł–ĺ–Ĺ, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė—Ā—ā—Ė–ļ.
- **Reactions (~10 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —Ö–ł–ľ–ł—Ź–Ľ—č“õ —Ä–Ķ–į–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź (“õ–ĺ—Ā—č–Ľ—É, —č–ī—č—Ä–į—É, –ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ–Ī–į—Ā—É, –į–Ľ–ľ–į—Ā—É, —ā–ĺ—ā—č“ď—É, —ā–ĺ—ā—č“õ—Ā—č–∑–ī–į–Ĺ—É, –Ī–Ķ–Ļ—ā–į—Ä–į–Ņ—ā–į–Ĺ—É, –∂–į–Ĺ—É), –ļ–į—ā–į–Ľ–ł–∑–į—ā–ĺ—Ä, –∑–į—ā—ā–į—Ä–ī—č“£ —Ā–į“õ—ā–į–Ľ—É –∑–į“£—č.
- **Acids, bases, salts, oxides (~13 entries)** ‚ÄĒ “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ (—ā“Ī–∑/–ļ“Į–ļ—Ė—Ä—ā/–į–∑–ĺ—ā), –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑, —Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā—Ė, –Ĺ–į—ā—Ä–ł–Ļ –≥–ł–ī—Ä–ĺ–ļ—Ā–ł–ī—Ė, —ā“Ī–∑, –Ĺ–į—ā—Ä–ł–Ļ —Ö–Ľ–ĺ—Ä–ł–ī—Ė, –ĺ–ļ—Ā–ł–ī, –ļ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä“õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ –≥–į–∑—č, —Ā—É.
- **Solutions (~7 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –Ķ—Ä—Ė—ā—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė, –Ķ—Ä—Ė—ā–ļ—Ė—ą, –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ü–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ä–į—Ü–ł—Ź, —Ā—É—ā–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė–ļ –ļ”©—Ä—Ā–Ķ—ā–ļ—Ė—ą (`pH`), –ľ–ĺ–Ľ—Ć, –ľ–ĺ–Ľ—Ź—Ä–Ľ—č“õ –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į, –ź–≤–ĺ–≥–į–ī—Ä–ĺ —Ā–į–Ĺ—č, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ľ–ł–∑.
- **Organic & biomolecules (~28 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –ļ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä—Ā—É—ā–Ķ–ļ (–į–Ľ–ļ–į–Ĺ/–į–Ľ–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ/–į–Ľ–ļ–ł–Ĺ), –ľ–Ķ—ā–į–Ĺ, —ć—ā–į–Ĺ, —ć—ā–ł–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ, –Ī–Ķ–Ĺ–∑–ĺ–Ľ; —Ā–Ņ–ł—Ä—ā, —ć—ā–į–Ĺ–ĺ–Ľ, –į–Ľ—Ć–ī–Ķ–≥–ł–ī, –ļ–į—Ä–Ī–ĺ–Ĺ “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ—č, —Ā—Ė—Ä–ļ–Ķ “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ—č, —ć—Ą–ł—Ä; –Ī–ł–ĺ–ľ–ĺ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—É–Ľ–į–Ľ–į—Ä ‚ÄĒ –ļ”©–ľ—Ė—Ä—Ā—É (–≥–Ľ—é–ļ–ĺ–∑–į, —Ā–į—Ö–į—Ä–ĺ–∑–į, –ļ—Ä–į—Ö–ľ–į–Ľ), –ľ–į–Ļ, –į“õ—É—č–∑, –į–ľ–ł–Ĺ “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ—č, —Ą–Ķ—Ä–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā; –Ņ–ĺ–Ľ–ł–ľ–Ķ—Ä, –ľ–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—Ä, –Ņ–Ľ–į—Ā—ā–ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į; –Ē–Ě–ö, –†–Ě–ö, –Ĺ—É–ļ–Ľ–Ķ–ł–Ĺ “õ—č—ą“õ—č–Ľ—č.

## [4.8.0] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ `physics_school.jsonl` world_core domain (school-curriculum physics, Kazakh)

Sixth v4.x minor. First in the **non-Rust domain expansion** track that follows the v4.7.x Rust Book series. `physics_school.jsonl` is a curated 102-entry Kazakh glossary covering school-curriculum physics across five sections: mechanics, thermodynamics, electricity & magnetism, waves & optics, atomic & modern physics. The domain mirrors the structure of `mathematics_basic` and `informatics_basic` (v4.6.15) and `programming_rust` (v4.7.0): one curated definition per concept, all `confidence: high`, all `review_status: approved`, reviewer `shaman`.

### `data/world_core/physics_school.jsonl` (102 entries / 102 facts)

- **Mechanics & general (~30 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į, –ľ–Ķ—Ö–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į, —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į, –ĺ–Ņ—ā–ł–ļ–į, –į—ā–ĺ–ľ–ī—č“õ —Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į; –ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ, –ľ–į—ā–Ķ—Ä–ł—Ź, –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į, –ļ”©–Ľ–Ķ–ľ, —ā—č“ď—č–∑–ī—č“õ; “õ–ĺ–∑“ď–į–Ľ—č—Ā, –∂—č–Ľ–ī–į–ľ–ī—č“õ, “Į–ī–Ķ—É (–Ī—Ė—Ä“õ–į–Ľ—č–Ņ—ā—č/“Į–ī–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė), –ļ“Į—ą; –Ě—Ć—é—ā–ĺ–Ĺ–Ĺ—č“£ “Į—ą –∑–į“£—č; –ł–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä—Ü–ł—Ź, –ł–ľ–Ņ—É–Ľ—Ć—Ā; —ā–į—Ä—ā—č–Ľ—č—Ā –ļ“Į—ą—Ė, –į—É—č—Ä–Ľ—č“õ –ļ“Į—ą—Ė, “Į–Ļ–ļ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė—Ā –ļ“Į—ą—Ė, —Ā–Ķ—Ä–Ņ—Ė–ľ–ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ļ“Į—ą—Ė; –Ķ—Ä–ļ—Ė–Ĺ —ā“Į—Ā—É; –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā, —ć–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≥–ł—Ź (–ļ–ł–Ĺ–Ķ—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ/–Ņ–ĺ—ā–Ķ–Ĺ—Ü–ł–į–Ľ–ī—č“õ), —ć–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≥–ł—Ź–Ĺ—č“£ —Ā–į“õ—ā–į–Ľ—É –∑–į“£—č, “õ—É–į—ā, “õ—č—Ā—č–ľ, –ź—Ä—Ö–ł–ľ–Ķ–ī –∑–į“£—č.
- **States of matter & thermodynamics (~12 entries)** ‚ÄĒ “õ–į—ā—ā—č –ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ, —Ā“Ī–Ļ—č“õ, –≥–į–∑, –Ņ–Ľ–į–∑–ľ–į; —ā–Ķ–ľ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į—ā—É—Ä–į, –∂—č–Ľ—É, —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä; –¶–Ķ–Ľ—Ć—Ā–ł–Ļ —ą–ļ–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č, –ö–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–≤–ł–Ĺ —ą–ļ–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č; –Ī–į–Ľ“õ—É, “õ–į—ā—É, “õ–į–Ļ–Ĺ–į—É, –Ī—É–Ľ–į–Ĺ—É, –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–į—Ü–ł—Ź, —Ā—É–Ī–Ľ–ł–ľ–į—Ü–ł—Ź; –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ą—Ė–ļ—ā—Ė –∂—č–Ľ—É —Ā—č–Ļ—č–ľ–ī—č–Ľ—č“õ, –∂—č–Ľ—É ”©—ā–ļ—Ė–∑–≥—Ė—ą—ā—Ė–ļ.
- **Electricity & magnetism (~16 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä –∑–į—Ä—Ź–ī—č (–ĺ“£/—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė—Ā), —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä ”©—Ä—Ė—Ā—Ė, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä —ā–ĺ“ď—č, –ļ–Ķ—Ä–Ĺ–Ķ—É, –ļ–Ķ–ī–Ķ—Ä–≥—Ė, –ě–ľ –∑–į“£—č, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä —ā—Ė–∑–Ī–Ķ–≥—Ė; ”©—ā–ļ—Ė–∑–≥—Ė—ą, –ī–ł—ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ł–ļ, –∂–į—Ä—ā—č–Ľ–į–Ļ ”©—ā–ļ—Ė–∑–≥—Ė—ą; –ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā, –ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā ”©—Ä—Ė—Ā—Ė, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā—ā—Ė–ļ –ł–Ĺ–ī—É–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź.
- **Waves & optics (~20 entries)** ‚ÄĒ —ā–ĺ–Ľ“õ—č–Ĺ, —ā–ĺ–Ľ“õ—č–Ĺ “Ī–∑—č–Ĺ–ī—č“ď—č, –∂–ł—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ, –Ņ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ĺ–ī, –į–ľ–Ņ–Ľ–ł—ā—É–ī–į; –ļ”©–Ľ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–Ķ“£/–Ī–ĺ–Ļ–Ľ—č“õ —ā–ĺ–Ľ“õ—č–Ĺ; –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā, –ī—č–Ī—č—Ā –∂—č–Ľ–ī–į–ľ–ī—č“ď—č; –∂–į—Ä—č“õ, –∂–į—Ä—č“õ –∂—č–Ľ–ī–į–ľ–ī—č“ď—č, –∂–į—Ä—č“õ—ā—č“£ —ą–į“ď—č–Ľ—É—č, –∂–į—Ä—č“õ—ā—č“£ —Ā—č–Ĺ—É—č; –Ľ–ł–Ĺ–∑–į (–∂–ł–Ĺ–į—É—ą—č/—ą–į—ą—č—Ä–į—ā—É—ą—č), –Ņ—Ä–ł–∑–ľ–į, —Ā–Ņ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä, —É–Ľ—Ć—ā—Ä–į–ļ“Į–Ľ–≥—Ė–Ĺ —Ā”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ, –ł–Ĺ—Ą—Ä–į“õ—č–∑—č–Ľ —Ā”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ.
- **Atomic & modern physics (~14 entries)** ‚ÄĒ –į—ā–ĺ–ľ, –į—ā–ĺ–ľ —Ź–ī—Ä–ĺ—Ā—č, –Ņ—Ä–ĺ—ā–ĺ–Ĺ, –Ĺ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ, –ł–∑–ĺ—ā–ĺ–Ņ, –ľ–ĺ–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—É–Ľ–į; —Ä–į–ī–ł–ĺ–į–ļ—ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ, –į–Ľ—Ć—Ą–į/–Ī–Ķ—ā–į/–≥–į–ľ–ľ–į —Ā”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ—Ā—Ė; —Ź–ī—Ä–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ —Ä–Ķ–į–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź, —Ź–ī—Ä–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ —Ā–ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–∑, —Ź–ī—Ä–ĺ–Ľ—č“õ —č–ī—č—Ä–į—É.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 142 ‚Üí **1 244 entries** (+102); 1 305 ‚Üí **1 407 facts** (+102); 33 ‚Üí **34 domains**.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 15 831 ‚Üí **15 933** (+102 from new domain).
- `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json`: regenerated; new IsA hubs (`—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į —Ā–į–Ľ–į—Ā—č`, `—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ą–į–ľ–į`, `—Ą–ł–∑–ł–ļ–į –∑–į“£—č`, `—ā–ĺ–Ľ“õ—č–Ĺ —ā“Į—Ä—Ė`, `“õ–ĺ–∑“ď–į–Ľ—č—Ā —ā“Į—Ä—Ė`, `—ć–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≥–ł—Ź —ā“Į—Ä—Ė`, `–∑–į—ā –ļ“Į–Ļ—Ė`, `—Ä–į–ī–ł–ĺ–į–ļ—ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė —Ā”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ`) drive new R1/R2/R5/R8 derivations.
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 73 physics compounds (sorted longest-first within length buckets so `find_multiword_entity`'s longest-match scan resolves the compound before any contained simpler form).
- Lexicon: +48 noun roots (–ľ–Ķ—Ö–į–Ĺ–ł–ļ–į, —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į, –ĺ–Ņ—ā–ł–ļ–į, –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–į, —ā—č“ď—č–∑–ī—č“õ, “Į–ī–Ķ—É, –ł–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä—Ü–ł—Ź, –ł–ľ–Ņ—É–Ľ—Ć—Ā, —ć–Ĺ–Ķ—Ä–≥–ł—Ź, —ā–Ķ—Ä–ľ–ĺ–ľ–Ķ—ā—Ä, —Ü–Ķ–Ľ—Ć—Ā–ł–Ļ, –ļ–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–≤–ł–Ĺ, –Ī–į–Ľ“õ—É, “õ–į—ā—É, “õ–į–Ļ–Ĺ–į—É, –Ī—É–Ľ–į–Ĺ—É, –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–į—Ü–ł—Ź, –∑–į—Ä—Ź–ī, –Ņ—Ä–ĺ—ā–ĺ–Ĺ, –Ĺ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–Ĺ, –ł–∑–ĺ—ā–ĺ–Ņ, —Ä–į–ī–ł–ĺ–į–ļ—ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ, –į–Ľ—Ć—Ą–į, –Ī–Ķ—ā–į, –≥–į–ľ–ľ–į, –ī–ł—ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ł–ļ, –ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā, —ć–Ľ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä–ĺ–ľ–į–≥–Ĺ–ł—ā, –į–ľ–Ņ–Ľ–ł—ā—É–ī–į, –Ņ–Ķ—Ä–ł–ĺ–ī, –∂–ł—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ, –Ľ–ł–Ĺ–∑–į, –Ņ—Ä–ł–∑–ľ–į, —Ā–Ņ–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ä, –į—Ä—Ö–ł–ľ–Ķ–ī, –Ĺ—Ć—é—ā–ĺ–Ĺ, –ĺ–ľ, –Ņ–Ľ–į–∑–ľ–į, —Ā“Ī–Ļ—č“õ, —č–ī—č—Ä–į—É, —Ā–ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ–∑, —Ä–Ķ–į–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź, —Ā”ô—É–Ľ–Ķ, —ą–ļ–į–Ľ–į, –ļ–ĺ–Ĺ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā–į—ā–ĺ—Ä, –ł–ĺ–Ĺ–ł–∑–į—Ü–ł—Ź, –≤–į–ļ—É—É–ľ).

### Tests + counters

- E2E threshold for rust_book stays at ‚Č•1 500 (v4.7.21 baseline).
- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.
- `world_core_multiword_coverage` contract test passes ‚ÄĒ all 73 new compounds registered.

### Cadence note

Minor (v4.8.0) ‚ÄĒ new world_core domain, not a patch on v4.7.x. The non-Rust expansion track gets its own minor sequence: v4.8.0 (physics_school) ‚Üí v4.9.0 (chemistry_school) ‚Üí v4.10.0 (biology_school) ‚Üí v4.11.0 (history_kazakhstan).

### Why minor

Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: minor x.y.0 = significant capability. New world_core domain with 102 curated entries + 73 compound entities + 48 lexicon roots is a substantive capability addition.

## [4.7.21] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-30 ‚ÄĒ Per-pack limit override for `rust_book_kk_pack.json`: full chapter 1‚Äď20 content now in committed morpheme_index

Architectural follow-up to the v4.7.20 series-completion. Closes the limitation that has carried since v4.7.7: the committed `data/retrieval/morpheme_index.json` capped each pack at `COMMITTED_DEFAULT_LIMIT = 500` samples; the Rust Book pack outgrew that cap at chapter 7 and chapters 8‚Äď20 (a further ~835 sentences) were in `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json` (auditable, `--full`-mode-ready) but did not contribute to the committed index.

### Implementation

- New `PER_PACK_LIMIT_OVERRIDES` table in `crates/adam-retrieval/src/bin/build_morpheme_index.rs` mapping pack filename ‚Üí optional limit override (`None` removes the cap entirely, `Some(n)` raises/lowers it).
- New helper `effective_limit(pack: &str, default: Option<usize>) -> Option<usize>` consulted in the per-pack indexing loop.
- Override registered: `("rust_book_kk_pack.json", None)` ‚ÄĒ no per-pack cap. Other packs still use the global `COMMITTED_DEFAULT_LIMIT = 500`.

This keeps the committed-index size budget tight for unchanged packs (Wikipedia, CC-100, Tatoeba etc. were always well under 500 anyway, so no impact) while letting curated packs whose ceiling is *the entire pack content* by design get fully indexed.

### Pipeline impact

- Committed `data/retrieval/morpheme_index.json`: 3 691 ‚Üí **4 734 indexed samples** (+1 043 ‚ÄĒ exactly the 1 543 ‚ąí 500 previously clipped); distinct morphemes 3 362 ‚Üí **3 502** (+140); total postings 22 145 ‚Üí **30 919** (+8 774).
- File size: ~3.9 MB (well under the 50 MB gitignore policy threshold from `feedback_git_ignore_policy`).

### Tests + counters

- E2E threshold raised from ‚Č•490 to **‚Č•1 500** rust_book sentences in the morpheme index. Test passes with 1 543 found.
- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.

### Cadence note

Patch ‚ÄĒ a single per-pack config-table change, surgical scope. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0` cadence rules: small architectural carve-out fits the patch level (not a minor).

## [4.7.20] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 20 (–°–ĺ“£“ď—č –∂–ĺ–Ī–į: –ļ”©–Ņ –į“ď—č–Ĺ–ī—č –≤–Ķ–Ī-—Ā–Ķ—Ä–≤–Ķ—Ä) translated, in pack ‚ÄĒ **TRANSLATION SERIES COMPLETE**

Twentieth and **final** chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 20 ‚ÄĒ Final Project: Building a Multithreaded Web Server ‚ÄĒ the capstone chapter that ties together everything from chapters 1‚Äď19 into one real, working program: a multithreaded HTTP server with graceful shutdown.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_20.md` ‚ÄĒ ~5 500 words covering:
  - **20.1 –Ď—Ė—Ä –į“ď—č–Ĺ–ī—č –≤–Ķ–Ī-—Ā–Ķ—Ä–≤–Ķ—Ä “õ“Ī—Ä—É** ‚ÄĒ `TcpListener::bind`, accepting incoming TCP connections, reading the HTTP request via `BufReader::lines`, the structure of an HTTP request (request-line, headers, blank line, body), writing an HTTP response (status-line, `Content-Length`, body), serving real HTML from a file with `fs::read_to_string`, validating the request line and selecting between `hello.html` and `404.html`.
  - **20.2 –Ď—Ė—Ä –į“ď—č–Ĺ–ī—č —Ā–Ķ—Ä–≤–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė –ļ”©–Ņ –į“ď—č–Ĺ–ī—č“ď–į –į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ–ī—č—Ä—É** ‚ÄĒ the `/sleep` endpoint demonstrating the single-threaded bottleneck, why per-request `thread::spawn` is wrong (resource exhaustion), the **thread pool** design, the `compiler-driven development` workflow, building `ThreadPool::new(size)` with `mpsc::channel` + `Arc<Mutex<Receiver>>` to share the receiver across workers, the `Worker` struct with id and `JoinHandle`, the `ThreadPool::execute(F: FnOnce() + Send + 'static)` API, the `Job = Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send + 'static>` type alias.
  - **20.3 –Ė–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ņ —ā–ĺ“õ—ā–į—É –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā–į–∑–į–Ľ–į—É** ‚ÄĒ the `Drop` trait on `ThreadPool`, dropping `sender` (closing the channel) so workers exit their `recv` loop with `Err`, joining all worker threads, the `Option::take` pattern for safely consuming `JoinHandle` from `&mut self`, the `take(2)` integration test demonstrating clean shutdown.
- All earlier-chapter terminology applied (web server ‚Üí –≤–Ķ–Ī-—Ā–Ķ—Ä–≤–Ķ—Ä, thread pool ‚Üí –į“ď—č–Ĺ-–∂–ł–Ĺ–į“ď—č, worker ‚Üí –∂“Ī–ľ—č—Ā—ą—č, graceful shutdown ‚Üí –∂–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ņ —ā–ĺ“õ—ā–į—É, request-line ‚Üí —Ā“Ī—Ä–į–Ĺ—č—Ā-–∂–ĺ–Ľ, status-line ‚Üí –∂–į“ď–ī–į–Ļ-–∂–ĺ–Ľ).

**Aggregate:** ~95 000 Kazakh words across 20 chapters, ~140 KB of structured prose; 1 543 sentence-level samples in `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`. Code blocks preserved verbatim from the original throughout. All terminology decisions documented in `data/world_core/programming_rust.jsonl` (110-entry curated glossary) and chapter-by-chapter changelogs above.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 19 chapters / 1 459 samples ‚Üí **20 chapters / 1 543 samples** (+84 from chapter 20).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** at 3 362 morphemes / 22 145 postings / 3 691 indexed samples ‚ÄĒ pack is at the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling. To take full advantage of all chapters in retrieval, switching the committed index to `--full` mode is the next architectural step.

### Tests + counters

- E2E threshold stays at ‚Č•490.
- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.

### Cadence

Twenty consecutive patches (v4.7.1 through v4.7.20) under ¬ę–ļ–į–∂–ī—É—é –≥–Ľ–į–≤—É —Ā—á–ł—ā–į—ā—Ć –∑–į –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence, no skips. The Rust Book translation thread is now closed.

### What's next (post-v4.7.20)

The Rust Book series is complete. Future v4.7.x patches will likely focus on:
- Switching the morpheme-index build to `--full` mode so all 1 543 rust_book sentences contribute to retrieval.
- Other curated knowledge domains (mathematics_basic, informatics_basic) that may benefit from similar chapter-by-chapter expansions.
- Pipeline tuning ‚ÄĒ the retrieval ranker now has substantial Rust-domain content to work with.

License & attribution: the Kazakh translation is offered under the same MIT/Apache-2.0 dual license as the original Rust Book, full attribution in `data/raw/rust_book_kk/LICENSE.md`.

## [4.7.19] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 19 (–Ė–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė—Ä—Ė–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä) translated, in pack

Nineteenth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 19 ‚ÄĒ Advanced Features ‚ÄĒ the broadest chapter of the book, surveying five distinct advanced topic areas: **`unsafe` Rust** (the five superpowers ‚ÄĒ dereferencing raw pointers `*const T` / `*mut T`, calling `unsafe fn`, accessing/modifying `static mut`, implementing an `unsafe trait`, accessing `union` fields; the FFI layer `extern "C"` block and `#[no_mangle]` for outbound; building safe abstractions over unsafe code with the `split_at_mut` worked example); **advanced traits** (associated types `type Item` vs generic `<T>` and why each fits, default generic type parameters with the `Add<Rhs = Self>` operator-overloading example, fully-qualified syntax `<Type as Trait>::method` for disambiguating same-named methods, supertraits with `OutlinePrint: fmt::Display`, the **newtype pattern** for working around the orphan rule); **advanced types** (newtype for type-safety wrappers `struct Years(i64)` / `struct Days(i64)`, type aliases with `type` keyword, the never type `!` and how it unifies with any type, dynamically-sized types `?Sized` and the implicit `Sized` bound on generics); **advanced functions and closures** (function pointers `fn(T) -> U` distinct from `Fn` traits, returning closures via `Box<dyn Fn(...) -> ...>` or `impl Fn(...) -> ...`); **macros** (declarative `macro_rules!` with the `vec!` walkthrough, procedural macros split into derive / attribute / function-like, the `proc-macro` crate convention with `syn` + `quote`).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_19.md` ‚ÄĒ ~6 500 words (the largest single-chapter translation), code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-19-specific terminology decisions: raw pointer ‚Üí **—ą–ł–ļ—Ė –ļ”©—Ä—Ā–Ķ—ā–ļ—Ė—ą**, FFI ‚Üí **—ą–Ķ—ā–Ķ–Ľ —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź–Ľ–į—Ä—č–Ĺ—č“£ –ł–Ĺ—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ą–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—Ė**, union ‚Üí **–ĺ–ī–į“õ**, associated type ‚Üí **–Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č —ā–ł–Ņ**, supertrait ‚Üí **–į—ā–į-—ā—Ä–Ķ–Ļ—ā**, newtype pattern ‚Üí **–∂–į“£–į—ā–ł–Ņ “Į–Ľ–≥—Ė—Ā—Ė**, type alias ‚Üí **—ā–ł–Ņ –Ī“Į—Ä–ļ–Ķ–Ĺ—ą–Ķ–≥—Ė**, never type ‚Üí **–Ķ—ą“õ–į—ą–į–Ĺ-—ā–ł–Ņ `!`**, dynamically-sized type ‚Üí **–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ ”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ľ–ī—Ė —ā–ł–Ņ**, function pointer ‚Üí **—Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź –ļ”©—Ä—Ā–Ķ—ā–ļ—Ė—ą—Ė**, declarative macro ‚Üí **–ī–Ķ–ļ–Ľ–į—Ä–į—ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė –ľ–į–ļ—Ä–ĺ—Ā**, procedural macro ‚Üí **–Ņ—Ä–ĺ—Ü–Ķ–ī—É—Ä–į–Ľ—č“õ –ľ–į–ļ—Ä–ĺ—Ā**, attribute macro ‚Üí **–į—ā—Ä–ł–Ī—É—ā –ľ–į–ļ—Ä–ĺ—Ā**, function-like macro ‚Üí **—Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź-—Ā–ł—Ź“õ—ā—č –ľ–į–ļ—Ä–ĺ—Ā**.

## [4.7.18] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 18 (“ģ–Ľ–≥—Ė–Ľ–Ķ—Ä –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —Ā–į–Ļ –ļ–Ķ–Ľ—ā—Ė—Ä—É) translated, in pack

Eighteenth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 18 ‚ÄĒ Patterns and Matching ‚ÄĒ covering pattern syntax across all the places it can appear in `Rust`: `match` arms, `if let` (with chained `else if let`), `while let` (the stack-popping example), `for` loops (with `enumerate` destructuring), `let` statements (irrefutable destructuring), function parameters (the `&(x, y): &(i32, i32)` example); the **refutability** distinction (irrefutable patterns always succeed and are required by `let`/`for`/parameters; refutable patterns may fail and are required by `if let`/`while let`/`match`, with the `let else` form for the early-return on failure idiom). Pattern syntax taxonomy: matching literals, named-variable patterns and shadowing inside `match` scopes, multiple patterns with `|`, range patterns `..=` (numeric and `char`), destructuring (struct, enum, tuple, deeply-nested combinations), ignoring values (`_` placeholder vs `_x` named-but-unused ‚ÄĒ and how `_` doesn't move ownership while `_x` does), `..` for ignoring remaining parts of structs/tuples, **match guards** (`Some(x) if x % 2 == 0`), and the `@` binding operator for combining a range check with capture.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_18.md` ‚ÄĒ ~4 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-18-specific terminology decisions: pattern ‚Üí **“Į–Ľ–≥—Ė** (already locked); refutable ‚Üí **–∂–ĺ“õ“õ–į —ą—č“ď–į—Ä—É“ď–į –ļ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—ā—Ė–Ĺ**; irrefutable ‚Üí **–∂–ĺ“õ“õ–į —ą—č“ď–į—Ä—É“ď–į –ļ–Ķ–Ľ–ľ–Ķ–Ļ—ā—Ė–Ĺ**; match guard ‚Üí **match —ą–į—Ä—ā—č**; `@` binding ‚Üí **`@` –Ī–į–Ļ–Ľ–į–Ĺ—č—Ā—ā—č—Ä—É**; destructuring ‚Üí **–Ī”©–Ľ—ą–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—É** (already locked since v4.7.3).

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 17 chapters / 1 289 samples ‚Üí **18 chapters / 1 339 samples** (+50 from chapter 18).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** ‚ÄĒ pack still at the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling.

## [4.7.17] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 17 (Rust-—ā—č“£ –ĺ–Ī—ä–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė-–Ī–į“ď—č—ā—ā–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė) translated, in pack

Seventeenth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 17 ‚ÄĒ Object-Oriented Programming Features of Rust ‚ÄĒ covering Rust's relationship to OOP: characteristics of OO languages and how Rust delivers (or deliberately doesn't deliver) each ‚ÄĒ encapsulation via `pub` keyword (`AveragedCollection` worked example with private fields and a public API maintaining an internal invariant); Rust's deliberate absence of inheritance and the trade-off reasoning (over-coupling, hierarchy rigidity), with default trait implementations and trait objects as the practical replacements; static vs dynamic polymorphism (generics with `<T: Trait>` for compile-time monomorphization vs `Box<dyn Trait>` trait objects for runtime dispatch); using **trait objects** for collections of mixed types (the GUI `Draw` / `Screen` example, `Vec<Box<dyn Draw>>`, vtable-based dynamic dispatch, the **object-safe trait** rules ‚ÄĒ methods on `&self`/`&mut self`, no generic parameters, no `Self` return, why `Clone` cannot be made into `Box<dyn Clone>`); implementing the **state pattern** (the blog-post lifecycle Draft ‚Üí PendingReview ‚Üí Published example), first in classical OOP style with `Box<dyn State>` and the `self: Box<Self>` consuming method pattern, then in idiomatic Rust style with each state as a separate type (`DraftPost` / `PendingReviewPost` / `Post`), with the trade-off discussion: classical OOP allows runtime extensibility; type-encoded states catch errors at compile time.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_17.md` ‚ÄĒ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-17-specific terminology decisions: object-oriented programming ‚Üí **–ĺ–Ī—ä–Ķ–ļ—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė-–Ī–į“ď—č—ā—ā–į–Ľ“ď–į–Ĺ –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į–Ľ–į—É**, encapsulation ‚Üí **—ā“Ī—Ā–į—É–Ľ–į—É**, inheritance ‚Üí **–ľ“Ī—Ä–į–≥–Ķ—Ä–Ľ—Ė–ļ**, polymorphism ‚Üí **–ļ”©–Ņ –Ņ—Ė—ą—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ**, dynamic dispatch ‚Üí **–ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ā–į—Ä–į—ā–Ņ–į**, static dispatch ‚Üí **—Ā—ā–į—ā–ł–ļ–į–Ľ—č“õ —ā–į—Ä–į—ā–Ņ–į**, vtable ‚Üí kept verbatim, object-safe ‚Üí **–Ĺ—č—Ā–į–Ĺ-“õ–į—É—Ė–Ņ—Ā—Ė–∑**, state pattern ‚Üí **–ļ“Į–Ļ “Į–Ľ–≥—Ė—Ā—Ė**, design pattern ‚Üí **–∂–ĺ–Ī–į–Ľ–į—É “Į–Ľ–≥—Ė—Ā—Ė**, trait object ‚Üí **—ā—Ä–Ķ–Ļ—ā-–Ĺ—č—Ā–į–Ĺ** (already locked since v4.7.0).

## [4.7.16] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 16 (“ö–ĺ—Ä“õ—č–Ĺ—č—ą—Ā—č–∑ “õ–į—ā–į—Ä–Ľ–į—Ā –ĺ—Ä—č–Ĺ–ī–į—É) translated, in pack

Sixteenth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 16 ‚ÄĒ Fearless Concurrency ‚ÄĒ covering Rust's compile-time-enforced concurrency primitives: spawning OS threads with `std::thread::spawn` and waiting for them with `JoinHandle::join`, the closure-with-`move` pattern for transferring ownership into a thread; message-passing concurrency via `std::sync::mpsc::channel` (transmitter `tx` / receiver `rx`, ownership transfer on `send`, blocking `recv` vs non-blocking `try_recv`, iterating `for received in rx`, multiple producers via `tx.clone()`); shared-state concurrency via `Mutex<T>` (the `lock()` API returning a `MutexGuard`, `Drop`-based auto-release, the `Arc<Mutex<T>>` combination for cross-thread shared mutable state with the 10-thread counter example); the `Send` and `Sync` marker traits as the type-system foundation that makes this all safe (the `Rc<T>` / `Arc<T>` distinction explained via these traits, `RefCell<T>` not being `Sync`, why manual `unsafe impl Send/Sync` is rare and dangerous).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_16.md` ‚ÄĒ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-16-specific terminology decisions: thread ‚Üí **–į“ď—č–Ĺ** (already locked); message passing ‚Üí **—Ö–į–Ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į –į—Ä“õ—č–Ľ—č –į–Ľ–ľ–į—Ā—É**; channel ‚Üí **–ļ–į–Ĺ–į–Ľ** (already); transmitter ‚Üí **–∂—Ė–Ī–Ķ—Ä—É—ą—Ė**; receiver ‚Üí **“õ–į–Ī—č–Ľ–ī–į—É—ą—č**; mutex ‚Üí kept as `Mutex` (the `Rust` type) plus the conceptual term **”©–∑–į—Ä–į —ć–ļ—Ā–ļ–Ľ—é–∑–ł–≤—ā—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ**; shared state ‚Üí **–ĺ—Ä—ā–į“õ –ļ“Į–Ļ**; atomic reference counting ‚Üí **–į—ā–ĺ–ľ–ī—č“õ —Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ-–Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É**; `Send` / `Sync` ‚Üí kept verbatim (marker trait names); deadlock ‚Üí **”©–∑–į—Ä–į —ā–ĺ—Ā“õ–į—É—č–Ľ**.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 15 chapters / 1 136 samples ‚Üí **16 chapters / 1 200 samples** (+64 from chapter 16).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** ‚ÄĒ pack still at the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling.

## [4.7.15] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 15 (–ź“õ—č–Ľ–ī—č —Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä) translated, in pack

Fifteenth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 15 ‚ÄĒ Smart Pointers ‚ÄĒ covering Rust's smart-pointer ecosystem and how each kind extends the basic reference model: `Box<T>` for heap allocation and recursive types (the cons-list example showing why a recursive enum's size is unbounded without `Box`); the `Deref` trait and how it enables `*` on smart pointers (writing a `MyBox<T>` from scratch and implementing `Deref::deref`), deref coercion as the compiler-driven chain `&MyBox<String>` ‚Üí `&String` ‚Üí `&str`; the `Drop` trait for cleanup logic that runs when a value goes out of scope, why `drop()` may not be called manually but `std::mem::drop` may be; `Rc<T>` for multiple ownership in single-threaded contexts (the cons-list example showing how cloning increments `strong_count`, why `Rc<T>` is read-only); `RefCell<T>` and the **interior mutability** pattern that defers borrow checking to runtime (the mock-object pattern, panic on rule violation); combining `Rc<RefCell<T>>` for the shared-ownership-with-mutation use case; reference cycles as a memory-leak shape Rust's ownership system does not prevent, and `Weak<T>` references that don't extend the lifetime of the pointed-to value (the parent-child node tree example with strong children + weak parent links).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_15.md` ‚ÄĒ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-15-specific terminology decisions: smart pointer ‚Üí **–į“õ—č–Ľ–ī—č —Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ** (already locked since v4.7.0), recursive type ‚Üí **—Ä–Ķ–ļ—É—Ä—Ā–ł–≤—ā—Ė —ā–ł–Ņ**, deref coercion ‚Üí **–ī–Ķref-–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä—É**, interior mutability ‚Üí **—Ė—ą–ļ—Ė ”©–∑–≥–Ķ—Ä–ľ–Ķ–Ľ—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ**, mock object ‚Üí **mock-–Ĺ—č—Ā–į–Ĺ**, reference count ‚Üí **—Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä —Ā–į–Ĺ–į“ď—č**, reference cycle ‚Üí **—Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ —Ü–ł–ļ–Ľ—č**, memory leak ‚Üí **–∂–į–ī –į“ď—č—ā–Ņ–į—Ā—č**, weak reference ‚Üí **”ô–Ľ—Ā—Ė–∑ —Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ**, `Rc::strong_count` / `Rc::weak_count` ‚Üí kept verbatim.

## [4.7.13] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 13 (–§—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–į–Ľ —ā—Ė–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ –ľ“Į–ľ–ļ—Ė–Ĺ–ī—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä: –ł—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—ā–ĺ—Ä–Ľ–į—Ä –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ –∂–į–Ī—É–Ľ–į—Ä) translated, in pack

Thirteenth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 13 ‚ÄĒ Functional Language Features: Iterators and Closures ‚ÄĒ covering Rust's two key functional-programming primitives: closures (anonymous functions that capture their environment, the three-tier `FnOnce` / `FnMut` / `Fn` trait hierarchy, `move` keyword for ownership transfer to a closure body, the `Vec::sort_by_key` worked example) and iterators (the `Iterator` trait and the `next` method, `iter` / `iter_mut` / `into_iter` distinction, lazy evaluation, consuming adapters like `sum` / `count` / `collect` vs producing adapters like `map` / `filter`, chaining `(1..=10).filter(...).map(...).sum()`, capturing closures inside iterator chains). Then refactoring the v4.7.12 minigrep using these tools (removing `clone` from `Config::build` by passing an iterator instead of a slice, condensing the `search` / `search_case_insensitive` functions to one-line iterator chains, the loops-vs-iterators discussion). Closes with the **zero-cost abstraction** explanation: iterator chains compile to assembly indistinguishable from hand-rolled loops; sometimes more efficient because the rigid abstraction shape gives the compiler stronger optimisation guarantees.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_13.md` ‚ÄĒ ~5 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-13-specific terminology decisions: closure ‚Üí **–∂–į–Ī—É** (already locked since v4.7.0 programming_rust glossary), capture ‚Üí **“Ī—Ā—ā–į—É**, `Fn`/`FnMut`/`FnOnce` ‚Üí **`Fn` / `FnMut` / `FnOnce` —ā—Ä–Ķ–Ļ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė** (transliterated, kept verbatim), iterator adapter ‚Üí **–ł—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—ā–ĺ—Ä-–Ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ—É—Ė—ą**, lazy evaluation ‚Üí **–Ľ–į–∑–į–Ļ –Ķ—Ā–Ķ–Ņ—ā–Ķ—É**, zero-cost abstraction ‚Üí **–Ĺ”©–Ľ–ī—Ė–ļ —ą—č“ď—č–Ĺ–ī—č –į–Ī—Ā—ā—Ä–į–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź**, consuming adapter ‚Üí **—ā“Ī—ā—č–Ĺ–į—ā—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ—É**, producing adapter ‚Üí **–∂–į“£–į –ł—ā–Ķ—Ä–į—ā–ĺ—Ä —ą—č“ď–į—Ä–į—ā—č–Ĺ –Ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ė–ľ–ī–Ķ—É**.

## [4.7.12] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 12 (–ö—Ė—Ä—Ė—Ā-—ą—č“ď—č—Ā –∂–ĺ–Ī–į—Ā—č: –ļ–ĺ–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī–į –∂–ĺ–Ľ—č –Ī–į“ď–ī–į—Ä–Ľ–į–ľ–į—Ā—č–Ĺ “õ“Ī—Ä—É) translated, in pack

Twelfth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 12 ‚ÄĒ An I/O Project: Building a Command Line Program (mini-grep) ‚ÄĒ the largest practical chapter that ties together everything from chapters 1‚Äď11 into one real working CLI program. Sections: 12.1 accepting command-line arguments via `std::env::args`; 12.2 reading a file with `std::fs::read_to_string`; 12.3 refactoring for modularity and error handling (separation of concerns, extracting `parse_config` and then a `Config` struct, the `Config::build` constructor pattern, fixing error handling with `Result` + `unwrap_or_else` + `eprintln!` + `process::exit`, extracting a `run` function, splitting code into a library crate `src/lib.rs`); 12.4 TDD development of the `search` function (writing a failing test first, implementing the minimum code to pass, then refactoring); 12.5 working with environment variables (`env::var("IGNORE_CASE")`, the `search_case_insensitive` companion function); 12.6 writing error messages to standard error instead of standard output (`eprintln!` vs `println!`, the Unix stdout/stderr separation, `> output.txt` redirection demonstration).

This is the practical chapter that demonstrates how all earlier chapters' concepts come together: modules, ownership, references, traits, error handling, tests ‚ÄĒ all in one ~150-line program.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_12.md` ‚ÄĒ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-12-specific terminology decisions: command-line argument ‚Üí **–ļ–ĺ–ľ–į–Ĺ–ī–į–Ľ—č“õ –∂–ĺ–Ľ –į—Ä–≥—É–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—ā—Ė**, separation of concerns ‚Üí **–∂–į—É–į–Ņ–ļ–Ķ—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ—ā–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė –Ī”©–Ľ—É**, test-driven development (TDD) ‚Üí **—ā–Ķ—Ā—ā–ļ–Ķ –Ĺ–Ķ–≥—Ė–∑–ī–Ķ–Ľ–≥–Ķ–Ĺ ”ô–∑—Ė—Ä–Ľ–Ķ—É**, standard output ‚Üí **—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä—ā—ā—č —ą—č“ď–į—Ä—É**, standard error ‚Üí **—Ā—ā–į–Ĺ–ī–į—Ä—ā—ā—č “õ–į—ā–Ķ**, environment variable ‚Üí **–ĺ—Ä—ā–į –į–Ļ–Ĺ—č–ľ–į–Ľ—č—Ā—č**, case-insensitive ‚Üí **”ô—Ä—Ė–Ņ —Ä–Ķ–≥–ł—Ā—ā—Ä—Ė–Ĺ –Ķ—Ā–ļ–Ķ—Ä–ľ–Ķ—É**, constructor ‚Üí **–ļ–ĺ–Ĺ—Ā—ā—Ä—É–ļ—ā–ĺ—Ä** (transliteration), trait object ‚Üí **—ā—Ä–Ķ–Ļ—ā-–Ĺ—č—Ā–į–Ĺ**.

## [4.7.10] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 10 (–Ė–į–Ľ–Ņ—č–Ľ–į–ľ–į —ā–ł–Ņ—ā–Ķ—Ä, —ā—Ä–Ķ–Ļ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ —ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ľ–Ķ—Ä–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė) translated, in pack

Tenth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 10 ‚ÄĒ Generic Types, Traits, and Lifetimes ‚ÄĒ the most theoretically dense chapter of the entire book. Three abstraction layers in one chapter: types (via generic type parameters), behaviour (via traits) and references' validity (via lifetimes).

### Translation

Three sections cover the full Rust abstraction stack:

- **10.1 –Ė–į–Ľ–Ņ—č–Ľ–į–ľ–į –ľ”ô–Ľ—Ė–ľ–Ķ—ā —ā“Į—Ä–Ľ–Ķ—Ä—Ė** ‚ÄĒ generics in functions (`largest<T: PartialOrd>`), structs (`Point<T>`, `Point<T, U>`), enums (`Option<T>`, `Result<T, E>`), and methods (`impl<T> Point<T>`, type-restricted `impl Point<f64>`); the **monomorphization** explanation ‚ÄĒ generic abstractions cost zero at runtime because the compiler emits a specialized copy per concrete type.
- **10.2 –Ę—Ä–Ķ–Ļ—ā—ā–Ķ—Ä: –ĺ—Ä—ā–į“õ —ā”ô—Ä—ā—Ė–Ņ—ā—Ė –į–Ĺ—č“õ—ā–į—É** ‚ÄĒ defining traits (`Summary`), implementing them on types (`impl Summary for NewsArticle`), the **orphan rule** (you can implement a trait on a type only if either the trait or the type is defined in your crate), default implementations and how they can call other trait methods, traits as parameters via `&impl Summary` shorthand vs `<T: Summary>` trait-bound syntax, multiple bounds with `+`, the `where` clause for readability, returning `impl Trait`, conditionally-implemented methods (`impl<T: Display + PartialOrd> Pair<T>`), blanket implementations (`impl<T: Display> ToString for T`).
- **10.3 –Ę—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ľ–Ķ—Ä–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė –į—Ä“õ—č–Ľ—č —Ā—Ė–Ľ—ā–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ľ–Ķ—Ä–ī—Ė —Ä–į—Ā—ā–į—É** ‚ÄĒ preventing dangling references, the borrow checker, generic lifetimes in functions (`fn longest<'a>(x: &'a str, y: &'a str) -> &'a str`), thinking in terms of lifetimes, lifetimes in struct definitions (`ImportantExcerpt<'a>`), the three lifetime elision rules, lifetimes in method definitions (`impl<'a> ImportantExcerpt<'a>`), the `'static` lifetime (string literals, when not to use it), generic types + trait bounds + lifetimes combined in one signature.

### Translation notes

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_10.md` ‚ÄĒ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-10-specific terminology decisions: monomorphization ‚Üí **–ľ–ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ–ĺ—Ä—Ą—ā–į—É**, default implementation ‚Üí **”ô–ī–Ķ–Ņ–ļ—Ė —Ė—Ā–ļ–Ķ –į—Ā—č—Ä—É**, blanket implementation ‚Üí **–∂–į–Ľ–Ņ—č —Ė—Ā–ļ–Ķ –į—Ā—č—Ä—É**, trait bound ‚Üí **—ā—Ä–Ķ–Ļ—ā —ą–Ķ–ļ—ā–Ķ—É—Ė**, where clause ‚Üí **where –ļ–Ľ–į—É–∑–į—Ā—č**, lifetime elision ‚Üí **—ā—Ė—Ä—ą—Ė–Ľ—Ė–ļ –ľ–Ķ—Ä–∑—Ė–ľ—Ė–Ĺ —ā“Į—Ā—Ė—Ä—Ė–Ņ –∂–į–∑—É**, orphan rule ‚Üí **–ĺ—Ä—Ą–į–Ĺ-–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–∂–Ķ**, `impl Trait` syntax ‚Üí kept as `impl Trait`.

### Pipeline impact

## [4.7.9] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 9 (“ö–į—ā–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė ”©“£–ī–Ķ—É) translated, in pack

Ninth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 9 ‚ÄĒ Error Handling ‚ÄĒ covering Rust's two-tier error model: **unrecoverable errors with `panic!`** (the panic message format, the array-indexing example, `RUST_BACKTRACE=1`, the `panic = "abort"` profile setting and unwind-vs-abort trade-off) and **recoverable errors with `Result<T, E>`** (the `Result` enum definition, `File::open` returning `Result<File, io::Error>`, matching on the error to take different actions, distinguishing error kinds via `error.kind()` and the deeply-nested-match trade-off, `unwrap` and `expect` as shortcuts for prototypes/tests, propagating errors with explicit `match`, the `?` operator and how it short-circuits to return `Err` from the function, the `?`-chained call style, the standard-library `fs::read_to_string` as the canonical fully-condensed form, error type conversion via the `From` trait, where `?` may be used (`Result`, `Option`, `main` returning `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>`)). Tarau ends with **when to panic vs when to return Result** guidelines (prototypes/tests, contract violations, parsing user input, trait-encoded invariants like the `Guess` 1‚Äď100 example).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_09.md` ‚ÄĒ ~4 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-9-specific terminology decisions: error propagation ‚Üí **“õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė —ā–į—Ä–į—ā—É**, error conversion ‚Üí **“õ–į—ā–Ķ–Ĺ—Ė —ā“Į—Ä–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ī—Ė—Ä—É**, backtrace ‚Üí **—ą–Ķ–≥—Ė–Ĺ—Ė—Ā —Ė–∑—Ė**, stack unwinding ‚Üí **—Ā—ā–Ķ–ļ –∂–į–ī—č–Ĺ –ļ–Ķ—Ä—Ė –į–Ļ–Ĺ–į–Ľ–ī—č—Ä—É**, abort ‚Üí **“Į–∑—É**, type alias ‚Üí **—ā–ł–Ņ –Ľ–į“õ–į–Ņ –į—ā—č** (deferred ‚ÄĒ not used in this chapter), panic ‚Üí **panic** (kept as `panic!` macro reference); `Result<T, E>` and `Option<T>` keep the v4.7.0/4.7.6-locked enam-–Ĺ“Ī—Ā“õ–į–Ľ–į—Ä—č terminology.

### Pipeline impact

## [4.7.8] ‚ÄĒ 2026-04-29 ‚ÄĒ Rust Book Chapter 8 (–Ė–į–Ľ–Ņ—č “Ī–∂—č–ľ–ī–į—Ä) translated, in pack (past committed-index ceiling)

Eighth chapter under ¬ę–≥–Ľ–į–≤–į = –Ņ–į—ā—á¬Ľ cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 8 ‚ÄĒ Common Collections ‚ÄĒ covering the three most-used standard-library collection types: `Vec<T>` (creating with `Vec::new` and `vec!` macro, updating via `push`, reading with `&v[i]` panic vs. `v.get(i)` `Option`, the borrow rule preventing concurrent index reads with `push` due to potential reallocation, iterating over `&v` and `&mut v` with `*i` dereferencing, storing multiple types via enum variants, drop semantics); `String` (UTF-8 commitment as the source of complexity, creating with `String::new` / `to_string` / `String::from`, updating with `push_str` / `push` / `+` / `format!`, why indexing is forbidden with the `–ó–ī—Ä–į–≤—Ā—ā–≤—É–Ļ—ā–Ķ` 24-byte example, byte-aligned slicing with `&s[a..b]` and panic on mid-codepoint cut, iterating with `chars` for Unicode scalars and `bytes` for raw bytes, why grapheme clusters require external crates); `HashMap<K, V>` (creating, `get` returning `Option<&V>` with `.copied().unwrap_or(0)` idiom, ownership transfer for non-`Copy` keys/values, three update strategies ‚ÄĒ `insert` overwriting, `entry().or_insert()` for missing-key insertion, the word-counter `*count += 1` pattern with mutable references, the SipHash default and DoS-resistance trade-off).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_08.md` ‚ÄĒ ~4 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-8-specific terminology decisions: collection ‚Üí **“Ī–∂—č–ľ** (already in lexicon), grapheme cluster ‚Üí **–≥—Ä–į—Ą–Ķ–ľ–į –ļ–Ľ–į—Ā—ā–Ķ—Ä—Ė**, hash function ‚Üí **—Ö—ć—ą —Ą—É–Ĺ–ļ—Ü–ł—Ź—Ā—č**, dereference ‚Üí **–ī–Ķ—Ä–Ķ—Ą–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–Ĺ—Ā**, byte boundary ‚Üí **–Ī–į–Ļ—ā —ą–Ķ–≥—Ė**, Unicode scalar ‚Üí **Unicode —Ā–ļ–į–Ľ—Ź—Ä-–ľ”ô–Ĺ**, SipHash ‚Üí **SipHash** (kept as-is, named algorithm).

Chapter-8 sentences live in the pack file (auditable, `--full`-mode ready) but do not contribute to the committed-mode morpheme index.

### Architectural note

We are now past the committed-mode morpheme-index ceiling for the rust_book pack. Future chapters continue to grow `data/raw/rust_book_kk/` and `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json` (auditable record + ready for full-mode reindex), but the committed `data/retrieval/morpheme_index.json` does not change for rust_book content. To take advantage of all chapters in retrieval, a follow-up patch will either (a) raise the per-pack limit specifically for rust_book, or (b) switch to `--full` mode for the committed index. Decision deferred until the chapter set is more complete.
